  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant. Business justification: Tenants exercising right-of-first-refusal or purchase options become named deal parties in a transaction. deal_party.party_name is a denormalized field; a direct FK to tenant enables ROFR tracking, cl']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute dollar amount of commission allocated to this party for the transaction. Derived from the commission split percentage applied to the gross commission, stored for audit and 1099 reporting purposes.']
  commission_split_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of the total transaction commission allocated to this party, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%). Applicable for broker and agent roles. Drives commission disbursement and 1099-MISC reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this deal party association record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM for the transaction documents executed by this party. Enables audit trail of digital signature events and document execution status per party.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s role and obligations in the transaction became legally effective. May differ from signature_date for parties added via amendment or assignment.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal entity classification of the party. Determines applicable tax treatment, reporting requirements (e.g., FIRPTA, 1099-S), and deal structuring considerations. Values: individual, corporation, REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust), trust, partnership, LLC.']
  firpta_withholding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount withheld from disposition proceeds under FIRPTA for foreign sellers. Applicable only when is_foreign_person is true. Reported on IRS Form 8288.']
  is_1099s_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party\'s proceeds from the real estate transaction must be reported to the IRS on Form 1099-S. Typically true for sellers receiving proceeds above the IRS threshold.']
  is_foreign_person boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is a foreign person or entity subject to FIRPTA (Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act) withholding requirements. When true, triggers mandatory IRS withholding on disposition proceeds.']
  is_primary_party boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is the primary representative for their role in the transaction (e.g., lead buyer among multiple buyers, primary lender). Used to resolve reporting and notification routing when multiple parties share the same role.']
  lender_loan_number varchar [note: 'Loan reference number assigned by the lender party for the financing associated with this transaction. Applicable when party_role is \'lender\'. Used for CMBS/RMBS tracking and debt instrument reconciliation.']
  loan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financing instrument provided by the lender party. Applicable when party_role is \'lender\'. Drives LTV (Loan-to-Value) ratio analysis, DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) modeling, and regulatory capital treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conventional|FHA|VA|CMBS|bridge|mezzanine|construction — promote to reference product]']
  mailing_address varchar [note: 'Full mailing address for the party used for closing document delivery, 1099 tax form mailing, and legal notice service. Includes street, city, state, and ZIP.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for transaction coordinators and asset managers to capture deal-specific context about this party\'s role, special conditions, or instructions not captured in structured fields (e.g., POA restrictions, trust certification requirements).']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this party\'s involvement in the transaction. Tracks whether the party is actively engaged, has withdrawn, is pending confirmation, has confirmed participation, or has declined.']
  party_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the party used for transaction correspondence, DocuSign CLM execution, and closing coordination.']
  party_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party as it appears on the purchase agreement, closing statement, or title instrument. Used for 1099-S reporting, ALTA title commitments, and deed preparation.']
  party_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the party for transaction coordination and closing communications.']
  party_role varchar [note: 'The functional role this party plays in the transaction. Drives commission allocation, 1099-S reporting obligations, and deal workflow routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: buyer|seller|buyers_broker|sellers_broker|lender|title_company|escrow_agent|qualified_intermediary|attorney|appraiser|guarantor — promote to reference product]']
  proceeds_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross proceeds allocated to this party from the transaction closing, as reported on IRS Form 1099-S. Applicable to seller parties. Used for tax reporting and settlement reconciliation.']
  qi_exchange_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the Qualified Intermediary (QI) for a 1031 like-kind exchange transaction. Applicable when party_role is \'qualified_intermediary\'. Required for IRS Section 1031 compliance tracking and exchange deadline monitoring.']
  representation_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the agency relationship for broker parties: exclusive buyer/seller representation, non-exclusive, dual agency (representing both sides), or transaction broker (facilitating without fiduciary duty). Governs disclosure obligations under NAR Code of Ethics.']
  salesforce_contact_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this party in Salesforce CRM (Tenant and Prospect Management; Deal Pipeline). Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse deal_party record and the CRM contact or account record.']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date on which this party executed (signed) the primary transaction document (purchase agreement, closing statement, or deed). Used for contract formation analysis and closing timeline tracking.']
  signing_authority varchar [note: 'The capacity in which the party is authorized to execute transaction documents. Determines document execution requirements and legal validity of signatures on purchase agreements, deeds, and closing statements.']
  tax_id_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax identification number provided: SSN (Social Security Number), EIN (Employer Identification Number), ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number), or foreign_tin for non-US entities. Determines IRS withholding and reporting rules.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal Tax Identification Number (TIN), Employer Identification Number (EIN), or Social Security Number (SSN) for the party. Required for IRS 1099-S and 1099-MISC reporting at closing. Stored in encrypted form.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this deal party association record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which this party formally withdrew from or was removed from the transaction. Populated when participation_status is \'withdrawn\' or \'declined\'. Used for deal timeline and contingency analysis.']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing all parties and their roles in a property transaction — buyer, seller, buyer\'s broker, seller\'s broker, lender, title company, escrow agent, qualified intermediary (QI), attorney, appraiser, and guarantor. Records role type, entity classification (individual, corporation, REIT, trust, partnership, LLC), contact reference (FK to owner or external party), participation status, and commission split percentage where applicable. Enables multi-party deal tracking, commission allocation, and 1099-S reporting.'
}

Table transaction.offer {
  offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for offer']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property against which this offer is submitted.']
  bpo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.bpo. Business justification: Buyer agents and lenders commission BPOs to validate offer pricing before submission. Named process: \'offer price validation via BPO\'. A real estate professional would expect the offer to reference th']
  buyer_owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner. Business justification: Owner acquisition pipeline reporting, proof-of-funds verification against owner accreditation records, and offer-to-close conversion tracking by owner entity require linking offers to the submitting o']
  buyer_representation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.buyer_representation. Business justification: NAR rules (effective Aug 2024) require a signed buyer representation agreement before submitting offers. Linking offer to buyer_representation enables compliance verification, commission structure val']
  cma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cma. Business justification: A CMA is the primary tool buyer and listing agents use to determine offer price. Named process: \'offer price support via CMA\'. Every real estate agent runs a CMA before submitting an offer; linking th']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Multi-currency offer comparison for cross-border property transactions requires normalized currency reference. Listing brokers and sellers need to compare competing offers in a consistent currency for']
  fair_housing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.fair_housing_record. Business justification: A specific offer may be the subject of a fair housing complaint (e.g., discriminatory rejection). Linking offer to fair_housing_record enables compliance tracking of fair housing matters at the offer ']
  investment_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.investment_deal. Business justification: Investment deal pipeline reporting requires tracking which offers have been submitted for each deal. IC committees review offer history per investment_deal to assess pricing and competition. No existi']
  lead_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.lead. Business justification: Buyer offer-to-lead traceability: real estate CRM processes require linking a submitted offer back to the originating marketing lead to measure lead quality, channel effectiveness, and offer conversio']
  listing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active property listing (MLS or internal) against which this offer was submitted.']
  listing_syndication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.listing_syndication. Business justification: Syndication-to-offer conversion tracking: a core real estate digital marketing metric is which syndication platform generated each offer. Offer already has listing_id (brokerage) but not the specific ']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Land sale offers are made on specific parcels, not improved assets. offer has asset_id and unit_id but no parcel_id. A land broker submitting an offer on raw land must reference the parcel directly. T']
  parent_offer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking a counter-offer or revised offer back to the original offer record. Enables reconstruction of the full negotiation chain for a given property transaction.']
  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.property_sale. Business justification: An accepted offer leads to the creation of a property_sale record. offer currently has no FK to property_sale. Adding property_sale_id (nullable) links the accepted offer to the resulting sale transac']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Real estate offers must comply with fair housing regulatory obligations and state disclosure mandates. Linking offer to the governing regulatory_obligation documents which compliance rule governs the ']
  showing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.showing. Business justification: Brokerage conversion funnel reporting and agent performance reviews require tracking which showing generated which offer. This showing-to-offer conversion is a named business process in real estate br']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Offers are made in the context of a transaction type (standard, short sale, REO, auction) which governs offer expiry rules, required contingencies, loi_required, and broker_commission_applicable. Buye']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: In condo, multifamily, and mixed-use transactions, offers are made on specific units. Linking offer.unit_id → property.unit enables unit-level offer tracking, absorption analysis, and MLS unit-level r']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which the seller formally accepted this offer, creating a binding purchase agreement. Triggers the due diligence and inspection contingency period clocks.']
  appraisal_contingency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer includes an appraisal contingency, allowing the buyer to renegotiate or withdraw if the property appraises below the offer price. Relevant to Appraisal Institute and IVSC valuation standards.']
  buyer_agent_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued real estate license number of the buyer\'s agent. Required for regulatory compliance and commission disbursement verification.']
  buyer_agent_name varchar [note: 'The name of the licensed real estate agent or broker representing the buyer in this transaction. Used for commission split calculations and co-broker agreement tracking.']
  competing_offer_count varchar [note: 'The number of competing offers disclosed by the seller or known to the listing agent at the time this offer was submitted. Used in multi-offer analytics and negotiation strategy reporting.']
  competing_offer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seller has disclosed the existence of one or more competing offers on the same property at the time this offer was submitted. Supports multi-offer scenario management and deal pipeline analytics.']
  counter_offer_number varchar [note: 'Sequential counter indicating which round of negotiation this offer record represents (1 = original offer, 2 = first counter, 3 = second counter, etc.). Supports negotiation history tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this offer record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'The unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM for the digitally executed offer document. Enables traceability between the offer record and the executed contract lifecycle management system.']
  due_diligence_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days requested by the buyer for due diligence activities including title review, environmental assessment, financial analysis, and physical inspection. Particularly relevant for commercial real estate (CRE) transactions.']
  earnest_money_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The good-faith deposit amount offered by the buyer to demonstrate commitment to the transaction. Held in escrow pending closing or offer disposition.']
  earnest_money_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the buyer must deliver the earnest money deposit to the escrow or title company.']
  escalation_ceiling_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum purchase price the buyer is willing to pay under the escalation clause. The offer will not escalate beyond this ceiling regardless of competing offers.']
  escalation_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer contains an escalation clause that automatically increases the offer price above competing offers up to a defined ceiling. Critical for multi-offer scenario management.']
  escalation_increment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount by which the offer price will automatically escalate above a competing offer when an escalation clause is active. Null when escalation_clause_flag is false.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date and time by which the seller must respond to the offer before it automatically expires. Critical for multi-offer scenario management.']
  financing_contingency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum loan amount the buyer is seeking to finance. If the buyer cannot secure financing up to this amount, the contingency allows withdrawal without forfeiting earnest money. Supports LTV (Loan-to-Value) ratio analysis.']
  financing_type varchar [note: 'The type of financing the buyer intends to use to fund the purchase. Distinguishes all-cash offers from financed offers and identifies loan program type (e.g., FHA, VA, ARM — Adjustable Rate Mortgage, Conventional).']
  inspection_contingency_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days from offer acceptance during which the buyer has the right to conduct property inspections and withdraw without penalty if findings are unsatisfactory.']
  inspection_contingency_end_date date [note: 'The absolute calendar date on which the inspection contingency period expires. Derived from offer acceptance date plus inspection_contingency_days; stored explicitly for operational tracking.']
  is_1031_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the buyer intends to use this acquisition as part of a 1031 like-kind exchange to defer capital gains taxes. Affects transaction timeline and documentation requirements.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio expressed as a percentage, derived from the financing contingency amount relative to the offer price. Key underwriting metric used in investment analysis and lender qualification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional terms, special conditions, personal property inclusions/exclusions, or negotiation notes associated with this offer that are not captured in structured fields.']
  offer_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the buyer formally submitted the purchase offer. Principal business event date for the offer lifecycle.']
  offer_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the offer, used in correspondence, Salesforce CRM deal tracking, and closing documentation. Format: OFF-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase offer. Tracks progression from initial submission through counter, acceptance, rejection, or buyer withdrawal. Drives deal pipeline reporting in Salesforce CRM.']
  pre_approval_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum loan amount for which the buyer has received a lender pre-approval letter. Validates the buyer\'s financial capacity relative to the offer price.']
  price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross purchase price proposed by the buyer in the offer. Base monetary amount before any concessions or adjustments. Used in PSF (Per Square Foot) pricing analytics and comparable sales benchmarking via CoStar.']
  price_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'The offered purchase price expressed on a Per Square Foot (PSF) basis, calculated from offer_price divided by the property\'s GLA or NLA. Enables direct comparison against CoStar comparable sales benchmarks and market PSF rates.']
  proof_of_funds_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the buyer has provided and the listing agent has verified proof of funds or mortgage pre-approval sufficient to support the offer price. Critical qualifier for offer strength assessment.']
  proposed_closing_date date [note: 'The target closing date proposed by the buyer in the offer. Used for deal timeline planning and pipeline forecasting in Salesforce CRM.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for offer rejection or withdrawal. Used in deal pipeline analytics and negotiation strategy reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_too_low|competing_offer_accepted|contingencies|financing_concerns|buyer_withdrew|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  sale_contingency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer is contingent on the buyer successfully selling their existing property before closing. A key risk factor in multi-offer scenario evaluation.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM Opportunity record ID linked to this offer, enabling bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM deal pipeline.']
  seller_concessions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar value of concessions requested from the seller in this offer, such as closing cost credits, repair allowances, or Tenant Improvement (TI) contributions. Reduces the net effective purchase price.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this offer record was last modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Records individual purchase offers submitted on a property, capturing offer price, offer date, financing contingency amount, inspection contingency period, closing date proposed, earnest money offered, escalation clause details, offer status (submitted, countered, accepted, rejected, withdrawn), and competing offer indicator. Supports multi-offer scenario management and deal pipeline tracking in Salesforce CRM.'
}

Table transaction.escrow_account {
  escrow_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the escrow account record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset associated with this escrow account, enabling property-level reconciliation and reporting.']

This link normalizes the denormalized bank fields on escrow_account and enables escrow bank reconciliation, a required']

Business justification: Repair holdback escrow accounts are established specifically to fund post-closing capital improvement projects. The escrow account must reference the capex project to define fund release conditions an']
  capital_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.capital_call. Business justification: Capital call wire proceeds are deposited into the acquisition escrow account. Investment operations teams reconcile capital call funded amounts against escrow deposits to confirm equity is in place be']
  closing_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.closing_statement. Business justification: An escrow account is reconciled and closed at the same closing event documented by the closing statement. escrow_account already has property_sale_id → property_sale, but adding closing_statement_id c']

Business justification: Trust account reconciliation, state escrow law compliance, and RESPA reporting require normalized currency reference for escrow accounts. Multi-currency escrow balances require validated currency for ']
  financing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.financing. Linking escrow_account to the financing record connects the escro']
  investment_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.investment_deal. Business justification: Escrow accounts are opened specifically for investment deal closings. Investment deal tracking requires knowing which escrow account holds earnest money and closing funds for each deal — standard for ']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner. Business justification: Escrow accounts are opened on behalf of a specific owner party. Linking to owner.owner supports disbursement authorization, FIRPTA withholding compliance, and 1031 exchange qualified intermediary trac']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Escrow accounts for land sales are opened against specific parcels. escrow_account has asset_id but no parcel_id. Escrow officers managing land transaction escrows must reference the parcel for proper']
  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent property transaction (acquisition, disposition, or sale event) that this escrow account supports.']
  purchase_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.purchase_agreement. Business justification: An escrow account is opened upon execution of a purchase agreement. The PSA defines earnest money amounts, release conditions, and closing timeline that govern the escrow. Linking escrow_account direc']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Linking escrow_account to the regulatory_filing documents the compliance s']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: RESPA Section 10 and state escrow trust account laws impose specific regulatory obligations on real estate escrow accounts. Linking escrow_account to the governing regulatory_obligation documents the ']
  tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: Mortgage escrow impound accounts hold property tax reserves calculated from the tax assessment. Named process: \'property tax impound escrow calculation\'. The impound_tax_amount on escrow_account is di']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential and condo escrow accounts are unit-specific. escrow_account has asset_id but no unit_id. Escrow officers managing condo sales must reference the specific unit for HOA fee proration, unit-l']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Repair holdback escrow accounts are established to fund specific work orders post-closing. The escrow account references the work order to define fund release conditions and track completion — a named']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known escrow account number assigned by the escrow holder or title company (e.g., Yardi Voyager trust account reference). Used for reconciliation with AP/AR trust accounts.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the escrow account: open (active and accepting funds), pending_close (disbursements in progress), closed (fully disbursed and reconciled), suspended (temporarily frozen pending resolution), or cancelled (terminated without completion).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the escrow account by its business purpose: purchase (earnest money and closing funds), impound (tax and insurance reserves), reserve (capital reserve), 1031_exchange (tax-deferred exchange proceeds), construction (draw-based development funding), or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase|impound|reserve|1031_exchange|construction|other — promote to reference product]']
  actual_closing_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the escrow account was closed and all funds were fully disbursed. Null until closure is complete.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total brokerage commission amount to be disbursed from escrow at closing, covering both listing and buyer\'s agent sides as specified in the commission instructions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this escrow account record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail start point for SOX compliance and data lineage.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current fund balance in the escrow account reflecting all deposits received and disbursements made to date. Reconciled against Yardi Voyager trust account ledger.']
  earnest_money_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of earnest money deposited by the buyer into escrow as a good-faith deposit per the purchase agreement. Tracked separately for contingency release and forfeiture analysis.']
  earnest_money_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the earnest money deposit was received and confirmed in the escrow account.']
  earnest_money_status varchar [note: 'Current disposition status of the earnest money deposit: pending (not yet received), received (in escrow), applied (credited toward purchase price at closing), refunded (returned to buyer), or forfeited (retained by seller upon buyer default).']
  escrow_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the escrow holder entity (title company, escrow company, or attorney) responsible for managing and disbursing funds. Corresponds to the escrow_company field in property_sale.']
  escrow_officer_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the escrow officer for communication and document delivery purposes.']
  escrow_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual escrow officer or closing agent at the escrow holder entity who is responsible for managing this account.']
  escrow_officer_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number of the escrow officer for transaction coordination.']
  exchange_deadline_date date [note: 'The IRS-mandated deadline by which replacement property must be identified (45-day rule) or acquired (180-day rule) for a 1031 exchange. Null for non-exchange accounts.']
  expected_closing_date date [note: 'The anticipated date on which all escrow conditions will be satisfied and final disbursements will be made. Used for pipeline tracking and settlement scheduling.']
  impound_insurance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount held in impound/reserve within the escrow account for property insurance premium obligations. Applicable for impound-type escrow accounts.']
  impound_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount held in impound/reserve within the escrow account for property tax obligations. Applicable for impound-type escrow accounts associated with mortgage servicing.']
  is_1031_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this escrow account is associated with an IRS Section 1031 tax-deferred exchange transaction, requiring a qualified intermediary and strict timeline compliance.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the escrow account balance was reconciled against the Yardi Voyager trust account ledger and bank statement. Critical for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  loan_payoff_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount required to satisfy and discharge the seller\'s existing mortgage or deed of trust from escrow proceeds at closing.']
  net_proceeds_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount to be disbursed to the seller after deducting loan payoffs, commissions, title fees, transfer taxes, and other closing costs from the purchase price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for escrow officer or transaction coordinator notes regarding special instructions, exceptions, amendments, or conditions not captured in structured fields.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial fund balance at the time the escrow account was opened, typically representing the earnest money deposit or initial impound amount.']
  opening_date date [note: 'The date on which the escrow account was formally opened and became active for receiving funds. Marks the start of the escrow lifecycle.']
  purchase_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed-upon contract purchase price for the property transaction that this escrow account supports. Used to validate that total funds received are sufficient for closing.']
  qualified_intermediary_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the qualified intermediary holding exchange proceeds for a 1031 exchange escrow. Required by IRS regulations to maintain tax-deferred status.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current reconciliation status of the escrow account against the trust account ledger: reconciled (balances match), unreconciled (discrepancy identified), in_progress (reconciliation underway), or exception (requires manual resolution).']
  release_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current authorization status for fund release from escrow: pending (awaiting authorization), authorized (all parties have approved disbursement), partially_authorized (some disbursements approved), or rejected (authorization denied).']
  release_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the conditions that must be satisfied before escrow funds can be released or disbursed (e.g., clear title, financing approval, inspection sign-off, COO issuance). Sourced from DocuSign CLM escrow instructions.']
  title_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total title insurance premium and title-related fees (search, examination, endorsements) to be disbursed from escrow at closing per the ALTA settlement statement.']
  total_deposits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all funds deposited into the escrow account since opening, including earnest money, loan proceeds, and buyer closing funds.']
  total_disbursements decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all funds disbursed from the escrow account since opening, including commissions, title fees, transfer taxes, loan payoffs, and net proceeds to seller.']
  transfer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'State and local real estate transfer tax amount to be collected and remitted from escrow at closing. Varies by jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this escrow account record was last modified, supporting change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  yardi_trust_account_ref varchar [note: 'The trust account reference number in Yardi Voyager AP/AR module used for reconciliation of escrow fund movements against the general ledger trust account.']

  Note: 'Manages escrow accounts and the complete ledger of fund movements for property transactions — earnest money deposits, impound holds, disbursements (commissions, title fees, transfer taxes, loan payoffs, net proceeds), and releases. Captures escrow account number, escrow holder, escrow type (purchase, impound, reserve), opening balance, current balance, and account status. Each fund event records date, payee/payor, amount, payment method, reference number, authorization status, and release conditions. Provides a complete audit trail from account opening through final disbursement. Integrates with Yardi Voyager AP/AR for trust account reconciliation.'
}

  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent property sale or acquisition transaction associated with this escrow disbursement. Links disbursement to the originating deal event.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: FIRPTA withholding disbursements to the IRS require regulatory filings (Form 8288-A). Transfer tax disbursements require state filings. Linking escrow_disbursement to the regulatory_filing documents t']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Escrow disbursements for FIRPTA withholding, transfer taxes, and remediation holdbacks are governed by specific regulatory obligations. Linking escrow_disbursement to the governing regulatory_obligati']
  remediation_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.remediation_action. Business justification: Environmental remediation holdbacks are disbursed from escrow upon completion of specific remediation actions. Linking escrow_disbursement to the remediation_action documents which remediation trigger']
  tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: Escrow disbursements for property tax payments reference the specific tax assessment to confirm the correct amount and installment. Named process: \'property tax escrow disbursement reconciliation\'. Se']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Post-closing escrow disbursements pay for specific work orders (repair holdbacks, punch-list items, warranty work). The disbursement must reference the work order for GL reconciliation, vendor payment']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status indicating whether the disbursement has been authorized by the required approving parties (e.g., escrow officer, title company, lender). Supports dual-control authorization requirements and SOX financial controls.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disbursement was formally authorized by the approving party. Provides a precise audit trail event for SOX compliance and escrow reconciliation.']
  bank_account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Masked bank account number of the payee\'s receiving account, showing only the last four digits (e.g., ****1234). Stored for reconciliation and audit purposes without exposing full PCI-sensitive account data.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number of the payee\'s receiving financial institution. Required for ACH and wire transfer processing. Nine-digit numeric code identifying the destination bank.']
  commission_basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross sale price or transaction value upon which the commission rate is applied to calculate the commission disbursement amount. Applicable when disbursement_type is \'commission\'. Supports commission audit and RESPA Section 8 compliance.']
  commission_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Brokerage commission rate expressed as a decimal percentage of the sale price (e.g., 0.0300 = 3.00%). Applicable only when disbursement_type is \'commission\'. Used for commission reconciliation and broker reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this escrow disbursement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX record retention requirements.']
  disbursement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount released from escrow to the payee in this disbursement event. Represents the base amount before any withholding or adjustments. Expressed in the transaction currency.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which funds were released from the escrow account to the payee. Represents the principal real-world event date for this disbursement. Aligns with closing date or post-closing settlement schedule.']
  disbursement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for escrow officer or closing agent notes regarding special circumstances, conditions, or instructions associated with this disbursement. May include holdback conditions, lender instructions, or dispute notations.']
  disbursement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this disbursement by the escrow company or title company. Used for reconciliation with closing statements and Yardi Voyager AR/AP records.']
  disbursement_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the disbursement. Tracks progression from pending authorization through final release or voiding. Supports audit trail and reconciliation reporting.']
  disbursement_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the nature of the disbursement. Identifies whether funds represent a brokerage commission, title/settlement fee, transfer tax, mortgage loan payoff, net sale proceeds to seller, recording fee, prorated property taxes, Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance, or other closing cost. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commission|title_fee|transfer_tax|loan_payoff|net_proceeds|recording_fee|prorated_taxes|tenant_improvement|other — promote to reference product]']
  escrow_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the licensed escrow officer or closing agent responsible for processing and releasing this disbursement. Required for audit trail, regulatory compliance, and ALTA closing disclosure documentation.']
  holdback_release_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disbursement represents the release of a previously held-back escrow amount (True). Holdbacks are common in commercial transactions for repair escrows, environmental remediation reserves, or tenant improvement allowances.']
  is_1031_exchange_proceeds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disbursement represents proceeds being directed to a Qualified Intermediary (QI) as part of an IRS Section 1031 like-kind exchange. When True, funds must be held by the QI and not constructively received by the seller.']
  is_post_closing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disbursement was made after the official closing date (True) or at closing (False). Post-closing disbursements may include holdback releases, prorated adjustments, or deferred commission payments.']
  loan_payoff_reference varchar [note: 'Lender-assigned loan number or payoff statement reference for the mortgage being satisfied by this disbursement. Used to confirm lien release and coordinate with title company for deed recording.']
  net_disbursement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount actually paid to the payee after deducting any withholding amounts from the gross disbursement amount. Represents the actual funds transferred. Calculated as disbursement_amount minus withholding_amount.']
  payee_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual, company, or institution receiving the disbursed funds. May be a broker, title company, lender, seller, buyer, government entity, or service provider. Required for closing statement reconciliation and 1099 reporting.']
  payee_tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal Tax Identification Number (TIN), Employer Identification Number (EIN), or Social Security Number (SSN) of the payee. Required for IRS 1099-S real estate proceeds reporting and backup withholding compliance.']
  payee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payee entity receiving the disbursement. Distinguishes between brokers (commission recipients), lenders (loan payoff), sellers (net proceeds), buyers (refunds), title companies, government agencies (transfer tax), and other service providers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broker|lender|seller|buyer|title_company|government|service_provider|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or mechanism used to transfer the disbursed funds to the payee. Common methods include wire transfer, ACH electronic transfer, cashier\'s check, or certified check. Critical for bank reconciliation and fraud prevention controls.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank-assigned or system-generated reference number for the payment instrument used (e.g., wire confirmation number, ACH trace number, check number). Used for bank reconciliation and payment tracing.']
  qualified_intermediary_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Qualified Intermediary (QI) entity receiving 1031 exchange proceeds on behalf of the seller. Applicable when is_1031_exchange_proceeds is True. Required for IRS Section 1031 compliance documentation.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date on which a disbursed amount was returned to the escrow account due to a failed payment, rejected wire, or stale check. Populated only when disbursement_status is \'returned\'. Triggers re-disbursement workflow.']
  title_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the title company or settlement agent managing the escrow account from which this disbursement is made. Provides institutional context for the disbursement and links to ALTA title policy records.']
  transfer_tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental jurisdiction (state, county, or municipality) to which transfer tax disbursements are remitted. Applicable when disbursement_type is \'transfer_tax\'. Required for tax compliance and regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this escrow disbursement record was last modified. Tracks status changes, authorization updates, and corrections. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a disbursement was voided or cancelled. Populated only when disbursement_status is \'voided\'. Required for audit trail integrity and SOX internal control documentation.']
  withholding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount withheld from the gross disbursement for tax withholding obligations, such as FIRPTA (Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act) withholding on foreign sellers or backup withholding. Reduces the net amount actually paid to the payee.']
  yardi_disbursement_ref varchar [note: 'Source system reference identifier from Yardi Voyager Accounts Payable or Accounts Receivable module corresponding to this disbursement record. Enables bi-directional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']

  Note: 'Records individual disbursement events from an escrow account, including disbursement date, payee, disbursement amount, disbursement type (commission, title fee, transfer tax, loan payoff, net proceeds), payment method, reference number, and authorization status. Provides a complete audit trail of all funds released from escrow at or after closing.'
}

Table owner.owner {
  owner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each property owner or investor entity in the master registry. Primary key for the owner data product, sourced from Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  domicile_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Owner domicile country drives FATCA/CRS classification, foreign ownership restrictions, withholding tax rates, and tax treaty eligibility. Compliance officers and relationship managers require this li']
  domicile_geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Investor domicile geographic hierarchy is used in AML/KYC geographic risk scoring, investor concentration reporting by market, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping. A real estate domain expert expects ']
  functional_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Owner\'s functional/reporting currency drives multi-currency distribution calculations, K-1 preparation, and investor reporting. Real estate fund administrators must know each owner\'s functional curren']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Owner domicile determines which jurisdiction\'s KYC, FATCA, CRS, and AML regulations apply. Compliance officers use this link to identify applicable regulatory obligations per owner for investor onboar']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Investor classification by market segment (core, value-add, opportunistic) drives deal matching, IRR benchmarking, and portfolio reporting. Relationship managers use this to align owner investment pre']
  mri_investor_id varchar [note: 'Source system investor identifier assigned in MRI Software\'s Investment Management module. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking between MRI Software and the Databricks silver layer master registry.']
  accredited_investor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner entity qualifies as an accredited investor under SEC Regulation D. True = meets net worth or income thresholds; False = does not qualify. Determines eligibility for private placement offerings and Regulation D exempt securities.']
  aum_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier based on the owner entity\'s total Assets Under Management (AUM). Used for investor segmentation, relationship management prioritization, and fee schedule assignment. Micro = <$10M; Small = $10M-$100M; Mid = $100M-$1B; Large = $1B-$10B; Mega = >$10B.']
  bank_account_ref varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the owner entity\'s designated bank account for distribution payments and capital call receipts. Full account details are stored in the secure payment vault; this field holds only the reference token or last-4 identifier for operational use.']
  beneficial_owner_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entity is identified as a beneficial owner (i.e., the natural person who ultimately owns or controls the entity) per FinCEN Customer Due Diligence rules. True = beneficial owner identified and disclosed; False = entity is an intermediary or nominee. Critical for AML compliance.']
  beneficial_ownership_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of beneficial ownership held by this entity in the associated investment vehicle or property. Used for FinCEN reporting (25% threshold rule), equity distribution calculations, and waterfall modeling. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25%).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the owner record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the authoritative audit trail creation marker for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  distribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which investment distributions (income and/or return of capital) are made to the owner entity. Drives cash flow scheduling in Yardi Voyager AR module and investor reporting cycles.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for receiving investment distributions. Wire Transfer = international/domestic wire; ACH = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; Check = paper check; DRIP = Distribution Reinvestment Plan (reinvest distributions into additional units).']
  domicile_state varchar [note: 'State or province of legal domicile or incorporation for the owner entity. Used for state-level tax compliance, regulatory filings, and jurisdictional reporting. Expressed as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code where applicable.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal classification of the owner entity. Determines applicable regulatory, tax, and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|corporation|partnership|trust|reit|sovereign_wealth_fund|family_office|joint_venture|llc|government_entity — promote to reference product]']
  esg_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner entity has a formal ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) investment mandate requiring portfolio properties to meet specific sustainability criteria (e.g., LEED, BREEAM certifications). True = ESG mandate in place; False = no formal ESG requirement.']
  fatca_status varchar [note: 'FATCA (Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act) classification of the owner entity. US Person = US citizen/resident/entity; FFI = Foreign Financial Institution; NFFE = Non-Financial Foreign Entity; Exempt = exempt from FATCA reporting; Unknown = not yet determined. Drives IRS Form W-8/W-9 requirements.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) due diligence process for the owner entity. Required for AML compliance and investor onboarding. Tracks whether identity verification, beneficial ownership disclosure, and sanctions screening have been completed.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the KYC verification was last successfully completed and approved for this owner entity. Used to track compliance currency and trigger periodic re-verification workflows.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the owner or investor entity as registered with the relevant government authority. For individuals, this is the full legal name; for entities, this is the registered corporate or trust name. Used as the authoritative identity label across all portfolio records.']
  lei_code varchar [note: '20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) code assigned by a GLEIF-accredited Local Operating Unit. Required for institutional investors and publicly traded REITs for regulatory reporting and counterparty identification in financial transactions.']
  mailing_address_city varchar [note: 'City component of the owner entity\'s primary mailing address. Used in conjunction with state and postal code for address validation and regulatory correspondence.']
  mailing_address_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the owner entity\'s primary mailing address. Determines applicable postal regulations, cross-border tax withholding, and FATCA/CRS reporting.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the owner entity\'s primary mailing address, including street number and street name. Used for legal notices, distribution checks, K-1 tax forms, and investor correspondence.']
  offboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the owner entity was formally offboarded or terminated from the platform, following full disposition of holdings and settlement of obligations. Null if the owner is still active.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the owner entity was formally onboarded and activated in the platform, completing all KYC, AML, and documentation requirements. Marks the start of the active relationship lifecycle.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the owner entity within the platform. Drives access controls, KYC compliance gating, and operational workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software. Pending = KYC in progress; Active = fully onboarded; Suspended = compliance hold; Inactive = no current holdings; Terminated = relationship ended.']
  owner_class varchar [note: 'High-level investor classification used for portfolio segmentation, AUM (Assets Under Management) reporting, and investment advisory targeting. Distinguishes institutional investors from private individuals, public REITs, family offices, and sovereign wealth funds.']
  politically_exposed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner entity or its beneficial owners are classified as Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) — individuals who hold or have held prominent public positions. True = PEP identified; False = no PEP association. Triggers enhanced due diligence (EDD) requirements under AML regulations.']
  preferred_asset_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of real estate asset classes preferred by the owner for investment (e.g., office, retail, multifamily, industrial, hospitality, mixed-use). Used for deal matching, investment advisory targeting, and portfolio strategy alignment in Argus Enterprise and Salesforce CRM.']
  preferred_hold_period_years varchar [note: 'Owner\'s stated preferred investment hold period in years. Used for portfolio strategy alignment, DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) modeling in Argus Enterprise, and WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) matching during acquisition underwriting.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official communications with the owner entity. Used for investor reporting, distribution notices, KYC correspondence, and portal access credentials. Sourced from Yardi Voyager and Salesforce CRM.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the owner entity, stored in E.164 international format. Used for investor relations, KYC verification callbacks, and urgent operational communications.']
  qualified_purchaser_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner entity qualifies as a qualified purchaser under the Investment Company Act of 1940 (Section 2(a)(51)), typically requiring $5M+ in investments. Determines eligibility for Section 3(c)(7) fund offerings.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or incorporation number issued by the relevant government authority (e.g., Secretary of State, Companies House). Used to verify legal existence and cross-reference with public registries. Applicable to corporations, LLCs, partnerships, and trusts.']
  risk_tolerance varchar [note: 'Owner\'s stated investment risk tolerance profile, aligned with standard CRE (Commercial Real Estate) investment strategy classifications. Core = low risk/stable income; Core Plus = moderate risk; Value Add = higher risk/repositioning; Opportunistic = highest risk/development. Used in Argus Enterprise for portfolio strategy modeling.']
  sanctions_screened_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner entity has been screened against OFAC SDN (Specially Designated Nationals) list, EU sanctions lists, and other applicable sanctions databases. True = screening completed with no match; False = not yet screened or match found requiring review.']
  target_cap_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Owner\'s stated target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) for property acquisitions, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.50 = 5.5%). Used for deal screening and investment underwriting in Argus Enterprise. CAP Rate = NOI / Property Value.']
  target_irr_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Owner\'s stated minimum target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) hurdle rate for investment decisions, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 12.00 = 12%). Used in Argus Enterprise for deal screening, investment committee approvals, and portfolio performance benchmarking.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner entity holds tax-exempt status (e.g., pension funds, endowments, sovereign wealth funds, charitable organizations). True = tax-exempt; False = taxable entity. Affects UBTI (Unrelated Business Taxable Income) calculations and withholding tax obligations.']
  tax_id_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of tax identification number stored in tax_id. Determines applicable IRS reporting forms and withholding rules. EIN = Employer Identification Number; SSN = Social Security Number; TIN = Tax Identification Number (generic); ITIN = Individual Taxpayer Identification Number.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the owner entity. For US entities: Employer Identification Number (EIN) for corporations/partnerships or Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals. For non-US entities: equivalent Tax Identification Number (TIN). Used for IRS 1099 reporting, withholding tax, and FATCA compliance.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Operating or trade name (Doing Business As) used by the owner entity in commercial dealings, if different from the legal name. Common for partnerships, family offices, and REITs operating under a brand name.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the owner record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance.']
  yardi_entity_code varchar [note: 'Source system entity code assigned to this owner in Yardi Voyager. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the silver layer master registry and the Yardi Voyager operational system of record. Enables traceability back to the source.']

  Note: 'SSOT master registry for all property owner and investor identities — individuals, institutional investors, REITs, corporations, trusts, family offices, and sovereign wealth funds. Captures legal entity type, registration details, domicile jurisdiction, tax identification numbers (EIN/SSN/TIN), beneficial ownership flags, AUM classification, onboarding status, and owner preferences (target asset classes, risk tolerance, IRR hurdles, ESG mandates). This is the authoritative golden record for every owner entity across the portfolio, sourced from Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.'
}

Table owner.owner_contact {
  owner_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each owner contact record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the owner_contact data product.']
  mailing_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Contact mailing country determines GDPR applicability, sanctions screening jurisdiction, and communication compliance requirements. Compliance teams require normalized country reference for cross-bord']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent owner or investor entity (individual, institutional, REIT, JV, or partnership) to whom this contact record belongs. Supports multi-contact hierarchies for institutional owners.']
  consent_obtained_date date [note: 'Date on which explicit consent was obtained from this contact for electronic communications and data processing. Required for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis documentation and CAN-SPAM/TCPA compliance for marketing and investor communications.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal basis or consent obtained for processing and communicating with this contact under GDPR Article 6. Explicit: affirmative opt-in consent. Implied: consent inferred from business relationship. Legitimate_interest: processing based on legitimate business interest. Not_obtained: consent not yet collected.']
  contact_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or channel from which this contact record was created or imported. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Salesforce CRM.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this contact record. Active: contact is current and reachable. Inactive: contact is no longer in use but retained for historical reference. Do_not_contact: contact has opted out or is legally restricted. Pending_verification: contact details awaiting KYC/AML validation. Deceased: individual contact is deceased.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this contact record represents an individual person (e.g., named representative) or an organizational entity (e.g., law firm, accounting firm) acting on behalf of the owner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and GDPR data processing records.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department within the owner\'s organization that this contact represents (e.g., Legal, Finance, Asset Management, Investor Relations, Operations). Supports targeted communication routing for institutional owners.']
  do_not_contact_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this contact has been flagged as do-not-contact. Applicable when contact_status is \'do_not_contact\'. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure and CAN-SPAM opt-out compliance tracking.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact record is considered active and valid for the owner entity. Supports temporal validity tracking for contact records, particularly relevant when contacts change roles or when new authorized representatives are designated.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact record ceases to be active for the owner entity. Null indicates the contact is currently active with no defined end date. Used to manage contact record lifecycle and historical audit trails.']
  external_contact_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier for this contact record in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi Voyager contact ID, Salesforce CRM contact ID, MRI contact code). Supports cross-system reconciliation and data lineage in the Silver Layer.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the individual contact person. Applicable when contact_type is \'individual\'. Used for personalized correspondence and legal documentation.']
  is_authorized_signatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is authorized to execute legal documents, contracts, and agreements on behalf of the owner entity. Critical for DocuSign CLM workflow routing and lease execution authorization under SNDA and ALTA requirements.']
  is_kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has completed the Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification process as part of investor onboarding. Required for AML compliance and regulatory reporting for REIT investors and institutional owners.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact record is designated as the primary point of contact for the owner entity. Only one contact per owner should have this flag set to True. Used to determine default routing for communications and notifications.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or designation of the individual contact (e.g., Chief Financial Officer, Asset Manager, General Counsel, Investor Relations Director). Supports routing of communications to the appropriate decision-maker.']
  kyc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current KYC verification for this contact expires and re-verification is required. Supports proactive compliance management and automated re-verification workflow triggers.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the KYC identity verification was completed for this contact. Used to track compliance currency and trigger re-verification workflows when KYC records expire per regulatory requirements.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the individual contact person. Applicable when contact_type is \'individual\'. Used in combination with first_name for full legal name construction.']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for this contact. Used by brokerage and investor relations teams for professional background verification, relationship management, and deal sourcing activities tracked in Salesforce CRM.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address for this contact, typically containing the street number and street name. Used for formal correspondence, legal notices, distribution of financial statements, and regulatory filings.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address for this contact, typically containing suite, unit, floor, or building number. Supplements mailing_address_line1 for complete address specification.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the mailing address for this contact. Used in conjunction with mailing_state and mailing_postal_code for complete address validation and geographic reporting.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or postal code of the mailing address for this contact. Used for mail delivery, geographic segmentation, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  mailing_state varchar [note: 'State, province, or territory of the mailing address for this contact. For US addresses, use the two-letter USPS state abbreviation (e.g., CA, NY, TX). For international addresses, use the appropriate regional subdivision code.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or relationship notes about this contact. Examples include communication preferences, relationship history, or special handling instructions for investor relations. Classified confidential due to potential sensitivity of relationship notes.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the organization acting as the contact, applicable when contact_type is \'organization\' (e.g., law firm, accounting firm, property management company). Also used for the employer/firm name when the contact is an individual representative of an institution.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred method for receiving communications from the real estate organization. Drives automated notification routing in Yardi Voyager and Salesforce CRM. Options: email (electronic mail), phone (voice call), mail (postal mail), portal (owner/investor portal), fax.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'BCP 47 language tag representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, es-MX, zh-CN). Supports multilingual correspondence for international investors and cross-border real estate transactions.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for this contact. Used for routine correspondence, distribution of financial reports, lease notifications, and investor communications. Governed by GDPR and CAN-SPAM Act for electronic communications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for this contact in E.164 international format. Used for direct voice communication, urgent notifications, and deal-related discussions. Governed by GDPR and TCPA.']
  primary_phone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary phone number indicating the type of line: mobile (cell phone), office (main switchboard), direct (direct dial line), home (residential), or fax. Supports appropriate communication channel selection.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role of this contact within the owner entity hierarchy. Determines routing of communications: primary (main point of contact), legal (attorney or legal counsel), financial (CFO, accountant, or investor relations), operational (property or asset manager), emergency (24/7 escalation), secondary (backup contact). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|legal|financial|operational|emergency|secondary — promote to reference product if additional roles are needed]']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the individual contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr., Prof.). Used in formal correspondence, legal notices, and investor communications to ensure appropriate professional address.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Name suffix for the individual contact (e.g., Jr., Sr., III, Esq., CPA, CFA). Used in formal legal and financial correspondence to accurately represent the contact\'s full legal name.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact\'s primary location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Singapore). Used to schedule communications, meetings, and automated notifications at appropriate local times for international investors and cross-border transactions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit trail requirements under SOX.']

  Note: 'Contact information records for owner entities — primary and secondary contacts, mailing addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, preferred communication channels, and contact role types (primary, legal, financial, operational). Supports multi-contact hierarchies for institutional owners with multiple authorized representatives.'

  interest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal form of ownership interest held. Fee simple denotes absolute ownership; leasehold denotes ownership of a lease term; fractional denotes partial undivided interest; beneficial denotes equitable ownership without legal title; tenancy in common and joint tenancy denote co-ownership structures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_simple|leasehold|fractional|beneficial|tenancy_in_common|joint_tenancy|usufruct|ground_lease — promote to reference product]']

  owner_ownership_interest_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ownership interest record. Pending: interest recorded but not yet legally effective. Active: interest is legally valid and in force. Encumbered: interest is subject to a lien, mortgage, or other encumbrance. Disputed: interest is subject to legal challenge. Transferred: interest has been conveyed to another party. Terminated: interest has been extinguished.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage equity stake held by the owner in the subject property or investment vehicle, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 25.0000 = 25%). Must sum to 100% across all active co-owners of the same property. Critical for NOI (Net Operating Income) distribution, capital call allocation, and investor reporting.']

  status_change_date date [note: 'The effective date of the most recent status transition for this ownership interest record. Used for audit trail reconstruction, compliance reporting, and ownership lifecycle analytics.']
  status_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason describing why the ownership interest status was changed (e.g., \'Mortgage recorded against title\', \'Ownership transferred via sale\', \'Legal dispute filed\'). Required for audit trail completeness and compliance documentation.']

  vesting_schedule_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership interest is subject to a vesting schedule (True) or is fully vested at acquisition (False). Relevant for employee equity programs, JV promote structures, and deferred ownership arrangements.']

  Note: 'Records the equity stake or ownership interest held by an owner in a specific property or investment vehicle, including full status lifecycle and audit history.   ownership_structure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ownership structure. Drives eligibility for new property acquisitions, investor distributions, and regulatory filings. Active structures are operational; dissolved structures have completed wind-down.']
  preferred_return_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The annual preferred return percentage (hurdle rate) guaranteed to limited partners or preferred equity holders before the managing member participates in profits. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 = 8%). Key input for IRR and DCF waterfall modeling in Argus Enterprise.']
  promote_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage of profits allocated to the managing member or general partner above the preferred return threshold, commonly known as the carried interest or promote. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2000 = 20%). Used in waterfall distribution modeling.']
  publicly_traded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ownership structure\'s equity interests are publicly traded on a stock exchange. Determines SEC reporting obligations (10-K, 10-Q), SOX compliance requirements, and CMBS/RMBS disclosure rules.']
  registered_address varchar [note: 'The official registered address of the ownership structure entity as filed with the governing jurisdiction\'s secretary of state or equivalent authority. Used for legal notices, regulatory filings, and tax correspondence.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'The official registration or filing number assigned by the governing jurisdiction\'s secretary of state or equivalent authority (e.g., LLC filing number, LP certificate number). Used for legal verification and regulatory compliance.']
  reit_qualified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership structure qualifies as a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) under IRS Section 856-860 and SEC regulations. Drives tax treatment, distribution requirements (90% of taxable income), and SEC reporting obligations.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this ownership structure record in the originating operational system (e.g., Yardi Voyager entity ID, MRI Software entity code, Argus Enterprise fund ID). Enables cross-system lineage tracing and reconciliation.']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record from which this ownership structure record was sourced. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  structure_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the ownership structure across systems such as Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise. Used for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.']
  structure_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the ownership structure entity (e.g., \'Maple Grove Realty LLC\', \'Westside JV Partners LP\'). Used in legal documents, operating agreements, and investor reporting.']
  structure_type varchar [note: 'Legal and organizational classification of the ownership structure. Determines governance rules, tax treatment, and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_owner|joint_venture|limited_partnership|llc|reit|tenancy_in_common|trust|general_partnership — promote to reference product]']
  target_equity_multiple decimal(8,4) [note: 'The target equity multiple (total distributions divided by total invested capital) for the ownership structure as underwritten. Expressed as a ratio (e.g., 2.0000 = 2.0x). Used alongside IRR for investor return benchmarking.']
  target_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for the ownership structure as underwritten in the investment thesis. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used for performance benchmarking and investor reporting in Argus Enterprise.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or equivalent tax identification number assigned to the ownership structure entity. Required for IRS filings, K-1 distributions, and REIT qualification reporting.']
  ticker_symbol varchar [note: 'The stock exchange ticker symbol for publicly traded ownership structures (REITs). Null for private structures. Used for market data integration with CoStar Suite and financial reporting.']
  total_committed_capital decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total capital committed by all equity partners or members to the ownership structure, expressed in the base currency. Used for LTV calculations, DSCR analysis, and investor reporting in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  total_contributed_capital decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total capital actually funded and contributed by all equity partners or members to date. Distinct from committed capital; the difference represents uncalled capital. Used for NAV calculations and investor reporting.']
  total_equity_interests varchar [note: 'The total number of authorized equity interests, units, shares, or membership interests in the ownership structure. Used to calculate individual ownership percentages and dilution analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership structure record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, SOX compliance, and incremental data processing.']

  Note: 'Defines the legal and organizational structure through which ownership is held — single owner, joint venture, limited partnership (LP), LLC, REIT, tenancy-in-common (TIC), or trust. Captures structure type, formation date, governing jurisdiction, operating agreement reference, managing member designation, and dissolution date. Enables modeling of complex multi-tier ownership hierarchies.'
}
