  child_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child owner entity in the hierarchy (e.g., subsidiary, SPV, operating partnership, joint-venture entity). References the owner entity that sits below the parent in the ownership structure.']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Entity hierarchies undergo group-level compliance assessments — consolidated AML reviews, REIT qualification audits at the structure level, and group-wide ESG assessments. Compliance officers track th']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Real estate fund structures (GP/LP, REIT subsidiaries, fund-of-funds) are modeled as entity hierarchies. Linking entity_hierarchy to fund enables fund structure reporting, consolidation analysis, REIT']
  governing_owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: entity_hierarchy has a governing_agreement_ref STRING column that stores a reference to the governing agreement for the hierarchy relationship. This should be normalized into a proper FK to owner_agre']
  jurisdiction_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Entity hierarchy jurisdiction country determines consolidation accounting standards (ASC 810 vs IFRS 10), foreign subsidiary reporting requirements, and cross-border ownership disclosure obligations. ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Entity hierarchies span multiple jurisdictions for consolidation and regulatory reporting (GAAP, IFRS, country-by-country reporting). The owner_entity_hierarchy has jurisdiction_country_code and juris']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Entity hierarchies define consolidation structures; each hierarchy level corresponds to a consolidation ledger. Linking entity_hierarchy to ledger enables automated consolidation ledger assignment for']
  ownership_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.ownership_structure. Business justification: entity_hierarchy defines organizational hierarchy among owner entities, and each hierarchy node is typically associated with a specific ownership structure (LLC, LP, REIT, JV). Linking entity_hierarch']
  parent_owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner. Business justification: entity_hierarchy models the parent-child organizational hierarchy among owner entities. The parent entity in the hierarchy IS an owner record. Adding parent_owner_id FK normalizes the parent side of t']
  primary_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent owner entity in the hierarchy (e.g., holding company, REIT umbrella partnership, fund-of-funds). References the owner entity that sits above the child in the ownership structure.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Entity hierarchies (parent-subsidiary structures) carry distinct regulatory obligations — consolidated REIT qualification, group-level AML program requirements, and SEC consolidated reporting. Complia']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the parent entity acquired its ownership interest in the child entity. Used for holding period calculations, capital gains tax analysis, and investment performance measurement.']
  agreement_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the governing legal agreement establishing this ownership relationship was formally executed and signed by all parties.']
  aum_attribution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the child entity\'s Assets Under Management (AUM) attributable to the parent entity for portfolio-level AUM reporting. May differ from equity ownership percentage due to management fee structures or co-investment arrangements.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting consolidation method applied to this parent-child relationship for financial reporting purposes. Determines how the child entity\'s financials are incorporated into the parent\'s consolidated statements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy relationship record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the parent entity disposed of or transferred its ownership interest in the child entity. Null for currently active relationships. Used for realized gain/loss reporting and portfolio disposition tracking.']
  economic_interest_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of economic interest (profit/loss participation) held by the parent entity in the child entity. Relevant for joint ventures and operating partnerships where economic interest differs from voting rights or equity ownership.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date on which this parent-child ownership hierarchy relationship became legally effective. Used for point-in-time ownership reporting and historical roll-up analysis.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this parent-child ownership hierarchy relationship ceased to be effective. Null for currently active relationships. Supports temporal querying and historical ownership reconstruction.']
  entity_structure_type varchar [note: 'Legal or organizational structure type of the child entity within the hierarchy. Drives applicable regulatory reporting, tax treatment, and consolidation rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holding_company|spv|reit|operating_partnership|joint_venture|fund|partnership|subsidiary — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  equity_ownership_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of equity interest held by the parent entity in the child entity, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 100.0000 for wholly owned, 51.0000 for majority controlled). Critical for NAV, GAV, and AUM roll-up calculations.']
  gav_attribution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the child entity\'s Gross Asset Value (GAV) attributable to the parent entity for consolidated GAV reporting. Supports portfolio-level investment performance analysis.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business code uniquely identifying this parent-child hierarchy relationship, used for cross-system reconciliation with Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise portfolio structures.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child entity within the ownership hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents a direct subsidiary of the ultimate parent. Enables roll-up reporting of AUM, NAV, and GAV across multi-tier structures.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path string representing the full ancestry chain from the ultimate root entity to the child entity (e.g., \'/RootHoldCo/SubHoldCo/SPV-001\'). Enables efficient tree traversal and roll-up queries in Databricks without recursive CTEs.']
  hierarchy_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which this hierarchy relationship is defined. A single pair of entities may have multiple hierarchy records serving different purposes (e.g., legal ownership vs. management reporting vs. tax consolidation).']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the parent-child hierarchy relationship. Active indicates the relationship is currently in force; dissolved indicates the child entity has been wound down or the relationship terminated.']
  hierarchy_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this hierarchy relationship record, incremented each time the relationship is modified (e.g., ownership percentage change, consolidation method change). Supports temporal versioning and audit trail.']
  is_consolidation_entity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity is included in the parent\'s consolidated financial statements for regulatory and statutory reporting purposes. True if consolidated; False if excluded (e.g., minority interest below consolidation threshold).']
  is_primary_hierarchy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship represents the primary (canonical) parent-child link for the child entity. A child entity may have multiple hierarchy records (e.g., legal vs. management reporting), but only one is designated primary.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 two-letter state or province code of the sub-national jurisdiction governing this ownership relationship. Used for state-level regulatory filings and tax reporting.']
  minority_interest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship represents a non-controlling (minority) interest position where the parent holds less than 50% equity or voting rights. Drives non-controlling interest disclosure in consolidated financial statements.']
  nav_attribution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the child entity\'s Net Asset Value (NAV) attributable to the parent entity for consolidated NAV reporting. Used in REIT and fund-of-funds NAV roll-up calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, qualifications, or commentary about this ownership hierarchy relationship that cannot be expressed in structured fields (e.g., special voting arrangements, earn-out provisions, pending regulatory approvals).']
  reit_qualifying_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity qualifies as a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) or REIT subsidiary for tax and regulatory purposes. Drives REIT compliance testing and SEC reporting obligations.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership relationship between parent and child entities. Drives consolidation treatment and financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wholly_owned|majority_controlled|minority_interest|joint_venture|fund_of_funds|operating_partnership — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this hierarchy relationship record was sourced (e.g., Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, Argus Enterprise, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this hierarchy relationship record in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi entity tree node ID, MRI entity relationship ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  tax_transparent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity is tax-transparent (pass-through) for income tax purposes (e.g., partnership, LLC treated as disregarded entity). Affects tax consolidation and investor-level tax reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy relationship record was last modified in the Silver Layer data product. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  variable_interest_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity qualifies as a Variable Interest Entity (VIE) under FASB ASC 810, requiring consolidation by the primary beneficiary regardless of voting interest percentage.']
  voting_rights_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of voting rights held by the parent entity in the child entity. May differ from equity ownership percentage in structures with preferred equity, non-voting shares, or dual-class share arrangements.']

  Note: 'Models the parent-child organizational hierarchy among owner entities — holding companies, subsidiaries, SPVs, fund-of-funds structures, and REIT umbrella partnerships. Captures parent entity, child entity, hierarchy level, relationship type (wholly owned, majority controlled, minority interest), effective date, and consolidation flag. Enables roll-up reporting of AUM, NAV, and GAV across complex multi-tier ownership structures.'
}

  appraisal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the appraisal engagement. Tracks progression from initial order through field work, internal review, and final delivery or rejection. Supports SLA monitoring and workflow management in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise.']
  appraisal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the appraisal scope and inspection methodology. Full appraisals involve complete interior/exterior inspection; limited, desktop, and drive-by involve reduced scope. Retrospective appraisals value a property as of a historical date. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full|limited|desktop|drive-by|retrospective|automated — promote to reference product]']
  appraiser_credential varchar [note: 'Professional designation held by the assigned appraiser at the time of the engagement. MAI and SRA are Appraisal Institute designations for commercial and residential specialists respectively. AI-GRS and AI-RRS are review designations. MRICS is the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors designation. Required for USPAP and IVSC compliance.']
  appraiser_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued appraiser license or certification number for the assigned appraiser. Required for regulatory compliance and lender review. Confidential business record used to verify appraiser eligibility on each engagement.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Argus Enterprise cash flow model associated with this appraisal engagement. Enables direct traceability between the appraisal record and the underlying DCF model file for audit and replication purposes.']
  cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The overall capitalization rate applied in the income capitalization approach, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 for 5.50%). Derived from market comparable sales and used to convert Net Operating Income (NOI) into an indicated value. Key input for Argus Enterprise DCF models.']
  client_reference varchar [note: 'The client\'s internal reference number or loan number associated with this appraisal order. Used by lenders, investment managers, or asset managers to cross-reference the appraisal with their own systems (e.g., MRI Software investment records).']
  concluded_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The appraiser\'s final concluded value opinion for the subject property as of the effective value date, expressed in the engagement currency. This is the primary output of the appraisal engagement and feeds NAV/GAV calculations, LTV ratios, and DSCR analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the appraisal engagement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The risk-adjusted discount rate applied to future cash flows in the DCF analysis, expressed as a decimal. Reflects the required rate of return for the asset class, location, and risk profile. Key Argus Enterprise model input.']
  engagement_date date [note: 'The date the appraisal was formally ordered and the engagement letter was executed. Marks the start of the appraisal lifecycle and the SLA turnaround clock.']
  engagement_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'The agreed fee payable to the appraisal firm for the engagement, in the engagement currency. Recorded for accounts payable processing in Yardi Voyager and budget tracking against the valuation OPEX budget.']
  engagement_letter_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or DocuSign CLM contract ID for the executed engagement letter between the client and the appraisal firm. Provides legal traceability and supports contract lifecycle management.']
  engagement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the appraisal engagement at the time of ordering. Used for cross-referencing with the engagement letter, Argus Enterprise, and Yardi Voyager fixed asset records.']
  firm_name varchar [note: 'The name of the external appraisal firm engaged to perform the appraisal. Supports vendor management, conflict-of-interest tracking, and appraiser rotation policies required by lenders and regulators.']
  holding_period_years varchar [note: 'The number of years in the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis projection period. Typically 10 years for commercial real estate. Defines the time horizon over which cash flows are modeled in Argus Enterprise.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date the appraiser physically inspected the subject property. Required disclosure in USPAP-compliant reports. For desktop or drive-by appraisals, this field may be null or reflect the date of external observation.']
  intended_user varchar [note: 'The party or parties identified in the engagement letter as the intended user(s) of the appraisal report (e.g., lender name, investment committee, asset manager). USPAP requires explicit identification of intended users to define the scope of work.']
  irr decimal(8,6) [note: 'The discount rate at which the net present value of projected cash flows equals zero, as concluded in the DCF analysis. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0850 for 8.50%). Key output metric for investment decision-making and portfolio performance benchmarking.']
  ivsc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appraisal methodology and report comply with International Valuation Standards (IVS) as published by the IVSC. Required for cross-border investment reporting and IFRS-based financial statements.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'The primary valuation methodology applied in the appraisal. Sales Comparison, Income Capitalization (direct CAP rate), Cost Approach, Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), Broker Price Opinion (BPO), or Comparative Market Analysis (CMA). USPAP and IVSC require disclosure of methodology in the report.']
  noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'The stabilized Net Operating Income used as the primary income input in the income capitalization approach. Calculated as Effective Gross Income (EGI) less operating expenses, excluding debt service and capital expenditures. Sourced from Yardi Voyager or Argus Enterprise.']
  npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net present value of projected future cash flows discounted at the required rate of return, as output from the DCF model. Positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return threshold. Sourced from Argus Enterprise DCF model.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'The intended use of the appraisal report as stated in the engagement letter. Drives the applicable standard of value and methodology selection. Common purposes include financing (LTV/DSCR support), disposition, insurance replacement cost, tax appeal, portfolio review (NAV/GAV), and litigation support.']
  report_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the completed appraisal report was delivered to the client or intended user. Used to measure SLA turnaround compliance from engagement date to delivery.']
  report_document_ref varchar [note: 'Document management system reference or DocuSign CLM document ID for the final appraisal report deliverable. Enables direct retrieval of the report PDF or narrative document from the document repository.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'The format of the appraisal report deliverable. URAR (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) is the standard Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac form for residential properties. Narrative reports provide full written analysis. Restricted and Summary reports are abbreviated formats per USPAP.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Indicates the hypothetical condition or premise of the concluded value. As-Is reflects current physical and economic condition. As-Stabilized assumes the property reaches stabilized occupancy. As-Complete assumes a development or renovation project is finished.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date the appraisal review was completed by the review appraiser. Tracks the review stage of the appraisal lifecycle and supports SLA measurement for the review turnaround.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the internal or external appraisal review. Approved reports proceed to final use; rejected or revision-required reports are returned to the appraiser. Drives the status lifecycle transition.']
  sla_turnaround_days varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed maximum number of calendar days from engagement date to report delivery. Used to monitor appraiser performance and manage investment decision timelines.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The reversion or terminal capitalization rate applied to the projected NOI at the end of the DCF holding period to estimate the residual/reversion value. Expressed as a decimal. Standard Argus Enterprise DCF model parameter.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the appraisal engagement record was last modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer and audit trail requirements under SOX.']
  uspap_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appraisal was performed in compliance with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP) as promulgated by The Appraisal Foundation. Required for federally related transactions under FIRREA.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date of the concluded value opinion, which may differ from the report delivery date. For retrospective appraisals, this is a historical date. Critical for FASB ASC 820 fair value measurement and IFRS 16 lease accounting purposes.']
  value_range_high decimal(18,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the appraiser\'s value range opinion when a range rather than a point estimate is concluded. Used alongside value_range_low for sensitivity analysis and investment committee reporting.']
  value_range_low decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the appraiser\'s value range opinion when a range rather than a point estimate is concluded. Used in portfolio stress testing and sensitivity analysis in Argus Enterprise.']
  value_type varchar [note: 'The standard of value concluded in the appraisal report. Market Value is the most common; Investment Value reflects a specific investor\'s criteria; Liquidation Value assumes a forced sale; Insurable Value is used for insurance replacement cost; Assessed Value is used for property tax purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for a formal property appraisal engagement, its deliverable report(s), and status lifecycle. Captures the appraisal order, assigned certified appraiser (MAI, SRA, AI-GRS), appraisal type (full, limited, desktop, drive-by), purpose (financing, disposition, insurance, tax appeal, portfolio review, litigation), USPAP/IVSC-compliant methodology, engagement and report delivery dates, status lifecycle (ordered → in-progress → review → completed/rejected), concluded market value, value date, report format (URAR, narrative, restricted, summary), value type (market, investment, liquidation, insurable), report version (as-is, as-stabilized, as-complete), engagement letter reference, fee, turnaround SLA, and Argus Enterprise model ID where applicable. SSOT for all formal appraisal engagements and their output reports across the portfolio.'
}

USPAP']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Appraisal reports must document the subject property\'s zoning classification as part of the legal description and highest-and-best-use analysis — a mandatory USPAP requirement.   argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'The file name, version identifier, or document reference for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model used to support the DCF valuation in this report. Enables traceability back to the source financial model.']
  as_is_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The appraised value of the property in its current physical and economic condition as of the effective date, without consideration of any proposed improvements or stabilization. Commonly used alongside as-stabilized value for development and repositioning assets.']
  as_stabilized_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The prospective appraised value of the property upon reaching stabilized occupancy and income levels, assuming completion of any proposed improvements. Used in development financing and investment underwriting.']
  cap_rate_applied decimal(8,4) [note: 'The overall capitalization rate (CAP rate) applied in the income capitalization approach, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0650 for 6.50%). Derived from comparable sales analysis and market data. Key input for NOI-based valuation.']
  concluded_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The appraiser\'s final concluded value opinion for the subject property as of the effective date, expressed in the report currency. This is the primary quantitative output of the appraisal engagement and is used for investment decisions, financial reporting, and loan underwriting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this appraisal report record was first created in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The discount rate applied in the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 for 8.00%). Reflects the required rate of return for the investment, incorporating risk and market conditions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date as of which the concluded value opinion is valid (also known as the value date). This is the principal real-world event date for the appraisal and may differ from the report date. Critical for investment decision-making and financial reporting under ASC 842 and IFRS 16.']
  extraordinary_assumptions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any extraordinary assumptions made in the appraisal that, if found to be false, could alter the appraiser\'s opinions or conclusions. Required USPAP disclosure. Examples include assumed lease-up timelines or assumed environmental clearance.']
  gross_building_area_sqf decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total gross building area of the subject property in square feet (SQF) as measured or verified by the appraiser. Used to calculate per-square-foot (PSF) value indicators and compare to market comparables.']
  holding_period_years varchar [note: 'The assumed investment holding period in years used in the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis. Typically ranges from 5 to 15 years for commercial real estate. Drives the terminal value calculation.']
  hypothetical_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any hypothetical conditions applied in the appraisal that are contrary to known facts but assumed for the purpose of analysis (e.g., assuming a proposed building is complete as of the effective date). Required USPAP disclosure.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the appraiser physically inspected the subject property. A desktop or drive-by appraisal may have no inspection date. Required for USPAP compliance certification.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'The stated purpose for which the appraisal report is intended to be used, as required by USPAP (e.g., mortgage financing, financial reporting, portfolio valuation, acquisition due diligence, estate planning, litigation support). Determines the scope of work and applicable standards.']
  intended_user varchar [note: 'The party or parties identified by the appraiser as the intended user(s) of the appraisal report (e.g., lender name, client organization, REIT investment committee). Required USPAP disclosure. Restricts reliance on the report to named parties.']
  irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Internal Rate of Return (IRR) output from the DCF analysis, expressed as a decimal. Represents the annualized rate of return that equates the present value of projected cash flows to the concluded value. Key metric for investment decision-making.']
  limiting_conditions varchar [note: 'Summary of the general and specific limiting conditions that govern the scope and reliance of the appraisal report. Standard USPAP certification element included in all appraisal reports.']
  npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Present Value (NPV) output from the DCF analysis, representing the present value of projected future cash flows discounted at the applied discount rate. Supports investment advisory and portfolio management decisions.']
  number_of_comparables varchar [note: 'The count of comparable sales transactions analyzed and used in the sales comparison approach. Provides a quality indicator for the depth of market evidence supporting the concluded value.']
  report_date date [note: 'The date on which the appraisal report was signed and issued by the appraiser. May differ from the effective date of value, particularly for retrospective or prospective appraisals.']
  report_format varchar [note: 'The format type of the appraisal report as defined by USPAP and Fannie Mae guidelines. URAR (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) is standard for residential; narrative and summary formats are common for commercial CRE engagements.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the report by the appraisal firm or internal valuation team. Used for cross-referencing with Argus Enterprise and client deliverables.']
  report_scenario varchar [note: 'The valuation scenario represented by this report. As-Is reflects current condition; As-Stabilized assumes full occupancy; As-Complete assumes construction completion; As-Dark assumes vacant/non-operating; Prospective reflects a future date. One engagement may produce multiple scenario reports.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the appraisal report workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through internal review, approval, client issuance, and potential supersession or withdrawal.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the internal or external review process for this appraisal report. Tracks whether a supervisory or desk review has been initiated, completed, approved, or rejected per Appraisal Institute and USPAP Standards Rule 3.']
  reviewer_sign_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the designated reviewer completed and approved the review of this appraisal report. Null if review is not yet completed or not required.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Description of the type and extent of research and analyses applied in the appraisal assignment, as required by USPAP Scope of Work Rule. Includes data sources consulted, inspection type, and approaches to value considered.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The reversion or terminal CAP rate applied to the projected NOI at the end of the DCF holding period to estimate the terminal/residual value of the property. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this appraisal report record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.']
  uspap_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the report was prepared in compliance with USPAP (Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice) as required by the Appraisal Institute and applicable US regulations.']
  valuation_approach varchar [note: 'Primary valuation methodology applied in the report. Sales Comparison uses comparable transactions; Income Capitalization applies CAP rate to NOI; Cost approach estimates replacement cost less depreciation; DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) models future cash flows; Hybrid combines multiple approaches.']
  value_psf decimal(14,2) [note: 'The concluded market value expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis, calculated as concluded market value divided by gross building area. Key benchmarking metric for CRE investment analysis and market comparison.']
  value_type varchar [note: 'The basis of value concluded in the report. Market Value is the most common standard; Investment Value reflects a specific investor\'s criteria; Liquidation Value applies to distressed scenarios; Insurable Value is used for insurance purposes; Assessed Value is used for tax purposes.']

  Note: 'The formal deliverable output of an appraisal engagement. Stores report format (URAR, narrative, restricted, summary), concluded market value, value date (effective date of value), report date, value type (market value, investment value, liquidation value, insurable value), IVSC standard applied, report file reference (DocuSign CLM document ID), review status, and reviewer sign-off. One appraisal engagement may produce one or more reports (e.g., as-is vs. as-stabilized).'
}

Business justification: BPOs classify the subject property\'s building class to select appropriate comparable sales and support the broker\'s opinion of value.   currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code.   as_repaired_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The broker\'s estimated market value of the subject property assuming all identified repairs and deferred maintenance items have been completed. Used to assess post-rehabilitation value for REO and distressed asset management.']
  basis_for_opinion varchar [note: 'A narrative description of the primary methodological basis and evidence used by the broker to arrive at the opinion of value, including comparable sales, active listings, and market data sources.']

  bpo_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the BPO engagement lifecycle, from initial order through completion, review, and potential expiry or cancellation.']
  bpo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BPO inspection methodology: drive-by (exterior-only observation), interior (full interior and exterior inspection), or desktop (no physical inspection; relies on MLS and public data).']
  broker_company_name varchar [note: 'The name of the brokerage firm or company under which the preparing broker operates, as it appears on the BPO report.']
  broker_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued real estate broker license number of the individual who prepared and certified the BPO. Required for regulatory compliance and to validate the broker\'s authority to provide a price opinion.']
  broker_license_state varchar [note: 'The U.S. state (two-letter USPS abbreviation) in which the broker\'s license is issued and under which the BPO is prepared.']
  completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the broker submitted the completed BPO report to the requesting party.']
  costar_property_reference varchar [note: 'The CoStar Suite property identifier for the subject property, enabling cross-reference to CoStar market research, comparable sales, and property analytics data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the BPO record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which the broker\'s opinion of value is valid. Market conditions and comparable evidence are assessed relative to this date.']
  engagement_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged by the broker for completing the BPO engagement, as agreed with the requesting party. Used for accounts payable processing and vendor cost tracking.']
  estimated_dom varchar [note: 'The broker\'s estimate of the number of days the subject property would remain on the market before a sale at the recommended list price under current market conditions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the BPO opinion of value is considered stale and no longer reliable for decision-making, typically 90–180 days from the effective date.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the broker physically inspected the subject property (applicable for drive-by and interior BPO types). Null for desktop BPOs.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'The stated purpose for which the BPO is being prepared, such as REO pricing, loss mitigation, pre-listing assessment, or portfolio valuation. Governs scope and reliance limitations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: loss-mitigation|reo-pricing|pre-listing|portfolio-valuation|loan-origination|other — promote to reference product]']
  is_rush_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the BPO was ordered on an expedited or rush basis, typically associated with a higher engagement fee and shorter turnaround SLA.']
  market_conditions_narrative varchar [note: 'The broker\'s qualitative description of current local market conditions, including supply/demand dynamics, absorption rates, price trends, and competitive environment influencing the subject property\'s value.']
  market_trend varchar [note: 'The broker\'s assessment of the directional trend in property values in the subject market area at the time of the BPO effective date.']
  mls_number varchar [note: 'The MLS listing number of the subject property if it is currently or was recently listed on the MLS, used to cross-reference active listing data in CoStar or local MLS systems.']
  num_comparable_listings varchar [note: 'The count of active or pending comparable listings used as competitive market evidence in the BPO.']
  num_comparable_sales varchar [note: 'The count of closed comparable sales transactions used as evidence in the BPO\'s sales comparison approach.']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'The occupancy status of the subject property at the time of the BPO inspection, which affects marketability, condition assessment, and value conclusions.']
  opinion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The broker\'s point-estimate opinion of the subject property\'s market value as of the effective date, expressed in the local currency (USD). This is the primary valuation output of the BPO.']
  ordered_date date [note: 'The date on which the BPO was formally ordered or requested by the requesting party.']
  property_condition varchar [note: 'The broker\'s assessment of the overall physical condition of the subject property at the time of inspection, used as a key input to value adjustments.']
  quick_sale_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated value achievable in an accelerated or distressed sale scenario (e.g., REO liquidation), typically below the as-is market value. Used by lenders for loss mitigation analysis.']
  recommended_list_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The broker\'s recommended listing price for the subject property if placed on the open market, which may differ from the opinion of value based on current market absorption and competitive positioning.']
  repair_cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The broker\'s estimate of the total cost of repairs and deferred maintenance items identified during the inspection, used to bridge the as-is value to the as-repaired value.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'The URL or file path to the completed BPO report document stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM or enterprise document repository).']
  requester_reference varchar [note: 'The requesting party\'s own internal reference or order number for this BPO engagement, used for cross-referencing and reconciliation with the client\'s systems.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'The quality review status of the completed BPO, indicating whether it has been approved by the requesting party\'s review team, rejected, or requires revision.']
  reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Qualitative notes or feedback provided by the reviewing party upon quality review of the BPO, including reasons for rejection or required revisions.']
  turnaround_sla_hours varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed turnaround time in hours from order placement to BPO delivery, as specified in the engagement agreement with the requesting party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the BPO record was last modified in the system, supporting audit trail, change tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  value_range_high decimal(18,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the broker\'s estimated value range for the subject property, reflecting upside market conditions or superior comparable evidence.']
  value_range_low decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the broker\'s estimated value range for the subject property, reflecting downside market conditions or property condition adjustments.']

  Note: 'Broker Price Opinion record capturing informal property value estimates prepared by licensed real estate brokers. Stores BPO type (drive-by, interior, desktop), requesting party, intended use, broker license number, opinion of value (point estimate and low/high range), effective date, market conditions narrative, days-on-market estimate, recommended list price, basis for opinion, engagement fee, turnaround SLA, requester reference, and comparable evidence references. Used for quick portfolio valuations, REO pricing, pre-listing assessments, and lender loss mitigation where a full USPAP appraisal is not required.'
}

Table valuation.cma {
  cma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Comparative Market Analysis record. Primary key for the cma data product in the valuation domain.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.address. Business justification: CMA has subject_address, subject_city, subject_state, subject_zip as plain text — clear denormalization of property.address. Normalizing via FK to property.address enforces 3NF, enables geocoding/mapp']

  broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.brokerage_broker. Linking CMA to the deal enables deal pipeline analytics ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CMA preparation costs are allocated to cost centers in property management and brokerage operations. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK enables consistent currency ha']

Linking cma to dev_project allows the development team to track market pri']

Business justification: CMAs are the primary pricing tool prepared under a listing agreement to set the recommended list price. The listing agreement mandates the broker provide a CMA to the seller. This link supports listin']
  listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.listing. Business justification: CMAs are the primary tool brokers use to set recommended list prices for a specific listing. A CMA is almost always produced in direct support of a listing engagement. Linking CMA to listing enables l']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: CMAs use market research for demand/supply trend analysis, absorption rates, and competitive set context. The cma.market_condition and market_trend fields are populated from market research findings —']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: CMAs are prepared for specific market segments (luxury, affordable, mid-market) which determines the comparable pool and pricing strategy. Brokerage firms segment CMA production by market tier for age']
  market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: CMAs are directly informed by market survey data — avg asking rent PSF, vacancy rates, months of supply, and concessions. A real estate analyst preparing a CMA pulls the current market survey for the ']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: CMAs are property-type specific (SFR, condo, multifamily, commercial) which determines comparable selection criteria and pricing methodology. The cma product has a plain property_type column that shou']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: CMAs prepared for lending, litigation, or investment decisions are subject to regulatory obligations (USPAP, state licensing statutes). Tracking the governing obligation enables compliance audits of C']
  space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial lease advisory CMAs are performed for specific leasable spaces to determine market rent for a suite. CMA links to asset and unit but not space. Space-level CMAs are standard in tenant rep a']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: CMAs are prepared for specific transaction types (listing for sale, refinance, estate, divorce) which determines the pricing strategy and comparable selection criteria. Brokers and agents always speci']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: CMAs are conducted for specific residential units (condos, apartments) to support listing price decisions. cma has subject_sqft, subject_bedrooms, subject_bathrooms, subject_year_built — all denormali']
  absorption_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The rate at which available properties in the subject market are sold over a given period, expressed as a percentage or number of homes sold per month. A key indicator of market velocity and pricing power.']
  active_comparable_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently active listing comparables included in the analysis. Active listings represent current competition and establish the upper bound of buyer expectations.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'The date on which the Comparative Market Analysis was conducted and the market data was assembled. This is the effective date of the analysis, not the delivery date.']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative commentary from the broker or agent explaining the comparable selection rationale, market observations, adjustments made, and any qualifications on the concluded value range.']
  avg_comparable_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average sale price per square foot (PSF) across all comparable sales used in the analysis. Key metric for value benchmarking and pricing strategy validation.']
  avg_days_on_market varchar [note: 'Average number of days comparable sold properties were listed on the market before going under contract. Informs realistic time-to-sale expectations for the seller.']
  cma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the CMA engagement, used in client-facing reports and broker correspondence. Format: CMA-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  cma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CMA record. Draft indicates work-in-progress; delivered indicates client receipt; superseded indicates a newer CMA has replaced this one for the same property.']
  comparable_count varchar [note: 'Total number of comparable properties (sold, active, and pending listings) used in the analysis. A higher count generally increases the reliability of the concluded value range.']
  comparable_lookback_months varchar [note: 'The number of months of historical sales data reviewed when selecting comparable sold properties. Typically 3–12 months depending on market activity and data availability.']
  comparable_search_radius_miles decimal(6,2) [note: 'The geographic radius in miles used to define the comparable property search area around the subject property. Smaller radii indicate tighter, more homogeneous market areas.']
  concluded_value_high decimal(18,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the analyst\'s concluded market value range for the subject property, expressed in USD. Together with concluded_value_low, defines the recommended pricing corridor.']
  concluded_value_low decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the analyst\'s concluded market value range for the subject property, expressed in USD. Together with concluded_value_high, defines the recommended pricing corridor for seller consultations.']
  costar_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the CoStar Suite market report or comparable data export used as a data source for this CMA. Supports data lineage and source traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this CMA record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data governance and SOX compliance for financial reporting inputs.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The date the completed CMA report was delivered to the client or seller. May differ from analysis_date if preparation spans multiple days.']
  engagement_type varchar [note: 'The business purpose or context for which this CMA was prepared. Drives the analytical focus and recommended pricing strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: listing_price_recommendation|seller_consultation|buyer_advisory|refinance_support|estate_settlement|other — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the CMA conclusions are considered stale and should not be relied upon for pricing decisions, typically 90–180 days from analysis date given market volatility.']
  is_uspap_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CMA was prepared in accordance with USPAP standards. CMAs are typically NOT USPAP-compliant (False) as they are broker-prepared market analyses, not certified appraisals. This flag distinguishes CMAs from formal appraisals for regulatory and disclosure purposes.']
  list_to_sale_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'The average ratio of final sale price to original list price across comparable sold properties, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.97 = 97%). Indicates negotiation dynamics and pricing accuracy in the local market.']
  market_condition varchar [note: 'Classification of the current supply-demand balance in the subject property\'s local market. Sellers markets have low inventory and high demand; buyers markets have high inventory and low demand.']
  market_trend varchar [note: 'Analyst\'s assessment of the current directional trend in the subject property\'s local market. Influences pricing strategy recommendations — appreciating markets may support aggressive pricing; declining markets warrant conservative pricing.']
  mls_area_code varchar [note: 'The MLS-defined geographic area or submarket code within which the subject property and comparables are located. Ensures comparables are drawn from the same MLS market area.']
  months_of_supply decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated number of months it would take to sell all current active listings at the current absorption rate. Less than 3 months indicates a sellers market; more than 6 months indicates a buyers market.']
  pending_comparable_count varchar [note: 'Number of pending (under contract) comparable listings included in the analysis. Pending comparables indicate near-term market clearing prices.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'The analyst\'s recommended pricing approach for the listing based on market conditions, seller objectives, and CMA findings. Aggressive pricing targets above-market value; below-market pricing aims for rapid sale.']
  recommended_list_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific listing price recommended by the analyst based on the CMA findings, market trend assessment, and pricing strategy. Falls within or near the concluded value range.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the generated CMA report document (PDF or presentation format) stored in the document management system or DocuSign CLM. Supports client delivery and audit trail.']
  sold_comparable_count varchar [note: 'Number of recently sold comparable properties included in the analysis. Sold comparables are the primary evidence of market value in a CMA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this CMA record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Comparative Market Analysis record used by brokers and agents to estimate property value based on recent comparable sales and active listings. Stores subject property reference, analysis date, analyst (broker/agent), concluded value range, number of comparables used, market trend assessment (appreciating, stable, declining), absorption rate, average PSF of comparables, recommended pricing strategy, and engagement context. Distinct from BPO (less formal, agent-prepared, not broker-licensed requirement) and from full appraisal (not USPAP-compliant). Self-contained engagement record used primarily for listing price recommendations and seller consultations.'
}

Table valuation.comparable_sale {
  comparable_sale_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for comparable_sale']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent appraisal, BPO, or CMA engagement for which this comparable sale record was selected and analyzed.']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Comparable sale price_psf and price_psm calculations require a defined area measurement unit (SF, SM, acres). Cross-market comparable analysis requires UOM normalization; linking to uom enables area-u']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property record in the property master data, linking the comparable sale to the property being appraised or valued.']
  bpo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.bpo. Business justification: BPOs (Broker Price Opinions) use comparable sales as evidence for the opinion of value. comparable_sale already has appraisal_id for appraisal-linked comps. Adding bpo_id (nullable) allows comparable ']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Comparable sales are filtered and adjusted by building class (A/B/C) in the sales comparison approach — a primary selection criterion in USPAP-compliant appraisals. Appraisers must document building c']
  cma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cma. Business justification: CMAs (Comparative Market Analyses) are built on comparable sales data. comparable_sale already has appraisal_id for appraisal-linked comps. Adding cma_id (nullable) allows comparable sales to be linke']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Comparable sales document construction type for physical adjustment calculations in the sales comparison approach. Appraisers must justify physical_adjustment_pct based on construction quality differe']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Comparable sales in international portfolios require country context for legal system, measurement standard, and currency normalization. The plain country_code column is denormalized; normalizing to a']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Comparable sale prices (sale_price, adjusted_sale_price, price_psf) are currency-denominated. Normalizing to a FK is essential for cross-border comparable analysis and currency-adjusted appraisal adju']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Comparable sales require submarket-level geographic hierarchy for market analysis and location adjustment calculations. Appraisers filter comparable sales by submarket to ensure geographic comparabili']
  income_approach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.valuation_approach. Business justification: The sales comparison approach (valuation_approach with approach_type = \'SALES_COMPARISON\') relies on comparable sales as primary evidence. Adding income_approach_id (nullable, using the PK of valuatio']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Comparable sales are jurisdiction-specific: transfer tax rules, disclosure requirements, and data availability vary by jurisdiction. Appraisers filter comps by jurisdiction for regulatory compliance. ']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Land and development site appraisals use parcel-level comparable sales. When the comparable is an in-portfolio parcel, linking comparable_sale to property.parcel enables direct land value benchmarking']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Comparable sales are filtered and adjusted by property type; the property_type plain column is a denormalized reference. Normalizing to a FK enables property-type-specific comparable databases, adjust']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Comparable sales are classified by space use type to ensure apples-to-apples comparison in the sales comparison approach. Appraisers filter comparable sales by space use type as a primary selection cr']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Comparable sale transaction type (arm\'s length, REO, short sale, portfolio trade) determines whether the sale is a valid market indicator and what adjustments are required. The plain sale_type column ']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Comparable sales document zoning for legal conformity analysis and adjustment in the sales comparison approach. Appraisers must verify that comparable properties have similar zoning to the subject to ']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the comparable sale property (street number and street name). Used to identify the physical location of the comp for geographic adjustment analysis.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information for the comparable sale property (suite, unit, floor, building name). Supplements the primary address for precise property identification.']
  adjusted_price_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'The adjusted sale price divided by the gross leasable area, expressed as adjusted price per square foot (PSF). The primary reconciled unit-of-comparison metric used to bracket and support the subject property\'s value conclusion.']
  adjusted_sale_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The comparable sale price after application of all adjustments (location, physical, time, and other). This is the final adjusted value indicator used in the reconciliation of the sales comparison approach to derive the subject property\'s market value opinion.']
  appraiser_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes entered by the appraiser describing the rationale for selecting this comparable, the basis for adjustments applied, and any special circumstances affecting the sale. Required for USPAP Standard 1 documentation of the sales comparison approach.']
  arms_length_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the comparable sale was an arm\'s length transaction between unrelated parties with no undue influence (True) or a non-arm\'s length transaction (False) such as a foreclosure, related-party sale, or distressed sale. Non-arm\'s length transactions require additional adjustment or exclusion per USPAP.']
  cap_rate_at_sale decimal(8,4) [note: 'The implied capitalization rate (CAP rate) at the time of the comparable sale, calculated as the net operating income (NOI) divided by the sale price. A key market indicator used in income approach cross-checks and investment analysis.']
  comp_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known identifier for this comparable sale record, typically sourced from CoStar Suite, MLS, or public record systems (e.g., CoStar property ID, MLS listing number, or county assessor parcel number).']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Overall physical condition rating of the comparable sale property at the time of sale. Used to determine physical adjustment requirements in the sales comparison approach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this comparable sale record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary source from which the comparable sale data was obtained (e.g., CoStar Suite, MLS data feed, county deed/public record, appraiser files). Supports USPAP verification and data provenance requirements.']
  data_source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this comparable sale record within the originating source system (e.g., CoStar property ID, MLS listing number, county assessor parcel number). Enables traceability back to the source system for verification.']
  distressed_sale_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the comparable sale was a distressed transaction (True), such as a foreclosure, Real Estate Owned (REO) sale, short sale, or bankruptcy sale. Distressed sales may require exclusion or significant adjustment in USPAP-compliant appraisals.']
  financing_type varchar [note: 'Type of financing used in the comparable sale transaction (e.g., conventional mortgage, cash, seller financing, assumed mortgage, CMBS). Atypical financing may require a cash equivalency adjustment per USPAP.']
  gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross leasable area (GLA) of the comparable sale property measured in square feet (SQF). Primary size metric used to calculate price per square foot (PSF) and to assess comparability with the subject property.']
  location_adjustment_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable sale price to account for locational differences between the comparable property and the subject property (e.g., submarket quality, access, visibility, corner location). Positive values indicate the subject is superior; negative values indicate the comparable is superior.']
  lot_size_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total land area of the comparable sale property parcel measured in square feet. Critical for land valuation, FAR analysis, and comparability assessment for properties where land value is a significant component.']
  nla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net leasable area (NLA) of the comparable sale property measured in square feet, excluding common areas. Used as an alternative unit of comparison for properties where NLA is the market-standard measurement basis.']
  noi_at_sale decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net operating income (NOI) of the comparable property at the time of sale, used to calculate the implied CAP rate and to support income approach cross-validation in the appraisal.']
  number_of_stories varchar [note: 'Total number of above-grade floors or stories in the comparable sale property. Used as a physical characteristic for comparability assessment and adjustment.']
  occupancy_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the comparable property that was occupied at the time of sale. Used to assess income-producing capacity and to determine whether occupancy-related adjustments are required.']
  parking_spaces varchar [note: 'Total number of parking spaces (surface, structured, or underground) associated with the comparable sale property. Used to assess parking ratio and determine parking-related adjustments.']
  physical_adjustment_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable sale price to account for physical differences between the comparable and subject property (e.g., size, age, condition, quality, amenities). Positive values indicate the subject is superior.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the comparable sale property location. Used for submarket delineation, geographic clustering, and location adjustment analysis.']
  price_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Sale price divided by the gross leasable area or building area, expressed as price per square foot (PSF). A primary unit-of-comparison metric used in the sales comparison approach for commercial and residential appraisals.']
  price_psm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Sale price divided by the building area expressed in square meters (SQM). Used for international valuations and IVSC-compliant reporting where metric units are required.']
  property_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification of the comparable property within its property type (e.g., Class A Office, Strip Center, Bulk Warehouse, Garden Apartment). Supports refined comparability analysis.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the construction quality and finish level of the comparable sale property. Used to assess quality-based adjustments in the sales comparison approach.']
  sale_date date [note: 'The date on which the comparable property sale was recorded or closed, used as the principal business event date for time adjustment calculations in the sales comparison approach.']
  sale_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross transaction price at which the comparable property sold, expressed in the local currency. This is the unadjusted arm\'s-length sale price as recorded in deed or public record.']
  time_adjustment_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable sale price to account for market conditions changes between the comparable sale date and the effective appraisal date. Reflects market appreciation or depreciation over the intervening period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this comparable sale record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail, data lineage, and change tracking requirements.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the comparable sale data was verified against the primary source. Supports USPAP compliance documentation and audit trail requirements.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the accuracy of the comparable sale data (e.g., public deed record, direct buyer/seller confirmation, broker confirmation, CoStar verification). Required by USPAP Standard 1 for sales comparison approach documentation.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the comparable sale record indicating whether the sale data has been confirmed against primary sources (deed records, CoStar, MLS, or direct buyer/seller confirmation) per USPAP verification requirements.']
  year_built varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the comparable sale property was originally constructed. Used to assess age-related physical depreciation and comparability with the subject property.']

  Note: 'Individual comparable sale (comp) record used as evidence in appraisals, BPOs, and CMAs. Stores comp property address, sale date, sale price, price PSF, GLA/NLA, property type, year built, condition, location adjustments, physical adjustments, time adjustments, adjusted sale price, data source (CoStar, MLS, public record), and verification status. Supports the sales comparison approach in USPAP/IVSC-compliant valuations. Sourced from CoStar Suite and MLS data feeds.'
}

Table valuation.comparable_lease {
  comparable_lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the comparable lease transaction record used as market evidence in valuation analysis.']

  bpo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.bpo. Business justification: BPOs for income-producing properties use comparable lease transactions as market evidence for rental income assumptions. comparable_lease already has appraisal_id for appraisal-linked lease comps. Add']

  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy.   reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type.
