  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential rent comparables in appraisals and CMAs reference specific units. comparable_lease links to asset but not unit. For multifamily income-approach appraisals, tying rent comps to specific in-']
  adjusted_rent_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base rent per square foot after applying all grid adjustments (time, location, condition, size, etc.) to normalize the comparable lease to the subject property characteristics. The final adjusted rent used in market rent conclusions.']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for appraiser or analyst commentary on the comparable lease, including any unusual terms, market conditions at time of execution, or qualitative factors affecting comparability. Supports USPAP narrative appraisal report requirements.']
  base_rent_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Annual base rent expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis for the comparable lease. The primary rent metric used in income capitalization and market rent conclusions. Classified as confidential due to commercially sensitive deal terms.']
  cam_charges_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges expressed on a per-square-foot annual basis for the comparable lease. Used to gross up NNN rents to a gross equivalent for cross-lease comparisons.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the comparable property is located. Used for geographic market segmentation and submarket assignment.']
  comp_property_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the comparable property including street number, street name, suite or floor where applicable. Used for geographic proximity analysis in market rent conclusions.']
  comp_property_name varchar [note: 'Common or marketing name of the comparable property (e.g., \'One Embarcadero Center\', \'Westfield Mall\'). Used for identification and reporting in valuation narratives.']
  comp_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known identifier or deal reference number assigned to this comparable lease transaction, as sourced from CoStar, MLS, or direct broker confirmation. Serves as the business-facing unique code for the comp record.']
  condition_adjustment_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for differences in building condition, quality, or finish level between the comparable and subject properties.']
  costar_property_reference varchar [note: 'CoStar Suite\'s unique property identifier for the comparable property. Enables direct cross-reference to CoStar market data for automated comp retrieval and market analytics.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary source from which the comparable lease transaction data was obtained (e.g., CoStar Suite, MLS, direct landlord/tenant confirmation, broker, public record). Determines the reliability tier of the comp in IVSC-compliant methodology documentation.']
  effective_rent_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Net effective rent per square foot after accounting for free rent periods, tenant improvement allowances, and other landlord concessions amortized over the lease term. Provides a true economic rent comparison across leases with varying concession packages.']
  expansion_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the comparable lease includes a tenant expansion option for additional space. Relevant for assessing tenant demand and flexibility provisions in market analysis.']
  free_rent_months decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of months of rent abatement (free rent) granted to the tenant at lease commencement. A landlord concession that reduces effective rent; used to normalize comparable leases to a common economic basis.']
  lease_commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the comparable lease term officially commenced and the tenant took occupancy or began paying rent. Used to determine the effective market date and calculate free rent periods.']
  lease_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the comparable lease agreement was fully executed (signed) by both landlord and tenant. Establishes the market date for the transaction, critical for time-adjustment analysis in income approach valuations.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Scheduled expiration date of the comparable lease term. Used in conjunction with commencement date to validate the stated lease term in months.']
  lease_size_sqf decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total leased area of the comparable lease expressed in square feet (SQF). Used to assess scale comparability with the subject property and to weight rent conclusions in market analysis.']
  lease_size_sqm decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total leased area of the comparable lease expressed in square meters (SQM). Used for international market comparisons and IVSC-compliant valuation reports.']
  lease_term_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the comparable lease expressed in months from commencement to expiration. A key comparability factor in market rent analysis; longer terms typically command lower effective rents.']
  location_adjustment_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for locational differences between the comparable and subject properties. Part of the grid adjustment methodology in income approach valuations.']
  operating_expense_stop_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base year operating expense stop per square foot above which the tenant is responsible for expense increases. Common in modified gross leases; used to normalize rent comparisons across different expense structures.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the comparable property location. Supports geographic clustering and submarket boundary analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this comparable lease record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this comparable lease record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the comparable lease includes one or more tenant renewal options. Presence of renewal options affects the economic value of the lease and is noted in market rent analysis.']
  renewal_option_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each renewal option period available to the tenant under the comparable lease. Used to assess the total potential lease commitment and WALT calculations.']
  rent_escalation_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Annual rent escalation rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%) applicable to fixed or percentage-based escalation structures. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models to project future rent cash flows.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Method by which base rent escalates over the lease term (e.g., fixed annual percentage, CPI-linked, stepped increases, or flat). Used to assess the economic profile of the comparable lease in DCF-based valuation models.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province in which the comparable property is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping and geographic market analysis.']
  ti_allowance_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance provided by the landlord expressed on a per-square-foot basis. A key concession metric used to normalize comparable leases and assess true economic rent in income approach valuations.']
  time_adjustment_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for market conditions between the lease execution date and the valuation date. Used in the sales comparison and income approaches to normalize historical comps to current market.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the comparable lease transaction data was verified through a reliable source. Required for IVSC-compliant methodology documentation and appraisal report quality control.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the comparable lease data indicating whether the transaction details have been confirmed through a reliable source such as broker confirmation, public record, or direct tenant/landlord contact. Drives reliability weighting in rent analysis.']

  Note: 'Comparable lease transaction record used as market evidence in income capitalization and rent analysis. Stores comp property address, tenant name, lease execution date, lease commencement date, lease term (months), base rent PSF, rent type (NNN, gross, modified gross), TI allowance PSF, free rent period, lease size (SQF/SQM), property class, submarket, data source (CoStar, MLS, direct), and verification status. Supports market rent conclusions in income approach valuations.'
}

Table valuation.approach {
  approach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approach']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent valuation engagement or appraisal report to which this approach record belongs. Links the approach to its overarching valuation assignment.']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Approach outputs (concluded_market_rent_psf, replacement_cost_new) are expressed per area unit; the UOM reference ensures consistent measurement standards across cross-border portfolios. IVSC and RICS']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being valued under this approach. Ties the valuation approach to the specific real estate asset in the property master record.']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Valuation approaches are calibrated by building class which affects cap rates, depreciation rates, and market rent benchmarks. The income approach and cost approach produce materially different output']
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: valuation_approach uses cap_rate in direct capitalization. The applied cap rate should be benchmarked against market data. Adding cap_rate_benchmark_id links the approach model to the market benchmark']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: The cost approach requires construction type to determine replacement_cost_new, depreciation method, and useful economic life. USPAP cost approach methodology explicitly requires construction type cla']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Valuation approach financial outputs (concluded_value, pgi, egi, noi, land_value, replacement_cost_new) are currency-denominated. Normalizing to a FK enables consistent currency handling in multi-curr']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Valuation approaches require submarket-level geographic context for market rent benchmarking and cap rate selection. The income approach uses submarket vacancy rates and rent growth assumptions; geogr']
  lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: The income approach requires lease type classification to determine expense recovery structure, effective gross income, and NOI. NNN vs gross lease structures produce materially different NOI calculat']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Valuation approaches are calibrated to specific investment market segments (core, value-add, opportunistic) which determines discount rates, hold period assumptions, and investor return expectations. ']
  market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: Income approach and sales comparison approach valuations rely on market survey data for concluded_market_rent_psf, vacancy_credit_loss_rate, and pgi inputs. Appraisers cite the specific market survey ']
  noi_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.noi_statement. Business justification: The income approach in valuation_approach uses NOI as its primary input. Linking to the authoritative NOI statement documents the source of the NOI figure used in the income approach, supporting USPAP']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: The land residual and cost approaches in appraisals require direct reference to the parcel for land value extraction. Approach links to asset but not parcel. For land valuation and development feasibi']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Valuation approach parameters (cap rate, GRM, cost indices, depreciation rates) are property-type-specific. A domain expert would expect the income, sales comparison, and cost approaches to reference ']
  space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Valuation approaches for commercial leasehold interests are applied at the space level. The income approach for a specific suite requires space-level rent and area data. Approach links to asset but no']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Income and cost approaches are calibrated differently by space use type (office vs retail vs industrial vs multifamily). Space use type drives concluded_market_rent_psf, load_factor, and expense recov']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Valuation approaches are prepared for specific transaction types (sale, financing, estate, litigation) which determines the standard of value and methodology applied. USPAP requires the intended trans']
  approach_date date [note: 'Effective date as of which the valuation approach analysis is performed. Represents the appraisal effective date for this specific methodology, which may differ from the report date.']
  approach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this valuation approach record within the appraisal workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through peer review, approval, and potential supersession by a revised approach.']
  approach_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the valuation methodology applied in this record. Supported types: direct_cap (Direct Capitalization), DCF (Discounted Cash Flow), cost (Cost Approach), sales_comparison (Sales Comparison Approach), GRM (Gross Rent Multiplier). Drives which set of methodology-specific fields are populated.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this valuation approach was formally approved by the reviewing appraiser or valuation committee. Marks the transition from in_review to approved status in the approach lifecycle.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model associated with this DCF valuation approach. Enables traceability back to the source Argus model for audit, review, and replication purposes. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  cap_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Overall capitalization rate (CAP rate) applied to stabilized NOI to derive the direct capitalization value. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). Derived from market comparable sales analysis and reflects investor return expectations for the asset class and submarket.']
  comparable_count varchar [note: 'Number of comparable sales or lease transactions used to support the approach inputs (e.g., CAP rate derivation for direct cap, market rent for income approaches, adjusted sales for sales comparison). Indicates the depth of market evidence supporting the concluded value.']
  concluded_market_rent_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Appraiser\'s concluded market rent per square foot (PSF) used as the basis for PGI estimation in income capitalization approaches. Derived from comparable lease analysis and market surveys. Expressed in the reporting currency per square foot per year.']
  concluded_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The indicated market value produced by this specific valuation approach before reconciliation weighting. Represents the raw output of the methodology (e.g., direct capitalization value, DCF NPV, depreciated cost, or sales comparison adjusted value) in the reporting currency.']
  cost_index_source varchar [note: 'Source of the construction cost index or cost manual used to estimate RCN in the cost approach. Common sources include Marshall & Swift Valuation Service and RSMeans Cost Data. Supports methodology transparency and IVSC IVS 105 compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when this valuation approach record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls for valuation records used in financial reporting.']
  depreciated_cost_of_improvements decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net value of the improvements after deducting total accrued depreciation (physical, functional, and external obsolescence) from RCN. Added to land value to derive the cost approach indicated value. Populated for cost approach type.']
  direct_cap_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicated value derived by dividing stabilized NOI by the overall CAP rate (NOI / CAP Rate). Represents the concluded value under the direct capitalization method. Populated only when approach_type = direct_cap.']
  discount_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Risk-adjusted discount rate (expressed as a decimal) used in the DCF analysis to convert projected future cash flows to present value. Reflects the required rate of return for the investment, incorporating market risk, asset type, and leverage. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  economic_life_years varchar [note: 'Total estimated economic life of the improvements in years — the period over which the building is expected to contribute positively to property value. Used with effective age to calculate the age-life depreciation ratio in the cost approach.']
  effective_age_years varchar [note: 'Appraiser\'s estimate of the effective age of the improvements in years, reflecting the actual condition and utility of the building relative to a new structure of similar type. May differ from chronological age due to renovations or deferred maintenance. Used in cost approach depreciation calculations.']
  egi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Effective Gross Income — PGI adjusted for vacancy, credit loss, and other income (e.g., parking, ancillary revenue). Represents the realistic annual income the property is expected to generate. Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.']
  external_obsolescence decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss in value caused by factors external to the property, such as adverse market conditions, neighborhood decline, or proximity to negative influences. Also known as economic obsolescence. Deducted from RCN in the cost approach.']
  functional_obsolescence decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss in value resulting from deficiencies or superadequacies in the design, layout, or functionality of the improvements relative to current market standards (e.g., outdated floor plans, inadequate HVAC systems). Deducted from RCN in the cost approach.']
  grm decimal(7,4) [note: 'Gross Rent Multiplier — the ratio of property value to gross annual (or monthly) rental income, derived from comparable sales. Applied to the subject property\'s gross rent to estimate value under the GRM approach. Populated when approach_type = GRM.']
  hold_period_years varchar [note: 'Projected investment hold period in years used in the DCF analysis. Defines the number of annual cash flow periods modeled before the terminal reversion value is calculated. Typically ranges from 5 to 15 years for commercial real estate. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  irr decimal(7,6) [note: 'Internal Rate of Return — the discount rate at which the net present value of all projected cash flows (including acquisition cost and terminal reversion) equals zero. Key investment performance metric output from the DCF model. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  ivsc_methodology_code varchar [note: 'IVSC IVS 105 methodology classification code identifying the specific valuation technique applied (e.g., IVS105-IC for Income Capitalization, IVS105-CA for Cost Approach, IVS105-SC for Sales Comparison). Supports regulatory compliance documentation and cross-border reporting.']
  land_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated market value of the land component as if vacant and available for its highest and best use. Used in the cost approach as the foundation to which depreciated cost of improvements is added. Populated for cost approach type.']
  methodology_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing appraiser commentary on the rationale, assumptions, limitations, and adjustments specific to this valuation approach. Supports IVSC IVS 105 compliance, USPAP disclosure requirements, and peer review documentation.']
  noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Operating Income — EGI minus total OPEX. The primary income metric used in direct capitalization and DCF valuation. Represents the annual pre-debt, pre-tax cash flow generated by the property. Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.']
  npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Present Value — the sum of all discounted future cash flows minus the initial investment cost, as output by the DCF model. A positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  pgi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Potential Gross Income — the maximum annual rental revenue the property could generate at 100% occupancy at market rent. Used as the starting point for income capitalization approaches (direct cap and DCF). Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.']
  physical_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss in value attributable to physical deterioration of the improvements, including curable deferred maintenance and incurable structural wear. Deducted from RCN in the cost approach. Populated for cost approach type.']
  rent_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the concluded market rent is expressed. Indicates whether the rent is annual or monthly and the lease structure (NNN — Triple Net, gross, or modified gross) that underpins the income analysis.']
  replacement_cost_new decimal(18,2) [note: 'Replacement Cost New (RCN) — the estimated cost to construct a building of equivalent utility using current materials, standards, and design at current prices. Starting point for the cost approach before depreciation deductions. Populated for cost approach type.']
  sensitivity_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of key sensitivity scenarios tested for this approach, such as the impact of a 25 bps change in CAP rate or discount rate on concluded value. Supports investment decision-making and risk disclosure in appraisal reports.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Reversion or terminal CAP rate applied to the projected NOI in the final year of the hold period to estimate the property\'s residual (terminal) value in a DCF analysis. Typically higher than the going-in CAP rate to reflect future uncertainty. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  total_opex decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual operating expenses (OPEX) for the subject property, including property taxes, insurance, management fees, maintenance, utilities, and reserves for replacement. Deducted from EGI to derive NOI. Populated for income capitalization approaches.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording the most recent modification to this valuation approach record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vacancy_credit_loss_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated rate (decimal fraction) applied to PGI to account for expected vacancy and uncollectible rent. For example, 0.05 represents a 5% vacancy and credit loss allowance. Used in income capitalization to derive EGI from PGI.']
  weight_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Weighting factor (expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1) assigned to this approach in the final value reconciliation. For example, 0.60 indicates this approach contributes 60% to the concluded value. All approach weights for a given valuation should sum to 1.0.']

  Note: 'Valuation approach model record capturing the quantitative analysis for income capitalization and cost approach methodologies. For income capitalization: stores PGI, vacancy/credit loss rate, EGI, total OPEX, NOI, CAP rate, direct capitalization value, terminal CAP rate, discount rate, hold period, IRR/NPV outputs, and concluded market rent inputs. For cost approach: stores land value, replacement cost new (RCN), depreciation components (physical, functional, external obsolescence), depreciated cost of improvements, effective age, economic life, and cost index source (Marshall & Swift, RSMeans). Includes approach type discriminator (direct_cap, DCF, cost, sales_comparison, GRM) and approach weight in final reconciliation. Supports IVSC IVS 105 compliance. After merge with cost_approach, this is the unified approach model for all valuation methodologies.'
}

Table valuation.dcf_model {
  dcf_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the DCF model record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the valuation_dcf_model product.']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.appraisal. Business justification: A DCF model is frequently prepared as part of a formal appraisal engagement in real estate investment analysis. Linking dcf_model.appraisal_id to appraisal establishes the parent engagement context fo']
  approach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.approach. Business justification: The approach table captures income capitalization and cost approach models. A DCF model is a specific, detailed implementation of the income approach. Linking dcf_model.approach_id to approach.approac']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: DCF models used in fund reporting are subject to fair value compliance assessments (ASC 820, AIFMD). The compliance assessment validates model methodology and assumptions.   asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being underwritten or valued through this DCF model.']
  brokerage_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.brokerage_deal.   budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: DCF models incorporate budgeted income and expense assumptions.   building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class.   cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: valuation_dcf_model uses going_in_cap_rate and terminal_cap_rate assumptions. These should be benchmarked against market cap rate data. Adding cap_rate_benchmark_id links the DCF model to the market b']

Institutional underwriters link the DCF model to the specific capex_project driving those assumptions for audit t']

Linking DCF model to cost center enables direct comparison of modeled vs. actual NOI by cost center, a stan']

Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK is required for multi']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. The model has `debt_amount` and `ltv_ratio` as manual inputs; linking to the actua']
