Lenders and investment committees require a project-level DC']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: DCF model assumptions (rent growth rate, vacancy rate, exit cap rate) are submarket-specific. Linking to geographic_hierarchy enables submarket-level DCF assumption validation, market-driven sensitivi']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: DCF models incorporate jurisdiction-specific tax treatment, depreciation rules, and regulatory hold-period constraints that directly affect IRR and NPV outputs. Investment analysts and compliance team']
  lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: DCF models for income-producing properties require lease type to model expense recovery, NOI, and cash flow projections. NNN vs gross lease structures produce materially different levered_irr and equi']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: DCF models are built from ledger-specific financial data (GAAP vs. IFRS vs. tax basis). Knowing which ledger the model\'s income and expense inputs derive from is essential for audit and investment com']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: DCF model assumptions (discount rate, terminal cap rate, hold period, leverage) are calibrated to investment strategy. The market_segment defines target IRR ranges, LTV limits, and hold periods that v']
  market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: DCF models use market survey data for rent_growth_rate, vacancy_rate_assumption, and market rent benchmarks. The model_assumptions_narrative must cite the market survey used. Argus Enterprise models e']
  occupancy_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.occupancy_record. Business justification: DCF models use occupancy data as the baseline for revenue projections. dcf_model.vacancy_rate_assumption is a denormalized plain attribute. Linking to occupancy_record identifies the specific occupanc']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: DCF models for land development projects are tied to specific parcels (entitlement value, land carry costs). DCF links to asset and building but not parcel. Development feasibility DCFs require parcel']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: DCF models are built for specific property types which drive rent growth rates, vacancy assumptions, and terminal cap rate selection. Argus Enterprise and similar DCF platforms require property type a']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: DCF models used for REIT reporting, fund NAV, or SEC filings must comply with specific regulatory obligations (ASC 820 fair value hierarchy, SEC Regulation S-X, INREV guidelines). Compliance teams tra']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reporting_period. Business justification: DCF models are versioned and approved within specific reporting periods for investment committee presentations and lender reporting. Linking to reporting_period enables period-over-period model compar']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: DCF models are calibrated by space use type (office, retail, industrial, multifamily) which drives lease-up assumptions, market rent inputs, and expense recovery modeling. Argus Enterprise requires sp']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: DCF models are prepared for specific transaction types (acquisition, disposition, refinance, joint venture) which determines the modeling perspective, equity structure, and return metrics reported. mo']
  acquisition_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assumed purchase price of the property at the start of the hold period, used as the basis for LTV, equity multiple, and IRR calculations.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this DCF model was formally approved by the designated reviewer or investment committee. Required for SOX audit trail and governance compliance.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Native model identifier assigned by Argus Enterprise to the source cash flow model file. Used to trace back to the originating Argus Enterprise model for audit and reconciliation.']
  argus_model_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the Argus Enterprise model file (e.g., v1.0, v2.3-final) to distinguish iterative revisions of the same model.']
  avg_dscr decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average annual Debt Service Coverage Ratio across the hold period, calculated as NOI divided by annual debt service. Lenders typically require a minimum DSCR of 1.20x–1.25x.']
  capex_reserve_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual capital expenditure reserve amount per square foot budgeted for building improvements, tenant improvements (TI), and leasing commissions over the hold period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DCF model record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  debt_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total debt (mortgage or loan) assumed at acquisition used to finance the property purchase. Drives LTV and DSCR calculations within the model.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) used as the discount rate to compute Net Present Value (NPV) of projected cash flows. Reflects the required return on investment for this asset.']
  equity_investment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total equity capital contributed at acquisition, representing the difference between acquisition price and debt financing. Used to compute levered IRR and equity multiple.']
  equity_multiple decimal(8,4) [note: 'Total equity distributions (cash flows plus net reversion proceeds) divided by total equity invested. An equity multiple of 2.0x means the investor doubles their equity over the hold period.']
  expense_growth_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assumed annual percentage increase in operating expenses (OPEX) applied across the projection period. Typically benchmarked to CPI or market inflation indices.']
  going_in_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The capitalization rate implied by the acquisition price and Year 1 NOI. Used to benchmark the entry pricing against market comparables and assess acquisition risk.']
  hold_period_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the modeled investment hold period ends, representing the assumed disposition or reversion date.']
  hold_period_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the modeled investment hold period commences, typically the assumed acquisition or ownership transfer date.']
  hold_period_years varchar [note: 'Total duration of the modeled investment hold period expressed in whole years (e.g., 10 for a 10-year hold). Drives the number of annual cash flow projection periods.']
  is_levered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DCF model incorporates debt financing (levered analysis). True = levered model with debt service; False = unlevered all-equity model.']
  levered_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Internal Rate of Return computed on levered (equity) cash flows after debt service. Reflects the actual return to equity investors accounting for financial leverage.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of the debt amount to the acquisition price at the time of underwriting, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 for 65% LTV). Key metric for lender and investor risk assessment.']
  methodology_standard varchar [note: 'The governing valuation methodology standard applied in constructing this DCF model. Ensures compliance with applicable appraisal and financial reporting frameworks.']
  min_dscr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The lowest annual DSCR observed across all projection periods in the hold period. Identifies the most stressed year for debt coverage, critical for lender covenant compliance.']
  model_assumptions_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the key assumptions, market conditions, data sources, and qualifications underlying this DCF model. Required for IVSC-compliant methodology disclosure and investment committee review.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for this DCF model record, formatted as DCF-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in investment committee presentations, audit trails, and cross-system references.']
  model_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which this DCF model was constructed. Determines the applicable methodology standards and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition_underwriting|disposition_analysis|refinancing|annual_valuation|portfolio_reporting|impairment_testing|loan_origination — promote to reference product]']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DCF model record. Controls which version is authoritative for investment decisions and financial reporting.']
  net_reversion_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross reversion value less estimated selling costs, representing the net cash inflow to the investor upon disposition at the end of the hold period.']
  npv_at_discount_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Present Value of all projected cash flows (including reversion) discounted at the model\'s target discount rate. Positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return threshold.']
  rent_growth_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assumed annual percentage increase in market rents applied across the projection period. Drives revenue escalation in the DCF cash flow schedule.']
  reversion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated gross sale proceeds at the end of the hold period, calculated by applying the terminal cap rate to the projected NOI in the reversion year. Represents the largest single cash inflow in the DCF model.']
  selling_costs_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage of reversion value deducted for estimated disposition costs (brokerage commissions, transfer taxes, legal fees) to arrive at net reversion proceeds.']
  sensitivity_irr_high decimal(8,4) [note: 'Levered IRR under the upside/bull case sensitivity scenario, reflecting optimistic assumptions (lower vacancy, higher rent growth, lower cap rate). Bounds the investment return range.']
  sensitivity_irr_low decimal(8,4) [note: 'Levered IRR under the downside/bear case sensitivity scenario, reflecting stressed assumptions (higher vacancy, lower rent growth, higher cap rate). Used in investment committee risk analysis.']
  stabilized_noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected NOI at stabilized occupancy, representing the normalized income potential of the property once lease-up or repositioning is complete. Used for terminal cap rate application.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The capitalization rate applied to the final year Net Operating Income (NOI) to derive the reversion/terminal value at the end of the hold period. Critical assumption in DCF reversion calculation.']
  unlevered_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Internal Rate of Return computed on unlevered (all-equity, pre-financing) cash flows. Measures the intrinsic return of the asset independent of capital structure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DCF model record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale models and enforce model refresh policies.']
  vacancy_rate_assumption decimal(8,4) [note: 'Blended vacancy and credit loss rate applied to Potential Gross Income (PGI) to derive Effective Gross Income (EGI) in the model. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The effective date as of which the DCF model value conclusion is stated. This is the principal real-world event date for the model, distinct from the model creation or approval date.']
  year1_noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected Net Operating Income for the first year of the hold period, representing Effective Gross Income (EGI) less operating expenses. Foundational input for going-in cap rate and debt sizing.']

  Note: 'Discounted Cash Flow model record linked to an Argus Enterprise model file. Stores Argus model ID, model version, hold period start and end dates, annual cash flow projections (year 1–10+), reversion/terminal value, discount rate (IRR target), NPV at various discount rates, levered vs. unlevered IRR, equity multiple, DSCR per period, LTV at acquisition, and model assumptions narrative. Serves as the quantitative backbone for investment-grade valuations and acquisition underwriting.'
}

Table valuation.cap_rate_benchmark {
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CAP rate benchmark record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are segmented by building class (Class A, B, C) alongside property type; the asset_class plain column is a denormalized building class reference. Normalizing to a FK enables struct']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are country-specific for international real estate portfolios; the plain country_code column is a denormalized country reference. Normalizing to a FK enables country-level benchmar']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Cap rate benchmark NOI and value PSF figures (noi_psf_low/high, implied_value_psf_low/high) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK enables cross-currency benchmar']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are defined by submarket/MSA; the submarket_name, msa_name, msa_code, and state_code plain columns are all denormalized geographic hierarchy fields. Normalizing to a FK enables str']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are jurisdiction-specific market data used in regulated fund reporting (NCREIF, GRESB, SEC). Compliance teams need jurisdiction context to validate that benchmarks used in appraisa']
  lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are segmented by lease type (NNN vs gross) in commercial real estate surveys — NNN retail trades at materially different cap rates than gross office. CBRE and JLL cap rate reports ']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are segmented by investment strategy (core, value-add, opportunistic); institutional-grade core assets trade at different cap rates than value-add. Linking to market_segment enable']
  market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are derived from or corroborated by market surveys of investor sentiment and transaction activity. The cap_rate_benchmark.survey_methodology and source_organization fields indicate']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are primarily organized by property type; this is the core classification axis for benchmark lookup in appraisals and DCF models. The plain property_type column is denormalized; no']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reporting_period. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are published on a defined survey schedule (quarterly, annually). Linking to reporting_period enables time-series analysis of cap rate trends aligned with financial reporting perio']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are segmented by space use type (office, retail, industrial, multifamily) as a primary dimension in CBRE, JLL, NCREIF, and MSCI cap rate surveys. Space use type is a finer-grained ']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for valuation analyst commentary on this benchmark record, including observations about market conditions, anomalies in the survey data, adjustments made during data ingestion, or guidance for appraisers on appropriate application of this benchmark.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier or file path of the Argus Enterprise valuation model that incorporates or was calibrated against this benchmark record. Enables traceability between market benchmark data and specific DCF/cash flow models used in investment analysis and portfolio reporting.']
  benchmark_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this benchmark record, used for cross-system referencing and CoStar/Argus Enterprise integration (e.g., CRB-2024Q3-OFF-CLA-NYC). Serves as the business identifier for this benchmark.']
  benchmark_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this benchmark record indicating whether it is currently active for use in valuations, has been superseded by a newer survey, is in draft pending publication, archived for historical reference, or under review for accuracy.']
  cap_rate_high decimal(8,4) [note: 'The upper bound of the surveyed CAP rate range for this property type, asset class, and submarket combination, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0650 = 6.50%). Represents the most conservative (highest) CAP rate observed, typically associated with lower-quality assets or less competitive submarkets.']
  cap_rate_low decimal(8,4) [note: 'The lower bound of the surveyed CAP rate range for this property type, asset class, and submarket combination, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0450 = 4.50%). Represents the most aggressive (lowest) CAP rate observed in the market, typically associated with the highest-quality assets or most competitive submarkets.']
  cap_rate_mid decimal(8,4) [note: 'The midpoint or median of the surveyed CAP rate range, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). This is the primary benchmark value used by appraisers for CAP rate selection validation and by asset managers for NAV/GAV calculations in Argus Enterprise models.']
  cap_rate_prior_mid decimal(8,4) [note: 'The midpoint CAP rate from the immediately preceding survey period for the same property type, asset class, and submarket. Used to calculate period-over-period movement and validate the trend_direction field. Supports time-series analysis in investment committee presentations.']
  costar_market_reference varchar [note: 'The CoStar Suite internal market identifier for the submarket or market area covered by this benchmark. Enables direct linkage to CoStar market analytics, comparable transaction data, and property analytics for benchmark validation and enrichment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this benchmark record was first created and loaded into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record management in compliance with SOX financial controls and GDPR data governance requirements.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this benchmark record based on sample size, source credibility, methodology transparency, and recency. Used by the data governance team to flag low-confidence benchmarks and by appraisers to weight multiple sources.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this benchmark record is considered valid and applicable for use in appraisals, NAV/GAV calculations, and investment committee presentations. May differ from survey_date if there is a publication lag.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this benchmark record is no longer considered current and should not be used for new valuations without review. Typically set to the next expected survey publication date.']
  implied_value_psf_high decimal(10,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the implied market value per square foot derived from the CAP rate range and prevailing NOI levels for this segment. Used in conjunction with implied_value_psf_low to establish a market value range for portfolio-level reporting.']
  implied_value_psf_low decimal(10,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the implied market value per square foot derived from the CAP rate range and prevailing NOI levels for this segment. Provides a sanity-check metric for appraiser CAP rate selections and supports portfolio-level GAV/NAV estimation.']
  investor_sentiment varchar [note: 'The prevailing investor sentiment toward this property type and market segment as reported in the source survey (bullish, neutral, bearish). Provides qualitative market context for investment committee presentations and portfolio strategy discussions.']
  is_institutional_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benchmark applies specifically to institutional-grade investment properties meeting NCREIF/REIT portfolio standards (True) or to the broader market including non-institutional assets (False). Relevant for REIT portfolio NAV/GAV calculations and SEC reporting.']
  is_primary_benchmark boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is designated as the primary (preferred) benchmark for the given property type, asset class, and submarket combination when multiple sources exist for the same period. True = primary benchmark to be used in appraisal validation; False = secondary/supplemental benchmark.']
  market_liquidity_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the transaction liquidity in this market segment at the time of the survey (high, moderate, low, illiquid). Informs the reliability weighting of the benchmark and the appropriate discount for illiquidity in investment advisory and REIT portfolio reporting.']
  ncreif_property_index_code varchar [note: 'The NCREIF (National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries) Property Index segment code corresponding to this benchmark\'s property type and geographic classification. Enables alignment with NCREIF NPI return benchmarks for institutional investment performance reporting.']
  noi_psf_high decimal(10,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the surveyed Net Operating Income (NOI) per square foot (PSF) range for this market segment. Used alongside noi_psf_low to bracket NOI assumptions in Argus Enterprise DCF models and appraisal income approach analyses.']
  noi_psf_low decimal(10,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the surveyed Net Operating Income (NOI) per square foot (PSF) range for this market segment, expressed in the currency per square foot. Used as an input cross-check for DCF models in Argus Enterprise and for validating NOI assumptions in appraisal reports.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of transactions, properties, or investor respondents included in the source survey for this specific property type, asset class, and submarket segment. A key indicator of statistical reliability; smaller samples warrant greater caution in appraisal application.']
  source_organization varchar [note: 'The name of the industry research organization or brokerage firm that published the survey from which this benchmark data was sourced (e.g., CoStar, CBRE, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield, NCREIF, PwC/Korpacz). Governs the credibility weighting applied during appraiser CAP rate selection validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CoStar|CBRE|JLL|Cushman & Wakefield|NCREIF|PwC/Korpacz|Marcus & Millichap|Colliers|Avison Young|Green Street — promote to reference product]']
  source_report_name varchar [note: 'The formal name of the survey report or publication from which this benchmark data was extracted (e.g., \'CBRE Cap Rate Survey H1 2024\', \'PwC Real Estate Investor Survey Q3 2024\', \'NCREIF Property Index Q2 2024\'). Enables traceability back to the original source document for audit and IVSC compliance.']
  source_report_url varchar [note: 'The URL or digital reference link to the original published survey report. Supports audit trail requirements and allows appraisers to access the primary source document when validating CAP rate selections.']
  survey_date date [note: 'The date on which the market survey or data collection was conducted or published by the source organization. This is the principal real-world event date for this benchmark record, representing when the CAP rate data was captured from the market.']
  survey_frequency varchar [note: 'The publication cadence of the source survey (quarterly, semi-annual, annual, monthly). Used to determine the expected expiry_date of each benchmark record and to schedule automated refresh workflows in the data pipeline.']
  survey_methodology varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the data collection and analysis methodology used by the source organization to derive the CAP rate benchmarks (e.g., transaction-based, investor survey, broker opinion, hedonic regression). Required for IVSC-compliant methodology metadata and appraiser disclosure in appraisal reports.']
  total_transaction_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate dollar value of all transactions included in the benchmark dataset for this segment and period, expressed in the currency indicated by currency_code. Provides context for the depth and liquidity of the market segment being benchmarked.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'The number of actual closed sale transactions underlying the CAP rate benchmark data for this segment and period, where the source provides transaction-based data. Distinct from sample_size which may include survey respondents. Supports statistical confidence assessment.']
  trend_basis_points varchar [note: 'The period-over-period change in the midpoint CAP rate expressed in basis points (bps), where 1 bps = 0.01%. Negative values indicate compression; positive values indicate expansion. Provides a precise quantitative measure of market movement to complement the qualitative trend_direction field.']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'Directional trend of CAP rates in this market segment relative to the prior survey period. Compressing indicates CAP rates are decreasing (values rising); stable indicates no material change; expanding indicates CAP rates are increasing (values falling). Critical context for investment committee presentations and portfolio NAV/GAV assessments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this benchmark record was most recently modified, including updates to CAP rate values, status changes, or analyst note revisions. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and SOX audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Market CAP rate benchmark record by property type, submarket, and asset class sourced from industry surveys. Stores survey date, property type (office, retail, industrial, multifamily, hotel, data center), submarket/MSA, asset class (Class A/B/C), CAP rate range (low, mid, high), source (CoStar, CBRE, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield, NCREIF, PwC/Korpacz), survey methodology, sample size, and trend direction (compressing, stable, expanding). Used to validate appraiser CAP rate selections, support portfolio-level NAV/GAV calculations, and provide market context for investment committee presentations.'
}

Table valuation.nav_calculation {
  nav_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for nav_calculation']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: NAV calculations for real estate funds require compliance assessments (third-party audits, SEC/AIFMD fair value reviews). nav_calculation has is_audited and third_party_appraiser attributes indicating']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.asset. Business justification: NAV calculations require per-asset fair value attribution for IFRS 13 and ASC 820 reporting. nav_calculation links to portfolio and fund but not individual assets. Asset-level NAV attribution is requi']
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: NAV calculations use weighted average cap rates to derive gross asset value from NOI. The nav_calculation table has a weighted_avg_cap_rate field that should reference the market cap rate benchmark us']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: NAV calculation financial outputs (gross_asset_value, net_asset_value, nav_per_unit, ffo, affo, aum) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK is required for multi-']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. Business justification: NAV calculations net total debt against gross asset value. Linking to the primary debt instrument enables drill-down from NAV to individual loans, supporting REIT debt covenant monitoring and NAV audi']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific real estate fund (e.g., REIT, private equity fund) for which this NAV calculation is prepared. Supports multi-fund reporting and SEC REIT disclosure requirements.']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: NAV calculations require geographic hierarchy for portfolio geographic attribution in investor reports and regulatory filings. INREV and NCREIF reporting require geographic breakdown of NAV by market/']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: NAV calculations are drawn from a specific accounting ledger (GAAP, IFRS, tax). Fund administrators must know which ledger the NAV figures derive from for audit, regulatory reporting, and investor dis']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: NAV calculations are strategy-specific; core, value-add, and opportunistic funds use different valuation methodologies, discount rates, and benchmark comparisons. Linking to market_segment enables str']
  market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: NAV calculations for real estate funds use market survey data (vacancy rates, market rents, cap rates) to validate and stress-test appraised values. Fund managers and auditors reference the market sur']
  noi_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.noi_statement. Business justification: NAV calculations use period NOI as a primary input for cap rate-based property valuation. Linking to the authoritative NOI statement enables fund accountants to trace the NOI figure used in NAV to the']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio or fund for which this NAV/GAV calculation is performed. Links to the portfolio or fund master record in MRI Software or Argus Enterprise.']
  prior_calculation_nav_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding NAV calculation record for this portfolio/fund. Enables calculation chain traceability and period-over-period reconciliation.']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: NAV calculations for REITs and open-end funds are segmented by property type for portfolio attribution and investor reporting. INREV, NCREIF, and SEC reporting frameworks require property type attribu']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reporting_period. Business justification: NAV calculations are produced on a defined reporting schedule (monthly, quarterly, annually). The reporting_period governs when NAV is published for investor reporting, REIT compliance, and fund admin']
  affo decimal(20,2) [note: 'Adjusted Funds From Operations (AFFO) for the reporting period, representing FFO adjusted for recurring capital expenditures, straight-line rent adjustments, and other non-cash items. Considered a more accurate proxy for distributable cash flow than FFO.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this NAV/GAV calculation was formally approved by the designated reviewer or investment committee. Supports SOX control documentation and audit trail.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the Argus Enterprise cash flow model(s) used as the source for property valuations feeding into this NAV/GAV calculation. Supports audit traceability back to the originating valuation model.']
  aum decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total Assets Under Management (AUM) for the fund or portfolio as of the calculation date, which may differ from GAV by including non-real-estate assets, cash, and receivables. Used in investment advisory fee calculations and investor reporting.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which the NAV and GAV are calculated. This is the principal business event date — the valuation reference point used in investor reports, REIT filings, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.']
  calculation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this NAV/GAV calculation run (e.g., NAV-2024-000123). Used in investor reports, SEC filings, and audit trails to uniquely reference a specific calculation event.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the NAV/GAV calculation record. Controls workflow from initial preparation through review, approval, and publication to investors or regulators. \'Superseded\' indicates a revised calculation replaced this one.']
  capex_reserve decimal(20,2) [note: 'Estimated capital expenditure (CAPEX) reserve set aside for future property improvements, TI (Tenant Improvements), and major maintenance across the portfolio. Deducted in AFFO calculation and considered in NAV adjustments.']
  cash_and_equivalents decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total cash and cash equivalent balances held at the portfolio or fund level as of the calculation date. Included in the NAV bridge as a non-real-estate asset component.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NAV calculation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, SOX control documentation, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The discount rate (required rate of return) applied in DCF-based property valuations feeding into this NAV calculation. Reflects risk-adjusted cost of capital for the portfolio. Applicable when valuation_methodology is \'dcf\' or \'blended\'.']
  fair_value_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'ASC 820 / IFRS 13 fair value hierarchy classification for the inputs used in this NAV calculation. Level 1 = quoted market prices; Level 2 = observable inputs; Level 3 = unobservable inputs (most common for real estate). Required for SEC and financial statement disclosures.']
  ffo decimal(20,2) [note: 'Funds From Operations (FFO) for the reporting period, calculated per NAREIT definition as net income plus real estate depreciation and amortization, excluding gains/losses on property sales. Standard REIT performance metric reported to investors and the SEC.']
  gross_asset_value decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total Gross Asset Value (GAV) of the portfolio as of the calculation date, representing the sum of all property market values before deducting debt and liabilities. Core output of the NAV model used in REIT reporting, investor communications, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.']
  is_audited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NAV/GAV calculation has been subject to external audit review (True) or is an unaudited management estimate (False). Relevant for annual REIT financial statement audits and SEC filings.']
  is_published boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NAV calculation has been formally published and communicated to investors or regulators (True) or remains an internal working estimate (False). Controls investor disclosure workflow.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this NAV calculation record. Supports change tracking, SOX audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'Portfolio-level Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio, calculated as total debt outstanding divided by GAV. Key leverage metric monitored by lenders, investors, and rating agencies for CMBS and REIT credit analysis.']
  methodology_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing key assumptions, methodology choices, significant judgments, and any departures from standard methodology applied in this NAV/GAV calculation. Required for FASB ASC 820 Level 3 disclosure and investor transparency.']
  nav_change decimal(20,2) [note: 'Absolute change in NAV from the prior period to the current calculation date (current NAV minus prior NAV). Supports period-over-period performance reporting to investors and the board.']
  nav_per_unit decimal(20,6) [note: 'NAV divided by total units or shares outstanding, representing the per-unit intrinsic value of the fund or REIT. Key metric for investor reporting, unit pricing, and performance benchmarking.']
  net_asset_value decimal(20,2) [note: 'Net Asset Value (NAV) of the portfolio or fund, calculated as GAV minus total debt outstanding minus other liabilities. The primary output metric used in REIT investor reporting, unit pricing, and SEC fair value disclosures.']
  noi_total decimal(20,2) [note: 'Aggregate Net Operating Income (NOI) across all properties included in this NAV calculation, representing total effective gross income minus operating expenses before debt service. Primary input to direct capitalization and DCF valuation methodologies.']
  prior_nav decimal(20,2) [note: 'NAV from the immediately preceding calculation period, used to compute period-over-period NAV change and growth rate for investor reporting and performance attribution.']
  property_count varchar [note: 'Total count of properties included in this NAV/GAV calculation. Provides a completeness check and supports reconciliation against the portfolio property register in Argus Enterprise or MRI Software.']
  publication_date date [note: 'The date on which this NAV calculation was formally published and made available to investors or filed with regulators. Null if not yet published.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The financial reporting period this NAV calculation corresponds to (e.g., Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, Annual, Monthly). Aligns the calculation with the fund\'s financial reporting calendar for investor and SEC reporting.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal or calendar year associated with this NAV calculation (e.g., 2024). Used in conjunction with reporting_period to uniquely identify the reporting cycle.']
  straight_line_rent_adjustment decimal(20,2) [note: 'Non-cash straight-line rent adjustment applied to AFFO, representing the difference between contractual rent escalations recognized on a straight-line basis under ASC 842 and actual cash rent received. Standard REIT AFFO adjustment.']
  third_party_appraiser varchar [note: 'Name of the external appraisal firm or certified appraiser (MAI designation, Appraisal Institute member) engaged to provide independent property valuations used in this NAV calculation. Required when valuation_methodology includes third-party appraisal.']
  total_debt_outstanding decimal(20,2) [note: 'Aggregate outstanding debt balance (mortgages, credit facilities, CMBS, and other borrowings) secured against or attributable to the portfolio as of the calculation date. Deducted from GAV to arrive at NAV.']
  units_outstanding decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total number of fund units or REIT shares outstanding as of the calculation date. Used as the denominator to compute NAV per unit/share for investor reporting and unit pricing.']
  valuation_methodology varchar [note: 'The primary methodology used to determine property market values feeding into the GAV calculation. Options include direct capitalization (applying a CAP rate to NOI), Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), third-party appraisal, a blended approach, or a proprietary NAV model. Aligns with IVSC and FASB ASC 820 fair value hierarchy.']
  weighted_avg_cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Portfolio-level weighted average CAP rate (Capitalization Rate) applied across all properties in the NAV calculation, weighted by property value. Used to validate the implied GAV against market benchmarks and support investor reporting.']

  Note: 'Net Asset Value (NAV) and Gross Asset Value (GAV) calculation record at the portfolio or fund level. Stores calculation date, portfolio/fund reference, total GAV (sum of market values), total debt outstanding, NAV (GAV minus debt), NAV per unit/share, AFFO adjustment, FFO, methodology (direct capitalization, DCF, third-party appraisal blend), number of properties included, and calculation preparer. Supports REIT financial reporting (SEC), investor reporting, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.'
}

Table valuation.tax_assessment {
  tax_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_assessment']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Tax assessments express tax_psf (tax per square foot/meter); the area UOM reference ensures consistent measurement standards across jurisdictions. Multi-state portfolio tax management requires UOM sta']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Tax assessment appeals frequently reference compliance assessments (environmental findings, building condition assessments) that support adjusted value claims. Tax counsel and assessors use compliance']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assessed property in the property master data product. Links the tax assessment to the specific real estate asset.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Annual property tax assessments are a key input to the finance budget (property_tax_expense line). Budget vs. actual variance analysis for tax expense requires linking the assessed tax to the budget r']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Tax assessors classify buildings by class (A/B/C) to determine assessment methodology and select comparable properties for the sales comparison approach in mass appraisal. Building class affects the a']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Tax assessments separately value land and improvements (assessed_improvement_value). The improvement value is building-specific. Assessors issue building-level improvement assessments. Linking to buil']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Tax assessors use construction type to determine replacement cost and depreciation in the cost approach to mass appraisal. ISO construction class codes are used by assessors to set insurance class and']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Property tax expense is tracked and allocated through cost centers. Tax assessments drive the property_tax_expense line in NOI statements and must be coded to a cost center for GL posting and budget v']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Tax assessment financial figures (assessed_land_value, total_assessed_value, gross_annual_tax, net_annual_tax) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK supports mul']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Tax assessors issue assessments on properties under active construction. Development teams track these to manage tax liability in project budgets and appeal assessments on partially-completed building']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Tax assessments are jurisdiction-specific; the taxing_jurisdiction_name and jurisdiction_code plain columns are denormalized geographic hierarchy fields. Normalizing to a FK enables jurisdiction-level']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Tax assessments are issued by specific jurisdictions; appeal deadlines, filing requirements, and penalty authority are all jurisdiction-governed. taxing_jurisdiction_name and jurisdiction_code are den']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Tax assessments and related tax expense postings are recorded in a specific ledger (GAAP vs. tax basis). Linking tax_assessment to ledger supports tax basis vs. book basis reconciliation, a standard r']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Tax assessments are issued by jurisdiction against a specific parcel (APN). tax_assessment.parcel_number is denormalized from parcel. The parcel record is the authoritative source for APN, tax_distric']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Tax assessors use building permit records to identify permitted improvements and assess their value. Unpermitted improvements are assessed differently or excluded. Linking tax_assessment to the govern']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Tax assessments classify properties by property type which determines the assessment methodology, millage rate, and exemption eligibility. Assessors use property type as the primary classification for']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Property tax assessments are governed by specific regulatory obligations (state assessment statutes, appeal procedures, disclosure requirements). Compliance teams track which regulatory obligation gov']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Tax assessments classify space use type for property tax classification and exemption eligibility. Mixed-use properties require space use type breakdown for apportioning assessed value between commerc']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Tax assessments are triggered by specific transaction types (sale, transfer, new construction, renovation) which determines the reassessment trigger and transfer tax applicability. Many jurisdictions ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Condominium and co-op units receive individual tax assessments and tax bills. tax_assessment links to asset, building, and parcel but not unit. Unit-level tax assessment tracking is required for condo']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Tax assessments reference zoning code for property classification and exemption eligibility determination. Assessors use zoning to classify properties for agricultural, commercial, or residential tax ']
  abatement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of any tax abatement granted by the taxing authority (e.g., development incentive abatement, payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (PILOT) arrangement). Distinct from exemptions; abatements are typically time-limited incentives tied to development agreements.']
  abatement_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which any tax abatement arrangement expires, after which the full tax liability applies. Critical for long-term cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise and investment underwriting — abatement burn-off materially impacts NOI (Net Operating Income) projections.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which a formal appeal of the assessed value must be filed with the taxing authority. Critical compliance date tracked in Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM for deadline management.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal appeal was submitted to the taxing authority or appeals board. Null if no appeal has been filed. Used for tracking appeal timelines and legal deadlines.']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the taxing authority or appeals board issued a final decision on the appeal. Null if appeal is still pending. Triggers revised tax liability calculations.']
  appeal_settled_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final assessed value agreed upon or ordered as a result of the appeal process. Null if appeal is pending or not filed. Replaces the original assessed value for tax liability calculation upon resolution.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any formal appeal filed against this assessment. Tracks the appeal lifecycle from filing through final determination. Informs contingent liability disclosures under FASB ASC 450. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_appealed|appeal_filed|hearing_scheduled|decision_pending|appeal_granted|appeal_denied — promote to reference product]']
  appeal_target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The assessed value the property owner is seeking through the appeal process. Represents the owner\'s position on fair assessed value, typically supported by an independent appraisal or BPO (Broker Price Opinion). Used to quantify potential tax savings from a successful appeal.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model in which this tax assessment\'s OPEX (Operating Expenditure) is incorporated. Enables traceability between the assessed tax expense and the DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuation model.']
  assessed_improvement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value assigned by the taxing authority to the building and structural improvements on the property, exclusive of land. Drives depreciation analysis and CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) planning in Argus Enterprise models.']
  assessed_land_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value assigned by the taxing authority to the land component of the property, exclusive of improvements. Used in land vs. improvement allocation analysis and depreciation studies. Sourced from Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and Argus Enterprise.']
  assessment_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'The ratio of assessed value to estimated market value as applied by the taxing jurisdiction (e.g., 0.80 = 80% of market value). Varies by jurisdiction and property class. Used to back-calculate implied market value from assessed value for investment analysis.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tax assessment record. Tracks progression from preliminary notice through certification, appeal, and final determination. Drives workflow in Yardi Voyager property accounting.']
  assessment_year varchar [note: 'The tax year (calendar year) for which this assessment applies. Drives annual property tax expense calculations and year-over-year valuation trend analysis. Critical for NOI (Net Operating Income) OPEX reporting.']
  assessor_contact varchar [note: 'Phone number or contact detail for the assessor\'s office. Used for inquiry and appeal coordination. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government assessor or assessment office responsible for this assessment. Used for correspondence, appeal filings, and audit documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax assessment record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial controls.']
  effective_tax_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The effective property tax rate expressed as a decimal percentage of the total assessed value (e.g., 0.0125 = 1.25%). Accounts for all applicable levies across overlapping jurisdictions. Used in NOI (Net Operating Income) modeling and CAP Rate (Capitalization Rate) analysis in Argus Enterprise.']
  exemption_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of tax exemptions applied to this assessment (e.g., homestead exemption, REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) exemption, historic preservation, enterprise zone). Reduces the taxable assessed value or gross tax liability.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary tax exemption applied to this assessment. Determines eligibility criteria and renewal requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homestead|senior|veteran|nonprofit|historic|enterprise_zone|reit — promote to reference product]']
  gross_annual_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross annual property tax liability before application of any exemptions, abatements, or credits. Computed from assessed value and millage rate. Key OPEX (Operating Expenditure) component in NOI (Net Operating Income) calculations and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.']
  implied_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated market value implied by the taxing authority\'s assessment, calculated as total assessed value divided by the assessment ratio. Provides a reference point for comparing assessed value against independent appraisals and Argus Enterprise DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuations.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Number of installment payments into which the annual tax liability is divided (e.g., 1 = lump sum, 2 = semi-annual, 4 = quarterly). Determines AP (Accounts Payable) payment scheduling and cash flow timing in Yardi Voyager.']
  is_nnn_tenant_recoverable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property tax expense is recoverable from tenants under NNN (Triple Net Lease) or modified gross lease structures. True = tax is passed through to tenants as a CAM (Common Area Maintenance) or operating expense recovery; False = landlord bears the tax expense. Critical for EGI (Effective Gross Income) and NOI (Net Operating Income) modeling.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the taxing authority by governmental level. Properties may have multiple overlapping jurisdictions; this field identifies the type for each assessment record.']
  millage_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The tax rate expressed in mills (dollars of tax per $1,000 of assessed value) levied by the taxing jurisdiction. Multiplied by the assessed value to compute the gross annual tax liability before exemptions.']
  net_annual_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net annual property tax liability after deducting all exemptions, abatements, and credits from the gross tax. This is the actual cash tax expense recorded in Yardi Voyager GL (General Ledger) and reported as a property OPEX (Operating Expenditure) line item in NOI (Net Operating Income) statements.']
  notice_date date [note: 'Date the taxing authority issued the official assessment notice to the property owner. Establishes the start of the appeal window and triggers internal review processes.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the property tax payment must be remitted to the taxing authority to avoid penalties and interest. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) scheduling in Yardi Voyager and cash flow planning.']
  prior_year_assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assessed value from the immediately preceding tax year. Enables year-over-year change analysis and supports appeal arguments based on disproportionate value increases. Used in Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling.']
  property_class_code varchar [note: 'Assessor\'s classification code for the property type (e.g., residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural). Determines applicable assessment methodology, exemption eligibility, and tax rate schedule.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the internal reviewer documenting assessment analysis, appeal rationale, comparable evidence, or instructions for the AP (Accounts Payable) team. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act).']
  tax_bill_number varchar [note: 'Official tax bill number issued by the taxing authority for the annual tax payment. Used for AP (Accounts Payable) invoice matching in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA to ensure accurate tax remittance.']
  tax_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Annual net property tax liability expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis. Enables benchmarking across properties of different sizes and is a standard metric in NNN (Triple Net Lease) lease analysis and CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliations.']
  total_assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assessed value of the property as determined by the taxing authority, representing the sum of assessed land value and assessed improvement value. This is the ad valorem tax base and is typically distinct from market value. Key input to NOI (Net Operating Income) OPEX calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax assessment record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks revisions from appeal outcomes, value corrections, or payment status updates. Required for SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) audit trail.']
  yardi_property_code varchar [note: 'Property code as assigned in Yardi Voyager, the system of record for property accounting and tax expense tracking. Enables direct reconciliation between this Silver Layer record and the source system\'s tax ledger entries.']

  Note: 'Property tax assessment record capturing the assessed value assigned by the taxing authority for ad valorem tax purposes. Stores assessment year, assessed land value, assessed improvement value, total assessed value, assessment ratio, effective tax rate, annual tax liability, taxing jurisdiction, assessment notice date, appeal deadline, and appeal status. Distinct from market value appraisals — assessed value often differs materially from market value and drives property tax expense (a key OPEX component in NOI calculations).'
}

Table valuation.nav_appraisal_input {
  nav_appraisal_input_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the nav_appraisal_input association']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the formal property appraisal engagement whose concluded value was used as input to the NAV calculation']
  nav_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the NAV/GAV calculation record that consumed this appraisal as an input']
  appraisal_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appraisal input qualifies as an independent third-party appraisal for the purpose of calculating the portfolio-level appraisal_coverage_pct on the NAV calculation. Internal desk valuations or stale appraisals beyond the staleness threshold would be flagged FALSE. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data and directly drives the nav_calculation.appraisal_coverage_pct aggregate.']
  appraisal_coverage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the portfolio GAV supported by independent third-party appraisals in this calculation cycle (e.g., 75.00 means 75% of GAV is appraiser-supported). Relevant for FASB ASC 820 Level 3 disclosure and investor transparency. [Moved from nav_calculation: This aggregate percentage on nav_calculation is DERIVED from the individual appraisal_coverage_flag values on each nav_appraisal_input record. It is a computed rollup of the association data, not an independently maintained attribute of the NAV calculation itself. It should be computed from the association table rather than stored redundantly on nav_calculation. If retained on nav_calculation for reporting performance, it should be treated as a derived/materialized field, not a source-of-truth attribute.]']
  contribution_value decimal [note: 'The absolute dollar amount this appraisal\'s concluded market value contributes to the total Gross Asset Value (GAV) in the NAV calculation. May differ from the appraisal\'s full concluded value if a partial interest or ownership percentage is applied. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  valuation_effective_date date [note: 'The as-of date of the appraisal value used in this NAV calculation. May differ from the appraisal report date (e.g., a Sept 30 appraisal may be carried forward and used in a Dec 31 NAV calculation under a recertification). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  weight_pct decimal [note: 'The proportional weight (as a percentage of total GAV) assigned to this appraisal\'s value within the NAV calculation. Reflects the relative size of the appraised property within the portfolio. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal input record (Contract/Schedule) between a NAV calculation and a constituent appraisal. It captures which appraisal values were used as inputs to a specific NAV/GAV calculation, at what proportional weight, and what dollar contribution each appraisal made to the total GAV. Each record links one nav_calculation to one appraisal and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this specific input relationship — the weight, contribution, effective date used, and coverage flag. This is the operational record that fund accountants and valuation teams manage to support REIT financial reporting, SEC disclosures, and ASC 820 fair value documentation.. Existence Justification: In real estate fund management, NAV calculations are formal periodic exercises (monthly, quarterly, annually) that aggregate appraised values across all portfolio properties — a single NAV calculation genuinely consumes many appraisals as inputs. Conversely, a single appraisal (e.g., a Q3 independent appraisal of a Class A office building) feeds into multiple NAV calculations over time: the current period\'s fund-level NAV, a prior-period restatement, and potentially multiple fund structures that hold the same asset. This is an operationally managed relationship — fund accountants and valuation teams actively track which appraisals were used in which NAV calculation, at what weight, and what value contribution each appraisal made.'
}

Triggers formal handoff to asset management and property management teams.']

  coo_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) was officially issued by the local building authority (yyyy-MM-dd). Marks the legal completion of the development and triggers lease commencement dates, asset capitalization in Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets, and handoff to property management.']
  coo_number varchar [note: 'Official Certificate of Occupancy (COO) number issued by the local building authority upon successful final inspection. Required before tenants can legally occupy the building. Critical closeout milestone for lease commencement and asset handoff to property management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the development project record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial controls compliance for CAPEX project records.']
  current_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase within the project lifecycle following standard AIA/construction industry phase gates: entitlement, schematic design, design development, construction documents, bidding, construction, substantial completion, and closeout. More granular than project_status and used for milestone-level tracking and Procore phase management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entitlement|schematic_design|design_development|construction_documents|bidding|construction|substantial_completion|closeout — promote to reference product]']
  esg_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this development project has been formally designated as an ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) initiative for green bond financing, sustainability reporting, or REIT ESG disclosure purposes (True = ESG designated, False = standard project).']
  estimated_completion_date date [note: 'Currently projected substantial completion date for the development project (yyyy-MM-dd). Updated through the project lifecycle as schedule changes occur. Used for lease commencement planning, Certificate of Occupancy (COO) target setting, and investment return modeling in Argus Enterprise.']
  estimated_start_date date [note: 'Originally planned or currently projected construction start date (yyyy-MM-dd). Used for contractor mobilization scheduling, financing draw schedule setup, and portfolio CAPEX forecasting. May differ from actual_start_date due to entitlement delays or permitting timelines.']
  far_allowed decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Floor Area Ratio (FAR) permitted by the applicable zoning ordinance for the project site. FAR is the ratio of total building floor area to the area of the land parcel. Used to validate design compliance and entitlement feasibility analysis.']
  far_proposed decimal(10,4) [note: 'Proposed Floor Area Ratio (FAR) for the development as submitted in entitlement and design documents. Compared against far_allowed to confirm zoning compliance. Tracked through design phase iterations.']
  final_retainage_release_date date [note: 'Date on which the final construction retainage amount was released to the general contractor (yyyy-MM-dd). Retainage is typically 5-10% of contract value withheld until project closeout conditions are met. Triggers final AP payment processing in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  hard_cost_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total project budget allocated to hard costs — direct construction costs including site work, structural, MEP (mechanical, electrical, plumbing), finishes, and contractor fees. Tracked separately from soft costs for CAPEX draw schedule management and contractor payment applications.']
  om_manual_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor has submitted the Operations and Maintenance (O&M) manual package to the owner as part of project closeout (True = submitted, False = pending). O&M manuals cover HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and building systems. Required for Building Engines facility operations onboarding.']
  procore_project_reference varchar [note: 'Source-system project identifier from Procore (Construction and Development Project Management). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer record and the operational system of record. Used for data lineage and cross-system joins during ETL.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the development or capital improvement project (e.g., \'Riverside Tower Ground-Up\', \'500 Main St Lobby Renovation\'). Used in all stakeholder communications, board reporting, and portfolio dashboards.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric project code assigned by the development team or project management office (PMO). Used on contracts, RFIs, submittals, permits, and all project correspondence. Serves as the business-facing identifier across Procore, SAP S/4HANA Project Systems, and internal reporting.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the development project from pipeline identification through final closeout and handoff to asset management. Drives milestone tracking, CAPEX draw schedule activation, and portfolio reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pipeline|entitlement|design|construction|closeout|complete|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  soft_cost_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total project budget allocated to soft costs — indirect project costs including architecture and engineering fees, legal and entitlement costs, permitting fees, environmental studies, financing costs, and project management fees. Tracked separately for CAPEX classification and tax treatment.']
  target_gla_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Gross Leasable Area (GLA) for the completed development, measured in square feet (SQF). Represents the total floor area designed for tenant occupancy and lease revenue generation. Used in investment underwriting, Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling, and portfolio reporting.']
  target_nla_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Net Leasable Area (NLA) for the completed development, measured in square feet (SQF). Represents usable tenant space after deducting common areas, mechanical rooms, and circulation. Used for PSF (Per Square Foot) rent underwriting and lease absorption modeling.']
  total_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved project budget in US dollars (USD), encompassing all hard costs (construction), soft costs (design, legal, permitting), land costs, financing costs, and contingency reserves. The primary CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) authorization amount. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Project Systems and Procore budget module.']
  total_stories varchar [note: 'Total number of above-grade floors in the planned development. Used for structural engineering classification, fire code compliance, elevator planning, and building permit applications.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of residential apartments, condominiums, or commercial suites planned for the development. Applicable to residential and mixed-use projects. Used for absorption modeling, HOA planning, and regulatory density compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the development project record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance per SOX financial controls.']
  warranty_registration_date date [note: 'Date on which contractor and equipment warranties were formally registered with the owner (yyyy-MM-dd). Initiates warranty tracking periods for building systems, roofing, and major equipment. Supports maintenance planning in Building Engines and CAPEX reserve scheduling.']
  zoning_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which final zoning approval or variance was granted by the local planning authority (yyyy-MM-dd). Marks completion of the entitlement phase and enables progression to design and permitting. Critical milestone for project schedule and investment commitment decisions.']

  Note: 'Master record for each real estate development or major capital improvement project — ground-up construction, redevelopment, or large-scale CAPEX programs. Captures project name, type (ground-up, renovation, mixed-use), current phase (entitlement, design, construction, closeout), total GLA/NLA targets, zoning classification, project address, estimated and actual start/completion dates, total project budget, and project manager assignment. Includes closeout lifecycle attributes: COO number and issuance date, as-built document delivery date, warranty registration, final retainage release date, O&M manual submission status, and closeout package completion. Also records Procore project ID for source-system traceability. SSOT for all development project identity and full lifecycle from inception through closeout and handoff to asset management.'
}

Business justification: Entitlements are issued by specific regulatory jurisdictions governing zoning codes, FAR allowances, hearing processes, and appeal deadlines. The existing \'jurisdiction\' text field is a denormalized r']

Business justification: Entitlement applications specify the proposed property type (residential, commercial, mixed-use), which determines FAR allowances, density limits, affordable housing requirements, and environmental re']

Linking enables compliance tracking of entitlement conditions against govern']
