Business justification: Construction contracts are executed by a specific legal entity (the owner/developer SPE). Multi-entity real estate portfolios require entity-level contract tracking for financial reporting, tax liabil']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractor or vendor entity (GC, subcontractor, design-build firm, or architect) that is party to this contract. Used for contractor performance tracking and payment processing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Construction contracts are tracked against cost centers for GL posting and CAPEX budget control. Finance teams require cost center assignment on contracts for project cost allocation, budget variance ']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Construction contracts are denominated in a specific currency. Multi-currency project reporting, lender draw disbursements, and FX risk management require a normalized currency reference. currency_cod']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development project to which this construction contract belongs. Links the contract to the parent ground-up development or capital improvement program.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Construction contracts are governed by specific jurisdictions determining prevailing wage laws, lien rights, and contractor licensing requirements. The existing \'governing_law_state\' text field is den']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Construction contracts incorporate prevailing wage, MBE/WBE, and environmental compliance obligations tracked as regulatory_obligations. This link supports compliance auditing of contractor adherence ']
  actual_substantial_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which substantial completion was achieved and certified. Compared against the contractual substantial_completion_date to measure schedule variance and assess liquidated damages eligibility.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the contractor submits applications for payment (pay apps) under this contract. Typically monthly for GC agreements. Drives AP invoice scheduling and CAPEX draw management.']
  bonding_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor is required to provide a performance bond and/or payment bond under this contract. True if bonding is contractually mandated. Critical for risk management on large CAPEX projects.']
  change_order_allowance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contingency amount included in the contract or project budget specifically reserved to cover anticipated change orders. Expressed in contract currency. Helps manage CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) budget risk.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which construction work is scheduled to commence under the contract. May differ from the executed date if a notice to proceed (NTP) is required. Used for schedule baseline and milestone tracking.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'Categorizes the contract by the role of the contracting party: general contractor (GC) agreement, subcontractor contract, owner-architect agreement, design-build contract, or construction manager at-risk. Used for procurement hierarchy and reporting.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or qualifications associated with the construction contract. Captures non-standard terms, owner-specific requirements, or project-specific conditions not covered by structured fields.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the construction contract, typically generated by Procore or DocuSign CLM. Used for cross-system reference, audit trails, and correspondence with contractors.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the construction contract. Tracks progression from draft through execution, active construction, substantial completion, final closeout, or termination. Drives workflow and payment eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|executed|active|on_hold|substantially_complete|closed|terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the construction contract record was first created in the system (Procore or DocuSign CLM). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance for financial record-keeping.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Contractually specified method for resolving disputes between the owner and contractor. Options include arbitration (AAA/JAMS), mediation, litigation, or a dispute review board. Relevant for legal risk management and compliance reporting.']
  docusign_envelope_reference varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier assigned by DocuSign CLM when the contract was routed for electronic signature. Enables direct retrieval of the executed contract document from the CLM system for audit and compliance purposes.']
  esg_requirements varchar [note: 'Specifies any ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) or green building certification requirements embedded in the contract, such as LEED or BREEAM compliance obligations for the contractor. Supports sustainability reporting and USGBC compliance.']
  executed_date date [note: 'Date on which the construction contract was fully executed (signed by all parties) via DocuSign CLM or physical signature. Establishes the binding effective date of the agreement and triggers mobilization timelines.']
  final_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which all punch list items must be resolved and the contractor must achieve final completion, enabling release of final retainage and contract closeout.']
  general_liability_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Required minimum general liability insurance coverage limit per occurrence as specified in the contract. Ensures the contractor carries adequate coverage to protect the owner from third-party claims.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the ACORD certificate of insurance (COI) provided by the contractor evidencing required coverage (general liability, workers compensation, builders risk). Tracked for compliance and risk management.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the contractor\'s insurance certificate. Used to trigger renewal reminders and ensure continuous coverage throughout the construction period. Compliance-critical for risk management.']
  lien_waiver_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor and subcontractors must provide conditional or unconditional lien waivers with each payment application. True if lien waivers are contractually required. Protects the owner\'s title and property from mechanic\'s liens.']
  liquidated_damages_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Pre-agreed daily monetary penalty assessed against the contractor for each calendar day of delay beyond the substantial completion date. Expressed in contract currency. Used for schedule enforcement and delay claim calculations.']
  minority_business_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes Minority Business Enterprise (MBE), Women Business Enterprise (WBE), or Disadvantaged Business Enterprise (DBE) participation requirements. True if diversity subcontracting goals are contractually mandated.']
  notice_to_proceed_date date [note: 'Date on which the owner issued the Notice to Proceed (NTP) to the contractor, authorizing commencement of construction work. Triggers the contract schedule and liquidated damages clock.']
  original_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original agreed contract sum at execution, expressed in the contract currency. For GMP contracts, this is the guaranteed maximum price. For lump sum, the fixed price. Baseline for CAPEX budget tracking and change order variance analysis.']
  payment_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of the payment bond required from the contractor, protecting subcontractors and suppliers from non-payment. Typically equal to 100% of the contract value. Null if bonding_required is False.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms governing when the owner must pay approved contractor invoices. Drives accounts payable (AP) scheduling in Yardi Voyager and cash flow forecasting for CAPEX draw schedules.']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of the performance bond required from the contractor, typically equal to 100% of the contract value. Protects the owner against contractor default. Null if bonding_required is False.']
  prevailing_wage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract requires payment of prevailing wages under the Davis-Bacon Act or applicable state prevailing wage laws. Relevant for publicly funded or government-assisted development projects.']
  procore_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier from Procore construction management platform. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and Procore source system for RFIs, submittals, and change order management.']
  retainage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each progress payment withheld by the owner as retainage (holdback) until substantial or final completion. Typically 5–10%. Governs accounts payable (AP) withholding calculations and contractor cash flow.']
  retainage_release_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of contract completion at which retainage may be reduced or released (e.g., 50% completion triggers reduction from 10% to 5%). Contractual milestone that affects CAPEX draw schedules.']
  revised_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current contract value inclusive of all approved change orders. Reflects the most up-to-date authorized contract sum. Used for CAPEX draw schedule management and budget-to-actual reporting.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the construction work covered by this contract, including trade scope, deliverables, and exclusions. Sourced from Procore contract documents and DocuSign CLM executed exhibits.']
  substantial_completion_date date [note: 'Contractually agreed target date by which the contractor must achieve substantial completion, meaning the work is sufficiently complete for the owner to occupy or use the facility for its intended purpose. Key milestone for Certificate of Occupancy (COO) pursuit.']
  trade_division varchar [note: 'Construction Specifications Institute (CSI) MasterFormat division code or trade category describing the primary work scope (e.g., Division 03 Concrete, Division 15 Mechanical, Division 16 Electrical). Enables cost segregation and trade-level reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full CSI MasterFormat division list]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the construction contract record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the contractor\'s warranty obligation following substantial completion. Typically 12 months for general construction. Drives post-completion defect tracking and O&M (Operations and Maintenance) handover planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for each construction contract executed on a development project — GC agreements, subcontractor contracts, design-build contracts, and owner-architect agreements. Captures contract type (GMP, lump sum, cost-plus), contractor entity reference, contract value, executed date, substantial completion date, retainage percentage, bonding requirements, insurance certificates, change order allowance, and contract status. Sourced from DocuSign CLM and Procore.'
}

Table development.contractor {
  contractor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the contractor record. Primary key for the contractor master registry within the development domain.']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Contractor office location is used for local hiring compliance, prevailing wage jurisdiction determination, and procurement sourcing analytics. office_city, office_state, office_zip are denormalized g']
  industry_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.industry_classification. Business justification: Contractors are classified by NAICS/SIC trade codes for diversity reporting (MBE/WBE/DBE compliance), procurement analytics, and vendor prequalification. trade_specialty is a denormalized industry cla']
  annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recently reported annual gross revenue of the contractor in USD. Used as a financial capacity indicator during prequalification to assess ability to execute large CAPEX contracts.']
  bim_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor has the software, hardware, and trained personnel to participate in BIM-coordinated project delivery. Required for projects mandating BIM model contributions and clash detection workflows.']
  bond_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the contractor\'s surety bond. Contractors must maintain valid bonding throughout the project engagement period. Triggers renewal alerts.']
  bonding_capacity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate bonding capacity (in USD) the contractor\'s surety has approved. Determines the maximum contract value the contractor can be awarded. Critical for CAPEX project risk management and bid qualification.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Legal registered business name of the contractor, subcontractor, or design professional entity as it appears on licensing and bonding documents.']
  contractor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractor\'s role in the development project hierarchy. Distinguishes general contractors (GC), specialty subcontractors, licensed design professionals (architects, engineers), and advisory consultants or owner representatives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contractor record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX record-keeping requirements.']
  dbe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor holds a valid Disadvantaged Business Enterprise (DBE) certification as defined by the U.S. Department of Transportation. Required for federally funded projects and diversity compliance reporting.']
  diversity_cert_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the contractor\'s active diversity certification (MBE, WBE, or DBE). Certifications must be renewed periodically; expired certifications cannot be counted toward diversity spend goals.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number uniquely identifying the contractor\'s business entity. Used for credit verification, financial due diligence, and vendor master deduplication.']
  federal_tax_number varchar [note: 'Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Federal Tax ID assigned by the IRS to the contractor entity. Required for 1099 reporting, contract execution, and AP payment processing in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  gl_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum coverage limit of the contractor\'s commercial general liability insurance policy, expressed in USD. Used to verify adequacy against project risk thresholds during prequalification.']
  insurance_gl_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the contractor\'s commercial general liability (GL) insurance policy. Must remain current for the contractor to be active on any project. Triggers compliance alerts 30–60 days prior to expiry.']
  insurance_professional_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the contractor\'s professional liability (errors and omissions) insurance policy. Required for architects, engineers, and design consultants engaged on development projects.']
  insurance_wc_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the contractor\'s workers\' compensation insurance policy. Mandatory for all contractors with employees working on project sites. Verified during prequalification and monitored continuously.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contractor record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  leed_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor has demonstrated capability and experience executing LEED-certified construction projects. Used to filter eligible contractors for ESG-aligned development projects.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the contractor\'s primary license expires. Triggers compliance alerts for renewal tracking. Contractors with expired licenses must not be issued new contracts.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'State or jurisdiction-issued contractor license number. Required for legal engagement on construction projects and verified during prequalification. Corresponds to the license type field.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the jurisdiction that issued the contractor\'s primary license. Used to verify geographic eligibility for project work.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractor\'s license as issued by the relevant state or municipal licensing authority (e.g., General Building Contractor, Electrical Contractor, Mechanical Contractor, Professional Engineer, Registered Architect). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general_building|electrical|mechanical|plumbing|civil|structural_engineering|architecture|specialty — promote to reference product]']
  mbe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor holds a valid Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) certification. Supports diversity spend tracking, regulatory reporting, and compliance with HUD and local government diversity requirements.']
  office_address varchar [note: 'Primary business office street address of the contractor. Used for contract execution, lien notices, certified mail, and vendor master records in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.']
  osha_recordable_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'OSHA Total Recordable Incident Rate (TRIR) for the contractor, calculated per 200,000 work hours. Used to assess site safety performance during prequalification. Lower values indicate better safety records.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Composite historical performance rating for the contractor on a 0.00–5.00 scale, aggregated from project closeout evaluations across all completed engagements. Inputs include schedule adherence, quality, safety record, and responsiveness. Used for bid shortlisting and prequalification renewal.']
  preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor has been designated as a preferred vendor based on demonstrated performance, pricing competitiveness, and strategic relationship. Preferred vendors receive priority bid invitations.']
  prequalification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the contractor\'s current prequalification approval expires. Contractors must resubmit prequalification packages before this date to remain eligible for new bid invitations.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Current prequalification standing of the contractor for engagement on development projects. Approved contractors are eligible for bid invitations; conditional status may require additional documentation; disqualified contractors are barred from new engagements.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the default primary contact at the contractor organization. Used for bid invitations, RFI notifications, submittal routing, and contract correspondence in Procore.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the default primary contact person at the contractor organization for project communications, RFI responses, and contract administration.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number of the default primary contact at the contractor organization. Used for urgent project communications and site coordination.']
  safety_emod_rate decimal(5,3) [note: 'OSHA Experience Modification Rate (EMod or EMR) reflecting the contractor\'s workers\' compensation claims history relative to industry average. A rate above 1.0 indicates above-average claims; many projects require EMod ≤ 1.0 for bid eligibility.']
  surety_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the surety company that issued the contractor\'s performance and payment bonds. Used for bond verification and claims processing.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing-business-as (DBA) or trade name used by the contractor if different from the legal company name. Used for operational communications and project directories.']
  wbe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor holds a valid Women-Owned Business Enterprise (WBE) certification. Supports diversity spend tracking and compliance with project-level diversity inclusion goals.']
  years_in_business varchar [note: 'Number of years the contractor has been in continuous operation. Used as a financial stability and experience indicator during prequalification scoring.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all contractors, subcontractors, and design professionals engaged on development projects — general contractors, specialty subcontractors, architects, engineers, and consultants. Captures company name, license number, license type, bonding capacity, insurance expiration, prequalification status, trade specialty, MBE/WBE/DBE certification, default contact, and historical performance rating. SSOT for contractor identity within the development domain. Supports prequalification workflows, insurance compliance tracking, and contractor performance evaluation across projects.'
}

Table development.construction_budget {
  construction_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the construction budget record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Construction budgets express cost benchmarks on a per-area basis (cost per sqft or sqm). International projects require explicit area UOM to correctly compute and compare cost-per-area metrics in budg']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset associated with this construction budget, enabling linkage to the property master for portfolio-level CAPEX reporting.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this budget record in Procore Construction Management platform. Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the Procore operational system of record.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Multi-building development projects require construction budgets allocated per building for cost reporting, lender draw reconciliation, and capitalization. Development accountants and project managers']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Construction budget line items map to GL accounts for CAPEX posting. Finance teams require COA mapping for budget-to-GL reconciliation and CAPEX/OPEX classification. gl_account_code is a denormalized ']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Remediation cost estimates from compliance assessments (Phase I/II ESA, structural audits) feed directly into construction budget line items. Lenders and project accountants require the budget to refe']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Construction budgets are allocated to cost centers for GL posting and CAPEX financial reporting. Real estate development accounting requires cost center assignment on every budget for NOI/CAPEX tracki']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Construction budgets are denominated in a specific currency. Multi-currency portfolio budget consolidation, lender reporting, and FX variance analysis require a normalized currency reference. currency']
  debt_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.debt_facility. Business justification: The construction budget is funded against a specific debt facility (construction loan). Lender-required budget-to-loan reconciliation, LTV compliance monitoring, and draw request approvals all require']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. Business justification: Construction budgets are funded by specific construction loan debt instruments. Budget-to-loan linkage is required for lender draw reporting, loan covenant compliance tracking, and reconciling budgete']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent development or capital improvement project to which this construction budget belongs. Links to the development project master record.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Construction budgets are funded by capital calls from a specific investment fund. Fund-level budget-to-actual reporting, capital call scheduling, and investor reporting on committed costs all require ']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Construction budgets must be assigned to a specific accounting ledger (e.g., CIP ledger) for GAAP capitalization and WBS cost reporting. Development accountants require budget-to-ledger alignment for ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Construction budgets belong to a legal entity for consolidated financial reporting, tax purposes, and investor reporting. Multi-entity real estate developers require entity-level budget tracking for R']
  owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: Construction budgets are approved under authority granted by the owner_agreement (capex_approval_threshold, opex_approval_threshold). This link supports budget approval workflow, audit trails for owne']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Portfolio asset records carry capex_budget and capex_spent_to_date fields that must reconcile against the detailed construction budget. Investment reporting systems pull from construction budgets to u']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reporting_period. Business justification: Construction budgets are period-specific financial plans. Linking to reporting_period enables period-over-period budget variance reporting and cost-to-complete forecasting — a standard development fin']
  actual_cost_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total construction costs actually incurred and recorded (invoiced and paid) as of the reporting date. Used for CAPEX draw schedule reconciliation and cost-to-complete analysis. Denominated in currency_code.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the construction budget was formally approved by the investment committee, board, or authorized approver. Required for SOX financial controls and audit trail.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The formally approved total budget amount reflecting all board- or committee-approved budget amendments to date. May differ from original_budget_amount due to scope changes approved through formal change control. Denominated in currency_code.']
  approved_change_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar value of all formally approved change orders (COs) that have been incorporated into the current budget. Tracks scope creep and budget evolution. Denominated in currency_code.']
  budget_end_date date [note: 'The projected or actual date on which the construction budget period ends, typically aligned with the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance or project substantial completion date.']
  budget_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the construction budget period commences, typically aligned with the project groundbreaking or Notice to Proceed (NTP) date. Used for CAPEX draw schedule management.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the construction budget. Drives workflow approvals, CAPEX draw eligibility, and reporting inclusion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|on_hold|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the construction budget by development program type. Determines applicable cost structures, approval thresholds, and regulatory reporting categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground_up_development|major_renovation|tenant_improvement|capital_improvement|infrastructure|mixed_use — promote to reference product]']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the construction budget, incremented each time the budget is formally revised and re-approved. Enables historical budget version tracking and variance analysis across revisions.']
  committed_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total costs legally committed through executed contracts, purchase orders, and subcontracts to date, regardless of whether invoiced or paid. Critical for cash flow forecasting and lender draw reporting. Denominated in currency_code.']
  contingency_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget reserve allocated for unforeseen construction costs and risk mitigation. Typically expressed as a percentage of hard costs. Drawdown of contingency requires formal approval. Denominated in currency_code.']
  contingency_remaining decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unused contingency budget remaining after approved contingency draws. Monitors risk buffer availability throughout the construction lifecycle. Denominated in currency_code.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'High-level cost category classification for this budget line, aligning with standard real estate development cost reporting categories. Enables roll-up reporting by category across projects.']
  cost_code varchar [note: 'The CSI MasterFormat division code or custom Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element code identifying the specific cost category for line-item budget records (e.g., \'03-0000\' for Concrete, \'05-0000\' for Metals). Null for summary-level records.']
  cost_code_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the cost code or WBS element (e.g., \'Concrete Work\', \'Structural Steel\', \'Mechanical Systems\'). Used in budget reports and investor packages.']
  cost_code_level varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record represents a summary-level budget (rolled-up by category) or a line-item-level budget (individual CSI division or WBS element). Drives reporting granularity and drill-down capability.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the current approved budget and the forecast at completion (current_budget_amount minus forecast_at_completion). Positive value indicates under-budget; negative indicates over-budget. Denominated in currency_code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this construction budget record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking in the lakehouse.']
  current_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current total budget amount inclusive of all approved change orders (COs). Represents the most up-to-date authorized spend ceiling for the project. Denominated in currency_code.']
  financing_cost_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the budget allocated to construction financing costs including loan origination fees, interest carry, and lender-required reserves. Relevant for DSCR and LTV monitoring. Denominated in currency_code.']
  forecast_at_completion decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost at project completion based on actual costs to date plus estimated cost to complete. Key metric for investor reporting, lender draw management, and budget variance analysis. Denominated in currency_code.']
  hard_cost_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the current budget allocated to hard costs — direct construction costs including site work, structural, MEP, finishes, and contractor fees. Key category for cost control and draw schedule management. Denominated in currency_code.']
  is_contingency_drawn boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether any contingency budget has been formally drawn down on this project budget. Triggers enhanced monitoring and lender notification requirements when True.']
  land_cost_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total development budget allocated to land acquisition costs, including purchase price, closing costs, and entitlement-related land carrying costs. Denominated in currency_code.']
  line_actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual costs incurred and recorded for this specific cost code line as of the reporting date. Enables granular cost tracking at the CSI division or WBS element level. Denominated in currency_code.']
  line_committed_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Committed costs for this specific cost code line through executed contracts and purchase orders. Used for line-level cost control and remaining budget calculation. Denominated in currency_code.']
  line_original_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original budget amount for this specific cost code or WBS line item at project inception. Baseline for line-level variance analysis. Applicable only for line-item-level records. Denominated in currency_code.']
  line_remaining_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining authorized budget for this cost code line (line_revised_budget minus line_actual_cost). Indicates available spend capacity at the line level for cost control purposes. Denominated in currency_code.']
  line_revised_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revised budget amount for this cost code or WBS line item after approved change orders. Reflects the current authorized spend for this line. Denominated in currency_code.']
  original_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial total CAPEX budget amount as approved at project inception, before any change orders or revisions. Serves as the baseline for variance analysis and investor reporting. Denominated in currency_code.']
  pending_change_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar value of change orders submitted but not yet formally approved. Represents potential budget exposure for risk management and lender notification purposes. Denominated in currency_code.']
  reporting_period_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which this budget snapshot is valid. Enables point-in-time budget reporting, period-over-period variance analysis, and investor reporting aligned to financial close periods.']
  sap_wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Project Systems Work Breakdown Structure element code corresponding to this budget record. Enables financial consolidation, GL posting, and CAPEX capitalization tracking in SAP.']
  soft_cost_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the current budget allocated to soft costs — indirect costs including architecture, engineering, legal, permitting, financing fees, and project management. Denominated in currency_code.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this construction budget record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks the approved CAPEX budget for each development project at both summary and cost-code-level detail. Summary level captures original budget, approved budget, current budget (including approved change orders), committed costs, actual costs to date, forecast at completion, and variance by category (hard costs, soft costs, land, financing, contingency). Line-item level captures individual cost codes (CSI division or custom WBS element), cost category, original and revised budget amounts, committed and actual spend, and remaining budget per line. Enables CAPEX draw schedule management, cost control, variance analysis, and investor reporting. Aligned with Procore budget module and SAP S/4HANA project systems for source-system integration.'
}

Table development.change_order {
  change_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the change order record. Primary key for the development.change_order data product.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Approved change orders generate AP invoices for contractor payment. Finance teams link change orders to resulting AP invoices for payment tracking, lien waiver management, and construction audit — a s']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPEX budget line item in the project budget against which this change order cost impact is recorded. Supports budget variance analysis and CAPEX draw schedule management.']
  capital_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.capital_call. Business justification: Approved change orders that exceed contingency trigger additional capital calls from investors to fund cost overruns. GPs issue capital calls specifically tied to approved change orders — a standard f']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Change orders that alter scope of work often require permit amendments or new permits. The change order must reference the compliance permit being amended or the new permit issued for the revised scop']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Change orders are frequently triggered by compliance violations — inspectors cite non-compliant work, requiring corrective change orders to remediate. Tracking which violation triggered a change order']
  construction_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_budget. Business justification: Change orders directly modify the approved construction budget — approved_change_order_amount on construction_budget is the aggregate of all approved change orders. Linking change_order to constructio']
  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating construction contract against which this change order is issued. Links the change order to its parent contract for cumulative value tracking.']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_permit. Business justification: Change orders frequently arise from permit-required scope changes — municipal corrections, code compliance modifications, or permit amendments that trigger additional work. The change_order table curr']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractor or subcontractor entity that is the counterparty to the change order. Used for contractor performance and dispute tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Change orders affect specific cost centers in the GL. Finance teams post change order cost impacts to cost centers for budget variance reporting and CAPEX tracking — standard in real estate developmen']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Change orders are denominated in a specific currency for cost impact tracking. Multi-currency project reporting and lender draw package preparation require a normalized currency reference. currency_co']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the real estate development or capital improvement project under which this change order is raised. Enables project-level budget variance and CAPEX draw analysis.']
  potential_change_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Potential Change Order (PCO) or Owner Change Order Request (OCOR) in Procore from which this formal change order was generated. Maintains the full change management audit trail from identification through execution.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Change orders triggered by regulatory requirements (code changes, ADA compliance, environmental findings) must reference the governing regulatory obligation. This supports audit trails for regulatory-']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the change order through the approval lifecycle. Drives contract value management and budget commitment recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|voided — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  approved_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the change order received final approval from the designated approving authority. Triggers contract value adjustment and schedule extension recognition.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role designation of the individual or body that granted final approval for the change order (e.g., Owner Representative, Project Director, Investment Committee). Required for audit trail and delegation-of-authority compliance.']
  capex_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the change order cost impact into CAPEX budget categories: hard costs (construction labor and materials), soft costs (design, permits, fees), contingency draws, owner contingency, or Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance. Supports CAPEX draw schedule and investment reporting.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the originating cause of the change order. Distinguishes owner-directed changes, contractor-initiated scope modifications, unforeseen site conditions, design revisions, regulatory-driven changes, and value engineering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: owner_initiated|contractor_initiated|unforeseen_conditions|design_revision|regulatory_requirement|value_engineering — promote to reference product]']
  cost_code varchar [note: 'Project cost code or CSI MasterFormat division code to which the change order cost impact is allocated. Enables cost tracking by trade, work type, and budget line item within the project CAPEX budget.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary cost impact of the change order on the contract value, expressed in the contract currency. Positive values indicate additions to contract value; negative values indicate deductions (credits). Core field for CAPEX budget variance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change order record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  cumulative_co_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Running total of all approved change order cost impacts against the originating contract as of this change order\'s approval, inclusive of the current change order. Represents the total contract value adjustment from original contract sum. Used for contract value management and budget variance reporting.']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the nature of the dispute if the change order is contested, including the basis of disagreement on scope, cost, or schedule impact. Populated only when is_disputed is True.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the approved change order takes contractual effect, which may differ from the approval date. Governs when the contractor is authorized to proceed with changed scope and when schedule adjustments commence.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code in SAP S/4HANA or Yardi Voyager to which the change order cost impact is posted for financial consolidation and CAPEX capitalization. Aligns with the chart of accounts for fixed asset and project accounting.']
  is_disputed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change order is currently subject to a formal dispute between the owner and contractor. Disputed change orders require separate tracking for legal and claims management purposes.']
  is_time_and_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change order is priced on a time-and-material basis rather than a lump sum or unit price. T&M change orders require field verification of labor hours and material quantities before final cost settlement.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business or technical justification for the change order, including root cause analysis, reference to RFIs, design bulletins, or site conditions that necessitated the change. Supports dispute resolution and owner approval.']
  lump_sum_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed lump sum price agreed for the change order scope when priced on a lump sum basis. Null when the change order is priced on a time-and-material or unit price basis.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overhead and profit markup percentage applied by the contractor to direct costs on this change order, as negotiated per the contract terms. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used for cost reasonableness review.']
  number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable identifier for the change order as assigned in Procore or the contract administration system. Used in correspondence, RFIs, and dispute documentation. Format: CO-{ProjectCode}-{Sequence}.']
  original_contract_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original executed contract sum prior to any change orders, captured at the time of this change order for reference and variance calculation. Supports contract value management and budget baseline reporting.']
  prime_contract_co_number varchar [note: 'Change order number as recorded on the prime (owner-contractor) contract, which may differ from the internal Procore sequence number. Used for cross-referencing with owner records and AIA G701 documentation.']
  procore_co_reference varchar [note: 'Native change order identifier from the Procore Construction Management system, used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational source system.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Retainage percentage withheld from progress payments on this change order, as specified in the contract. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Governs cash flow and payment application processing.']
  revised_contract_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Updated total contract value after incorporating this change order, equal to the original contract amount plus cumulative change order amounts. Represents the current contract sum for payment application and budget tracking purposes.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision counter for the change order, incremented each time the change order is revised prior to final approval. Revision 0 represents the original submission.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the change order extends or compresses the contract completion date. Positive values indicate schedule extensions; negative values indicate accelerations. Critical for milestone tracking and liquidated damages assessment.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scope change being requested or directed, including the specific work added, deleted, or modified relative to the original contract scope. Critical for dispute documentation and audit trails.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the change order was formally submitted by the contractor or owner for review and approval. Marks the start of the approval clock for contractual response-time obligations.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Applicable sales tax, use tax, or VAT amount included in the change order cost impact. Tracked separately for tax accounting and CAPEX capitalization purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the change order record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.']
  work_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the physical work associated with the change order was substantially completed in the field. Used for payment application timing and project milestone tracking.']

  Note: 'Records all change orders issued against construction contracts — owner-initiated changes, contractor-initiated scope modifications, unforeseen conditions, and design revisions. Captures change order number, originating contract, change type, description of scope change, cost impact, schedule impact (days), approval status, approval date, approving authority, and cumulative change order total against the contract. SSOT for construction change order tracking within the development domain. Critical for contract value management, budget variance analysis, and dispute documentation.'
}

Table development.draw_request {
  draw_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) draw request (pay application) submitted against a construction contract. Primary key for this record.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Construction draw disbursements are processed as AP invoices. Finance teams reconcile approved draws against AP invoices for lender audit and payment tracking — a core construction accounting workflow']
  capital_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.capital_call. Business justification: Approved draw requests trigger equity capital calls to investors. The draw request approval by the lender and owner initiates the capital call process. Real estate development finance professionals al']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Lenders require active permit status verification as a condition of draw disbursement. Draw packages must confirm the governing compliance permit is valid and in good standing before funds are release']
  construction_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_budget. Business justification: A draw request (AIA G702/G703 pay application) draws against the approved CAPEX construction budget. Linking draw_request to construction_budget enables tracking of budget consumption per draw, remain']
  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction contract (prime contract or subcontract) under which this draw request is submitted.']
  construction_loan_financing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.financing. Business justification: Construction loan draw process: each draw_request is submitted against a specific construction loan (transaction.financing). Lenders approve draws against the loan commitment. Role-prefixed \'construct']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general contractor or prime contractor entity submitting this pay application.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Draw disbursements are posted to cost centers in the GL for CAPEX tracking and budget variance reporting. Real estate development finance requires cost center assignment on every draw for project cost']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Draw request disbursements are denominated in a specific currency. Lender reporting, title company disbursements, and multi-currency construction loan management require a normalized currency referenc']
  debt_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.debt_facility. Business justification: Draw requests are submitted against a specific construction loan (debt facility). Lender approval workflows, disbursement tracking, and covenant compliance all require linking each draw request to the']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. Business justification: Each draw request draws against a specific construction loan. Lenders require draws to reference the loan instrument for covenant compliance, disbursement authorization, and outstanding balance tracki']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development or construction project against which this draw request is submitted. Links to the Procore project record.']
  disbursement_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Draw disbursements are funded from a specific construction escrow or operating bank account. Lenders require tracking of the funding bank account per draw for construction loan administration and audi']
  lender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.lender. Business justification: Draw requests are submitted to and approved by a specific lender. draw_request has lender_approval_status and lender_approval_date but no lender FK. Lender-level draw tracking is required for construc']
  owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: Draw requests require owner approval per thresholds defined in the owner_agreement (capex_approval_threshold). Linking draw_request to owner_agreement enables the draw approval workflow to enforce agr']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Approved draw disbursements are executed through treasury payment runs. Linking draw_request to payment_run connects the construction draw lifecycle to payment execution, enabling reconciliation of ap']
  title_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.title_record. Business justification: Construction loan draw requests require title endorsement (update to title insurance policy) before each disbursement. draw_request already tracks title_review_status and title_review_date — linking t']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible alphanumeric identifier for this pay application as assigned by the contractor or project management system (e.g., \'PA-2024-007\'). Used for cross-referencing with contractor invoices and lender draw packages.']
  approved_change_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net cumulative dollar value of all approved change orders to date, which adjusts the original contract sum. Corresponds to \'Net Change by Change Orders\' on AIA G702. Positive for additions, negative for deductions.']
  architect_certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the project architect certified the draw request as accurately representing work completed per AIA G702. Architect certification is a prerequisite for owner approval and lender disbursement.']
  architect_certified_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount certified by the architect for payment, which may differ from the contractor\'s requested amount if the architect adjusts the certified value based on site observation. Corresponds to \'Amount Certified\' on AIA G702.']
  balance_to_finish decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining contract value yet to be earned, including retainage (revised_contract_amount minus total_earned_less_retainage). Corresponds to \'Balance to Finish, Including Retainage\' on AIA G702. Key metric for construction loan budget monitoring.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this draw request. Defines the close of the work period for which payment is being requested per AIA G702.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this draw request. Defines the beginning of the work period for which payment is being requested per AIA G702.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this draw request record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  disbursed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual dollar amount disbursed to the contractor, which may differ from net_payment_due if the lender or owner makes adjustments. Null until disbursement occurs.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Actual date on which funds were disbursed from the construction loan or owner\'s equity account to the contractor for this draw. Null until disbursement occurs. Critical for CAPEX cash flow management and loan draw tracking.']
  draw_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying this draw request within the construction contract. Corresponds to the Application Number on AIA G702. Used to track draw sequence and ensure no gaps in the payment application series.']
  draw_package_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document storage reference for the complete draw package submitted to the lender, including the AIA G702/G703 forms, supporting invoices, stored materials documentation, and lien waivers.']
  draw_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the draw request through the approval and disbursement workflow. Tracks progression from contractor submission through lender approval to actual disbursement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|conditionally_approved|approved|rejected|disbursed|voided — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference for the third-party construction inspector\'s report commissioned by the lender to verify work-in-place prior to approving this draw. Lender inspections are standard practice in construction loan administration.']
  is_notarized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lien waiver for this draw has been notarized. Some states and lenders require notarized lien waivers as a condition of disbursement.']
  lender_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the construction lender formally approved this draw request for disbursement from the construction loan facility.']
  lender_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the construction lender\'s review and approval of this draw request. Lender approval is required before funds are disbursed from the construction loan facility. Critical for construction loan administration.']
  lien_waiver_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate status of lien waiver collection for this draw request across all contractors and subcontractors. Indicates whether all required conditional and/or unconditional lien waivers have been received and validated.']
  materials_stored decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of materials and equipment presently stored on-site or in bonded storage but not yet incorporated into the work (Column F on AIA G703). Subject to lender approval and may require stored materials agreement.']
  net_payment_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount due to the contractor for this draw request (total_earned_less_retainage minus previous_payments). Corresponds to \'Current Payment Due\' on AIA G702. This is the amount requested for disbursement.']
  original_contract_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original executed contract sum as stated in the construction contract before any approved change orders. Corresponds to the \'Original Contract Sum\' field on AIA G702. Used as the baseline for tracking contract value changes.']
  owner_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the property owner or owner\'s representative formally approved this draw request for submission to the construction lender.']
  owner_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the owner\'s review and approval of this draw request. Owner approval is required before lender disbursement can be initiated.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall percentage of the contract work completed to date, calculated as total_earned_to_date divided by revised_contract_amount. Corresponds to Column H on AIA G703. Critical metric for lender draw approval and construction loan monitoring.']
  previous_payments decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of all prior certified payments made to the contractor under this contract. Corresponds to \'Less Previous Certificates for Payment\' on AIA G702. Used to calculate the current net payment due.']
  procore_draw_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this draw request record within Procore Construction Management system. Used for system-of-record traceability and data lineage from the source operational system.']
  retainage_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Contractual retainage percentage applied to earned value for this draw (e.g., 0.1000 = 10%). May be reduced at substantial completion milestones per contract terms. Stored as a decimal fraction.']
  retainage_withheld_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'Retainage amount withheld from the current period\'s earned value. Corresponds to Column J on AIA G703. Calculated as the applicable retainage percentage applied to current period work completed.']
  retainage_withheld_previous decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative retainage withheld from all prior draw requests. Corresponds to Column I on AIA G703. Retainage is a percentage of earned value held back by the owner as security for contract completion.']
  revised_contract_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current contract sum including all approved change orders (original_contract_amount + approved_change_order_amount). Corresponds to \'Contract Sum to Date\' on AIA G702. Represents the total authorized contract value at time of this draw.']
  scheduled_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total scheduled value of all work items in the Schedule of Values (AIA G703) for this contract as of this draw. Represents the total value of work to be performed under the contract.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the contractor formally submitted this draw request / pay application to the owner or construction manager for review and approval.']
  title_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the title company completed its review and issued clearance (or noted exceptions) for this draw request.']
  title_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the title company\'s review of lien waivers and title endorsement for this draw. Title company clearance confirms no mechanic\'s liens have been filed and is typically required by the lender before disbursement.']
  total_earned_less_retainage decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount earned to date after deducting total retainage withheld (total_earned_to_date minus total_retainage_withheld). Corresponds to Column L on AIA G703. Represents the contractor\'s net certified entitlement.']
  total_earned_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative total amount earned to date including work completed in all prior periods, work completed in the current period, and materials presently stored (work_completed_previous + work_completed_current + materials_stored). Corresponds to Column G on AIA G703.']
  total_retainage_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative retainage withheld to date across all draw requests including current period (retainage_withheld_previous + retainage_withheld_current). Corresponds to Column K on AIA G703.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this draw request record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  waiver_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount covered by the lien waiver, representing the value of work and materials for which lien rights are being released. Should correspond to the net payment due or disbursed amount.']
  waiver_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document storage reference (URL, document management system path, or DocuSign envelope ID) for the executed lien waiver document associated with this draw. Enables retrieval for lender and title company review.']
  waiver_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the lien waiver was signed and executed by the contractor or subcontractor. Required for lien waiver validity and title company review.']
  waiver_through_date date [note: 'The date through which the lien waiver releases lien rights. Typically corresponds to the billing period end date. Ensures lien rights are waived for all work included in this draw.']
  waiver_type varchar [note: 'Type of lien waiver associated with this draw request per California Civil Code §8132-8138 or equivalent state standards. Conditional waivers are effective upon receipt of payment; unconditional waivers are effective immediately. Progress waivers cover partial work; final waivers cover all work through contract completion.']
  work_completed_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of work completed during the current billing period only (Column E on AIA G703). Represents the incremental progress achieved in this draw cycle.']
  work_completed_previous decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar value of work completed and certified in all prior draw requests (Column D on AIA G703). Used to calculate incremental work completed in the current billing period.']

  Note: 'Captures each CAPEX draw request (AIA G702/G703 pay application) submitted for payment against a construction contract, along with all associated lien waivers. Draw-level data: draw number, billing period, scheduled value, work completed to date, materials stored, total earned, retainage withheld, net payment due, lender approval status, title company review status, and disbursement date. Lien waiver detail per draw: waiver type (conditional/unconditional, progress/final), executing party (contractor or subcontractor), waiver amount, through-date, execution date, notarization status, and document storage reference. SSOT for construction payment applications and lien waiver compliance within the development domain. Critical for construction loan administration, CAPEX cash flow management, and lender/title requirements.'
}

Table development.inspection_event {
  inspection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each construction inspection event record in the development domain.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Construction inspection events for renovation and TI projects occur at specific existing buildings. Linking inspection_event to property.building enables inspection scheduling, compliance tracking, an']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Construction inspections resulting in building code or safety violations generate compliance violation records. Linking inspection_event to compliance.violation enables tracking of violation resolutio']
