Business justification: Inspections are conducted on work performed under a specific construction contract. Linking inspection_event to construction_contract enables tracking of inspection outcomes by contract, supporting co']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the building permit under which this inspection is conducted. Required for municipal and regulatory inspections.']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary contractor or subcontractor whose work scope is being inspected during this event.']
  corrective_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Construction inspection deficiencies trigger maintenance work orders for remediation tracking. Real estate operations teams create work orders directly from failed construction inspections to manage p']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development project against which this inspection event is recorded. Links to the Procore project record.']
  draw_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.draw_request. Business justification: inspection_event currently stores draw_request_number as a STRING. Lender draw inspections are a critical workflow: an inspector must certify work completion before a draw is disbursed. The lender_dra']
  floor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.floor. Business justification: Floor-by-floor construction inspections are standard in high-rise commercial development (e.g., floor shell completion, MEP rough-in per floor). Project managers and inspectors track floor-level inspe']
  reinspection_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this failed or partial-pass inspection to its subsequent re-inspection event record. Enables inspection chain traceability for COO documentation.']
  remediation_capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.capex_project. Business justification: Major construction inspection failures (structural, fire-life-safety) trigger capital remediation projects tracked in the maintenance capex domain. Real estate asset managers link inspection findings ']
  space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: TI completion inspections are conducted at the individual space/suite level in commercial development. Leasing and construction teams require space-level inspection sign-off before tenant delivery; le']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Unit-level construction inspections (final unit walk, punch list sign-off, COO per unit) are standard in multifamily development. Inspectors and project managers sign off on individual units before oc']
  areas_inspected varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific building areas, floors, zones, or grid locations covered during this inspection event (e.g., \'Level 3 east wing structural framing, columns C4 through C8\').']
  building_level varchar [note: 'Specific floor or level of the building structure where the inspection was conducted (e.g., \'B1\', \'L1\', \'Roof\'). Supports spatial tracking of inspection coverage across the development.']
  coo_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection event is on the critical path for Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance. True for all mandatory municipal inspections required before occupancy is permitted.']
  coo_sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspector or authority having jurisdiction (AHJ) provided final sign-off on this inspection, confirming it satisfies COO requirements. Null until formal sign-off is granted.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions required to remediate deficiencies identified during this inspection. Drives contractor work orders and re-inspection scheduling.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the contractor must complete all corrective actions identified in this inspection event. Used for milestone tracking and contractor compliance monitoring.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action must be completed before the next inspection or before work may proceed. True when inspection_result is fail or partial_pass.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection event record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct deficiencies or non-conformances identified during this inspection event. Used for quality trending and contractor performance analytics.']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of all deficiencies, non-conformances, or code violations identified during the inspection. Serves as the official record for corrective action planning and regulatory documentation.']
  deficiency_severity varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the most severe deficiency identified during this inspection. Critical deficiencies may trigger stop-work orders. Supports prioritization of corrective action and OSHA compliance reporting.']
  inspection_category varchar [note: 'Specific trade or discipline category being inspected (e.g., foundation, framing, electrical rough-in, plumbing rough-in, mechanical/HVAC, fire suppression, roofing, concrete, soils, waterproofing, final). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: foundation|framing|electrical|plumbing|mechanical_hvac|fire_suppression|roofing|concrete|soils|waterproofing|insulation|final — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the inspection was physically conducted at the construction site. This is the principal real-world event date for the inspection record.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection concluded on site. Combined with inspection_start_time to derive inspection duration for resource planning.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'General narrative notes recorded by the inspector or project manager during or after the inspection event. Captures observations not covered by structured fields.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this inspection event, used for cross-referencing with municipal records, Procore, and lender inspection reports. Format: INSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  inspection_report_number varchar [note: 'Official report number assigned to the inspection report document generated from this event. Used for document control and cross-referencing with Procore document management.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Formal outcome of the inspection as recorded by the inspector. Pass results advance the project toward Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance. Fail and partial_pass results require corrective action and re-inspection.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection commenced on site. Used for OSHA compliance documentation and inspector time-on-site tracking.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection event. Passed and partial_pass statuses contribute to Certificate of Occupancy (COO) milestone tracking. Failed status triggers corrective action workflow.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by the authority or party conducting it. Municipal inspections are required for Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance. Third-party special inspections cover structural, soils, and materials testing. Lender inspections support Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) draw schedule approvals. Owner quality control (QC) inspections are internal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: municipal|third_party_special|lender|owner_qc|fire_marshal|environmental|utility|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or agency the inspector represents (e.g., municipal building department, third-party inspection firm, lender, owner). Distinguishes authority of inspection for COO and compliance purposes.']
  inspector_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or certification number of the inspector, required for third-party special inspectors and municipal building officials. Supports regulatory compliance documentation.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who conducted the inspection. May be a municipal building official, third-party special inspector, lender representative, or owner quality control (QC) inspector.']
  lender_draw_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection event is associated with a Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) draw schedule request. Lender inspections must pass before draw funds are released.']
  osha_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection event includes OSHA safety compliance verification. Required for all inspections conducted on active construction sites under OSHA 29 CFR 1926 standards.']
  osha_violation_noted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any OSHA safety violations were identified during this inspection event. True triggers mandatory OSHA compliance reporting and corrective action documentation.']
  photo_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to photographic evidence captured during the inspection. Photos document both compliant work and deficiencies for legal and regulatory purposes.']
  reinspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date for the follow-up re-inspection after corrective actions have been completed. Null if reinspection_required is False.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up re-inspection is required after corrective actions are completed. Automatically set to True when inspection_result is fail or partial_pass.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to the official inspection report document stored in the document management system (Procore or DocuSign CLM). Supports audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for the inspection. Compared against inspection_date to track scheduling adherence and delays impacting the construction milestone schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection event record was last modified. Tracks changes to inspection results, corrective actions, or sign-off status for audit compliance.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather conditions at the time of the inspection. Relevant for outdoor inspections where weather may affect work quality assessment or inspector access. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|snow|high_wind|extreme_heat|fog — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records all construction inspections conducted on a development project — municipal building inspections, third-party special inspections, lender inspections, and owner quality control inspections. Captures inspection type, inspection date, inspector name and affiliation, areas inspected, pass/fail result, deficiencies noted, corrective action required, re-inspection date, and final sign-off. Supports COO issuance tracking and OSHA compliance documentation.'
}

Table development.project_schedule {
  project_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the project schedule record in the development domain. Primary key for the master schedule of a real estate development project.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Project schedules drive budget phasing — milestone dates determine when costs are expected to be incurred. Linking schedule to finance_budget enables cash flow forecasting and budget-to-schedule varia']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Multi-building development projects maintain building-level schedule milestones (shell completion, MEP, COO per building). Project managers and lenders track building-specific schedule performance for']
  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_contract. Business justification: A project schedule tracks CPM milestones that are often contract-specific — particularly for GC agreements and subcontractor scopes with defined commencement_date, substantial_completion_date, and fin']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_permit. Business justification: project_schedule currently stores permit_reference as a STRING. Permit issuance and inspections are key schedule milestones in CPM scheduling. Replacing the string reference with a proper FK project_s']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent development project this schedule belongs to. Links the schedule to the project entity in the development domain.']
  entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.entitlement. Business justification: project_schedule currently stores zoning_approval_ref as a STRING. The project schedule is directly dependent on entitlement milestones — construction cannot begin until entitlements are approved. Rep']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Project schedules are directly driven by regulatory obligation deadlines — entitlement expirations, permit renewal windows, environmental review milestones. Schedulers explicitly model regulatory dead']
  reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reporting_period. Business justification: Project schedules are aligned to financial reporting periods for development cost forecasting and schedule performance index (SPI) reporting. Period-end development reports require schedule data tied ']
  baseline_finish_date date [note: 'The originally approved project completion date from the baseline schedule. Serves as the contractual reference for substantial completion and COO issuance milestones.']
  baseline_start_date date [note: 'The originally approved project start date from the baseline schedule. Used as the reference point for schedule variance analysis and SPI calculation throughout the project lifecycle.']
  capex_draw_schedule_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this project schedule to the associated CAPEX draw schedule in the finance system (Yardi or SAP S/4HANA). Enables alignment of construction milestone completion with capital draw requests to lenders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project schedule record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  critical_path_activity_count varchar [note: 'Number of activities on the critical path (total float = 0 or negative) in the current schedule. Used to assess schedule complexity and focus project management attention on critical activities.']
  current_finish_date date [note: 'The current forecast or actual project completion date from the latest schedule update. Used for investor milestone reporting and CAPEX draw schedule alignment.']
  current_start_date date [note: 'The current forecast or actual project start date reflecting the latest approved schedule update. May differ from baseline_start_date due to approved schedule changes.']
  data_date date [note: 'The as-of date through which actual progress has been recorded in this schedule update (also called the status date or time-now date in CPM scheduling). All forecast dates are projected forward from this date.']
  external_schedule_file_ref varchar [note: 'Path or URI to the native scheduling software file (e.g., Primavera P6 .xer or MS Project .mpp) stored in the document management system or cloud storage. Enables drill-back to the source schedule file.']
  far_approved decimal(6,4) [note: 'The approved Floor Area Ratio for the development project as granted by the zoning authority. Used to verify that the construction schedule aligns with the approved development envelope and FAR compliance requirements.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date on which the schedule was last updated with progress data or revised logic. Indicates the currency of the schedule information for reporting and audit purposes.']
  milestone_actual_date date [note: 'The date on which the milestone was actually achieved. Null if the milestone has not yet been completed. Populated upon milestone completion and used for as-built schedule records and regulatory filings.']
  milestone_forecast_date date [note: 'Current forecast date for milestone achievement based on the latest schedule update. Reflects schedule changes, delays, or acceleration. Used for investor milestone updates and CAPEX draw schedule alignment.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Name of the key project milestone tracked in this schedule record (e.g., \'Entitlement Approval\', \'Groundbreaking\', \'Foundation Pour\', \'Topping Out\', \'Substantial Completion\', \'COO Issuance\'). Supports milestone-level reporting to investors and lenders.']
  milestone_planned_date date [note: 'The baseline-approved planned date for this milestone as established in the original project schedule. Used as the reference for milestone variance calculation and investor commitment tracking.']
  milestone_predecessor_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of schedule activity or milestone IDs that must be completed before this milestone can begin, representing predecessor relationships in the CPM network. Supports critical path analysis and dependency visualization.']
  milestone_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or role of the party accountable for achieving this milestone (e.g., General Contractor, Owner\'s Representative, Architect, Civil Engineer, Municipality). Used for accountability tracking and escalation management.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the milestone: not_started, in_progress, completed, delayed (past forecast date without completion), or at_risk (forecast date within risk threshold). Drives milestone dashboard RAG status reporting.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the milestone per real estate development lifecycle: entitlement_approval, groundbreaking, foundation_pour, topping_out, substantial_completion, or coo_issuance (Certificate of Occupancy). Enables cross-project milestone benchmarking.']
  milestone_variance_days varchar [note: 'Variance in calendar days between the milestone forecast (or actual) date and the milestone planned date. Positive value indicates delay; negative indicates early completion. Reported to investors and lenders as a key development performance indicator.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall schedule percent complete as of the data date, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Derived from the CPM schedule and reported to investors and lenders as a key construction progress indicator.']
  procore_project_reference varchar [note: 'The native project identifier from Procore Construction Management system. Used as the system-of-record cross-reference key for reconciling schedule data ingested from Procore into the lakehouse silver layer.']
  schedule_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this schedule version was formally approved by the authorized party. Establishes the effective date of the baseline or revised schedule for contractual and reporting purposes.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the project schedule (e.g., \'Phase 1 Baseline Schedule Rev 3\'). Used for display in dashboards and investor milestone reports.']
  schedule_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative accompanying the schedule update, describing key changes, delays, recovery actions, and risk items. Submitted with each schedule update to the owner and lender as part of the monthly construction report.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the schedule document, used in Procore and cross-referenced in investor reports and construction contracts. Follows the pattern SCH-{ProjectCode}.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule record: draft (under preparation), approved (formally accepted), active (in use for progress tracking), superseded (replaced by a newer version), or archived (historical record only).']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule version: baseline (original approved plan), current (live working schedule), recovery (corrective re-plan), what_if (scenario analysis), or as_built (post-completion record). Drives which schedule is used for SPI and variance reporting.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in calendar days between the current finish date and the baseline finish date (current_finish_date minus baseline_finish_date). Positive value indicates delay; negative indicates ahead of schedule. Key metric for construction oversight reporting.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the schedule (e.g., \'B0\' for baseline, \'R1\', \'R2\' for revisions). Tracks schedule evolution through the project lifecycle per CPM scheduling best practices.']
  scheduling_software varchar [note: 'CPM scheduling application used to produce this schedule (e.g., Primavera P6, MS Project). Required for interoperability and file format management when importing schedule data into Procore or the lakehouse.']
  spi decimal(6,4) [note: 'Schedule Performance Index from Earned Value Management: ratio of earned value to planned value (EV/PV). SPI < 1.0 indicates the project is behind schedule; SPI > 1.0 indicates ahead of schedule. Key metric for lender and investor reporting.']
  total_activity_count varchar [note: 'Total number of schedule activities (tasks) in this schedule version. Used to assess schedule density and compliance with owner-required schedule specifications.']
  total_float_days varchar [note: 'Total float (slack) of the project\'s critical path in calendar days as of the data date. Negative total float indicates the project is behind the contractual completion date. Critical for schedule risk assessment.']
  update_frequency varchar [note: 'Contractually required or agreed frequency at which the schedule is updated with progress data: weekly, bi_weekly, monthly, or milestone_driven. Governs the cadence of schedule submissions to the owner and lender.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project schedule record was last modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master schedule record for a development project — the baseline and current CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule with integrated milestone tracking. Schedule-level data: schedule version, baseline and current start/finish dates, total float, critical path activities count, schedule performance index (SPI), percent complete, last update date, and scheduling software reference (Primavera P6, MS Project). Milestone-level data: milestone name and type (entitlement approval, groundbreaking, foundation pour, topping out, substantial completion, COO issuance), planned date, forecast date, actual date, days variance, responsible party, predecessor relationships, and completion status. SSOT for project scheduling and milestone tracking within the development domain. Enables critical path analysis, schedule performance reporting, and investor milestone updates.'
}

Table investment.fund {
  fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the real estate investment fund record. Primary key for the fund master registry.']
  base_currency_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Fund base currency drives NAV calculation, investor capital account statements, and multi-currency consolidation reporting. Role-prefixed to distinguish from other potential currency FKs on fund; norm']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fund-level expenses (management fees, admin costs, audit fees) are tracked to a fund cost center for fund-level P&L reporting, fee calculations, and expense allocation to LPs per the LPA.']
  domicile_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Fund domicile country determines regulatory classification, tax treaty applicability, FATCA/CRS reporting obligations, and foreign investor withholding tax rates — all critical for fund administration']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Fund target geographies define investment mandate boundaries used in deal screening, geographic allocation reporting, and LP mandate compliance. Normalizing to geographic_hierarchy enables market-leve']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Funds are domiciled in specific jurisdictions governing SEC/AIFMD/local regulatory obligations. Fund compliance reporting, regulatory filings, and penalty risk management all require knowing the gover']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Fund financial statements (NAV, GAV, distributions, carried interest) are produced from a specific ledger. Fund accounting requires ledger association for GAAP audit, tax reporting, and ILPA-compliant']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Every real estate fund is structured as a legal entity for REIT election, SEC reporting, consolidation, and tax filing. Fund administrators and controllers require this link to map fund-level reportin']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Fund mandates are classified by market segment (core, value-add, opportunistic) for INREV/NCREIF benchmark reporting, LP marketing materials, and fund-of-funds allocation. A real estate fund manager w']
  parent_fund_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fund (parent_fund_id)']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Fund investment policy statements define target property types (office, industrial, multifamily) for deal screening and mandate compliance reporting. A real estate fund manager expects this FK to enfo']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Funds have direct regulatory obligations (SEC registration, AIFMD reporting, FATCA). Linking fund to its primary governing regulatory obligation is fundamental for compliance tracking, penalty risk ma']
  target_building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Fund investment mandates specify target building class (Class A, B, C) as a core underwriting constraint. Used in IC approval documents, LP offering memoranda, and portfolio concentration limit monito']
  actual_aum decimal(20,2) [note: 'Current total Assets Under Management (AUM) representing the fair market value of all assets held by the fund, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Sourced from MRI Software and Argus Enterprise valuations. Updated at each reporting period.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the independent external auditor engaged to audit the fund\'s annual financial statements. Required for SEC-registered funds and institutional LP due diligence.']
  called_capital decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cumulative capital drawn down from LP commitments to fund investments, fees, and expenses, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Used in DPI (Distributions to Paid-In) and TVPI (Total Value to Paid-In) multiple calculations.']
  carried_interest_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'GP\'s share of profits above the preferred return hurdle, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2000 = 20%). Represents the performance-based compensation to the General Partner. Sourced from LPA terms in DocuSign CLM.']
  concentration_limit decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum percentage of total fund GAV that may be invested in any single asset or property, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.20 = 20%). Enforces diversification requirements per the LPA investment mandate.']
  distributed_capital decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cumulative capital returned to Limited Partners (LPs) through asset dispositions, refinancing proceeds, and income distributions, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Key input for DPI multiple and IRR calculations.']
  esg_framework varchar [note: 'Primary ESG reporting framework adopted by the fund (e.g., GRESB for real estate, UN PRI, TCFD). Null if esg_mandate is false. Drives sustainability data collection and disclosure requirements.']
  esg_mandate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fund operates under a formal Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) investment mandate requiring ESG screening, LEED/BREEAM certification targets, or sustainability reporting obligations.']
  extension_options varchar [note: 'Number of permissible one-year extension options available to the General Partner (GP) beyond the base fund term, as specified in the LPA. Supports fund lifecycle planning and investor communication.']
  fee_basis varchar [note: 'The capital base upon which the annual management fee rate is applied. Committed capital is common during the investment period; invested capital or NAV is common during the harvest period.']
  fee_cap decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum absolute dollar amount of management fees that may be charged to the fund in any given year, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Null if no cap is specified in the LPA.']
  fee_waiver_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of conditions under which management fees may be waived or reduced (e.g., GP co-investment, fund underperformance, LP negotiated side letter terms). Sourced from LPA and side letter agreements in DocuSign CLM.']
  final_close_date date [note: 'Date on which the fund completed its capital raising period and accepted its last investor commitment. Marks the end of the fundraising phase and the start of the investment period. Null for open-end funds.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fund across systems (e.g., MRI Software, Argus Enterprise, SAP S/4HANA). Used as the business key in cross-system reconciliation and regulatory filings.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the investment fund or vehicle as registered with the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., SEC, FINRA). Used in investor communications, regulatory filings, and reporting.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fund. Drives operational workflows, investor reporting obligations, and capital call eligibility. Fundraising indicates the fund is accepting commitments; fully_invested indicates deployment is complete; harvesting indicates asset disposition phase.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investment vehicle structure. Determines regulatory treatment, investor redemption rights, and accounting framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_end|closed_end|reit|partnership|cmbs_trust|rmbs_trust|separate_account|co_investment — promote to reference product]']
  gav decimal(20,2) [note: 'Gross Asset Value (GAV) of the fund representing the total fair market value of all real estate assets before deducting fund-level debt and liabilities, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Used in leverage ratio and LTV calculations.']
  gp_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the General Partner entity responsible for managing the fund, making investment decisions, and fulfilling fiduciary obligations to Limited Partners (LPs). Sourced from MRI Software Investment Management module.']
  inception_date date [note: 'Date on which the fund was legally formed and commenced operations. Used as the start date for IRR, equity multiple, and since-inception performance calculations in Argus Enterprise.']
  investment_period_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the fund\'s investment period expires and the GP is no longer permitted to make new investments or call capital for new acquisitions. After this date, capital calls are limited to follow-on investments and fund expenses.']
  investment_strategy varchar [note: 'Risk-return strategy classification of the fund per industry convention. Core strategies target stabilized assets with lower risk; opportunistic strategies target higher-risk, higher-return repositioning or development. Drives benchmark selection and performance attribution in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  management_fee_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Annual management fee charged by the GP to the fund, expressed as a decimal of the fee basis (e.g., 0.0150 = 1.50% of committed capital). Sourced from LPA terms and tracked in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  max_deal_size decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum individual asset or transaction size (in base currency) that the fund is mandated to consider for investment. Prevents over-concentration in a single asset and ensures portfolio diversification per LPA guidelines.']
  min_deal_size decimal(20,2) [note: 'Minimum individual asset or transaction size (in base currency) that the fund is mandated to consider for investment. Ensures portfolio concentration and operational efficiency thresholds are maintained.']
  nav decimal(20,2) [note: 'Net Asset Value (NAV) of the fund as of the most recent valuation date, calculated as total assets minus total liabilities, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Primary metric for open-end fund unit pricing and investor redemption calculations.']
  preferred_return_hurdle decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum annualized return rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%) that must be distributed to Limited Partners (LPs) before the General Partner (GP) is entitled to receive carried interest. Also known as the hurdle rate or preferred return.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the fund produces and distributes financial and performance reports to Limited Partners (LPs). Drives reporting calendar in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  target_aum decimal(20,2) [note: 'Target total Assets Under Management (AUM) the fund intends to reach at full deployment, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Used in fundraising materials, investor presentations, and portfolio planning in Argus Enterprise.']
  target_equity_multiple decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target equity multiple (TVPI — Total Value to Paid-In) the fund aims to return to investors, expressed as a ratio (e.g., 1.80 = 1.8x). Represents total distributions plus residual NAV divided by total called capital.']
  target_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target net Internal Rate of Return (IRR) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1200 = 12.00%) that the fund aims to deliver to investors over its life. Used in fund marketing materials, LP reporting, and performance benchmarking in Argus Enterprise.']
  target_leverage_max decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio permitted under the fund\'s investment mandate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 = 65%). Defines the upper bound of the fund\'s leverage range. Exceeding this threshold may trigger LP notification requirements.']
  target_leverage_min decimal(6,4) [note: 'Minimum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio permitted under the fund\'s investment mandate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.40 = 40%). Defines the lower bound of the fund\'s leverage range for portfolio construction and risk management.']
  term_years varchar [note: 'Contractual life of the fund in years from inception to scheduled termination, as defined in the Limited Partnership Agreement (LPA) or fund prospectus. Null for open-end funds with no fixed term.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date on which the fund is dissolved and all assets are liquidated and distributed to investors. Null for open-end or perpetual-life funds. Drives WALT and WALE calculations for portfolio wind-down planning.']
  total_commitments decimal(20,2) [note: 'Aggregate capital committed by all Limited Partners (LPs) to the fund as of the final close, expressed in the fund\'s base currency. Represents the maximum capital available for investment and fee calculation basis.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal appraisal or valuation of the fund\'s portfolio used to calculate NAV and GAV. Typically quarterly for institutional funds. Sourced from Argus Enterprise valuation models.']

  Note: 'Master registry of real estate investment funds, REITs, and pooled investment vehicles. Captures fund name, type (open-end, closed-end, REIT, partnership, CMBS/RMBS trust), domicile jurisdiction, base currency, target and actual AUM, inception date, fund status, investment strategy (core, core-plus, value-add, opportunistic), target return metrics (IRR, equity multiple), fee terms (management fee rate, carried interest, preferred return hurdle, fee basis, fee cap, fee waiver conditions), fund term and extension options, GP entity, regulatory classification, ESG mandate, and investment mandate parameters (target property types, geographies, leverage range, deal size range, concentration limits). SSOT for fund identity, strategy, terms, fee structures, and mandate parameters.'
}

Table investment.portfolio {
  portfolio_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the investment portfolio. Primary key for the portfolio master record. Sourced from MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Cross-border portfolios report area metrics (GLA, NLA, occupancy) in either sqft or sqm. The UOM must be standardized at portfolio level for consistent investor reporting and INREV/NCREIF submissions.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment fund or vehicle (REIT, closed-end fund, open-end fund, partnership, CMBS/RMBS) under which this portfolio is structured and managed.']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Portfolio geographic focus defines investment mandate boundaries for geographic allocation reporting, deal screening, and LP mandate compliance monitoring against target market exposure.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Portfolios with geographic focus are subject to jurisdiction-specific compliance requirements (local property laws, ESG mandates, tax regimes). Portfolio compliance reporting and regulatory filing cal']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Portfolio financial statements (NAV, GAV, NOI) are produced from a specific ledger (GAAP, IFRS, tax). Portfolio-level reporting requires ledger association for audit, regulatory filings, and investor ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consolidation legal entity responsible for financial reporting and regulatory filings for this portfolio. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA financial consolidation structure.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Portfolio investment strategy (core, value-add, opportunistic) maps to market_segment for INREV/NCREIF benchmark attribution, performance reporting by strategy, and LP mandate compliance verification.']
  parent_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on portfolio (parent_portfolio_id)']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Portfolio property type focus defines sector allocation mandate used in asset acquisition screening, benchmark selection (NCREIF sector indices), and investor reporting on sector concentration.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Portfolios face regulatory obligations tied to asset class and geography (ERISA, Dodd-Frank, local ESG mandates). Portfolio-level obligation tracking drives compliance calendars, responsible departmen']
  reporting_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Portfolio reporting currency drives financial consolidation, investor capital account statements, and multi-currency NAV reporting. Role-prefixed to clarify reporting context; normalizes the denormali']
  target_building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Portfolio investment mandates define target building class for asset selection, benchmark peer group assignment, and investor reporting. Used in portfolio strategy documents and concentration limit co']
  target_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Portfolio strategy documents specify target lease structure (NNN, gross, modified gross) which drives NOI modeling, CAM recovery projections, and benchmark comparisons. Role-prefixed \'target_\' as port']
  benchmark_index varchar [note: 'The market index or peer benchmark against which portfolio performance (IRR, TWR, total return) is measured (e.g., NCREIF NPI, MSCI Real Estate Index, FTSE NAREIT All Equity REITs Index).']
  called_capital decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total equity capital drawn down (called) from investors and deployed into portfolio assets as of the most recent reporting date. Used to calculate remaining uncalled capital and deployment pace.']
  carried_interest_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'General partner\'s carried interest (promote) rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.20 = 20%) representing the share of profits above the preferred return. Core economic term in fund waterfall and partnership agreements.']
  committed_capital decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total equity capital formally committed by investors to the portfolio as of the most recent reporting date. Distinct from called/deployed capital. Tracks fundraising progress against target fund size.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the portfolio master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management per SOX and GDPR requirements.']
  distribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which income distributions (dividends, preferred returns) are made to investors from portfolio cash flows. Governs investor cash flow expectations and treasury planning.']
  ffo_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portfolio reports Funds From Operations (FFO) and Adjusted Funds From Operations (AFFO) per NAREIT standards. Applicable primarily to REIT-structured portfolios for SEC and investor reporting.']
  inception_date date [note: 'The official date on which the portfolio was established and began accepting capital commitments or acquiring assets. Used as the baseline for IRR, TWR, and performance period calculations.']
  leverage_policy varchar [note: 'Portfolio-level leverage policy classification governing the use of debt financing. Unleveraged = no debt; Low = LTV <30%; Moderate = LTV 30-60%; High = LTV >60%. Aligns with investment strategy and risk mandate.']
  management_fee_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Annual asset management fee rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0100 = 1.00% of AUM or GAV) charged by the investment manager. Disclosed in fund offering documents and regulatory filings.']
  open_end_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the portfolio is structured as an open-end fund allowing ongoing investor subscriptions and redemptions (True) versus a closed-end fund with a fixed capital raise and defined term (False).']
  portfolio_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the portfolio used in investor reporting, Argus Enterprise, MRI Software, and regulatory filings. Unique across all portfolios.']
  portfolio_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the investment portfolio as used in investor communications, fund documents, and management reporting (e.g., \'Core Office Fund III\', \'Diversified Income REIT Portfolio\').']
  portfolio_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the portfolio. Active = fully operational; Inactive = temporarily not deploying capital; Winding Down = in liquidation/disposition phase; Closed = fully liquidated; Pending Launch = pre-inception setup; Suspended = operations halted pending review.']
  portfolio_type varchar [note: 'Investment strategy classification of the portfolio reflecting risk-return profile. Core = stabilized low-risk assets; Core Plus = modest value enhancement; Value Add = repositioning required; Opportunistic = highest risk/return; Diversified = multi-sector; Sector Specific = single property type focus; Debt = mortgage/CMBS/RMBS instruments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: core|core_plus|value_add|opportunistic|diversified|sector_specific|debt — promote to reference product]']
  preferred_return_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Annualized preferred return rate (hurdle rate) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%) that must be paid to limited partners before the general partner participates in carried interest. Core term in fund waterfall structures.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this portfolio record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary sources are MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  sox_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portfolio is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) internal control requirements, applicable to publicly traded REITs and entities with SEC reporting obligations.']
  target_aum decimal(20,2) [note: 'Target total Assets Under Management (AUM) for the portfolio representing the aggregate market value of all real estate assets managed on behalf of investors. Used for fee calculation and capacity planning.']
  target_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target portfolio-level Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%) representing the expected NOI yield on asset value. Used for acquisition underwriting and portfolio valuation benchmarking.']
  target_equity_multiple decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target equity multiple (EM) representing the total expected return of invested capital as a ratio (e.g., 1.80x means $1.80 returned per $1.00 invested). Complements IRR as a return metric in investor presentations.']
  target_fund_size decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total target equity capital raise for the portfolio/fund expressed in the reporting currency. Represents the maximum committed capital the fund intends to raise from investors. Used for capital deployment planning.']
  target_gav decimal(20,2) [note: 'Target Gross Asset Value (GAV) representing the total undiscounted market value of all assets in the portfolio at full deployment, before deducting liabilities. Used for capital raising targets and AUM reporting.']
  target_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target annualized Internal Rate of Return (IRR) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0850 = 8.50%) representing the expected return on invested equity over the portfolio\'s life. Primary performance hurdle for investor commitments.']
  target_ltv decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target portfolio-level Loan-to-Value Ratio (LTV) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.60 = 60%) representing the maximum leverage policy for the portfolio. Governs debt capacity and risk management guidelines.']
  target_nav decimal(20,2) [note: 'Target Net Asset Value (NAV) representing the portfolio\'s equity value after deducting all liabilities from GAV. Key metric for open-end fund unit pricing, investor redemptions, and performance reporting.']
  target_noi decimal(20,2) [note: 'Target annual Net Operating Income (NOI) for the portfolio representing total revenue less operating expenses before debt service and capital expenditures. Core income metric for CAP rate valuation and DSCR analysis.']
  target_occupancy_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target portfolio-level physical occupancy rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 = 95%) representing the proportion of Net Leasable Area (NLA) that is leased and occupied. Core operational KPI for income forecasting.']
  target_wale decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target Weighted Average Lease Expiry (WALE) in years representing the income-weighted average time until lease expiry across all portfolio assets. Distinct from WALT as it measures time to expiry rather than total term.']
  target_walt decimal(8,4) [note: 'Target Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) in years representing the income-weighted average remaining lease duration across all portfolio assets. Key metric for income stability and refinancing risk assessment.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual date on which the portfolio is fully wound down and closed. Null for open-ended portfolios. Used for fund lifecycle management and investor distribution planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the portfolio master record. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance for investment management records.']
  valuation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which independent or internal valuations are conducted for portfolio assets to update NAV and GAV. Quarterly is standard for institutional funds; monthly for open-end funds with frequent redemptions.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Primary valuation methodology applied to assets within this portfolio for NAV and GAV calculations. DCF = Discounted Cash Flow; Direct Capitalization = NOI/CAP Rate; Sales Comparison = comparable transactions; Cost Approach = replacement cost; NAV Based = for fund-of-funds.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Legal and structural form of the investment vehicle housing this portfolio. Determines regulatory reporting obligations (SEC for REITs), tax treatment, and investor eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reit|open_end_fund|closed_end_fund|separate_account|partnership|joint_venture|cmbs|rmbs — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for an investment portfolio — a named collection of real estate assets managed under a unified strategy. Captures portfolio name, portfolio type (core, diversified, sector-specific), associated fund, asset manager (AM), benchmark index, target GAV, target NAV, target NOI, geographic focus, property type focus, WALT target, WALE target, portfolio inception date, reporting currency, consolidation entity, and portfolio status. Serves as the SSOT for portfolio-level identity and strategy. Sourced from MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.'
}

Table investment.investor {
  investor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the investor record in the real estate investment platform. Primary key for the investor entity.']

Business justification: Investors are domiciled in specific jurisdictions affecting tax treatment, AML requirements, and regulatory filings. Jurisdiction linkage is essential for FATCA/CRS classification, cross-border compli']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Institutional investors (pension funds, fund-of-funds, family offices) invest through a legal entity. Linking investor to their legal entity supports KYC/AML compliance, ERISA tracking, tax reporting,']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Investor investment strategy (core, value-add, opportunistic) maps to market_segment for fund-investor mandate matching, co-investment screening, and investor relations reporting on strategy alignment']
  preferred_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Investor preferred currency drives distribution payment processing, capital account statement generation, and FX conversion for multi-currency fund distributions. Role-prefixed to distinguish from oth']
  target_property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Investor mandate specifies target property type for deal matching, co-investment eligibility screening, and fund-investor mandate alignment reporting used in investor relations and capital raising.']
  accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the investor\'s accreditation verification expires. Accreditation must be re-verified periodically to maintain eligibility for private placement investments under SEC Regulation D. Drives compliance monitoring and investor eligibility gating.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory accreditation classification of the investor under SEC Regulation D. Accredited investors meet income or net worth thresholds permitting participation in private placements. Qualified purchaser status permits investment in 3(c)(7) funds. Critical for fund eligibility gating and regulatory compliance.']
  aml_status varchar [note: 'Current Anti-Money Laundering (AML) screening status for the investor. Cleared indicates the investor has passed sanctions screening and adverse media checks. Flagged indicates a potential match requiring enhanced due diligence. Mandatory for regulatory compliance under FinCEN and FATF guidelines.']
  co_investment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor is eligible to participate in co-investment opportunities alongside the fund. Co-investment rights are typically granted to anchor investors and platinum-tier relationships. True enables the investor to receive co-investment deal flow notifications and participate in direct deal allocations.']
  committed_capital_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total capital amount formally committed by the investor to real estate funds or investment vehicles, expressed in the investor\'s preferred currency. Represents the contractual obligation to fund capital calls up to this amount. Core metric for AUM (Assets Under Management) calculation and fund capacity management in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investor record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the audit trail creation event. Used for data lineage, compliance audit trails, and SOX financial controls reporting.']
  crs_classification varchar [note: 'OECD Common Reporting Standard (CRS) entity classification for automatic exchange of financial account information between tax authorities. Reportable Person indicates the investor\'s account information must be reported to their country of tax residency. Drives annual CRS reporting obligations.']
  distribution_bank_account_ref varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the investor\'s designated bank account for distribution wire transfers and capital return payments. Full account details are stored in the secure payment vault; this field holds only the reference token. Used by Yardi Voyager AP module for distribution processing.']
  distribution_preference varchar [note: 'Investor\'s preferred frequency and method for receiving investment distributions (income and return of capital). Quarterly is most common for real estate funds. Reinvest indicates the investor elects to reinvest distributions into additional fund units. Drives distribution waterfall processing in MRI Software and Yardi Voyager.']
  esg_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor has a formal ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) investment mandate requiring portfolio companies and assets to meet defined sustainability criteria. True triggers ESG screening in deal sourcing and LEED/BREEAM certification requirements in asset selection.']
  fatca_classification varchar [note: 'FATCA (Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act) entity classification for the investor. Determines US withholding tax obligations and IRS reporting requirements. US Person classification triggers Form W-9 requirements. Foreign Financial Institution (FFI) classification requires GIIN registration. NFFE (Non-Financial Foreign Entity) requires beneficial ownership disclosure.']
  investment_horizon_years varchar [note: 'Investor\'s preferred investment hold period expressed in years. Drives fund structure matching (open-end vs. closed-end funds), asset disposition timing, and WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) alignment in portfolio construction. Typical real estate investment horizons range from 3 to 10+ years.']
  investor_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier assigned to the investor upon onboarding. Used across MRI Software, Yardi Voyager, and Argus Enterprise to reference the investor in capital accounts, distribution statements, and fund reporting.']
  investor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investor record. Active indicates the investor is in good standing with capital deployed. Pending indicates onboarding in progress. Suspended indicates a hold due to KYC/AML review. Redeemed indicates full capital withdrawal. Closed indicates the investor relationship has been formally terminated.']
  investor_tier varchar [note: 'Relationship tier classification assigned to the investor based on committed capital, strategic importance, and relationship depth. Drives fee schedule application, co-investment priority access, reporting frequency, and relationship manager assignment. Platinum typically denotes anchor investors with largest AUM commitments.']
  investor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investor by entity category. Drives regulatory treatment, reporting obligations, and fee structures. Institutional includes insurance companies, banks, and asset managers. HNWI (High Net Worth Individual) denotes qualifying individuals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: institutional|hnwi|family_office|pension_fund|sovereign_wealth_fund|endowment|retail|reit|fund_of_funds — promote to reference product]']
  kyc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current Know Your Customer (KYC) documentation expires and renewal is required. Triggers automated alerts for re-verification workflows. Investors with expired KYC may be restricted from participating in new capital calls or fund subscriptions.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) due diligence process for the investor. Approved indicates all identity verification and background checks have been completed satisfactorily. Expired indicates KYC documentation has lapsed and requires renewal. Drives onboarding gates and capital call eligibility.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the investor as registered with regulatory authorities. For institutional investors, this is the registered entity name; for individuals, the full legal name as on government-issued identification. Used for KYC/AML compliance, subscription agreements, and capital account statements.']
  minimum_investment_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Minimum capital amount the investor is willing to deploy per investment vehicle or fund subscription. Used during fund structuring and deal allocation to ensure investment sizing meets investor thresholds. Expressed in the investor\'s preferred currency.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the investor completed the onboarding process and was formally admitted as an investor in the platform. Marks the start of the investor relationship lifecycle. Used for WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) equivalent calculations in investor tenure analytics and anniversary-based reporting.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor or any beneficial owner is classified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) under AML regulations. PEP status requires enhanced due diligence (EDD) and senior management approval for onboarding. True indicates PEP status confirmed; False indicates no PEP association identified.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for investor communications including capital call notices, distribution notices, quarterly reports, and K-1 tax documents. Must be a valid, monitored email address per investor subscription agreement requirements.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the investor organization. For individual investors, this is the investor themselves. For institutional investors, this is the designated relationship contact (e.g., portfolio manager, treasurer, or investment officer) responsible for fund communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the investor\'s designated contact. Used for urgent communications, capital call confirmations, and relationship management outreach. Stored in E.164 international format.']
  registered_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the investor\'s registered legal address. Used in conjunction with registered address fields for KYC verification, regulatory correspondence, and tax document delivery.']
  registered_address_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the investor\'s registered legal address country. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, sanctions screening, and tax treaty application.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the investor\'s registered legal address. Used for regulatory correspondence, K-1 mailing, subscription agreement execution, and AML/KYC address verification. For institutional investors, this is the registered office address.']
  sanctions_screened_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor has been screened against applicable sanctions lists including OFAC SDN List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, and UN Security Council Sanctions List. True indicates screening has been completed. Must be True before capital can be accepted from the investor.']
  side_letter_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor has a side letter agreement with the fund manager granting special terms, rights, or concessions beyond the standard limited partnership agreement (LPA). Side letters may include MFN (Most Favored Nation) clauses, reduced management fees, enhanced reporting rights, or co-investment priority. True triggers side letter terms application in fee and distribution calculations.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Declared source of the investor\'s capital being deployed into real estate investments. Required for AML compliance to verify the legitimacy of invested capital. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating_income|asset_sale|inheritance|pension_fund|endowment_corpus|debt_financing|equity_raise|insurance_reserves|sovereign_allocation — promote to reference product]']
  target_equity_multiple decimal(8,4) [note: 'Investor\'s target equity multiple (total distributions divided by total invested capital) for real estate investments. A multiple of 2.0x means the investor expects to receive twice their invested capital over the investment hold period. Used in Argus Enterprise for investment screening and portfolio performance benchmarking.']
  target_irr_max decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable Internal Rate of Return (IRR) threshold expressed as a decimal percentage. Defines the upper bound of the investor\'s risk-return appetite. Investors with low IRR maximums typically prefer core or core-plus strategies. Used alongside target_irr_min to define the investor\'s return corridor in Argus Enterprise.']
  target_irr_min decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable Internal Rate of Return (IRR) threshold expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%). Represents the investor\'s stated return hurdle below which investment opportunities are not considered. Used in Argus Enterprise for deal screening and portfolio matching against investor mandates.']
  tax_id_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax identification number provided. EIN (Employer Identification Number) for US entities, SSN (Social Security Number) for US individuals, ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number) for non-resident individuals, Foreign TIN for non-US entities, GIIN (Global Intermediary Identification Number) for FATCA-registered entities.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the investor. For US entities, this is the EIN (Employer Identification Number) or SSN (Social Security Number). For non-US entities, the equivalent national tax registration number. Used for IRS Form K-1 issuance, FATCA reporting, and withholding tax calculations.']
  tax_residency_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the investor\'s country of tax residency, which may differ from domicile country. Used for CRS (Common Reporting Standard) automatic exchange of information and applicable withholding tax treaty determination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the investor record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trail maintenance, and detecting stale KYC/AML data.']

  Note: 'SSOT for institutional and individual investor identities participating in real estate funds and portfolios. Captures investor legal name, investor type (institutional, HNWI, family office, pension fund, sovereign wealth fund, endowment, retail), KYC/AML status, accreditation status, domicile country, tax identification, preferred currency, investor tier, relationship manager, onboarding date, and investor status. Distinct from the owner domain (which tracks property ownership) — this entity tracks capital contributors to investment vehicles.'
}

Table investment.commitment {
  commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commitment']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: LP capital call proceeds are wired to a specific fund bank account designated in the subscription agreement. Commitment-level bank account tracking supports treasury reconciliation, wire instruction m']
  commitment_investor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investor (institutional or individual) making this capital commitment. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction.']
  commitment_transferee_investor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investor who received this commitment via secondary transfer. Populated only when commitment_status is \'transferred\'. Enables tracking of secondary market activity.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Commitment currency drives capital call calculations, FX conversion for multi-currency funds, uncalled capital reporting, and subscription agreement financial terms — essential for fund administration']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment fund or vehicle (REIT, partnership, CMBS/RMBS, closed-end fund) to which this capital commitment is made.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Investor commitments are made to specific fund legal entities governing subscription agreement jurisdiction, tax treatment, and ERISA compliance. Real estate fund counsel requires this link for entity']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Commitment-level investment strategy (often specified in side letters) maps to market_segment for allocation reporting, waterfall tier determination, and LP-specific mandate compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Capital commitments trigger regulatory filings (Form D exemption filings, ERISA plan asset filings, state blue sky filings). Compliance teams track which regulatory filing covers each commitment. A fu']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Investor commitments trigger regulatory obligations (subscription agreement compliance, ERISA, AML/KYC, FATCA). commitment.erisa_flag and aml_status indicate this relationship; linking to regulatory o']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Commitments are classified by transaction type (equity commitment, co-investment, preferred equity, mezzanine) for fund accounting, regulatory reporting, and LP capital account statements. Drives ERIS']
  amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The gross total capital amount the investor has committed to the fund or investment vehicle, expressed in the commitment currency. Represents the investor\'s maximum obligation under the subscription agreement. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  aum_contribution_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The portion of this commitment that contributes to the fund\'s reported Assets Under Management (AUM) figure, which may differ from the total commitment amount based on fund accounting policies and deployment stage.']
  called_capital_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total capital that has been called (drawn down) from the investor to date across all capital calls against this commitment. Represents cumulative funded capital. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  capital_call_schedule_type varchar [note: 'The methodology governing how and when capital calls are issued against this commitment. Determines cash flow planning for both the fund manager and the investor.']
  carried_interest_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The carried interest (promote) percentage applicable to this commitment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2000 for 20%). Represents the fund manager\'s share of profits above the preferred return.']
  close_date date [note: 'The fund closing date at which this commitment was accepted. Funds may have multiple closes (first close, second close, final close); this identifies which close the investor participated in.']
  co_investment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment is a co-investment alongside the main fund vehicle (True) or a standard fund commitment (False). Co-investments typically carry different fee structures and governance rights.']
  commitment_date date [note: 'The date on which the investor formally committed capital to the fund, typically the date the subscription agreement or LOI was executed. Serves as the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category).']
  commitment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the commitment, used in investor correspondence, subscription documents, and capital call notices. Sourced from MRI Software Investment Management.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capital commitment from LOI through full deployment. Tracks progression: pending (LOI received), executed (subscription agreement signed), funded (capital fully called and received), defaulted (investor failed to meet capital call), cancelled (commitment withdrawn), transferred (commitment assigned to another investor). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|executed|funded|defaulted|cancelled|transferred — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this capital commitment record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  default_date date [note: 'The date on which the investor defaulted on a capital call obligation. Populated only when commitment_status is \'defaulted\'. Triggers default remedies per the limited partnership agreement.']
  distributed_capital_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total capital returned to the investor through distributions (return of capital, realized gains, income distributions) against this commitment to date. Used in DPI (Distributions to Paid-In) calculations.']
  erisa_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor\'s assets are subject to ERISA (Employee Retirement Income Security Act) regulations. ERISA investors trigger plan asset rules and fiduciary obligations for the fund manager.']
  first_call_date date [note: 'The date of the first capital call issued against this commitment. Marks the beginning of the capital deployment phase and triggers the investment period clock.']
  fund_term_end_date date [note: 'The scheduled termination date of the fund, representing the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for the commitment\'s binding period. After this date, the fund is expected to be wound down and all capital returned.']
  investment_period_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the fund\'s investment period expires, after which no new capital calls may be issued for new investments (only follow-on investments and fund expenses). Critical for uncalled capital management.']
  loi_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Letter of Intent (LOI) that preceded the formal subscription agreement. Tracks the initial non-binding expression of interest in the commitment lifecycle.']
  management_fee_basis varchar [note: 'The basis on which the management fee is calculated for this commitment — committed capital (during investment period), invested capital (post-investment period), NAV (Net Asset Value), or GAV (Gross Asset Value).']
  management_fee_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The annual management fee rate applicable to this commitment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0150 for 1.5%). Typically charged on committed or invested capital depending on fund terms.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, or investor-specific terms associated with this commitment that are not captured in structured fields.']
  preferred_return_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The annual preferred return (hurdle rate) percentage applicable to this commitment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 for 8%). The fund manager earns carried interest only after this return threshold is met for the investor.']
  recallable_distributions_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The cumulative amount of distributions made to the investor that are subject to recall (clawback) for future capital calls, per the fund\'s limited partnership agreement. Affects the effective uncalled capital balance.']
  side_letter_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment is accompanied by a side letter granting the investor special rights, terms, or concessions beyond the standard limited partnership agreement (e.g., MFN rights, fee discounts, reporting preferences).']
  side_letter_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference identifier for the executed side letter associated with this commitment. Populated only when side_letter_flag is True. Links to DocuSign CLM for document retrieval.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by MRI Software Investment Management for this commitment record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  subscription_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the executed subscription agreement that formalizes the investor\'s capital commitment. Links to the DocuSign CLM contract lifecycle management system.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor is a tax-exempt entity (e.g., pension fund, endowment, sovereign wealth fund) for purposes of UBTI (Unrelated Business Taxable Income) and withholding tax calculations on distributions.']
  total_capital_calls_count varchar [note: 'The total number of capital calls that have been issued against this commitment to date. Used for tracking deployment pace and investor notification history.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The date on which this commitment was transferred to a new investor via secondary market transaction. Populated only when commitment_status is \'transferred\'.']
  uncalled_capital_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Remaining capital not yet called from the investor, calculated as commitment_amount minus called_capital_amount. Represents the investor\'s dry powder — capital committed but not yet deployed. Critical for fund liquidity and deployment planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this capital commitment record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change tracking, data lineage, and Silver layer incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Records the formal capital commitment made by an investor to a fund or investment vehicle. Captures commitment amount, commitment currency, commitment date, commitment status (pending, executed, funded, defaulted), subscription agreement reference, capital call schedule, total called capital, uncalled capital (dry powder), total distributed capital, and co-investment flag. Tracks the lifecycle from LOI through full deployment. Sourced from MRI Software Investment Management.'
}

Table investment.capital_call {
  capital_call_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each capital call notice record. Primary key for the capital_call data product.']

Business justification: Capital calls for asset acquisitions reference the appraisal supporting the acquisition price and LTV ratio presented to investors. Lender and LP notice requirements mandate citing the appraisal in th']
