RE controllers require cost center on building assets for depreciation posting, capital expendit']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Building assets (HVAC, elevators, fire suppression) are installed during development projects. Linking building_asset to dev_project enables warranty period tracking, as-built documentation retrieval,']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Every capitalized building asset has a corresponding fixed asset record for depreciation and book value tracking. RE asset managers require the physical-to-financial asset link for depreciation schedu']
  floor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.floor. Business justification: Physical building assets (HVAC units, electrical panels, fire suppression) are installed on specific floors. floor_location is a denormalized plain-text column. A proper FK to property.floor is essent']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial spaces may have dedicated assets (tenant-specific HVAC zone, dedicated electrical panel). Space-level asset tracking supports TI cost allocation, CAM reconciliation, and lease-end asset dis']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Asset registers in real estate are organized by property type for depreciation scheduling, insurance valuation, and capital planning. Asset lifecycle benchmarks (useful life, replacement cost) vary by']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Unit-specific assets (in-unit HVAC, water heater, appliances in residential) are tracked at the unit level. Unit-level asset register is required for tenant move-out inspections, warranty tracking, an']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping of the asset type used for portfolio-level O&M reporting and CAPEX planning (e.g., HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, Vertical Transportation, Fire Life Safety, Roofing, Structural, Generator, Building Envelope). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|Electrical|Plumbing|Vertical Transportation|Fire Life Safety|Roofing|Structural|Generator|Building Envelope|Other — promote to reference product]']
  asset_criticality varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the asset based on its impact on building operations, tenant services, and life safety if it fails. \'critical\' = life safety or total building operations impact; \'high\' = major tenant disruption; \'medium\' = partial service impact; \'low\' = minimal operational impact. Drives work order priority and PM frequency.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the asset. \'active\' = in normal service; \'inactive\' = temporarily out of service; \'decommissioned\' = permanently removed; \'under_repair\' = currently being serviced; \'pending_replacement\' = approved for capital replacement.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Externally-visible physical label or barcode affixed to the asset for field identification during inspections, work orders, and preventive maintenance rounds. Unique within a property. Sourced from Building Engines asset registry.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Specific equipment type within the asset category (e.g., \'Air Handling Unit\', \'Chiller\', \'Boiler\', \'Fire Suppression Sprinkler System\', \'Hydraulic Elevator\', \'Standby Generator\'). Drives preventive maintenance task templates in Building Engines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product given high cardinality]']
  bim_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique object identifier linking this asset to its corresponding component in the Building Information Modeling (BIM) model (e.g., Revit GUID or IFC GlobalId). Enables spatial visualization, clash detection, and digital twin integration.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification indicating whether maintenance and replacement activities for this asset are capitalized (CAPEX) or expensed (OPEX) per FASB ASC 360 and internal accounting policy. \'mixed\' applies to assets with both capital and operating components (e.g., partial HVAC overhaul).']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical condition rating of the asset as assessed during the most recent condition assessment. Drives prioritization of CAPEX replacement and preventive maintenance frequency. Scale: excellent=new/like-new; good=minor wear; fair=moderate wear; poor=significant deterioration; critical=imminent failure risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset record was first created in the Building Engines CMMS and ingested into the maintenance domain data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method applied to this asset for financial reporting purposes. \'straight_line\' is most common for real estate equipment. Aligns with Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA asset accounting configuration.']
  energy_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-specified energy efficiency rating or metric for the asset (e.g., SEER rating for HVAC, EER, COP, kW/ton). Null if not applicable. Used for ESG reporting, LEED documentation, and energy benchmarking under EPA ENERGY STAR.']
  energy_rated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset has an energy efficiency rating or certification (e.g., ENERGY STAR, LEED-qualifying equipment). Supports ESG reporting, LEED certification tracking, and sustainability benchmarking.']
  extended_warranty_expiry date [note: 'Date on which any purchased extended warranty coverage expires. Null if no extended warranty is in place. Used alongside manufacturer warranty to determine warranty claim eligibility for repair work orders.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the asset was physically installed and commissioned at the property. Anchors useful life calculations, warranty start dates, and CAPEX replacement year projections.']
  is_tenant_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is owned by a tenant rather than the property owner. Tenant-owned assets are tracked for maintenance coordination but excluded from owner CAPEX planning and depreciation schedules. Relevant for Tenant Improvement (TI) assets.']
  labor_warranty_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the installation contractor\'s labor warranty expires. Covers defects in workmanship separate from equipment manufacturer warranty. Null if no labor warranty applies.']
  last_condition_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal property condition assessment (PCA) or engineering inspection that produced the current condition rating. Indicates data freshness for capital planning purposes.']
  last_pm_date date [note: 'Date the most recent preventive maintenance (PM) service was completed on this asset. Used to calculate next scheduled PM date and identify overdue maintenance items.']
  last_regulatory_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent mandatory regulatory inspection or certification for this asset. Null if regulatory_inspection_required is false. Used for compliance reporting and COO maintenance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset record in the source system or data product. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  last_warranty_claim_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty claim submitted for this asset. Null if no warranty claims have been filed. Used to track warranty utilization and vendor responsiveness.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the asset. Used for warranty claims, spare parts procurement, and vendor dispatch coordination.']
  manufacturer_warranty_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty expires. After this date, repair costs are no longer recoverable from the manufacturer. Critical for work order cost routing decisions.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned model or part number for the asset. Required for ordering replacement parts, scheduling OEM-certified service, and cross-referencing BIM component libraries.']
  next_pm_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the next preventive maintenance (PM) service on this asset, calculated from last PM date and PM schedule frequency. Drives work order scheduling and compliance reporting in Building Engines.']
  pm_schedule_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of preventive maintenance (PM) inspections and servicing for this asset as defined in the O&M program. Drives automated PM work order generation in Building Engines CMMS.']
  purchase_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original acquisition cost of the asset in USD at time of purchase or installation. Used as the cost basis for fixed asset depreciation schedules in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets module.']
  recommended_replacement_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the asset is recommended for capital replacement based on condition assessment findings and remaining useful life estimate. Used to populate CAPEX schedules in Argus Enterprise and SAP S/4HANA project systems.']
  refrigerant_type varchar [note: 'Type of refrigerant used in HVAC or cooling equipment (e.g., R-410A, R-22, R-134a, R-32). Null for non-refrigerant assets. Required for EPA Section 608 refrigerant management compliance and ESG greenhouse gas reporting.']
  regulatory_inspection_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the current regulatory inspection certificate or permit expires. Null if not applicable. Expiry triggers mandatory re-inspection scheduling to maintain compliance and avoid operational shutdowns.']
  regulatory_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is subject to mandatory regulatory inspection and certification (e.g., elevators, boilers, fire suppression systems, pressure vessels). True triggers compliance tracking and certificate of occupancy (COO) dependencies.']
  remaining_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of years remaining before the asset requires capital replacement, as determined by the most recent condition assessment. Decimal precision supports mid-year estimates. Key input for CAPEX reserve modeling and Argus Enterprise cash flow projections.']
  replacement_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current estimated cost in USD to replace the asset with a new equivalent unit, inclusive of equipment, labor, and installation. Used for CAPEX budgeting, insurance valuation, and reserve fund analysis. Expressed in base currency (USD).']
  room_or_zone varchar [note: 'Specific room number, mechanical room designation, or building zone where the asset is installed (e.g., \'MER-301\', \'Penthouse Mechanical\', \'Zone A West Wing\'). Supports precise technician navigation and BIM spatial coordination.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the specific asset unit. Required for warranty registration, insurance claims, and regulatory compliance inspections (e.g., elevator certificates, boiler permits).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native asset identifier from the originating source system (Building Engines CMMS). Preserves the upstream system key for reconciliation, lineage tracing, and re-ingestion deduplication in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or engineering-estimated useful life of the asset in years from installation date. Used for depreciation schedules (FASB ASC 360), CAPEX reserve planning, and remaining useful life calculations.']
  warranty_claim_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims filed against any warranty (manufacturer, extended, or labor) for this asset since installation. Indicates reliability history and supports vendor performance evaluation.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all physical building equipment and infrastructure assets subject to maintenance management — HVAC units, elevators, boilers, generators, fire suppression systems, electrical panels, plumbing systems, roofing, and other O&M-tracked components. Captures asset tag, manufacturer, model, serial number, installation date, expected useful life, replacement cost, current condition rating, and BIM reference ID. Includes warranty records (manufacturer, extended, labor warranties with coverage dates and claim history) and periodic condition assessment history (condition ratings, remaining useful life estimates, recommended capital replacement year). SSOT for maintainable building equipment within the maintenance domain.'
}

Table maintenance.vendor_dispatch {
  vendor_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each vendor dispatch record. Primary key for the vendor_dispatch data product in the maintenance domain.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: 3-way matching (PO→dispatch→invoice) is standard real estate property management. When a vendor completes a dispatch, an AP invoice is created and must be matched to the dispatch record for payment ap']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the vendor dispatch is performed. Used for CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation and property-level maintenance cost allocation.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Vendor dispatches carry a gl_account_code for expense classification. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct GL coding for AP invoice processing, CAM recoverability determination, and OpE']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Vendor dispatches for regulated trade work (electrical, plumbing, HVAC) require an active permit. Property managers verify permit validity before dispatching vendors. Compliance officers audit dispatc']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor dispatches generate costs allocated to cost centers for property-level expense tracking and CAM pool management. RE property accountants require cost center on vendor dispatches for AP invoice ']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Vendor dispatch records track actual and estimated costs in multi-currency portfolios. The plain-text currency_code column is denormalized; a FK to reference.currency_code enforces valid currency code']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_schedule. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules frequently require dispatching external vendors or contractors (e.g., quarterly HVAC servicing, annual fire suppression testing). A vendor_dispatch can be directly tri']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.purchase_order. Business justification: Vendor dispatches are executed against purchase orders. 3-way matching requires dispatch-to-PO linkage for committed cost tracking and payment authorization. vendor_dispatch.purchase_order_number is a']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active service contract under which this dispatch is executed. Enables SLA validation and contract compliance tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order that triggered this vendor dispatch. Multiple dispatches may exist per work order (e.g., return visits, multi-trade coordination).']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the vendor technician physically arrived on-site at the property. Compared against estimated arrival window to measure SLA on-time arrival performance.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual invoiced or confirmed cost of the vendor dispatch upon completion. Compared against estimated cost for budget variance reporting and vendor cost performance analysis.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the vendor technician departed the property after completing or suspending work. Used to calculate on-site duration and supports OSHA site safety records.']
  building_access_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor requires escorted or key-controlled building access to perform the work. Drives property operations coordination and tenant notification requirements.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification of the maintenance expenditure as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or Operating Expenditure (OPEX). Drives GL (General Ledger) coding, asset capitalization, and financial reporting under FASB ASC 360.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the vendor confirmed all work was completed and the dispatch was closed. Used for SLA resolution-time measurement and work order closure workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable dispatch reference number used in vendor communications, purchase orders, and invoice matching. Format: DSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  dispatch_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this dispatch within the parent work order (e.g., 1 for initial visit, 2 for return visit). Supports multi-trade coordination tracking and return visit analysis.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor dispatch. Tracks progression from initial dispatch through on-site work completion or cancellation. Used for SLA monitoring and vendor performance reporting.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the vendor or contractor was formally dispatched to the property. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for SLA response-time measurement.']
  estimated_arrival_end timestamp [note: 'End of the estimated arrival window communicated to the property manager and tenant at time of dispatch. Together with estimated_arrival_start defines the committed arrival window for SLA tracking.']
  estimated_arrival_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the estimated arrival window communicated to the property manager and tenant at time of dispatch. Used for SLA compliance measurement and tenant communication.']
  estimated_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of the vendor dispatch at time of scheduling, in the property\'s operating currency. Used for budget variance analysis and OPEX/CAPEX forecasting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL (General Ledger) account code to which the dispatch cost is posted. Drives financial reporting, CAPEX/OPEX segregation, and property-level P&L allocation in Yardi Voyager.']
  is_cam_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of this dispatch is billable to tenants as part of CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation under NNN (Triple Net Lease) or gross lease structures.']
  is_return_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch is a return visit to address incomplete or unsatisfactory work from a prior dispatch on the same work order. Used for vendor quality and rework rate analysis.']
  parts_used_description varchar [note: 'Description of parts, materials, or consumables used during the dispatch. Supports CAPEX vs. OPEX classification, warranty tracking, and invoice reconciliation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification assigned to the dispatch at the time of creation. Determines SLA response and resolution time targets and drives vendor dispatch sequencing.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vendor dispatch is scheduled to occur. Used for day-level planning, preventive maintenance scheduling, and property operations calendaring.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the maintenance service type. Drives CAPEX vs. OPEX accounting treatment and CAM (Common Area Maintenance) billability determination. Preventive and corrective map to OPEX; capital improvement maps to CAPEX.']
  signoff_status varchar [note: 'Status of the work completion sign-off by the property manager or authorized representative. Required for invoice approval and work order closure. Disputed status triggers a return visit or escalation.']
  signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the authorized representative signed off on the completed dispatch work. Triggers invoice approval workflow and work order closure in Building Engines.']
  sla_resolution_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor met the contracted SLA resolution time target (work completed within sla_resolution_target_hours of dispatch). Populated upon dispatch completion. Drives vendor performance scorecarding.']
  sla_resolution_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contracted SLA (Service Level Agreement) target for total resolution time in hours from dispatch to work completion, as defined in the service contract. Supports vendor performance scorecarding.']
  sla_response_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor met the contracted SLA response time target (arrival within sla_response_target_hours of dispatch). Populated upon actual arrival. Drives vendor performance KPI reporting.']
  sla_response_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contracted SLA (Service Level Agreement) target for vendor response time in hours from dispatch to on-site arrival, as defined in the service contract. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (Building Engines CMMS). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  tenant_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant must be notified prior to the vendor accessing the leased premises. Driven by lease terms, building rules, and tenant service request origin.']
  trade_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade or technical discipline of the dispatched vendor (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, electrical, elevator, janitorial, roofing, fire safety). Used for multi-trade coordination and CAPEX vs. OPEX classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|plumbing|electrical|elevator|fire_safety|roofing|janitorial|structural|landscaping|pest_control — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual technician or field representative dispatched on behalf of the vendor. Used for on-site sign-off and OSHA safety compliance records.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or direct phone number of the dispatched technician or vendor representative for on-site coordination and emergency contact.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the vendor or contractor dispatched. Captured at dispatch time for historical accuracy independent of vendor master updates.']
  work_performed_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the work performed by the vendor during this dispatch. Captured by the technician or property manager at completion. Supports maintenance history, asset condition tracking, and audit documentation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a vendor or contractor dispatched to a property to fulfill a work order. Captures the dispatched vendor, dispatch timestamp, estimated arrival window, actual arrival and departure times, work performed summary, sign-off status, and invoice reference. Tracks vendor performance against SLA commitments and supports CAM reconciliation for billable maintenance costs. Multiple dispatches may occur per work order (e.g., return visits, multi-trade coordination).'
}

Table maintenance.service_contract {
  service_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the maintenance service contract record. Primary key for the service_contract data product in the maintenance domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary property covered by this service contract. For multi-property contracts, this represents the lead or billing property; additional covered properties are tracked in a separate association table.']
  brokerage_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.brokerage_deal. Business justification: During property sale or lease deal due diligence, buyers/tenants review and assume existing service contracts (HVAC, elevator, janitorial). Linking service_contract to brokerage_deal supports contract']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Service contracts are approved against annual maintenance budgets. RE asset managers track contracted spend vs. budget for each property. This link supports budget commitment tracking, annual budget p']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Service contracts (elevator maintenance, HVAC service, janitorial) are scoped to specific buildings. Building-level contract management, cost reporting, and vendor performance tracking are standard pr']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Service contracts carry a gl_account_code for expense classification and CAM recoverability determination. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts supports AP invoice coding, CAM reconciliation, and FASB ']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Service contracts for regulated activities (elevator maintenance, fire suppression, boiler service) must reference the authorizing permit. Compliance managers verify that each service contract operate']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Service contracts are structured to satisfy specific compliance requirements (annual elevator inspection requirement, fire suppression test mandate). Compliance officers map service contracts to requi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service contracts represent committed annual costs allocated to cost centers for property-level expense budgeting and CAM pool management. RE controllers require cost center assignment on service cont']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Service contracts in international real estate portfolios are denominated in local currencies. The plain-text currency_code column is denormalized; a FK to reference.currency_code enforces ISO currenc']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Multi-property service contracts (flagged by multi_property_flag) are scoped to geographic markets — e.g., a janitorial contract covering all properties in a metro submarket. Procurement teams negotia']
  owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: Property management agreements (owner_agreement) authorize and govern service contracts — setting approval thresholds, scope, and fee structures. Real estate PMs routinely link service contracts back ']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Service contracts in real estate are scoped by property type — elevator maintenance applies to commercial high-rise, pool service to residential. Contract procurement, vendor performance benchmarking,']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Service contracts for regulated equipment (elevators, boilers, fire suppression) are required by regulatory obligations. Compliance officers verifying that mandatory service agreements are in place li']
  annual_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annualized monetary value of the service contract in the operating currency. Used for OPEX/CAPEX budgeting, vendor spend analytics, and portfolio-level O&M cost reporting. Expressed in the property\'s functional currency.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at expiration unless notice of termination is provided within the required notice period. Critical for contract management and avoiding unintended renewals.']
  building_engines_contract_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this contract record in Building Engines CMMS. Used for data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and bi-directional sync between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification of the contract spend as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or Mixed. Drives GL account coding in Yardi and SAP S/4HANA. Critical for NOI calculation, asset capitalization, and REIT financial reporting under FASB and IFRS.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'The date on which the service contract becomes effective and the vendor is authorized to begin providing services. Aligns with FASB ASC 842 and IFRS 16 contract start date concepts for service agreement accounting.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL or document management system path to the fully executed contract document. Provides direct access to the contract for legal review, audit, and compliance purposes.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the service contract (e.g., \'Annual HVAC Maintenance Agreement — Tower A\', \'Janitorial Services Contract — Portfolio Wide\'). Used for display and search in Building Engines and Yardi.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, exceptions, or amendments not captured in structured fields. Used by property managers and asset managers for contract administration context.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned at execution. Used for vendor correspondence, purchase orders, and audit trails. Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Building Engines contract module.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service contract. Drives operational workflows: active contracts authorize vendor dispatch; expired/terminated contracts block new work orders against this contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service contract by the type of facility service covered. Drives routing to the appropriate operations team and CAPEX/OPEX classification rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|elevator|janitorial|landscaping|pest_control|security|fire_life_safety|plumbing|electrical|roofing|general_maintenance|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service contract record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM for the executed contract document. Enables direct retrieval of the signed contract document and audit trail of digital signatures.']
  esg_sustainability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes ESG or green building sustainability requirements (e.g., use of eco-friendly cleaning products, LEED-compliant HVAC maintenance practices, EPA-compliant pest control methods).']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties signed and the contract was fully executed. May differ from commencement date. Sourced from DocuSign CLM digital signature completion timestamp.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the service contract term ends and the vendor\'s obligation to provide services ceases, absent renewal. Nullable for evergreen/open-ended contracts. Critical for renewal management and WALE (Weighted Average Lease Expiry) analogous tracking.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which contract costs are posted in the financial system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA). Ensures correct cost allocation for NOI reporting, budget variance analysis, and REIT financial statements.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s current certificate of insurance (COI) on file. Triggers compliance alerts when insurance is approaching expiration to prevent lapsed coverage during active contract period.']
  insurance_liability_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required general liability insurance coverage amount (in contract currency) that the vendor must maintain throughout the contract term. Used for risk management and compliance verification.']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is contractually required to provide a certificate of insurance (COI) as a condition of performing services. Supports OSHA compliance and risk management workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this service contract record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer pipeline and SOX audit trail.']
  lien_waiver_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must provide a lien waiver upon payment as a condition of this contract. Relevant for CAPEX-classified contracts and construction-adjacent maintenance work to protect property title.']
  multi_property_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service contract covers multiple properties in the portfolio (e.g., a portfolio-wide janitorial contract). When true, additional covered properties are tracked in a separate association table.']
  osha_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must comply with specific OSHA safety standards as a condition of this contract (e.g., confined space entry, lockout/tagout, fall protection). Drives safety documentation requirements in Building Engines.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the vendor invoices and payment is due under the contract terms. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) accrual schedules in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  performance_bond_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is required to provide a performance bond guaranteeing contract completion. Typically required for high-value CAPEX contracts or critical building systems.']
  preventive_maintenance_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of preventive maintenance visits or inspections required under this contract. Drives PM schedule generation in Building Engines CMMS and supports OSHA and equipment warranty compliance.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each automatic or optional renewal period. Used to calculate the extended contract end date upon renewal and for WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) analogous vendor contract duration analytics.']
  service_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the services covered under this contract, including specific equipment, systems, buildings, or areas included. Defines the scope of work (SOW) for vendor dispatch and work order validation in Building Engines.']
  sla_resolution_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractually committed maximum time in hours from service request submission to full resolution and sign-off. Distinct from response time — measures end-to-end resolution commitment for SLA compliance reporting.']
  sla_response_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractually committed maximum response time in hours from service request submission to vendor on-site arrival for emergency or priority calls. Key SLA metric tracked in Building Engines for vendor performance management.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that run to natural expiration. Distinct from expiration_date — captures unscheduled early termination events.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract or prevent auto-renewal. Drives contract expiry alerts and renewal decision workflows.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for early contract termination. Used for vendor performance analytics, procurement strategy, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the service contract over its full term, including all renewal options if exercised. Used for capital planning, vendor spend management, and CAPEX vs. OPEX threshold analysis.']
  vendor_contract_reference varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s own internal contract or purchase order reference number for this agreement. Enables cross-referencing between the property manager\'s system and the vendor\'s billing system.']

  Note: 'Master record for a maintenance service contract or vendor agreement governing recurring facility services — HVAC maintenance contracts, elevator service agreements, janitorial contracts, landscaping, pest control, and security. Captures contract number, vendor, covered properties, service scope, contract start/end dates, renewal terms, annual contract value, SLA terms, insurance certificate requirements, and CAPEX vs. OPEX classification. Distinct from individual work orders — this is the standing contractual framework.'
}

Business justification: Many formal inspections (elevator certifications, fire suppression checks, HVAC compliance) are performed under the terms of a service contract.   termination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.termination. Business justification: Surrender/move-out inspections are directly triggered by lease terminations. termination records `space_condition_at_surrender` and `restoration_required` fields that depend on inspection findings. Pr']
  ti_allowance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.ti_allowance. Business justification: Post-construction inspections gate TI allowance disbursement milestones. ti_allowance tracks `milestones_completed_count` and `coo_received_date` that depend on inspection sign-off. Property managers ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Unit-level inspections (move-in/move-out, habitability, smoke detector compliance) are standard in residential property management. Housing code regulations require unit-level inspection records.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: An ops_inspection frequently generates or is associated with a work order for remediation of deficiencies found. For example, a fire safety inspection revealing a deficiency triggers a corrective work']
  actual_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred to remediate all deficiencies identified in this inspection, recorded upon completion of corrective work orders. Compared against estimated_remediation_cost for budget variance analysis.']
  building_area varchar [note: 'Specific floor, zone, wing, or common area of the building where the inspection was performed (e.g., \'Floor 3 East Wing\', \'Basement Mechanical Room\', \'Rooftop\'). Supports spatial analysis of deficiency patterns.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the remediation costs associated with deficiencies found in this inspection as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or a mix of both. Critical for financial reporting under FASB ASC 360 and internal budgeting.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the compliance certificate or permit issued as a result of a passed inspection (e.g., elevator operating certificate, fire safety certificate). Null if no certificate is issued.']
  coo_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection is required to maintain or renew the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) for the property. True when the inspection is a COO-mandated compliance event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the source system (Building Engines). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  critical_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings classified as critical severity — those posing immediate life-safety risk or requiring emergency corrective action. Drives escalation workflows and OSHA reporting obligations.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual deficiency findings identified during the inspection across all severity levels. Provides a summary count for compliance dashboards and trend analysis.']
  esg_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection has ESG reporting relevance (e.g., LEED commissioning, BREEAM assessment, EPA environmental compliance inspection). Supports ESG portfolio reporting for REITs and institutional investors.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in the base currency to remediate all deficiencies identified in this inspection. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting and asset lifecycle planning.']
  inspecting_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection was conducted by internal facility staff, an external third-party inspection firm, or a government/regulatory authority (e.g., fire marshal, building department).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the inspection was conducted. This is the principal real-world business event date for the inspection record.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection was concluded on-site. Combined with inspection_start_time to derive inspection duration for O&M analytics.']
  inspection_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party inspection company or regulatory agency that conducted the inspection. Null for internal inspections.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes recorded by the inspector or internal reviewer providing additional context, observations, or recommendations not captured in structured fields. Sourced from Building Engines work order comments.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the inspection event, sourced from Building Engines or the inspecting authority. Used for cross-referencing with regulatory filings, certificates, and work orders.']
  inspection_report_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL path to the formal inspection report issued by the inspecting party. Supports document management integration with DocuSign CLM or Building Engines document store.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the inspection as determined by the inspecting party. \'Conditional pass\' indicates the property passed subject to minor corrective actions within a defined timeframe.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the areas, systems, or equipment covered by the inspection (e.g., \'All fire suppression systems on floors 1-10\', \'Rooftop HVAC units RTU-01 through RTU-06\'). Sourced from Building Engines work order scope.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection commenced on-site. Used for duration tracking and scheduling conflict analysis.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection event workflow within Building Engines. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  inspection_subtype varchar [note: 'Further granular classification within the inspection type (e.g., \'Annual Fire Sprinkler Test\', \'Five-Year Elevator Full Load Test\', \'Pre-Occupancy HVAC Commissioning\'). Supports detailed regulatory citation mapping.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection category. Covers fire safety inspections, elevator certifications, HVAC commissioning, roof inspections, ADA/accessibility compliance walkthroughs, and workplace safety audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fire_safety|elevator_certification|hvac_commissioning|roof|ada_accessibility|workplace_safety|environmental|plumbing|electrical|general_property — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or certification number of the inspector as issued by the relevant regulatory or licensing authority (e.g., state elevator inspector license, fire protection engineer PE number).']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual inspector or lead inspector who conducted the inspection. May be an internal employee or a licensed third-party inspector.']
  leed_breeam_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or credit identifier linking this inspection to a specific LEED or BREEAM certification credit or prerequisite (e.g., \'LEED EA Prerequisite 1 — Fundamental Commissioning\'). Null when not ESG-relevant.']
  major_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings classified as major severity — significant non-conformances requiring corrective action within a defined regulatory or contractual timeframe.']
  minor_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings classified as minor severity — low-risk observations that should be addressed in routine maintenance cycles but do not pose immediate safety or compliance risk.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next recurring inspection of this type must be completed to maintain regulatory compliance and Certificate of Occupancy (COO) obligations.']
  open_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings from this inspection that remain unresolved (not yet remediated or closed). Key metric for tracking open compliance obligations and COO maintenance.']
  osha_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any findings from this inspection trigger mandatory OSHA reporting obligations (e.g., imminent danger, serious violations under 29 CFR 1910). Drives compliance escalation workflows.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal reviewer acknowledged and accepted the inspection report and findings. Marks the start of the internal remediation planning cycle.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the inspection was originally scheduled to occur. Used for maintenance planning and regulatory compliance scheduling.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating Building Engines CMMS system for this inspection event. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was most recently modified in the source system (Building Engines). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Record of a formal property or equipment inspection event — fire safety inspections, elevator certifications, HVAC commissioning, roof inspections, ADA/accessibility compliance walkthroughs, and workplace safety audits (OSHA, HSE, or equivalent). Captures inspection type, inspecting party (internal or third-party), inspection date, pass/fail result, and regulatory citation reference. Includes individual deficiency findings with severity classification (critical, major, minor), affected asset or area, recommended corrective action, responsible party, target and actual remediation dates, and remediation work order references. Supports regulatory compliance, certificate of occupancy maintenance obligations, and tracking of open deficiencies through to resolution.'
}
