  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date on which project construction or installation activities actually commenced. Compared against planned start date for schedule variance analysis and contractor performance evaluation.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Level of organizational authority that approved this capital project. Reflects the delegation of authority matrix for CAPEX approvals. Used for SOX controls documentation and audit trail. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: property_manager|asset_manager|vp_operations|cfo|investment_committee|board — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the capital project received formal investment committee or board approval. Required for SOX financial controls documentation and CAPEX authorization audit trail.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Board or investment committee approved budget for the capital project in the operating currency. Represents the authorized CAPEX spend ceiling. Used for variance analysis against actual spend and DSCR/LTV covenant compliance reporting.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset being created or improved by the capital project. Determines the depreciation method and useful life schedule applied upon capitalization. Aligns with the fixed asset register taxonomy in Yardi and SAP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: building_structure|mechanical_systems|electrical_systems|plumbing_systems|hvac|roof|elevators|parking|it_infrastructure|fit_out — promote to reference product]']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date on which the completed project costs were capitalized and transferred to the fixed asset register in the GL. Marks the start of the depreciation schedule. Critical for FASB ASC 360 compliance and balance sheet accuracy.']
  change_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative value of all approved change orders against the primary construction contract. Added to the original contract value to determine the revised contract sum. Used for budget variance analysis.']
  change_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of approved change orders issued against the primary construction contract. Indicates project scope stability and contractor performance. High change order counts may signal design deficiency or scope creep.']
  committed_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of purchase orders and contracts committed but not yet invoiced or paid for this project. Represents financial obligations already incurred. Used in cash flow forecasting and budget-to-complete analysis.']
  contract_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted value with the primary general contractor for execution of the capital project. May differ from approved budget due to contingency reserves or owner-direct procurement. Used for commitment tracking and lien exposure management.']
  coo_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) was issued by the relevant authority, confirming the completed improvement meets all building code and safety requirements. Required before tenant occupancy and asset capitalization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital project record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls documentation. Sourced from Building Engines or Procore at project initiation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method to be applied to the capitalized asset upon project completion. Determined by asset category and accounting policy. Straight-line is most common for real estate assets under FASB ASC 360.']
  erp_project_code varchar [note: 'Project code assigned in SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS module) for cost collection, procurement integration, and financial consolidation. Used to reconcile CAPEX spend between the maintenance CMMS and the ERP general ledger.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funds financing the capital project. Determines accounting treatment, loan covenant implications, and investor reporting requirements. Insurance proceeds and tenant reimbursements have distinct revenue recognition rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating_reserves|debt_financing|equity_contribution|insurance_proceeds|tenant_reimbursement|government_grant|refinancing_proceeds — promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA to which CAPEX project costs are posted. Ensures correct balance sheet classification (asset vs. expense) and supports financial consolidation and audit.']
  is_esg_initiative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital project is classified as an ESG initiative (e.g., LEED certification upgrade, BREEAM improvement, energy efficiency retrofit, EV charging installation). Supports ESG portfolio reporting and green financing eligibility.']
  is_tenant_improvement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital project constitutes a Tenant Improvement (TI) as defined under ASC 842 or IFRS 16. TI projects have distinct lease accounting treatment, amortization rules, and may be partially reimbursed by tenants.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the capital project record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  leed_certification_target varchar [note: 'Target LEED certification level sought for this capital project, if applicable. Relevant for green building upgrades and ESG-linked financing. Null or \'none\' if LEED certification is not a project objective.']
  osha_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital project has mandatory OSHA safety compliance requirements (e.g., asbestos abatement, fall protection systems, electrical safety upgrades). Triggers safety inspection and documentation workflows.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled date for substantial completion of the capital project. Used for tenant coordination, COO applications, and fixed asset capitalization planning.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled start date for project construction or installation activities. Used for project scheduling, contractor mobilization planning, and tenant disruption coordination.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification of the capital project, reflecting urgency, safety risk, regulatory compliance obligation, or strategic importance. Critical projects may involve OSHA safety compliance or COO requirements.']
  procore_project_code varchar [note: 'External project identifier from Procore construction management system. Enables cross-system traceability for RFIs, submittals, change orders, and document control records associated with this CAPEX project.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope of work, business justification, and expected outcome of the capital project. Supports investment committee review, LOI documentation, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive short name for the capital project (e.g., \'HVAC Chiller Replacement — Tower A\', \'Roof Membrane Renewal — Building 3\'). Used in reporting dashboards, budget approvals, and fixed asset registers.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable project identifier assigned at project initiation. Used across Procore, SAP S/4HANA Project Systems, and Yardi Fixed Assets for cross-system reference and audit trail. Format: CAPEX-{YYYY}-{NNNNN}.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capital project workflow. Drives approval routing, budget release, and fixed asset capitalization triggers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capital project by nature of work. Determines accounting treatment, depreciation schedule, and regulatory reporting category. Tenant Improvement (TI) projects may have separate amortization rules under ASC 842. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_construction|renovation|equipment_replacement|infrastructure_upgrade|tenant_improvement|environmental_remediation|safety_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recent approved revision to the original project budget, reflecting change orders, scope changes, or cost escalations. Null if no revision has been approved. Supports change order management and budget variance reporting.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years, used to calculate the annual depreciation charge upon capitalization. Set at project approval based on asset category and engineering assessment. Aligns with FASB ASC 360 and ISO 55000 asset lifecycle standards.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure project record for major building improvements, equipment replacements, or infrastructure upgrades classified as CAPEX rather than OPEX. Captures project name, description, property reference, asset reference, approved budget, actual spend to date, project start and end dates, funding source, approval authority, ERP project system reference, and capitalization date. Distinct from routine work orders — CAPEX projects involve asset capitalization and fixed asset creation in the GL.'
}

Table maintenance.billable_charge {
  billable_charge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billable_charge']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity operating charges (pool maintenance surcharge, amenity fee) may be billed to tenants. Amenity-level charge tracking supports amenity revenue/expense reporting and lease compliance.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Maintenance billable charges (tenant repairs, CAM charges) are invoiced through AR. The billable_charge.invoice_number is a denormalized reference to ar_invoice. This FK enables tenant billing reconci']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the maintenance activity occurred and from which the billable charge is generated.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Billable maintenance charges are allocated at the building level for multi-building assets. Building-level CAM reconciliation and operating expense reporting require linking charges to specific buildi']
  cam_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.cam_schedule. Business justification: billable_charge has `is_cam_reconciliation_charge` and `cam_allocation_method` fields. Linking to cam_schedule enables the annual CAM reconciliation process to aggregate all billable charges within a ']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Billable charges carry a gl_account_code for revenue recognition and CAM recovery classification. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct income coding for AR invoicing, CAM reconciliation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Billable charges are allocated to cost centers for CAM pool management and property-level revenue tracking. RE controllers require cost center assignment on billable charges to correctly allocate CAM ']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Billable charges in multi-currency real estate portfolios must reference a validated currency code for AR invoicing, CAM reconciliation, and tenant billing. The plain-text currency_code column is deno']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Maintenance billable charges (CAM, tenant repairs) generate GL journal entries for revenue recognition and accrual accounting. RE controllers require the charge-to-journal-entry link for period-close ']
  occupancy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.occupancy. Business justification: CAM reconciliation, tenant-billable repair invoicing, and pro-rata share calculations require linking charges to the specific occupancy period. Property accountants need occupancy_id to validate that ']
  owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: Owner NOI and management fee reporting: billable charges (CAM recoveries, maintenance costs) must be attributed to the governing owner agreement to compute net operating income, management fees, and o']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial tenant charges are allocated to specific spaces. Space-level charge tracking is the foundation of CAM reconciliation and tenant billing in commercial real estate.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract under which this charge was incurred, if the charge originates from a vendor service agreement rather than a discrete work order.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant responsible for or associated with this billable charge. Used for CAM charge-back processing and tenant billing under NNN or modified gross lease structures.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Tenant-billable maintenance charges are allocated to specific units. Unit-level charge history supports lease administration, tenant billing disputes, and move-out cost reconciliation.']
  vendor_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.vendor_dispatch. Business justification: Billable charges to tenants are frequently generated from vendor dispatch events — when a vendor performs work and submits an invoice, the cost may be recoverable from the tenant under CAM provisions ']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating work order in Building Engines that generated this billable charge. Links the maintenance activity to the recoverable cost.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the billable charge was approved by the property manager or asset manager for tenant invoicing. Part of the internal controls audit trail.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period to which this charge is allocated. Together with billing_period_start_date defines the CAM reconciliation window for this charge.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period to which this charge is allocated. Used in CAM reconciliation to match charges to the correct annual or monthly reconciliation cycle.']
  cam_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the CAM pool charge to this tenant. Pro-rata NLA/GLA allocates based on the tenant\'s proportionate share of leasable area; fixed percentage uses a lease-defined rate; direct assignment charges the full amount to one tenant.']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity type that generated this billable charge. Used for CAM pool segmentation, tenant billing transparency, and OPEX vs. CAPEX reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|janitorial|repairs|landscaping|security|electrical|plumbing|pest_control|elevator|general_maintenance — promote to reference product]']
  charge_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the billable maintenance charge was incurred or the service was rendered. Used as the basis for billing period assignment and CAM pool allocation.']
  charge_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable charge reference number used in tenant invoices, CAM reconciliation statements, and property management accounting systems such as Yardi Voyager AR. Format: BC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billable charge from creation through collection or write-off. Drives workflow routing in Yardi Voyager AR and CAM reconciliation processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|invoiced|collected|disputed|written_off|voided — promote to reference product]']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Distinguishes the nature of the billable charge: CAM recoverable (pooled allocation), direct tenant charge (tenant-caused damage or exclusive service), capital improvement (CAPEX), emergency response, or warranty claim. Drives GL coding and lease compliance.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance charge is classified as an Operating Expenditure (OPEX) or Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) per accounting policy. Critical for financial reporting, asset lifecycle tracking, and REIT compliance under FASB and IFRS standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billable charge record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date on which the tenant formally raised a dispute against this charge. Used to track dispute resolution timelines and SLA compliance for tenant relations.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the tenant\'s stated reason for disputing this charge. Populated when charge_status is \'disputed\'. Used for dispute resolution tracking and lease compliance review.']
  estimated_cam_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated CAM payments already collected from the tenant during the reconciliation period. Used to calculate the reconciliation true-up amount (over- or under-collection) at year-end.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA to which this billable charge is posted. Determines the financial statement classification (revenue, expense recovery, or liability) for the charge.']
  gross_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross cost of the maintenance activity before any tenant allocation, CAM pool proration, or adjustments. Represents the full vendor invoice or internal cost amount in the property\'s operating currency.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the tenant invoice containing this charge was issued. Used for payment due date calculation and aging analysis in Accounts Receivable.']
  is_cam_reconciliation_charge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is part of an annual CAM reconciliation adjustment (true) or a current-period direct billing (false). CAM reconciliation charges arise when actual expenses differ from estimated CAM payments collected during the year.']
  lease_structure_type varchar [note: 'The lease structure under which this charge is recoverable from the tenant. NNN (Triple Net) leases pass through all operating expenses; modified gross leases recover specific categories; gross leases are typically non-recoverable. Determines billing eligibility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for property managers to record additional context, special billing instructions, or explanatory notes related to this charge. Visible in tenant-facing CAM reconciliation statements.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the tenant is required to remit payment for this charge per the lease agreement terms. Used for AR aging, late fee assessment, and cash flow forecasting.']
  reconciliation_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal year for which the CAM reconciliation is being performed. Applicable only when is_cam_reconciliation_charge is true. Used to match reconciliation charges to the correct operating year.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which a disputed charge was resolved, either through adjustment, credit, or confirmation of the original amount. Marks the closure of the dispute lifecycle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this billable charge record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Building Engines CMMS and Yardi Voyager AR.']
  source_system_charge_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this charge record in the originating source system (e.g., Building Engines charge ID or Yardi Voyager transaction ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and deduplication in the Silver Layer.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Applicable sales or use tax assessed on the billable maintenance charge, where required by jurisdiction. Included in the tenant invoice and tracked separately for tax remittance compliance.']
  tenant_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross charge amount allocated to the specific tenant after applying the CAM pool allocation method (pro-rata share, fixed percentage, or direct assignment). This is the net amount billed to the tenant.']
  tenant_pro_rata_share decimal(8,6) [note: 'The tenant\'s proportionate share of the CAM pool expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.125000 = 12.5%). Derived from the ratio of the tenant\'s Net Leasable Area (NLA) to the total building NLA or applicable pool area as defined in the lease.']
  total_invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total amount invoiced to the tenant, equal to the tenant_allocated_amount plus tax_amount. Represents the final receivable amount recorded in Yardi Voyager AR for this charge.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this billable charge record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail compliance.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s own invoice reference number for the maintenance service. Used to reconcile the billable charge against the corresponding Accounts Payable entry in Yardi Voyager AP.']

  Note: 'Billable maintenance charge record generated when maintenance costs are recoverable from tenants under NNN or modified gross lease structures as part of CAM reconciliation. Captures the originating work order or service contract, tenant reference, charge amount, charge category (HVAC, janitorial, repairs, landscaping), billing period, CAM pool allocation method, and invoice reference in the property management accounting system. Bridges the maintenance domain to the finance and lease domains for CAM charge-back processing.'
}

Table maintenance.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each safety incident or near-miss event recorded at a managed property. Primary key for the safety_incident data product.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity-related incidents (pool drowning, gym injury, playground accident) are a distinct liability category. Amenity-level incident tracking supports insurance, regulatory reporting, and amenity risk']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the managed property where the safety incident occurred. Links to the property master record in the property domain, sourced from Yardi Voyager or Building Engines property registry.']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.building_asset. Business justification: Safety incidents in facility operations are frequently caused by or involve a specific building asset (e.g., HVAC failure, elevator malfunction, electrical equipment fault). The existing asset_id on s']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Safety incidents occur in specific buildings. Building-level incident rate reporting is required for OSHA compliance, insurance underwriting, ESG disclosure, and building safety certification.']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Safety incidents implicate specific compliance requirements (OSHA recordkeeping requirements, RIDDOR reporting mandates). Compliance officers link incidents to the requirements they trigger to manage ']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Safety incidents result in compliance violation citations (OSHA citations, local code violations). Compliance officers tracking penalty exposure and abatement deadlines need to link the incident to th']
  floor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.floor. Business justification: OSHA recordable incidents must be tracked to specific locations including floor. Floor-level incident reporting is required for safety audits, OSHA 300 log compliance, and insurance claims. Existing F']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Safety incidents in commercial spaces (electrical fire in a suite, chemical spill in a lab space) require space-level tracking for insurance claims, OSHA reporting, and tenant liability determination.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Safety incidents trigger specific regulatory obligations (OSHA 300 log entry, mandatory agency notification). Compliance managers link each incident to the governing regulatory obligation to track man']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant. Business justification: Safety incidents involving tenants (slip-and-fall, fire, hazmat) must be tracked against the tenant record for insurance claims, liability management, and OSHA/RIDDOR regulatory reporting. safety_inci']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Safety incidents in residential units (gas leak, electrical fire, slip-and-fall) require unit-level tracking for habitability compliance, insurance claims, and regulatory reporting.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order or tenant service request that was in progress at the time of the safety incident, if applicable. Links to the work order record in Building Engines CMMS.']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'The specific body part injured or affected in the safety incident, as required for OSHA 300 log recordkeeping. Examples include head, back, hand, foot, eye. Nullable for non-injury incident types such as near-miss or property damage.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the remediation or corrective action costs associated with the safety incident. CAPEX applies when the corrective action results in a capital improvement (e.g., equipment replacement, structural modification); OPEX applies for routine repairs and maintenance. Supports financial reporting and asset management under FASB ASC 360.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded at the time of incident closure, summarizing the final outcome, verification of corrective actions, and any residual risk considerations. Provides a complete audit trail for the incident lifecycle.']
  contributing_factors varchar [note: 'Narrative description of secondary or contributing factors that contributed to the safety incident beyond the primary root cause. May include environmental conditions, time pressure, staffing levels, or equipment age. Supports comprehensive incident investigation.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which all corrective and preventive actions were completed and verified. Null if corrective actions are still pending. Used to measure remediation timeliness and close the incident lifecycle.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date by which all corrective and preventive actions identified in the incident investigation must be implemented. Used for tracking remediation progress and accountability.']
  corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective and preventive actions taken or planned in response to the safety incident to prevent recurrence. May include equipment repair, procedural updates, additional training, or physical modifications to the facility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when the safety incident record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured party was away from work as a result of the safety incident. Required for OSHA 300 log (Column K) and OSHA 300A annual summary. Zero for incidents not resulting in lost time.']
  days_job_transfer_restriction varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured party was on job transfer or work restriction as a result of the safety incident. Required for OSHA 300 log (Column L). Distinct from days_away_from_work.']
  equipment_involved varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the building equipment, machinery, or system involved in the safety incident, such as HVAC unit, elevator, electrical panel, or scaffolding. Supports equipment lifecycle analysis and O&M safety reviews.']
  esg_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident is material for ESG reporting purposes, including sustainability disclosures, GRESB assessments, or SEC climate and social risk disclosures for publicly traded REITs. Supports ESG portfolio-level safety metrics.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of corrective actions and remediation activities required to address the safety incident, expressed in the local currency. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting and insurance claim estimation. Sourced from Building Engines work order cost estimates.']
  incident_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular categorization of the incident type, such as slip/trip/fall, struck-by object, electrical hazard, chemical exposure, equipment failure, or structural failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: slip_trip_fall|struck_by|electrical|chemical_exposure|equipment_failure|structural|ergonomic|fire_explosion|environmental_release|other — promote to reference product]']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the safety incident or near-miss event, including what happened, the sequence of events, conditions at the time, and any immediate actions taken. Free-text field captured by the reporting party.']
  incident_location_detail varchar [note: 'Specific location within the property where the incident occurred, such as floor number, suite, common area, rooftop, parking garage, mechanical room, or stairwell. Complements the property_id with sub-property granularity.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the safety incident at the time of reporting. Used for cross-referencing with regulatory filings, insurance claims, and Building Engines work order records. Format: INC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the safety incident record, tracking its lifecycle from initial report through investigation, corrective action, and closure. Valid values: open, under_investigation, corrective_action_pending, closed, voided.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the safety incident or near-miss event occurred at the property. This is the principal real-world event timestamp, distinct from the record creation timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the safety incident by nature of the event. Drives regulatory recordability determination and reporting pathway. Valid values: injury, near_miss, property_damage, environmental, fire, security.']
  injured_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the person involved in the safety incident by their relationship to the property or organization. Determines applicable regulatory reporting obligations and insurance coverage. Valid values: employee, contractor, tenant, visitor, vendor.']
  injury_nature varchar [note: 'Description of the nature of the injury or occupational illness sustained, such as laceration, fracture, sprain, burn, or respiratory condition. Required for OSHA 300 log and workers compensation filings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: laceration|fracture|sprain_strain|burn|contusion|respiratory|eye_injury|amputation|fatality|other — promote to reference product]']
  investigating_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the property manager, safety officer, or facility operations lead responsible for conducting the incident investigation. Supports accountability and audit trail for the investigation process.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal incident investigation was completed and findings were documented. Marks the transition from investigation to corrective action phase in the incident lifecycle.']
  is_environmental_release boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident involved a release of hazardous materials, chemicals, or pollutants into the environment. Triggers EPA reporting obligations and environmental compliance review when True.']
  is_osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR Part 1904 and must be entered on the OSHA 300 log. True if the incident resulted in medical treatment beyond first aid, days away from work, restricted work, job transfer, loss of consciousness, or diagnosis of a significant injury by a healthcare professional.']
  is_riddor_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident is reportable under the UK Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 2013 (RIDDOR). Applicable for properties under UK jurisdiction. Null for non-UK properties.']
  osha_300_log_entry_date date [note: 'The date on which the safety incident was entered onto the OSHA 300 log. OSHA requires recordable incidents to be logged within seven calendar days of receiving information that a recordable case occurred. Nullable for non-OSHA-recordable incidents.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The country jurisdiction governing the regulatory reporting obligations for this safety incident. Determines which reporting framework applies (OSHA 300 for USA, RIDDOR for GBR, etc.). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|CAN|AUS|DEU|FRA|SGP|ARE|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who initially reported the safety incident to the facility management team. May be the injured party, a witness, or a property manager. Supports incident reporting accountability.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety incident was formally reported to the facility management team or entered into the Building Engines CMMS system. May differ from incident_timestamp due to delayed reporting.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'The identified root cause of the safety incident as determined through investigation. Describes the underlying systemic or behavioral factor that allowed the incident to occur, such as inadequate PPE, equipment malfunction, procedural non-compliance, or environmental hazard.']
  treatment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the medical treatment provided following the safety incident. Determines OSHA recordability: first aid cases are generally not OSHA recordable, while medical treatment beyond first aid triggers recordability. Valid values: first_aid, medical_treatment, hospitalization, fatality, no_treatment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when the safety incident record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to investigation findings, corrective actions, or regulatory filing status.']
  witness_names varchar [note: 'Names of individuals who witnessed the safety incident. Recorded as a comma-separated list or narrative. Used during incident investigation and may be required for regulatory filings or legal proceedings. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of witness information.']

  Note: 'Safety incident or near-miss event occurring at a managed property during maintenance or facility operations activities. Captures incident date and time, property location, incident type (injury, near-miss, property damage, environmental), description, injured party details, regulatory recordability flag (OSHA 300 log in US, RIDDOR in UK, or equivalent jurisdiction-specific reporting), first aid vs. medical treatment classification, root cause, corrective actions taken, and regulatory filing reference. Supports workplace safety compliance and building safety governance across jurisdictions.'
}

Table maintenance.energy_meter {
  energy_meter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the energy meter master record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenities (pools, gyms, common lounges) have dedicated energy meters for amenity cost allocation. Amenity-level energy metering is used in high-end commercial and mixed-use properties for amenity oper']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the managed property at which this meter is installed. Used to associate utility consumption and cost allocation to a specific asset in the portfolio.']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific building asset record (from maintenance.building_asset) representing the physical structure or equipment system to which this meter is attached, enabling asset lifecycle and condition tracking.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Energy meters are installed in specific buildings. Building-level energy consumption reporting for Energy Star benchmarking, LEED certification, and ESG disclosure requires linking meters to buildings']

A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct GL coding for utility AP invoices, CAM utility recovery, and ENERGY STAR/ESG util']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Energy meters are subject to compliance requirements (utility commission submetering regulations, calibration mandates, benchmarking disclosure requirements). Energy managers link meters to requiremen']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Energy meters are assigned to cost centers for utility expense allocation and CAM recovery calculations. RE property accountants require cost center on energy meters for utility billing reconciliation']
  demised_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.lease_demised_space. Business justification: Sub-metered energy meters are assigned to specific demised spaces for tenant utility billing under lease terms. This link enables utility cost allocation per leased area, supports CAM reconciliation f']
  floor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.floor. Business justification: Energy meters are physically installed on specific floors; floor-level energy consumption reporting is a standard ESG and utility management requirement. The existing floor_number plain-text column is']
  occupancy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant_occupancy. Business justification: Sub-metering assigns dedicated energy meters to tenant-occupied spaces for direct utility billing and ESG reporting. Linking energy_meter to tenant_occupancy enables per-tenant utility charge allocati']
  parent_meter_energy_meter_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent (master) meter in a hierarchical metering configuration. Null for top-level main meters. Enables submeter-to-master reconciliation for tenant utility cost allocation and loss/variance analysis.']
  primary_energy_suite_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific leasable suite or unit served by this meter, enabling direct tenant utility cost allocation and submeter reconciliation against master meter readings.']
  primary_replaced_energy_meter_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on energy_meter (replaced_energy_meter_id)']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial spaces have dedicated sub-meters for tenant utility billing and green lease compliance. Space-level metering data supports CAM reconciliation and LEED/BREEAM energy reporting. Existing suit']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Energy meters are required by mandatory sub-metering laws and energy benchmarking ordinances (NYC Local Law 87, EU Energy Efficiency Directive). Compliance officers link meters to the regulatory oblig']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance service contract covering calibration, repair, or monitoring services for this meter. Links to maintenance.service_contract for vendor SLA tracking and OPEX cost classification.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Energy meters measure consumption in standardized units (kWh, therms, gallons, CCF). Linking to reference.uom enforces validated unit-of-measure codes required for ENERGY STAR benchmarking, ESG disclo']
  building_engines_meter_code varchar [note: 'The native meter identifier in the Building Engines CMMS source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record, and troubleshooting ETL discrepancies.']
  cam_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether utility costs recorded by this meter are included in the Common Area Maintenance (CAM) expense pool for tenant pro-rata billing. Critical for NNN and gross lease CAM reconciliation under FASB ASC 842.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The data communication protocol used by the smart meter to transmit readings to the building management system or energy monitoring platform (e.g., BACnet, Modbus, DLMS/COSEM). Relevant only for smart/AMI meters; null or \'none\' for manually read meters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Modbus|BACnet|DLMS_COSEM|ZigBee|LoRaWAN|pulse|none|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this energy meter master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'The date the meter was taken out of service. Null for active meters. Used to close out utility accounts, finalize billing periods, and update ESG reporting boundaries.']
  dial_digits varchar [note: 'Number of digits on the meter register dial. Used to detect meter rollover (when the reading resets to zero after reaching maximum) and ensure accurate consumption calculation between consecutive reads.']
  energy_star_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier in the EPA ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager platform to which this meter\'s consumption data is reported. Enables automated data push for energy benchmarking, ENERGY STAR certification, and regulatory benchmarking compliance (e.g., NYC Local Law 84).']
  esg_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether consumption data from this meter is included in the portfolio\'s ESG reporting boundary for greenhouse gas emissions, energy intensity, and sustainability benchmarking (e.g., ENERGY STAR, GRESB, LEED).']
  installation_date date [note: 'The date the meter was physically installed at the property. Used to establish the start of the meter\'s data history, warranty tracking, and asset lifecycle management.']
  is_smart_meter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meter is an Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter capable of automated, interval-based data transmission. Drives data ingestion pipeline routing and enables real-time energy monitoring and demand response programs.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date the meter was last calibrated or tested for accuracy. Supports preventive maintenance scheduling, regulatory compliance with utility measurement standards, and data quality assurance for energy benchmarking.']
  last_read_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent meter reading recorded for this meter. Used to identify stale or missing reads, trigger alerts for data collection failures, and support billing cycle management.']
  last_read_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The most recent consumption reading recorded for this meter, expressed in the meter\'s unit of measure. Used as the baseline for the next billing period calculation and for detecting anomalous consumption spikes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this energy meter master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the physical installation location of the meter within the building (e.g., \'Electrical Room B2, Panel MDP-1\', \'Roof Level — HVAC Unit 3\'). Supports field technician dispatch and maintenance work orders.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the meter (e.g., \'Landis+Gyr\', \'Itron\', \'Elster\', \'Badger Meter\'). Used for warranty management, spare parts procurement, and vendor performance tracking.']
  meter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive label for the meter (e.g., \'Main Electric Panel — Tower A\', \'Gas Meter — Basement Boiler Room\'). Used in operational dashboards, work orders, and tenant utility billing reports.']
  meter_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique identifier assigned to the meter by the utility provider or the property management system. Used for utility billing reconciliation, provider correspondence, and field identification.']
  meter_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the meter. Drives inclusion in active utility billing runs, ESG reporting, and preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  meter_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification of the meter within its type, distinguishing main utility meters from submeters used for tenant-level cost allocation, check meters for validation, or smart/interval meters for real-time monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: main|submeter|check_meter|interval|smart|pulse|other — promote to reference product]']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the utility commodity measured by this meter. Drives cost allocation methodology, ESG reporting categories, and benchmarking peer groups. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: electric|gas|water|steam|chilled_water|solar|other — promote to reference product]']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model designation for the meter. Used for procurement of compatible parts, firmware updates, and technical support escalation.']
  multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'The demand multiplier or meter constant applied to raw dial readings to calculate actual consumption. Common for high-voltage or high-volume meters where the dial registers a fraction of actual usage. Critical for accurate billing and ESG reporting.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next meter calibration or accuracy test. Drives preventive maintenance work order generation and ensures continuous compliance with utility measurement standards.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or known issues related to this meter (e.g., \'Meter located behind locked panel — contact building engineer\', \'Pending replacement Q3 2025\'). Used by facility operations and maintenance teams.']
  read_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which meter readings are collected, either automatically via AMI/smart meter or manually by a technician. Drives data ingestion scheduling, billing cycle alignment, and energy benchmarking cadence.']
  read_method varchar [note: 'Method by which meter readings are obtained. Automatic reads come from AMI/smart meter telemetry; manual reads are recorded by field technicians; estimated reads are utility-provider projections; remote reads use utility provider remote access.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the physical meter device. Used for warranty claims, regulatory certification tracking, and field identification during maintenance.']
  utility_rate_schedule varchar [note: 'The tariff or rate schedule code assigned by the utility provider (e.g., \'SC-9\', \'General Service Large\'). Determines the billing rate structure applied to consumption readings and is critical for utility cost forecasting and OPEX budgeting.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer\'s warranty for the meter expires. Used to determine whether repair or replacement costs are recoverable under warranty and to plan capital replacement budgets.']
  yardi_meter_code varchar [note: 'The meter identifier in Yardi Voyager used for utility billing and accounts payable invoice matching. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the CMMS meter record and the property accounting system.']

  Note: 'Master record for utility meters and energy monitoring devices installed at managed properties — electric, gas, water, and steam meters. Captures meter ID, meter type, utility provider account number, installation location (building, floor, unit), read frequency, and current status. Supports utility cost allocation, energy benchmarking, and sustainability reporting.'
}

Table maintenance.contract_asset_coverage {
  contract_asset_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the contract_asset_coverage association']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific building equipment asset covered under the associated service contract.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the maintenance service contract that provides coverage for the associated building asset.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'The date on which coverage for this specific building asset ends under this contract. May differ from the contract expiration date when assets are removed from scope before contract termination.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Describes the specific services, maintenance tasks, or obligations that apply to this particular asset under this contract. May differ per asset even within the same contract (e.g., full-coverage for primary units, parts-only for secondary units).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'The date on which coverage for this specific building asset becomes effective under this contract. May differ from the contract commencement date when assets are added to scope mid-term.']
  covered_equipment_description varchar [note: 'Description of specific building equipment, systems, or assets covered under this contract (e.g., \'All rooftop HVAC units — Building A and B\', \'Passenger elevators 1-4\'). Supports asset lifecycle tracking and BIM integration. [Moved from service_contract: This free-text description of covered equipment on service_contract is a denormalized summary of what should be formally represented as individual contract_asset_coverage records. Once the association table exists, the specific equipment scope is captured per-asset in coverage_scope, making this aggregate description on the contract redundant. It may be retained on service_contract as a human-readable summary but the authoritative scope is in the association.]']
  is_primary_contract boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service contract is the primary governing maintenance agreement for this building asset when multiple contracts provide overlapping coverage. Used to resolve which contract governs SLA response and compliance reporting for the asset.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between service_contract and building_asset. It captures the formal coverage relationship defining which specific building equipment assets are in scope for a given maintenance service contract. Each record links one service_contract to one building_asset and carries attributes — coverage scope, effective dates, and primary-contract designation — that exist only in the context of this specific contract-asset pairing. Operationally managed by facility managers when contracts are executed, amended, or when assets are added/removed from scope.. Existence Justification: In real estate facility management, a single service contract (e.g., an HVAC maintenance agreement) routinely covers a portfolio of specific building assets — multiple rooftop units, air handling units, and chillers — while a single building asset (e.g., a chiller) may be covered by multiple contracts over time or simultaneously by overlapping agreements (e.g., a manufacturer warranty-backed service contract AND a full-coverage HVAC contract). The business actively manages which assets are in scope for each contract, adds or removes assets when scope changes, and tracks coverage dates and primary-contract designation per asset. This is not derivable from existing FKs — the `asset_id` on `service_contract` is described as a reference to the \'primary property\', not to individual equipment assets.'
}

Table brokerage.listing {
  listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the property listing record in the brokerage data platform. Primary key for the listing entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying property asset being listed. Links the listing to the master property record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed agent or broker responsible for originating and managing this listing.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Listings are associated with marketing and leasing budgets. Asset managers track marketing spend per listing against approved budgets — a standard real estate asset management reporting process requir']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Building class (A/B/C) on a listing drives rent benchmarking, cap rate expectations, REIT eligibility assessment, and investor targeting. Brokers use standardized building class to position listings i']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Commercial listings reference a specific building within a multi-building asset for building class, floor count, LEED certification, and parking ratio. Brokers and tenants search by building. asset_id']

  entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.entitlement.   floor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.floor. Business justification: Full-floor commercial listings (e.g., \'entire floor 15 available\') are marketed before individual spaces are defined. Brokers need floor_id on listing to represent floor-plate availability in CoStar/M']

  close_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final agreed transaction price at which the property was sold or the lease was executed. Used for comparable sales analysis, commission calculation, and investment performance reporting.']
  co_broker_commission_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The portion of the total commission allocated to the cooperating or buy-side broker in a co-brokerage arrangement. Null if no co-broker is involved.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The agreed brokerage commission rate expressed as a percentage of the transaction price or lease value. Drives commission payable calculations and co-broker split agreements.']
  costar_code varchar [note: 'CoStar Suite-assigned identifier for this listing, used for commercial property syndication, market research, and comparable data alignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the listing record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  days_on_market varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the listing has been active on the market, calculated from listing_start_date to the current date or close date. Key performance metric for brokerage production reporting.']
  deal_stage varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM deal pipeline stage tracking the progression of the listing from initial prospect through close. Aligns with brokerage workflow from Letter of Intent (LOI) submission through contract execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|loi_submitted|under_contract|due_diligence|closed_won|closed_lost — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The contractual expiration date of the listing agreement. After this date, the brokerage\'s exclusive marketing rights lapse unless renewed.']
  gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the property or space being listed, measured in square feet. Used for PSF rate calculations, market comparables, and FAR compliance.']
  is_costar_syndicated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing has been published and syndicated to the CoStar Suite commercial property database for market exposure.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brokerage holds an exclusive listing agreement, granting sole marketing rights for the property during the listing term.']
  is_mls_syndicated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing has been syndicated to the Multiple Listing Service (MLS) network. Drives listing visibility and co-broker cooperation eligibility.']
  lease_rate_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Annual or monthly asking lease rate expressed on a Per Square Foot (PSF) basis for lease listings. Null for for-sale listings. Used for market comparables and deal underwriting.']
  listing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the listing. Active indicates available for marketing; pending indicates an accepted offer under contract; expired/withdrawn indicate the listing has ended without a transaction; sold/leased indicate a completed deal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|expired|withdrawn|sold|leased|on_hold|draft — promote to reference product]']
  listing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the listing agreement type governing the brokerage relationship. Exclusive grants sole marketing rights; open allows multiple brokers; co-exclusive is shared between two brokers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exclusive|open|co_exclusive|exclusive_agency|net — promote to reference product]']
  mls_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known Multiple Listing Service (MLS) identifier assigned to this listing upon syndication to the MLS network. Used for cross-brokerage identification and public-facing search.']
  nla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net Leasable Area (NLA) of the property, excluding common areas and structural elements. Used for tenant space calculations and lease rate benchmarking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for broker remarks, marketing highlights, or special conditions associated with the listing (e.g., owner financing available, sale-leaseback opportunity, as-is condition).']
  number_of_floors varchar [note: 'Total number of above-grade floors in the building. Used for property classification, elevator requirement analysis, and comparable selection.']
  parking_ratio decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of parking spaces per 1,000 square feet of rentable area. A key amenity metric for commercial lease listings and tenant prospect qualification.']
  price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The asking or offering price for a for-sale listing, expressed in the local currency. For lease listings, this field is null and lease_rate_psf is used instead.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding Salesforce CRM opportunity or deal record ID that tracks the brokerage pipeline activity associated with this listing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system of record from which this listing record was sourced (e.g., Salesforce CRM, CoStar Suite). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this listing in the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce Opportunity ID, CoStar property listing ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the listing became active and was authorized for marketing. Used to calculate Days on Market (DOM) and listing agreement compliance.']
  tenant_improvement_allowance decimal(14,2) [note: 'The landlord-offered Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance in absolute dollar terms for lease listings. Represents the capital contribution toward tenant fit-out and is a key deal negotiation lever.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the listing record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  year_built varchar [note: 'The year in which the property was originally constructed. Used for property age analysis, capital expenditure planning, and market comparable filtering.']

  Note: 'Master record for all property listings managed by the brokerage — commercial and residential, for sale or lease. Captures listing type (exclusive, open, co-exclusive), listing status (active, pending, expired, withdrawn, sold/leased), MLS/CoStar syndication flags, listing price/rate PSF, GLA/NLA, property class, submarket, listing start and expiration dates, days on market, and the originating source system (Salesforce CRM, CoStar). Serves as the SSOT for all brokerage-managed property offerings.'
}

Table brokerage.listing_agreement {
  listing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the listing agreement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property being listed under this brokerage agreement. Links to the property master record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary listing agent or broker responsible for marketing and transacting the property under this agreement.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Listing agreements include an approved marketing budget that must be tracked against the formal finance budget for variance reporting and spend authorization. Real estate firms require budget-to-actua']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Commercial listing agreement commission structures, marketing budgets, and broker assignment decisions are differentiated by building class (A/B/C). Brokerage firms track listing agreements by buildin']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Commercial listing agreements are frequently executed at the building level (exclusive listing of an entire office or retail building). Brokers and asset managers track which building is under exclusi']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Listing agreements for commercial properties reference compliance assessments (environmental, structural, ADA) conducted prior to listing. Assessment findings affect listing terms, price adjustments, ']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Listing agreements for properties under development or renovation must reference active permits governing the property. Permit status is a material term affecting the agreement\'s scope, marketing rest']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Commission amounts and financial terms in listing agreements require standardized currency classification for multi-market financial reporting and cross-border transaction compliance. currency_code is']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Listing agreements are governed by specific state/local jurisdictions determining mandatory disclosure requirements, commission rules, dual agency laws, and contract enforceability. governing_law_stat']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Listing agreements are executed by the property-owning legal entity (LLC/SPE). The contracting legal entity must be referenced for contract validity, commission liability attribution, and financial co']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Brokerage firms segment listing agreements by investment market segment (core, value-add, opportunistic) for deal pipeline reporting and investor matching. Investment sales teams require this classifi']
  market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: Brokers present a Comparative Market Analysis (market survey) to justify listing price and minimum_acceptable_price at agreement signing. Tracing which market survey supported the pricing decision is ']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property owner or seller who is the principal party authorizing the brokerage engagement.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Listing agreements for land/parcel sales must reference the specific parcel being listed. Legal description, APN, and title encumbrances are parcel-level attributes required for agreement execution an']

  property_ownership_interest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property_ownership_interest. Business justification: Listing agreements must identify which ownership interest authorizes the listing (managing member, controlling interest). This is a legal/compliance requirement — only the authorized ownership interes']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial sublease listing agreements are executed for specific spaces. The agreement grants marketing rights for that suite. Space-level listing agreements are common in commercial sublease transact']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Property type on a listing agreement determines applicable commission benchmarks, required disclosures, and agreement terms. Brokers structure agreements differently for industrial vs. retail vs. offi']
  reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Listing agreements for lease transactions must specify lease type (NNN, gross, modified gross) as it governs commission structure, CAM obligations, and agreement terms. A real estate attorney or broke']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Listing agreements must satisfy specific regulatory obligations (mandatory agency disclosure, lead paint disclosure, environmental disclosure, fair housing posting requirements) that vary by jurisdict']
  submarket_geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Exclusive territory enforcement and market reporting on listing agreements requires a structured geographic hierarchy reference. The existing exclusive_territory plain-text field is a denormalized rep']
  title_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.title_record. Business justification: Listing agreements require title encumbrance disclosure — brokers must reference the title record to confirm liens, easements, and encumbrances before listing. Title company and policy details in titl']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Listing agreement terms, commission structure, and required regulatory disclosures are governed by transaction type. A sale agreement has different legal requirements than a lease agreement. transacti']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential listing agreements (condos, co-ops, multifamily units) are executed for specific units. The agreement governs marketing rights for that unit. asset_id alone is insufficient; unit-level lis']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the listing agreement, used in DocuSign CLM and Salesforce CRM for cross-system reference and document tracking.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the listing agreement. Draft indicates the agreement is being prepared. Active indicates it is fully executed and in force. Expired indicates the term has lapsed without a transaction. Terminated indicates early cancellation by either party. Withdrawn indicates the property was removed from market. Closed indicates a successful transaction was completed.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the listing agreement defining the scope of brokerage authority and commission entitlement. Exclusive Right to Sell grants the broker commission regardless of who procures the buyer. Exclusive Agency allows the owner to sell without commission if they find the buyer themselves. Open Listing allows multiple brokers with commission only to the procuring broker. Net Listing pays the broker any amount above the owner\'s net price. Exclusive Right to Lease applies to leasing engagements.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of formal amendments executed against this listing agreement since its original execution, tracking changes to price, term, commission, or other material terms.']
  broker_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued real estate broker license number of the brokerage firm executing this listing agreement, required for regulatory compliance and agreement validity under state licensing laws.']
  broker_license_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code in which the broker\'s license is issued and under whose laws this listing agreement is governed.']
  brokerage_side varchar [note: 'Identifies the brokerage\'s role in the transaction: sell-side (representing the seller/landlord), buy-side (representing the buyer/tenant), lease listing (marketing a property for lease), tenant representation, or dual agency (representing both parties with disclosed consent).']
  co_broker_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing agreement authorizes the listing broker to cooperate with and compensate a co-broker (buyer\'s agent or tenant\'s representative) from the total commission. True enables MLS co-broker cooperation.']
  co_broker_split_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The portion of the total commission allocated to the cooperating co-broker (buyer\'s agent), expressed as a decimal percentage of the total commission or of the transaction price, as specified in the agreement. Null when co-broker authorization is false.']
  commission_flat_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed fixed brokerage commission amount in the agreement currency, applicable when the commission structure is a flat fee rather than a percentage. Null when a percentage-based commission rate is used.']
  commission_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The agreed brokerage commission expressed as a decimal percentage of the transaction price (e.g., 0.0600 = 6.00%). Applicable when the commission structure is percentage-based. Null when a flat fee structure is used.']
  commission_structure varchar [note: 'Defines the method by which the brokerage commission is calculated: percentage of transaction price, flat fee, tiered based on price thresholds, or net above a reserve price (net listing).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this listing agreement record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse, sourced from DocuSign CLM or Salesforce CRM ingestion. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'The DocuSign CLM envelope identifier for the executed listing agreement document, enabling retrieval of the signed contract and audit trail of digital signatures.']
  dual_agency_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner has provided informed written consent for the broker to act as a dual agent, representing both the seller/landlord and the buyer/tenant in the same transaction, as required by state licensing laws.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the listing agreement becomes legally binding and the brokerage is authorized to begin marketing the property. Corresponds to the commencement date in DocuSign CLM.']
  esg_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing agreement requires the broker to include Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) disclosures or sustainability certifications (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR) in the property marketing materials.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties signed and fully executed the listing agreement via DocuSign CLM or physical signature. May differ from the effective date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the listing agreement term ends and the brokerage authorization lapses if no transaction has been completed. Nullable for open-ended arrangements.']
  last_amended_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent formal amendment to the listing agreement. Null if no amendments have been executed.']
  lockbox_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner has authorized the broker to install a lockbox on the property to facilitate agent showings without requiring the owner to be present.']
  marketing_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed marketing expenditure budget allocated for promoting the listed property, covering advertising, photography, virtual tours, signage, and digital syndication costs as specified in the listing agreement.']
  minimum_acceptable_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lowest transaction price the owner has authorized the broker to accept or present for consideration, as agreed in the listing agreement. Used to guide offer evaluation and negotiation boundaries.']
  owner_disclosure_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner has completed and delivered all required property disclosure statements (e.g., seller\'s disclosure, lead paint, environmental hazards) as mandated by applicable state and federal regulations.']
  price_reduction_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing agreement grants the broker authority to reduce the listing price within defined parameters without requiring a formal amendment, enabling faster market responsiveness.']
  protection_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after the listing agreement expires during which the broker retains the right to earn a commission if the property is sold or leased to a prospect introduced by the broker during the listing term. Also known as the safety clause or tail period.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM Opportunity record ID associated with this listing agreement, enabling traceability from the deal pipeline stage through to agreement execution and transaction close.']
  showing_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions governing how and when the property may be shown to prospective buyers or tenants, including notice requirements, access restrictions, and contact protocols as specified by the owner.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this listing agreement record was ingested: DocuSign CLM (contract execution), Salesforce CRM (deal pipeline), or manual entry for legacy records.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the listing agreement was terminated prior to its scheduled expiration, whether by mutual consent, breach, or unilateral withdrawal. Null if the agreement ran to its natural expiration or closed successfully.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the listing agreement was terminated before its scheduled expiration. Used for brokerage performance analysis and pipeline reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mutual_consent|owner_withdrawal|broker_default|property_sold_off_market|expired_no_renewal|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this listing agreement record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, capturing any updates from source systems or manual corrections. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Formal contractual agreement between the brokerage and a property owner/seller authorizing the brokerage to market and transact on their behalf. Captures agreement type (exclusive right to sell, exclusive agency, open listing, net listing), term start and end dates, agreed commission rate or flat fee, co-broker split authorization, listing price, and execution status. Sourced from DocuSign CLM and Salesforce CRM. Distinct from a lease agreement (owned by the lease domain) — this governs the brokerage engagement itself.'
}
