  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system of record for this ledger (e.g., YARDI, SAP_S4HANA).']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance controls and audit requirements.']
  suspense_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code used for suspense or unallocated transactions in this ledger.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this ledger record, incremented with each modification for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ledger. Referenced by ledger_id.'
}

Table finance.reporting_period {
  reporting_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reporting_period']
  parent_period_reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent reporting period for hierarchical period structures (e.g., monthly period rolling up to quarterly period). Null for top-level periods.']
  primary_prior_reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reporting_period (prior_reporting_period_id)']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget associated with this reporting period (e.g., \'Original\', \'Revised Q2\', \'Final\'). Used to track budget revisions and forecasts.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month within the calendar year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter within the calendar year (1, 2, 3, or 4). Null for non-quarterly periods.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year to which this reporting period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when the reporting period was officially closed for new transactions. Null if period is still open. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reporting period record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last date of the reporting period (inclusive). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month within the fiscal year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (1, 2, 3, or 4). Null for non-quarterly periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this reporting period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is designated for year-end or period-end adjustments (e.g., 13th period in some accounting systems).']
  is_current_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the current active reporting period for financial transactions and reporting.']
  is_leap_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reporting period falls within a leap year (366 days). Relevant for daily and annual period calculations.']
  locked_date date [note: 'The date when the reporting period was locked and finalized. Null if period is not locked. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reporting period record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  number_of_business_days varchar [note: 'Total number of business days (excluding weekends and holidays) in the reporting period.']
  number_of_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in the reporting period.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reporting period, used for system integration and reporting (e.g., \'2024Q1\', \'202401\', \'FY2024\').']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the reporting period (e.g., \'Q1 2024\', \'January 2024\', \'FY2024\').']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reporting period granularity (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual).']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the primary reporting currency used in this period (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  reporting_period_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes about the reporting period, including any special circumstances or adjustments.']
  reporting_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reporting period. Open: transactions can be posted; Closed: period is closed for new transactions; Locked: period is finalized and cannot be reopened; Archived: historical period retained for compliance.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number for the reporting period within its fiscal year or parent period hierarchy.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this reporting period was created or synchronized (e.g., \'YARDI\', \'SAP\', \'MANUAL\').']
  start_date date [note: 'The first date of the reporting period (inclusive). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for reporting_period. Referenced by reporting_period_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_obligation {
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory obligation record in the master registry. Primary key for the regulatory_obligation data product.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Regulatory obligations define max_penalty_amount with a plain-text penalty_currency. Normalizing to reference.currency_code enables consistent penalty exposure aggregation across obligations in differ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: regulatory_obligation has jurisdiction_country, jurisdiction_state, and jurisdiction_municipality as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference m']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Obligations are scoped to property types (e.g., residential fire codes vs. commercial ADA requirements). Real estate compliance teams filter applicable obligations by property type during asset onboar']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Regulatory obligations are frequently zoning-specific — conditional use permit obligations, overlay district requirements, and affordable housing inclusionary zoning mandates apply only to properties ']
  applicable_asset_class varchar [note: 'Specific real estate asset class to which this obligation applies (e.g., \'Office\', \'Retail\', \'Multifamily\', \'Industrial\', \'Hotel\', \'Data Center\'). Narrows applicability scope beyond property type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: office|retail|multifamily|industrial|hotel|data_center|healthcare|self_storage|all — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory obligation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of the specific documentation, reports, or filings required to demonstrate compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Phase I ESA Report\', \'Annual 10-K Filing\', \'LEED Scorecard\', \'GDPR Data Processing Agreement\').']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory obligation becomes legally binding and enforceable. Marks the start of the compliance window.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG pillar classification for this obligation. Supports ESG reporting and portfolio sustainability analytics. \'None\' for obligations not related to ESG.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory obligation ceases to be enforceable or is superseded. Null for open-ended obligations with no defined sunset date.']
  external_portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the official regulatory body\'s online portal or filing system where compliance submissions for this obligation are made (e.g., EPA\'s CDX portal, SEC EDGAR, HUD IDIS).']
  filing_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the renewal date by which the compliance filing or documentation must be submitted to the governing body. Used to calculate advance preparation timelines.']
  gdpr_data_category varchar [note: 'Classification of personal data category under GDPR for privacy-related obligations. \'special_category\' refers to sensitive data per GDPR Article 9 (e.g., health, biometric). \'not_applicable\' for non-GDPR obligations.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or standards body that issued and enforces this obligation (e.g., \'Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)\', \'Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)\', \'U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)\').']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is legally mandatory (True) or voluntary/best-practice (False). Mandatory obligations carry legal enforcement risk; voluntary ones (e.g., LEED certification) are pursued for strategic or ESG reasons.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires periodic renewal or re-filing (True) or is a one-time requirement (False). Drives automated renewal scheduling in the compliance calendar.']
  leed_breeam_level varchar [note: 'Target or required certification level for LEED or BREEAM obligations. Applicable only when compliance_framework is \'LEED\' or \'BREEAM\'. \'not_applicable\' for non-certification obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certified|silver|gold|platinum|pass|good|very_good|excellent|outstanding|not_applicable — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty or fine that can be imposed for non-compliance with this obligation, expressed in the obligation currency. Supports risk quantification and materiality assessment.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which the compliance filing, certification, or permit renewal must be completed for this obligation. Drives compliance calendar and deadline management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, interpretive guidance, legal counsel annotations, or operational notes related to this regulatory obligation. Not used for structured data.']
  obligation_category varchar [note: 'Jurisdictional tier of the regulatory obligation, indicating whether it originates from a federal, state, local, or international governing body.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this regulatory obligation (e.g., EPA-PHASE1-001, OSHA-1910-001). Used as the business identifier across systems and filings.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the regulatory obligation, including its purpose, scope, and key compliance requirements as defined by the governing body.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive name of the regulatory obligation (e.g., \'Phase I Environmental Site Assessment\', \'OSHA Hazard Communication Standard\'). Used as the primary identity label in reports and dashboards.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the regulatory obligation by subject matter domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|fair_housing|privacy|financial_reporting|zoning|building_code|esg|certification|other — promote to reference product]']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the penalties, fines, or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Civil penalty up to $25,000 per day per violation under 40 CFR Part 312\').']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Official citation or reference number for the regulation, statute, or standard (e.g., \'40 CFR Part 312\', \'OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200\', \'ASC 842\', \'IFRS 16\', \'GDPR Article 5\'). Enables direct traceability to the source regulatory text.']
  regulation_version varchar [note: 'Version or amendment number of the regulation or standard to which this obligation corresponds. Tracks regulatory changes over time (e.g., \'2023 Amendment\', \'Rev. 4\', \'LEED v4.1\').']
  regulatory_obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory obligation in the master registry. \'Active\' means currently enforceable; \'superseded\' means replaced by a newer regulation; \'under_review\' means applicability is being assessed.']
  renewal_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which this regulatory obligation must be renewed, re-filed, or re-certified (e.g., \'annual\' for SEC REIT filings, \'triennial\' for LEED recertification, \'one_time\' for Phase I ESA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|biennial|triennial|quarterly|monthly|one_time|as_needed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requires_third_party_audit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this obligation must be verified by an independent third-party auditor or certifying body (e.g., LEED certification requires USGBC-approved verifier, Phase I ESA requires licensed environmental professional).']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Internal business department or team accountable for managing and fulfilling this regulatory obligation (e.g., \'Legal & Compliance\', \'Asset Management\', \'Property Management\', \'Finance\', \'ESG & Sustainability\').']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'Job title or role designation of the individual accountable for this obligation (e.g., \'Chief Compliance Officer\', \'Asset Manager (AM)\', \'Property Manager (PM)\', \'Environmental Health & Safety Manager\').']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of non-compliance with this obligation, reflecting the severity of potential penalties, reputational impact, and likelihood of regulatory scrutiny. Used to prioritize compliance activities.']
  sec_form_type varchar [note: 'SEC filing form type associated with this obligation for publicly traded REITs (e.g., \'10-K\', \'10-Q\', \'8-K\', \'S-11\', \'DEF 14A\'). Null for non-SEC obligations.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding record in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi Voyager compliance module record ID, SAP compliance item number). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this regulatory obligation record originates or is primarily managed (e.g., \'Yardi Voyager\' for lease compliance, \'SAP S/4HANA\' for financial regulatory obligations, \'Procore\' for construction permits). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Yardi Voyager|SAP S/4HANA|Procore|Building Engines|DocuSign CLM|Manual|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory obligation record. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all regulatory obligations applicable to the portfolio — federal, state, and local requirements including EPA environmental mandates, OSHA building safety codes, HUD fair housing rules, GDPR tenant data privacy, SEC REIT reporting, and municipal zoning compliance. Each obligation captures the governing body, regulation reference, jurisdiction, applicability scope (property type, asset class), effective dates, and renewal cadence. Serves as the authoritative catalog against which compliance activities are tracked.'
}

Table compliance.requirement {
  requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for requirement']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property or asset to which this compliance requirement applies. Null if the requirement applies portfolio-wide or at the legal entity level rather than a single property.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Compliance requirements (fire safety, ADA, energy benchmarking, elevator inspection) apply to specific buildings. Multi-building portfolios have building-specific obligations based on age, use, and lo']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Fire code, energy code, and structural inspection requirements are construction-type-specific under IBC and local building codes. Compliance teams use construction type to filter applicable requiremen']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Requirements track max_penalty_amount with a plain-text penalty_currency_code. Normalizing to reference.currency_code enables consistent multi-currency penalty risk aggregation across requirements for']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: requirement has jurisdiction_level and jurisdiction_code as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdic']
  lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Compliance requirements differ by lease type — NNN leases place tax/insurance/maintenance obligations on tenants while gross leases place them on landlords. ASC 842/IFRS 16 compliance requirements are']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (e.g., REIT, LLC, LP) responsible for fulfilling this compliance requirement. Critical for SEC, SOX, and multi-jurisdictional regulatory filings.']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Compliance requirements are scoped to property types (e.g., residential habitability standards vs. commercial fire suppression requirements). Real estate compliance teams filter applicable requirement']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory obligation from which this specific requirement is derived (e.g., EPA ASTM Phase I mandate, OSHA 29 CFR 1910, ASC 842 lease disclosure). Links requirement to its governing regulatory framework.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Compliance requirements specify measurable thresholds (e.g., max emissions per sqft, min energy efficiency rating in kBtu/sqft/yr, noise limits in dB) that require a unit of measure to define the thre']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Compliance requirements for conditional use permits, variance conditions, and overlay district standards are zoning-code-specific. Property managers need to identify which requirements apply to a give']
  applies_to_portfolio boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance requirement applies across the entire portfolio rather than to a specific property. True for entity-level requirements such as SEC 10-K REIT disclosures, SOX financial controls, and GDPR privacy program certifications.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'The organizational role required to formally approve or sign off on the completion of this compliance requirement (e.g., VP Compliance, CFO for SEC filings, General Counsel for GDPR certifications). Supports segregation of duties and SOX controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance requirement record was first created in the data platform. Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance requirement becomes binding and enforceable. Marks the start of the requirement\'s active lifecycle. Used to determine when due date calculations begin.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG pillar classification for this compliance requirement. Enables ESG reporting and sustainability performance tracking. Environmental includes LEED, BREEAM, EPA; Social includes HUD fair housing, OSHA safety; Governance includes SEC, SOX, GDPR.']
  evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether documentary evidence (inspection reports, certificates, signed forms, audit logs) must be collected and retained to demonstrate fulfillment of this requirement. Drives document management workflows in DocuSign CLM and Building Engines.']
  evidence_retention_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that evidence and documentation for this compliance requirement must be retained per regulatory mandate (e.g., OSHA records 5 years, GDPR processing records 3 years, SEC filings 7 years, SOX records 7 years).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance requirement ceases to be enforceable or is superseded. Null for open-ended, ongoing requirements. Used to retire requirements when regulations change.']
  filing_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or authority to which the formal filing or submission must be made (e.g., \'SEC\', \'HUD\', \'EPA Region 5\', \'OSHA Area Office\', \'State Fire Marshal\'). Null if no external filing is required.']
  filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement involves a formal submission or filing to a regulatory authority (e.g., SEC 10-K, HUD fair housing certification, EPA environmental report, OSHA 300A summary posting). Drives filing calendar management.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Recurrence frequency at which this compliance requirement must be fulfilled (e.g., annual fire inspection, quarterly OSHA audit, monthly HVAC filter check). Drives automated due date generation and scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: one_time|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric interval value paired with frequency_unit to define custom recurrence periods (e.g., every 2 years, every 6 months). Used when standard frequency values are insufficient for precise scheduling.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of time for the frequency_interval field (e.g., \'year\' with interval 2 = every 2 years). Together with frequency_interval, enables precise custom recurrence scheduling beyond standard frequency buckets.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or standards body that mandates this requirement (e.g., EPA, OSHA, HUD, SEC, GDPR Supervisory Authority, FASB, USGBC, NAR, Appraisal Institute). Used for regulatory correspondence and filing routing.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after the due date within which the requirement may still be fulfilled without triggering a formal non-compliance event or penalty. Reflects regulatory tolerance windows where applicable.']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance requirement was most recently fulfilled or satisfied. Used to calculate the next due date and to assess compliance currency.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the due date that preparation or initiation of this requirement must begin. Used to trigger advance notifications and task assignments to responsible parties (e.g., 30-day lead time for annual fire inspection scheduling).']
  leed_credit_category varchar [note: 'LEED credit category associated with this requirement when applicable (e.g., \'Energy & Atmosphere\', \'Water Efficiency\', \'Indoor Environmental Quality\', \'Sustainable Sites\'). Null for non-LEED requirements. Supports LEED certification tracking.']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty or fine that could be imposed for non-compliance with this requirement, expressed in the applicable currency. Used for risk quantification and prioritization of compliance activities (e.g., GDPR max €20M or 4% global turnover).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The next calendar date by which this compliance requirement must be fulfilled. Calculated from the last completion date and frequency, or from the effective date for new requirements. Drives compliance calendar and alert generation.']
  penalty_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of non-compliance with this requirement, reflecting the severity of potential regulatory penalties, fines, or sanctions. Critical = license revocation or criminal liability; High = significant fines (e.g., GDPR up to 4% global revenue); Medium = moderate fines; Low = administrative notices.']
  regulation_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory citation, code section, or standard reference that mandates this requirement (e.g., \'OSHA 29 CFR 1910.157\', \'GDPR Article 15\', \'ASC 842-20-50\', \'SEC Rule 10-K Item 2\', \'HUD 24 CFR Part 100\'). Enables precise legal traceability.']
  regulatory_domain varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory domain governing this requirement. Drives routing to the appropriate compliance team and applicable regulatory body. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|fair_housing|privacy|financial|esg|building_code|tax|employment|zoning|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this compliance requirement within the compliance management system (e.g., ENV-EPA-001, OSHA-FIRE-Q1, HUD-FH-ANN). Used for cross-system referencing and regulatory filing tracking.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the compliance requirement, including the specific action, control, or submission that must be completed, the regulatory basis, and any relevant procedural notes.']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short name of the compliance requirement (e.g., \'Annual Fire Inspection\', \'Quarterly OSHA Safety Audit\', \'GDPR Data Subject Access Request Process\', \'SEC 10-K REIT Disclosure\'). Used in dashboards, reports, and task assignments.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance requirement. \'Active\' means currently enforceable; \'Inactive\' means no longer applicable; \'Pending\' means awaiting activation; \'Waived\' means formally exempted; \'Superseded\' means replaced by a newer requirement.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the compliance requirement by the nature of the action required. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inspection|audit|filing|certification|disclosure|training|assessment|remediation|permit|notification|testing|monitoring — promote to reference product]']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The organizational department accountable for this compliance requirement (e.g., Property Management, Legal & Compliance, Finance, Facilities Operations, ESG & Sustainability). Used for workload reporting and escalation routing.']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'The organizational role or job function responsible for fulfilling this compliance requirement (e.g., Property Manager (PM), Asset Manager (AM), Compliance Officer, Facilities Director, Legal Counsel). Drives task routing and accountability assignment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this compliance requirement record originates or is managed (e.g., Building Engines for facility inspections, Yardi Voyager for lease compliance, Procore for construction permits, SAP S/4HANA for financial regulatory requirements).']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this compliance requirement in the originating source system (e.g., Building Engines work order template ID, Yardi compliance task ID, Procore inspection checklist ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  third_party_verification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance requirement must be verified or certified by an independent third party (e.g., licensed inspector, certified appraiser, external auditor, LEED commissioning agent). Drives vendor engagement and scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance requirement record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Granular, actionable compliance requirements derived from regulatory obligations — specific tasks, controls, or submissions that must be completed (e.g., annual fire inspection, quarterly OSHA safety audit, annual HUD fair housing certification, GDPR data subject access request process, SEC 10-K REIT disclosure). Links to the parent regulatory obligation and compliance program. Captures requirement type, frequency, responsible party role, due date logic, and applicable property or jurisdiction scope.'
}

Business justification: Regulatory filings are frequently based on or triggered by compliance assessment results (e.g., environmental assessment results filed with EPA, audit findings filed with SEC). Linking regulatory_fili']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Regulatory filings fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., quarterly SEC filing requirement, annual EPA report requirement).   compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Regulatory filings are required for mandatory reporting of confirmed violations (e.g., OSHA violation mandatory reporting, SEC material violation disclosure). Linking regulatory_filing to violation en']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Regulatory filings report financial amounts (penalty_exposure_amount) in specific currencies. The plain-text \'currency_code\' attribute is a denormalization of reference.currency_code; normalizing it e']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Regulatory filings (Form PF, AIFMD Annex IV, GRESB) are made at the fund level. Linking regulatory filings to funds is fundamental for fund compliance tracking, filing deadline management, and regulat']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: regulatory_filing has jurisdiction_level and jurisdiction_name as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-ju']

The existing regulatory_framework string is a denormalized representatio']

Business justification: Regulatory filings are triggered by transaction types — SEC Form 8-K for material acquisitions, HUD reports for FHA-financed transactions, transfer tax filings for dispositions. Linking filing to tran']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the governing body formally accepted or acknowledged the filing as complete and compliant. Distinct from filing_date (submission date). Null if not yet accepted.']

  certification_level varchar [note: 'Achievement level attained or applied for under the certification program (e.g., LEED Certified/Silver/Gold/Platinum, BREEAM Pass/Good/Very Good/Excellent/Outstanding). Null for non-certification filings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certified|silver|gold|platinum|pass|good|very_good|excellent|outstanding — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'For certification-related filings, specifies the certification standard being applied for or renewed (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR, ISO 55000, Certificate of Occupancy). Null for non-certification filings.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High-level compliance domain category for the filing, enabling portfolio-wide compliance reporting by category (e.g., environmental for EPA filings, safety for OSHA, financial for SEC/SOX, fair_housing for HUD, privacy for GDPR, esg for ESG disclosures, certification for LEED/BREEAM). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|financial|fair_housing|privacy|zoning|esg|certification — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the filing content, governing access controls and distribution (public for SEC EDGAR public filings, confidential for internal compliance records, restricted for GDPR-sensitive or legally privileged filings).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the jurisdiction in which the filing is made (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Supports international portfolio compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory filing record was first created in the compliance data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX and GDPR.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Path, URL, or document management system reference to the filed document or supporting evidence package (e.g., DocuSign CLM document ID, SharePoint path, SEC EDGAR filing URL). Enables direct access to the source filing artifact.']
  due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the filing must be submitted to the governing body. Used to monitor compliance with statutory deadlines and trigger escalation workflows.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'For ESG-related filings, indicates whether the disclosure pertains to environmental, social, governance, or combined ESG metrics. Null for non-ESG filings. Supports ESG portfolio reporting and investor disclosures.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the regulatory filing was submitted to the governing body. This is the principal real-world event date for the filing transaction.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the regulatory filing\'s purpose, scope, and key disclosures. Provides context for compliance officers and auditors reviewing the filing record.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known filing number or submission identifier assigned by the business or the regulator at the time of submission (e.g., SEC accession number, HUD case number, EPA facility ID submission reference). Serves as the primary business identifier for tracking the filing across systems.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory filing as recognized by the governing body. Tracks progression from internal draft through regulator acceptance or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|accepted|rejected|withdrawn|under_review — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  filing_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title or name of the regulatory filing as it appears on the submission document (e.g., \'Annual Report on Form 10-K for Fiscal Year 2024\', \'OSHA 300 Log of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses\').']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory filing by form or report type (e.g., 10-K Annual Report, 10-Q Quarterly Report, 8-K Current Report, HUD Fair Housing Report, EPA Environmental Disclosure, OSHA Incident Report, State Permit Application, Municipal Zoning Filing, LEED Certification Submission, ESG Disclosure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 10-K|10-Q|8-K|HUD_fair_housing|EPA_environmental|OSHA_incident|state_permit|zoning_filing|LEED_submission|ESG_disclosure|BREEAM_submission|other — promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1–4) to which the filing pertains. Applicable for quarterly filings such as SEC 10-Q. Null for annual or non-periodic filings.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the filing pertains (e.g., 2024). Used for periodic SEC REIT filings (10-K, 10-Q), HUD annual reports, and ESG annual disclosures.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or governing body to which the filing is submitted (e.g., SEC, HUD, EPA, OSHA, state real estate commission, municipal planning department, USGBC, Appraisal Institute). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SEC|HUD|EPA|OSHA|USGBC|BREEAM|NAR|state_commission|municipal|FASB|IASB|other — promote to reference product]']
  is_amendment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment or correction to a previously submitted filing (e.g., SEC 10-K/A, 10-Q/A). True if amendment; False if original submission.']
  is_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is deemed material under applicable securities or regulatory law (e.g., SEC 8-K material event, material environmental violation). True if material; False otherwise. Drives escalation and investor notification workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record additional context, internal observations, legal counsel guidance, or follow-up actions related to the filing.']
  penalty_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary penalty or fine exposure (in base currency) associated with late, rejected, or non-compliant filing. Used for risk quantification and legal reserve provisioning.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by the filing. Together with period_start_date, defines the temporal scope of the regulatory disclosure.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by the filing (e.g., fiscal quarter start, annual period start). Required for periodic filings such as SEC 10-K/10-Q and HUD annual reports.']
  regulator_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number or accession number assigned by the governing body upon receipt or acceptance of the filing (e.g., SEC EDGAR accession number, EPA confirmation ID, HUD case number). Distinct from the internal filing_reference_number.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the governing body for rejecting or returning the filing. Populated only when filing_status is \'rejected\'. Used to drive remediation workflows.']
  resubmission_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which a rejected or deficient filing must be corrected and resubmitted to the governing body. Populated when filing_status is \'rejected\' or \'under_review\'.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer, legal counsel, or senior executive who reviewed and approved the filing prior to submission. Required for SOX certifications and SEC filings.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this filing record originated (e.g., Yardi Voyager for property compliance, SAP S/4HANA for financial filings, Procore for construction permits, DocuSign CLM for executed filings). Supports data lineage and audit trail. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Yardi_Voyager|SAP_S4HANA|Procore|Salesforce_CRM|Building_Engines|DocuSign_CLM|MRI_Software|manual — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Channel or mechanism used to submit the filing to the governing body (e.g., electronic via SEC EDGAR, online portal, paper mail, courier, in-person delivery). Supports audit trail and submission verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory filing record was last modified in the compliance data product. Tracks status changes, amendments, and corrections for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every regulatory filing submitted to a governing body — SEC REIT filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K), HUD fair housing reports, EPA environmental disclosures, OSHA incident reports, state and local permit applications, and municipal zoning filings. Captures filing type, governing body, jurisdiction, filing date, submission method, filing status (draft, submitted, accepted, rejected), reference number assigned by the regulator, and the property or portfolio scope covered.'
}

Business justification: Assessments can be scoped to specific granular compliance requirements derived from regulatory obligations. This FK enables traceability from assessment findings back to the specific requirement being']

Normalizing to reference.currency_code enables consistent multi-currency remediation cost reporting and exchange rate conv']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Compliance assessments (ESG audits, AML assessments, GIPS compliance reviews) are conducted at the fund level. Linking assessments to funds supports fund-level compliance reporting, investor due dilig']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: assessment has jurisdiction_level and jurisdiction_name as denormalized string fields. jurisdiction is the reference master for all regulatory jurisdictions. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these fi']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity or business unit that is the subject of this assessment when scope extends beyond a single property.']

  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type.   zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Municipal inspectors and due diligence teams conduct zoning conformance assessments']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment or audit engagement. Covers the full range of formal compliance evaluation activities conducted across the portfolio. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OSHA_safety_inspection|EPA_phase_I_esa|EPA_phase_II_esa|HUD_fair_housing_audit|fire_marshal_inspection|building_code_review|internal_compliance_audit|external_financial_audit|SOX_audit|SEC_regulatory_examination|LEED_certification_review|BREEAM_assessment|ESG_audit|third_party_compliance_audit — promote to reference product]']
  assessor_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external auditing firm, regulatory agency, or third-party inspection organization conducting the assessment (e.g., Deloitte, EPA Region 5, Bureau Veritas). Null for internal assessments.']
  assessor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment was conducted by an internal compliance team, an external auditing firm, a regulatory body, or an independent third-party inspector.']
  audit_opinion varchar [note: 'Formal audit opinion issued by the external auditor for financial or SOX audits (unqualified/clean, qualified, adverse, or disclaimer of opinion). Null or not_applicable for non-financial inspections.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current overall compliance status of the assessed property or business unit as determined by this assessment. Drives remediation workflows and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance assessment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical severity — those requiring immediate corrective action due to imminent risk to health, safety, legal standing, or financial reporting integrity.']
  environmental_condition_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Recognized Environmental Condition (REC) was identified during an EPA Phase I or Phase II Environmental Site Assessment (ESA). A REC triggers further investigation and potential remediation obligations under CERCLA.']
  esg_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite ESG score assigned to the property or portfolio as an outcome of an ESG assessment or audit. Expressed as a numeric score on the assessor\'s defined scale. Supports ESG reporting and investor disclosure requirements.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to complete all required corrective actions identified in this assessment. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting, financial disclosure, and reserve fund planning. Expressed in the entity\'s functional currency.']
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'Date on which fieldwork activities were completed. Used to track assessment duration and SLA compliance with regulatory or contractual deadlines.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'Date on which on-site or remote fieldwork activities commenced (site visits, document reviews, interviews, sampling). Distinct from the assessment period under review.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Executive narrative summary of the key findings identified during the assessment, including material deficiencies, violations, observations, and areas of concern. Sourced from the formal assessment report.']
  follow_up_assessment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up or re-inspection assessment. Supports compliance calendar management and ensures timely closure of open findings.']
  follow_up_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up or re-inspection assessment is required to verify remediation completion or to conduct Phase II investigation following a Phase I ESA finding.']
  high_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as high severity, requiring prompt corrective action within a defined short-term remediation window.']
  lead_assessor_credential varchar [note: 'Professional credential or license held by the lead assessor (e.g., CPA, MAI, PE, CISA, LEED AP, Certified Industrial Hygienist). Validates assessor qualification for the specific assessment type.']
  lead_assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor, inspector, or assessor responsible for conducting and signing off on this assessment. Used for accountability and follow-up communications.']
  material_weakness_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a material weakness in internal controls over financial reporting was identified during this assessment. Directly relevant to SOX Section 404 disclosures and SEC filings for publicly traded REITs.']
  overall_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Aggregate risk rating assigned to the assessed entity based on the totality of findings. Used to prioritize remediation resources and escalation to senior management.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period under review or evaluation. Together with assessment_period_start_date, defines the temporal scope of the compliance evaluation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period under review or evaluation (e.g., fiscal year start for a financial audit, or the beginning of the compliance measurement window). Distinct from fieldwork dates.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this assessment or audit engagement, used in regulatory filings, audit reports, and correspondence with assessors or regulators.']
  regulatory_filing_deadline date [note: 'Mandatory deadline by which the assessment results or remediation response must be filed with the relevant regulatory authority. Critical for avoiding penalties and enforcement actions.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which all required corrective actions must be completed and verified. Set by the assessor, regulator, or internal compliance team based on finding severity.']
  remediation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action or remediation is required as a result of this assessment. Triggers the creation of remediation plan records and tracks outstanding compliance obligations.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation or corrective action plan associated with this assessment. Tracks progress from identification of findings through to verified closure.']
  report_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document management system reference, DocuSign CLM contract ID, or file path to the formal assessment or audit report. Enables direct retrieval of source documentation for regulatory review or litigation support.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal assessment or audit report was issued and delivered to the responsible party. Triggers remediation timelines and regulatory filing deadlines.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope of this assessment, including which properties, business units, processes, systems, or lease portfolios are covered. Captures the boundaries agreed with the assessor or regulator.']
  significant_deficiency_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a significant deficiency in internal controls was identified. Less severe than a material weakness but still requires communication to the audit committee under SOX and PCAOB standards.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification within the assessment type (e.g., Phase I vs Phase II for EPA ESA, initial vs surveillance for LEED, interim vs annual for SOX). Provides granularity for reporting and scheduling.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual findings, violations, or observations identified during this assessment. Provides a quantitative measure of assessment severity for portfolio-level analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance assessment record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Records the results of all compliance assessments, audits, inspections, and formal audit engagements conducted against properties or business units. Covers OSHA safety inspections, EPA environmental site assessments (Phase I/II), HUD fair housing audits, fire marshal inspections, building code compliance reviews, internal compliance audits, external financial audits, SOX audits, regulatory examinations, and third-party compliance audits. Captures assessment/audit type, assessor or auditing firm, scope (properties, business units, processes), assessment period, fieldwork dates, findings summary with severity classification, overall compliance status or audit opinion, report issuance date, and remediation requirements. Serves as the single master log of all formal compliance evaluation activities.'
}

Table compliance.remediation_action {
  remediation_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each corrective and preventive action (CAPA) record within the compliance remediation tracking system.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent compliance assessment that generated this remediation action. Links the corrective action back to the triggering assessment event after the assessment_finding merge into assessment.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or asset against which this remediation action is required. Enables portfolio-level compliance tracking and property-specific regulatory reporting.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Asbestos abatement, mold remediation, and structural repairs ordered by regulators are executed at the building level. Construction lenders, insurers, and environmental regulators track remediation by']
  compliance_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.green_certification. Business justification: Remediation actions can address green certification gaps (e.g., LEED credit deficiencies, BREEAM assessment failures). The existing leed_breeam_credit_ref string field is a denormalized reference. Add']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Remediation actions can be directly triggered by permit conditions or permit inspection failures (e.g., a building permit inspection reveals a fire suppression deficiency requiring a CAPA). Linking re']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Remediation actions address specific compliance requirements that were found to be non-compliant. Linking remediation_action to requirement enables requirement-level remediation tracking and complianc']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Remediation actions track estimated_cost and actual_cost with a plain-text cost_currency. Normalizing to reference.currency_code enables consistent multi-currency remediation cost reporting and budget']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: remediation_action has jurisdiction and jurisdiction_code as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdi']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Remediation actions (environmental remediation, structural repairs, ADA compliance) are tied to portfolio assets and affect asset valuation, hold strategy, and capex budgets. Asset managers track reme']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Remediation actions are frequently triggered directly by a regulatory obligation (e.g., an EPA mandate requiring specific corrective measures). While an indirect path exists via requirement or violati']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Unit-level remediation (lead paint abatement, mold remediation in specific units) is a named process in residential compliance. HUD, local housing authorities, and tenant protection laws require unit-']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Remediation actions specify physical scope (area of contaminated soil removed in sqft/sqm, volume of hazardous material abated) requiring a unit of measure for cost estimation, contractor scoping, and']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory violation record that triggered this remediation action, when the action originates from a formal enforcement action rather than an internal assessment finding.']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific corrective or preventive steps required to resolve the compliance finding or regulatory violation. Supports OSHA corrective action plans, EPA remediation programs, and internal audit follow-up workflows.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the remediation action (e.g., REM-2024-000123). Used in regulatory correspondence, audit trails, and enforcement agency communications.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the remediation action: open (assigned but not started), in_progress (work underway), completed (work finished, pending verification), verified (independently confirmed complete), or cancelled (action voided with documented justification).']
  action_title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the corrective or preventive action required (e.g., \'Install secondary containment for HVAC refrigerant storage\'). Used in dashboards and regulatory filings.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the action as corrective (addressing an existing violation), preventive (avoiding future recurrence), interim (temporary measure pending permanent fix), permanent (long-term resolution), or monitoring (ongoing observation). Aligns with CAPA methodology.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the remediation action was actually completed by the responsible party. Compared against target_completion_date to measure on-time compliance performance.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost actually incurred to complete the remediation action. Compared against estimated_cost for budget variance analysis and regulatory cost recovery reporting.']
  building_engines_wo_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for the corresponding work order in Building Engines (Facility Operations and Work Order Management system) when the remediation action requires physical facility work or preventive maintenance.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Classifies the remediation cost as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or a mixed allocation. Drives correct accounting treatment under FASB ASC 840/842 and tax reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this remediation action record was first created in the compliance management system. Provides the audit trail start point for the action lifecycle.']
  due_date_extension_date date [note: 'Revised target completion date when a formal extension has been granted by the regulatory authority or approved internally. Tracks deadline modifications for audit trail purposes.']
  enforcement_action_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the regulatory authority for the enforcement action, citation, consent decree, or SEC comment letter that mandated this remediation action. Used for regulatory correspondence tracking.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which this remediation action was formally escalated to senior management or regulatory authorities due to non-compliance with the target completion date or increased risk.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remediation action has been escalated due to missed deadlines, increased risk severity, or regulatory enforcement pressure. Triggers management notification and priority review workflows.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'Classifies the remediation action under the Environmental, Social, or Governance (ESG) pillar for ESG reporting and sustainability disclosure purposes. Supports LEED, BREEAM, and GRESB portfolio-level ESG metrics.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost (CAPEX and/or OPEX) to complete the remediation action, including labor, materials, contractor fees, and regulatory filing costs. Used for budget planning and CAPEX/OPEX allocation.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the compliance finding that triggered this action. Supports cross-domain compliance analytics and regulatory reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|fair_housing|data_privacy|financial_reporting|building_code|esg|certification — promote to reference product]']
  gdpr_data_subject_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remediation action involves tenant personal data and has implications for GDPR data subject rights (e.g., right to erasure, data breach remediation, consent management). Triggers GDPR-specific workflow and DPO notification.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this remediation action record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change management tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, progress updates, regulatory correspondence summaries, or internal commentary related to the remediation action. Supplements the structured fields with qualitative information.']
  penalty_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated maximum financial penalty exposure if this remediation action is not completed by the regulatory deadline. Used for risk quantification, legal reserve provisioning, and prioritization of remediation resources.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Risk-based priority level assigned to the remediation action, reflecting the severity of the underlying finding and potential regulatory or safety consequences. Critical items require immediate action; low items are scheduled improvements.']
  procore_task_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for the corresponding task or punch list item in Procore (Construction and Development Project Management system) when the remediation action involves construction or physical building work.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remediation action addresses a recurring compliance finding (i.e., the same or similar violation has been identified previously at this property). Flags systemic issues requiring root cause analysis.']
  regulatory_deadline_date date [note: 'The externally mandated deadline imposed by the regulatory authority (OSHA, EPA, HUD, SEC) for completion of this remediation action. May differ from the internal target_completion_date if the organization sets an earlier internal deadline.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying root cause identified for the compliance finding, as determined through root cause analysis (RCA). Supports preventive action design and systemic risk management.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized category of the root cause to enable trend analysis across the portfolio. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process_failure|equipment_failure|human_error|policy_gap|training_deficiency|third_party|regulatory_change — promote to reference product]']
  supporting_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to supporting documentation stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM, Procore) including corrective action plans, inspection reports, contractor agreements, and regulatory correspondence.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'The planned or regulatory-mandated date by which the remediation action must be completed. Used for deadline tracking, regulatory filing compliance, and escalation workflows.']
  triggered_by varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating event that triggered this remediation action: an internal assessment finding, a formal regulatory violation notice, an enforcement action, an internal audit, a consent decree obligation, or an SEC comment letter response.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to independently verify that the remediation action has been completed satisfactorily. Options include physical site inspection, document review, third-party audit, regulatory sign-off, photographic evidence, or laboratory/test results.']
  verified_date date [note: 'The date on which an independent verifier (internal auditor, regulatory inspector, or third-party assessor) confirmed that the remediation action was completed satisfactorily and the finding is resolved.']
  verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, organization, or regulatory body that performed the verification of the completed remediation action (e.g., OSHA compliance officer, EPA inspector, internal audit manager, third-party assessor).']

  Note: 'Tracks corrective and preventive actions (CAPAs) required to resolve compliance assessment findings, regulatory violations, and enforcement actions. Each action captures the triggering event (assessment finding or violation), action description, responsible party, target and actual completion dates, estimated remediation cost, status (open, in-progress, completed, verified), and verification method. Supports OSHA corrective action plans, EPA remediation programs, HUD consent decree actions, SEC comment letter responses, and internal audit follow-up workflows. After merging assessment_finding into assessment, remediation actions link directly to the parent assessment or violation.'
}

  internal_case_number varchar [note: 'Organization\'s internal reference number assigned to this fair housing compliance matter, used for internal tracking, audit trails, and cross-referencing with legal and compliance management systems.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal or external investigation into the fair housing matter was formally initiated.']
  legal_counsel_assigned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal counsel has been engaged to represent the organization in connection with this fair housing matter (True) or not (False).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to document additional context, investigative findings, correspondence summaries, or case-specific observations not captured in structured fields. Classified as confidential.']
  protected_class_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of protected class categories involved in this fair housing record (e.g., race, color, national_origin, religion, sex, familial_status, disability, source_of_income). Captures all applicable protected classes per the Fair Housing Act and applicable state/local laws.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date on which the complaint, accommodation request, or compliance event was formally received and logged by the compliance team. Used to track response deadlines and regulatory timelines.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of this fair housing compliance record. Values include: complaint (discrimination allegation received), accommodation_request (ADA/FHA reasonable accommodation), training_completion (staff fair housing training), hud_audit_response (response to HUD audit), investigation (internal or external investigation), self_audit (proactive internal compliance review).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the fair housing matter was formally resolved, closed, or concluded — whether through dismissal, settlement, conciliation, or administrative closure.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination or outcome of the fair housing investigation or complaint. Captures the substantive result upon closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_cause_found|cause_found|conciliation_agreement|settlement|administrative_closure|referred_to_doj|other — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fair housing record. Tracks the workflow state from initial receipt through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_investigation|resolved|dismissed|settled|withdrawn|referred_to_hud — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any financial settlement reached in connection with a fair housing complaint or investigation. Expressed in USD. Classified as confidential business-sensitive data.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the settlement amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-jurisdictional portfolio operations.']
  settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the terms agreed upon in a conciliation agreement or settlement, including any non-monetary remedies, policy changes, or training requirements imposed. Classified as confidential.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this fair housing record originated (e.g., Yardi Voyager, Salesforce CRM, MRI Software, HUD portal, or manual entry by compliance staff).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fair housing compliance record. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Tracks HUD fair housing compliance activities across residential and commercial leasing operations — fair housing training completions, accommodation requests (ADA/FHA), discrimination complaints received, investigation outcomes, and HUD audit responses. Captures protected class categories involved, complaint type, resolution status, settlement terms if applicable, and HUD case reference numbers. Supports compliance with the Fair Housing Act, Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), and Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA).'
}

  compliance_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.green_certification. Business justification: ESG metrics are frequently captured in the context of green building certifications (LEED, BREEAM). The existing leed_certification_level and breeam_rating string fields are denormalized from the gree']
  compliance_remediation_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.remediation_action. Business justification: ESG reporting frameworks (GRESB, GRI, TCFD) require tracking of environmental and social remediation progress as performance metrics. An esg_metric record may directly measure the outcome or progress ']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: ESG metrics fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., energy intensity reporting requirement, water consumption disclosure requirement). Linking esg_metric to requirement enables requirement-lev']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: ESG energy intensity benchmarks and embodied carbon metrics are construction-type-dependent — GRESB and ENERGY STAR benchmarking requires segmenting metrics by construction type (steel, concrete, timb']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: ESG metrics are reported at fund level for GRESB submissions, investor ESG reporting, and fund-level sustainability disclosures. esg_metric already links to portfolio_id; fund-level ESG metrics suppor']
  fund_performance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund_performance. Business justification: ESG metrics feed into fund performance reporting (GRESB scores, ESG-adjusted returns, sustainability KPIs). Linking ESG metrics to fund performance periods supports integrated ESG and financial report']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: esg_metric has a jurisdiction string field. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes this to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdictional ESG reporting and benchmarking.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio or fund to which this ESG metric is attributed. Supports portfolio-level ESG roll-up reporting for REIT investor disclosures.']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: ESG metrics are segmented by property type for GRESB reporting (office vs. retail vs. industrial energy intensity benchmarks differ). Real estate sustainability teams must report ESG performance by pr']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: ESG metrics fulfill specific regulatory obligations (e.g., mandatory GHG emissions reporting under EPA regulations, energy disclosure under local benchmarking ordinances). Linking esg_metric to regula']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: GRESB and ENERGY STAR benchmarking requires ESG metrics (energy intensity, water use, waste diversion) segmented by space use type (office, retail, industrial, residential). This FK enables like-for-l']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant. Business justification: Green leases require tenant-level ESG data collection (energy consumption, waste, satisfaction scores). The esg_metric product already has tenant_satisfaction_score and is_landlord_controlled attribut']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: ESG metrics (energy kWh, water m³, GHG tCO2e, waste tonnes) require normalized units of measure for cross-portfolio comparisons and GRESB/GRI/TCFD regulatory submissions. Real estate sustainability te']
  affordable_housing_units varchar [note: 'Number of residential units designated as affordable housing within the property or portfolio for the reporting period. Applicable for Social pillar metrics; null for non-residential or non-applicable metrics. Supports HUD fair housing compliance reporting.']
  anti_corruption_training_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of employees who completed mandatory anti-corruption and ethics training during the reporting period. Applicable for Governance pillar anti-corruption metrics; null otherwise.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ESG metric record was formally approved for disclosure. Null when metric_status is not Approved. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  baseline_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Reference baseline value against which performance targets and progress are measured (e.g., 2019 energy consumption baseline for net-zero pathway). Null when no formal baseline has been established.']
  baseline_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the baseline measurement used for target-setting and progress tracking (e.g., 2019). Null when no formal baseline year has been established.']
  board_diversity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of board members from underrepresented groups (gender, ethnicity, or combined diversity metric as defined by the organization\'s governance policy) for the reporting period. Applicable for Governance pillar board diversity metrics; null otherwise.']
  community_investment_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of community investment, charitable contributions, and social programs funded by the organization for the reporting period. Applicable for Social pillar community investment metrics; null otherwise.']
  coverage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total portfolio floor area or units for which actual measured data (as opposed to estimates) is available for this metric in the reporting period. A coverage of 100% indicates fully metered data; lower values indicate partial coverage with estimation applied to gaps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ESG metric record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Operational system or external source from which the metric data was obtained (e.g., \'Yardi Voyager Utility Tracking\', \'Building Engines Work Order System\', \'Utility Provider Invoice\', \'ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager\', \'Manual Survey\'). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  esg_pillar varchar [note: 'High-level ESG pillar classification: Environmental, Social, or Governance. Drives framework mapping and investor disclosure categorization.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to fill data gaps where actual measured data is unavailable: Actual (fully metered), Extrapolation, Interpolation, Engineering Estimate, or Proxy. Supports GRESB data quality scoring and third-party assurance.']
  ethics_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of confirmed ethics policy violations or code-of-conduct breaches recorded during the reporting period. Applicable for Governance pillar ethics metrics. Classified confidential due to sensitive governance nature.']
  framework_indicator_code varchar [note: 'Specific indicator or disclosure code within the reporting framework (e.g., \'GRI 302-1\', \'SASB IF-RE-130a.1\', \'GRESB EC1\', \'TCFD Metrics and Targets\'). Enables precise framework alignment mapping for regulatory and investor filings.']
  ghg_scope varchar [note: 'GHG Protocol emission scope classification applicable when the metric category is GHG Emissions: Scope 1 (direct emissions), Scope 2 (purchased energy indirect), Scope 3 (value chain indirect). Set to N/A for non-emissions metrics.']
  intensity_denominator varchar [note: 'The normalization basis used to compute the intensity value: SQM (square meters), SQF (square feet), GLA (Gross Leasable Area), NLA (Net Leasable Area), or revenue_usd. Null or N/A when intensity is not reported.']
  intensity_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Normalized intensity metric expressing the ESG value per unit of floor area or revenue (e.g., kWh per SQM, CO2e per SQF, m3 per SQM). Enables like-for-like benchmarking across properties of different sizes. Null when intensity normalization is not applicable.']
  is_landlord_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the energy, water, or utility consumption represented by this metric is under landlord operational control (True) or tenant-controlled (False). Critical for determining boundary of operational control under GHG Protocol and GRESB data collection scope.']
  is_like_for_like boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this metric record qualifies for like-for-like (LfL) comparison with the prior period — i.e., the property was owned and operational for the full duration of both the current and prior reporting periods with no significant structural changes. Required for GRESB like-for-like performance scoring.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology, protocol, or standard used to measure or calculate this ESG metric (e.g., \'GHG Protocol Corporate Standard Scope 2 market-based\', \'ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager metered data\', \'Utility bill aggregation via Yardi Voyager\'). Critical for data quality and third-party assurance.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'Sub-category within the ESG pillar (e.g., Energy, Water, GHG Emissions, Waste, Biodiversity, Affordable Housing, Community Investment, Tenant Satisfaction, Board Diversity, Ethics, Anti-Corruption). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Energy|Water|GHG Emissions|Waste|Biodiversity|Affordable Housing|Community Investment|Tenant Satisfaction|Board Diversity|Ethics|Anti-Corruption|Renewable Energy|Indoor Air Quality — promote to reference product]']
  metric_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the ESG metric type within the enterprise taxonomy (e.g., ENV-ENERGY-KWH, SOC-AFFORD-UNITS, GOV-BOARD-DIV). Aligns with GRESB indicator codes and internal ESG data dictionary.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the ESG metric (e.g., \'Total Energy Consumption\', \'GHG Emissions Scope 1\', \'Affordable Housing Units\', \'Board Gender Diversity Ratio\'). Used in investor reports and regulatory filings.']
  metric_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this ESG metric record: Draft (data entry in progress), Submitted (submitted for review), Under Review (being reviewed by ESG team or auditor), Approved (finalized and approved for disclosure), Restated (previously published value corrected), Withdrawn (removed from disclosure).']
  metric_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Quantitative measured or reported value for the ESG metric for the specified reporting period (e.g., 1,250,000 kWh of energy consumed, 45.2 tonnes CO2e of GHG emissions, 120 affordable housing units). Precision supports both large energy figures and small fractional rates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, qualifications, or explanatory notes about this ESG metric record (e.g., reasons for data gaps, restatement explanations, boundary assumptions, or methodology deviations). Supports narrative disclosure in ESG reports.']
  prior_period_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Metric value for the immediately preceding comparable reporting period. Stored to support year-over-year variance analysis and trend reporting in investor ESG disclosures without requiring a self-join.']
  renewable_energy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total energy consumption sourced from renewable energy (solar, wind, geothermal, etc.) for the reporting period. Applicable when metric_category is Energy or Renewable Energy; null otherwise. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']
  reporting_framework varchar [note: 'Primary ESG reporting framework under which this metric is disclosed: GRI (Global Reporting Initiative), SASB (Sustainability Accounting Standards Board), TCFD (Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures), GRESB (Global Real Estate Sustainability Benchmark), CDP, UNPRI, SFDR, Multiple, or Other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GRI|SASB|TCFD|GRESB|CDP|UNPRI|SFDR|Multiple|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the measurement or reporting period for this ESG metric (inclusive). Together with reporting_period_start defines the temporal boundary for the metric value.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the measurement or reporting period for this ESG metric (inclusive). Typically aligns with fiscal year, calendar year, or quarterly reporting cycle.']
