  target_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Approved internal or externally committed target value for this metric for the reporting period (e.g., a 10% reduction in energy consumption vs. baseline). Supports performance-against-target reporting for ESG scorecards.']
  tenant_satisfaction_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate tenant satisfaction survey score for the property or portfolio for the reporting period, expressed on the survey scale (e.g., 0–100 or 1–5). Applicable for Social pillar tenant satisfaction metrics; null otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ESG metric record was most recently modified. Tracks data revisions and supports restatement audit trails required by SEC and SOX.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification or assurance status of the ESG metric data: Unverified (raw), Internal Review (reviewed by internal ESG team), Third-Party Assured (externally assured), Limited Assurance, or Reasonable Assurance. Drives investor confidence and regulatory disclosure quality.']
  verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external assurance provider or auditor who verified this ESG metric (e.g., \'Deloitte\', \'KPMG\', \'Bureau Veritas\'). Null when verification_status is Unverified or Internal Review.']
  waste_diversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total waste diverted from landfill through recycling, composting, or reuse for the reporting period. Applicable when metric_category is Waste; null otherwise. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']

  Note: 'Captures ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) performance metrics at the property and portfolio level for investor reporting and regulatory disclosure. Environmental metrics include energy consumption (kWh), water usage, GHG emissions (Scope 1/2/3 in CO2e), waste diversion rates, and renewable energy percentage. Social metrics cover affordable housing units, community investment, and tenant satisfaction. Governance metrics track board diversity, ethics violations, and anti-corruption measures. Each metric record captures the reporting period, measurement methodology, data source, and verification status. Supports ESG reporting frameworks (GRI, SASB, TCFD, GRESB) and REIT investor ESG disclosure requirements.'
}

Table compliance.green_certification {
  green_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for green_certification']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Green certifications record certified_area_sqft requiring a normalized UOM for cross-certification and cross-jurisdiction ESG reporting. Real estate sustainability teams need standardized area UOM for']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this green certification has been obtained or is being pursued. Links the certification record to the master property entity.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: LEED, BREEAM, and ENERGY STAR certifications are awarded to specific buildings. A multi-building asset may have buildings with different certification levels. GRESB submissions, investor ESG reporting']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Green building certifications (LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR) are typically tied to specific building permits for the construction or renovation work that achieves the certification. Linking green_certifi']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Green certifications fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., energy efficiency requirements, water conservation requirements). Linking green_certification to requirement enables requirement-le']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: LEED and BREEAM certification pathways, available credits, and achievable point totals differ by construction type (new construction vs. core-and-shell vs. existing buildings). Certifying bodies requi']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: green_certification has jurisdiction_country and jurisdiction_state as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent mul']
  previous_certification_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior certification record for the same property and certification type, enabling certification history chain tracking and renewal lineage. Null for the initial certification.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Green certifications fulfill specific regulatory obligations (e.g., mandatory energy benchmarking, green building code compliance). Linking green_certification to regulatory_obligation enables obligat']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: LEED for Homes and ENERGY STAR Homes certify individual residential units. In for-sale and rental residential real estate, unit-level green certifications are tracked for marketing, premium pricing, a']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal application or registration for the certification was submitted to the certifying body. Marks the start of the certification pursuit lifecycle.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal on-site or documentary assessment was conducted by the certifying body or licensed assessor. Distinct from the application date and the certification award date.']
  assessor_accreditation varchar [note: 'The professional accreditation or credential held by the assessor (e.g., LEED AP BD+C, LEED AP O+M, BREEAM Assessor, WELL AP). Validates the assessor\'s authority to conduct the certification assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the licensed or accredited assessor, consultant, or LEED Accredited Professional (LEED AP) who conducted the certification assessment or managed the certification submission on behalf of the property.']
  audit_trail_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification record requires a formal audit trail of supporting documentation for regulatory or certifying body verification purposes. True for certifications subject to third-party verification audits (e.g., ENERGY STAR verification, LEED documentation review).']
  breeam_category_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured summary of scores across BREEAM assessment categories (e.g., Energy, Water, Materials, Waste, Land Use & Ecology, Pollution, Transport, Health & Wellbeing, Management). Stored as a delimited string for BREEAM-type certifications; null for non-BREEAM certifications.']
  certification_date date [note: 'The date on which the certifying body officially awarded the certification to the property. Represents the commencement of the certified period. Used for ESG reporting timelines and green lease compliance.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'The tier or rating level achieved or targeted under the certification framework (e.g., LEED Platinum, LEED Gold, LEED Silver, LEED Certified; BREEAM Outstanding, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Pass; ENERGY STAR score tier). Varies by certification type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Platinum|Gold|Silver|Certified|Outstanding|Excellent|Very_Good|Good|Pass — promote to reference product]']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-issued unique identifier assigned by the certifying body (e.g., USGBC LEED project ID, ENERGY STAR certification number, BRE BREEAM certificate reference). Used for cross-referencing with the certifying body\'s registry.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification record. \'Pursuing\' indicates the property is in the application or assessment process; \'Certified\' indicates active certification; \'Expired\' indicates the certification period has lapsed; \'Withdrawn\' indicates the application was voluntarily withdrawn; \'Suspended\' indicates certification has been placed on hold by the certifying body.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the green building certification standard being applied. Identifies the framework under which the property is assessed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LEED|BREEAM|ENERGY_STAR|WELL|Fitwel|Living_Building_Challenge — promote to reference product if additional standards are adopted]']
  certification_version varchar [note: 'The specific version or edition of the certification standard under which the property was assessed (e.g., LEED v4.1, LEED v4, LEED 2009, BREEAM 2018, BREEAM In-Use v6). Critical for interpreting scoring criteria and prerequisites.']
  certified_area_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'The gross floor area (GFA) in square feet (SQF) covered by this certification. Used for ESG intensity metrics (e.g., energy use per SQF), green lease obligations, and portfolio-level certified area reporting.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'The organization responsible for issuing and administering the certification (e.g., USGBC for LEED, BRE for BREEAM, EPA for ENERGY STAR, IWBI for WELL Building Standard, Fitwel for Fitwel). Used for ESG reporting and audit trail purposes.']
  certifying_body_project_ref varchar [note: 'The project registration or tracking identifier assigned by the certifying body\'s online platform (e.g., USGBC LEED Online project ID, BREEAM Projects portal reference). Used for direct cross-referencing with the certifying body\'s registry and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this green certification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  energy_star_score varchar [note: 'The EPA ENERGY STAR portfolio manager score (1–100) reflecting the property\'s energy performance relative to similar buildings nationally. A score of 75 or above qualifies for ENERGY STAR certification. Applicable when certification_type is ENERGY_STAR or as a supplementary metric for LEED/BREEAM energy credits.']
  esg_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification record is included in the organization\'s ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) disclosures and sustainability reporting (e.g., GRESB submission, annual sustainability report, SEC ESG disclosures for REITs). True if reportable; False if excluded.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current certification expires and renewal is required to maintain certified status. Null if the certification does not have a defined expiry (e.g., some LEED versions). Critical for compliance monitoring and renewal workflow triggers.']
  green_lease_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is referenced in or required by one or more green lease agreements for the property. True if the certification underpins green lease obligations (e.g., tenant energy reporting, sustainability covenants); False otherwise.']
  gresb_submission_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this certification data was included in the organization\'s GRESB (Global Real Estate Sustainability Benchmark) submission. Used to track ESG reporting cycles and benchmark performance year-over-year.']
  incentive_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific financial incentives, tax credits, or grants for which the property is eligible as a result of this certification (e.g., IRS Section 179D deduction, state property tax abatement, utility rebate program). Populated when incentive_eligible is True.']
  incentive_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property qualifies for financial incentives (tax credits, grants, reduced permit fees, expedited permitting) as a result of achieving this green certification. Supports CAPEX planning and investment advisory analysis.']
  leed_category_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured summary of points earned across LEED credit categories (e.g., Sustainable Sites, Water Efficiency, Energy & Atmosphere, Materials & Resources, Indoor Environmental Quality, Innovation). Stored as a delimited string for LEED-type certifications; null for non-LEED certifications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, conditions, exceptions, or observations related to the certification record (e.g., conditional certification notes, outstanding credit appeals, assessor observations, special circumstances affecting the certification outcome).']
  points_available decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum number of credits or points available under the applicable certification version and building type. Used to calculate the percentage score and determine the achievable certification level.']
  points_scored decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total number of credits or points awarded to the property under the certification framework\'s scoring system (e.g., LEED points out of 110, BREEAM percentage score). Represents the principal quantitative outcome of the certification assessment.']
  prerequisites_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all mandatory prerequisites required by the certification framework have been satisfied. A value of True confirms all prerequisites are fulfilled; False indicates outstanding prerequisites that must be resolved before certification can be awarded.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the renewal application must be submitted to the certifying body to avoid lapse of certification. May differ from expiry_date if the certifying body requires advance submission. Supports compliance calendar and deadline management.']
  renewal_in_progress boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a renewal application for this certification is currently in progress with the certifying body. True if the renewal process has been initiated but not yet completed; False otherwise. Supports compliance monitoring and workflow management.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the physical scope of the certification — specifying which building, floor, wing, or portion of the property is covered by this certification record. Relevant for multi-building campuses or partial certifications.']
  score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The certification score expressed as a percentage of total available points (points_scored / points_available × 100). Used for cross-framework ESG benchmarking and portfolio-level green performance reporting.']
  supporting_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the certification documentation package stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM, Procore document repository). Includes the official certificate, assessment report, and supporting evidence submitted to the certifying body.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this green certification record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for green building certifications obtained or pursued for properties — LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design), BREEAM (Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method), ENERGY STAR, WELL Building Standard, and Fitwel certifications. Captures certification type, certifying body (USGBC, BRE, EPA), certification level (LEED Platinum/Gold/Silver/Certified), certification date, expiry date, points scored, prerequisites met, and renewal requirements. Supports ESG reporting and green lease obligations.'
}

Table compliance.permit {
  permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for permit']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Permits specify floor area (floor_area_sqft) which requires a normalized unit of measure for cross-jurisdiction comparisons (sqft vs. sqm). Real estate development teams operating internationally need']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset to which this permit applies. Links the permit to the master property registry for portfolio-level compliance tracking.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Building permits, certificates of occupancy, and fire safety permits are issued against specific buildings. Multi-building assets require building-level permit tracking for COO issuance, lender covena']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Building and environmental permits frequently require formal compliance assessments as a condition of issuance or renewal (e.g., a certificate of occupancy requires a fire safety inspection assessment']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Permits fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., building code compliance requirements, fire safety requirements). Linking permit to requirement enables requirement-level permit compliance trac']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Building permits are issued for specific construction types (IBC Type I-A through Type V-B). Real estate development teams and code compliance officers track construction type on permits for fire code']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: permit has jurisdiction and jurisdiction_level as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdictional per']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Land use permits, grading permits, subdivision permits, and environmental clearances are issued against parcels. Zoning variance approvals, lot split permits, and environmental permits reference the p']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Permits (building permits, occupancy certificates, environmental permits) are directly tied to portfolio assets. Asset managers track permit status as part of asset hold/disposition strategy, capex pl']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Permits are issued for specific property types (residential building permit vs. commercial certificate of occupancy). Real estate development and asset management teams track permits by property type ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Permits fulfill specific regulatory obligations (e.g., building permit obligation under local zoning code, certificate of occupancy obligation). Linking permit to regulatory_obligation enables obligat']
  space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Tenant improvement permits, change-of-use permits, and occupancy load certificates are issued for specific commercial spaces/suites. Commercial property managers and leasing teams track TI permit stat']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Building permits are issued for specific space uses — change-of-use permits, occupancy certificates, and conditional use permits are defined by space use type. Permit management systems require space ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential unit renovation permits and individual unit certificates of occupancy are tracked at unit level in property management. HUD, local housing authorities, and residential lenders require unit']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Permits are issued under specific zoning codes that govern permitted uses and development standards. Real estate development and land use compliance teams link permits to zoning codes for entitlement ']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit application was submitted to the issuing authority. Used to track application processing lead times and regulatory response windows.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit application was formally approved by the issuing authority, which may differ from the issue date in jurisdictions with staged approval processes.']
  breeam_rating varchar [note: 'BREEAM environmental assessment rating associated with this permit for international properties. Supports ESG compliance reporting and green building certification tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: outstanding|excellent|very_good|good|pass|unclassified|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special conditions, restrictions, or obligations attached to the permit by the issuing authority (e.g., noise restrictions, construction hours, environmental mitigation requirements). Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Procore document records.']
  contractor_license_number varchar [note: 'License number of the contractor or licensed professional (e.g., architect, engineer) named on the permit application. Required for building, electrical, plumbing, and mechanical permits. Sourced from Procore contractor records.']
  coo_number varchar [note: 'Official Certificate of Occupancy (COO) number issued by the local building authority, confirming the structure meets all applicable building codes and is legally habitable or operable. Populated only for COO-type permits.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction in which the permit was issued. Supports international portfolio compliance management (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'CAN\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this permit record was first created in the compliance data product. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX, GDPR, and SEC reporting for publicly traded REITs.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the digitized permit document stored in the document management system (DocuSign CLM, Procore, or SharePoint). Enables direct access to the official permit certificate for audit and due diligence purposes.']
  esg_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit is relevant to ESG reporting obligations (e.g., environmental permits, LEED/BREEAM certifications, energy efficiency permits). Drives ESG data aggregation for SEC and investor reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit expires and is no longer valid. Null for permits with no defined expiry. Drives renewal workflow triggers and compliance deadline monitoring.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total fee paid to the issuing authority for the permit application and issuance, expressed in the local currency. Used for CAPEX/OPEX tracking and development cost budgeting.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the permit fee amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'GBP\', \'EUR\'). Supports multi-currency portfolio accounting.']
  floor_area_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross floor area in square feet (SQF) covered by the scope of this permit. Relevant for building permits, COO, and zoning variance permits. Supports FAR (Floor Area Ratio) compliance calculations.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical inspection by the issuing authority is required as a condition of permit issuance or renewal. Drives inspection scheduling workflows in Building Engines.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit was officially issued by the governing authority. Marks the start of the permit\'s validity period and is the principal business event date for this record.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental body, regulatory agency, or municipality that issued the permit (e.g., \'City of New York Department of Buildings\', \'EPA Region 2\', \'OSHA Area Office\'). Critical for multi-jurisdictional compliance management.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent inspection conducted in connection with this permit. Used to assess inspection currency and schedule next required inspection.']
  last_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent inspection associated with this permit. \'conditional_pass\' indicates approval subject to remediation of minor deficiencies within a specified timeframe.']
  leed_certification_level varchar [note: 'LEED certification level associated with this permit, where the permit relates to green building compliance or construction. Supports ESG reporting and USGBC certification tracking.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled or required date for the next inspection associated with this permit. Populated by compliance scheduling workflows and used to drive proactive inspection management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, compliance observations, or internal commentary related to this permit record. Used by property managers and compliance officers for context not captured in structured fields.']
  permit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the permit as assigned by the issuing authority or internally for operational reference (e.g., \'Annual Elevator Operating Permit — Tower A\', \'LEED Construction Waste Management Permit\').']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned permit number issued by the governing authority or jurisdiction. This is the official reference used in regulatory filings, inspections, and correspondence. Sourced from the issuing authority\'s permit management system.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit. Drives compliance dashboards, renewal workflows, and regulatory reporting. \'pending_issuance\' indicates application submitted but not yet approved; \'pending_renewal\' indicates the permit is within the renewal window.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit by its regulatory purpose. Covers the full spectrum of permits required across the real estate portfolio lifecycle — from construction through operations and disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: building|certificate_of_occupancy|environmental|fire_safety|elevator|demolition|zoning_variance|electrical|plumbing|mechanical — promote to reference product]']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the permit renewal application must be submitted to avoid lapse. May precede the expiry date by a jurisdiction-defined lead time. Drives automated renewal reminders in Building Engines and Yardi Voyager.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'The number of months between required permit renewals. Null if renewal_required is False. Used to auto-calculate future renewal_due_date values and schedule compliance calendar events.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit requires periodic renewal. True for time-limited permits (e.g., annual elevator permits, fire safety certificates); False for one-time permits (e.g., demolition permits).']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit was revoked by the issuing authority, if applicable. Null for permits that have not been revoked. Critical for disposition compliance and regulatory audit trails.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Description of the specific construction, renovation, operational activity, or environmental action authorized by this permit. Aligns with Procore project scope documentation for development permits.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this permit record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation across Yardi Voyager, Procore, Building Engines, and DocuSign CLM.']
  source_system_permit_ref varchar [note: 'The native permit record identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Procore permit ID, Yardi Voyager compliance record ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province in which the permit was issued. Used for state-level regulatory compliance tracking and reporting (e.g., \'NY\', \'CA\', \'TX\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this permit record was most recently modified in the compliance data product. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and Silver Layer incremental load processing.']
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The declared construction or project valuation amount submitted with the permit application, as required by many jurisdictions to calculate permit fees and assess project scale. Expressed in the local currency.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all permits required and obtained for properties — building permits, certificates of occupancy (COO), environmental permits, fire safety permits, elevator operating permits, demolition permits, and zoning variances. Captures permit type, issuing authority, jurisdiction, permit number, issue date, expiry date, renewal requirements, permit conditions, inspection history, and current status (active, expired, pending renewal, revoked). Critical for development, operations, and disposition compliance. Includes inspection records associated with permit issuance and renewal.'
}

Table compliance.violation {
  violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for violation']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property asset where the violation or non-compliance event occurred. Links to the property master record in the portfolio.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Building code, fire code, and health code violations are cited against specific buildings by municipal inspectors. Multi-building asset owners require building-level violation tracking for COO holds, ']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Violations are frequently identified during compliance assessments. Linking violation to the identifying assessment enables traceability from assessment finding to confirmed violation, supporting enfo']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit violations (e.g., operating without a valid permit, violating permit conditions) are a common compliance violation type in real estate. Linking violation to permit enables traceability from per']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Violations are breaches of specific compliance requirements. Linking violation to requirement enables requirement-level violation tracking and identifies which requirements have the highest violation ']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Violations track penalty_amount_assessed and penalty_amount_settled with a plain-text penalty_currency_code. Normalizing to reference.currency_code enables consistent multi-currency penalty reporting,']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Compliance violations at the fund level (SEC violations, AML violations, ERISA violations) affect fund operations, investor reporting, and regulatory standing. Linking violations to funds supports fun']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: violation has jurisdiction_level, jurisdiction_state, and jurisdiction_country as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master and removes t']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (ownership entity, REIT subsidiary, or operating company) against which the violation was issued or that bears regulatory responsibility.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Environmental violations, zoning violations, and land use violations (illegal grading, wetlands encroachment, unpermitted structures) are cited against parcels by regulatory agencies. EPA, state envir']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Violation penalty structures, governing bodies, and remediation timelines differ materially by property type (residential vs. industrial vs. office). Regulatory reporting and portfolio-level complianc']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory obligation that was violated, linking this event to the obligation register for root-cause and gap analysis.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant. Business justification: Compliance violations are frequently caused by tenant behavior (unauthorized alterations, noise ordinance breaches, lease violations escalated to regulatory citations). Property managers and complianc']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Housing code violations (lead paint, mold, habitability) are cited at the unit level in residential properties. Tenant rights enforcement, rent escrow proceedings, and HUD inspections reference specif']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Zoning violations (unpermitted uses, FAR exceedances, setback breaches) are the most common municipal enforcement actions in real estate. Linking violation to zoning_code enables zoning-specific viola']
  abatement_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the corrective action or abatement of the cited condition was completed and verified. Used to calculate abatement lead time and confirm regulatory closure.']
  abatement_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the cited hazard or non-compliance condition must be corrected and abated. Failure to meet this date may result in additional penalties or willful reclassification.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the compliance officer, property manager, or responsible party assigned to manage the remediation and regulatory response for this violation.']
  citation_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory body formally issued the citation, notice of violation, or enforcement action. Triggers the statutory response and contest windows.']
  contest_deadline_date date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which the cited party must file a notice of contest to challenge the citation before the relevant review commission (e.g., OSHRC for OSHA citations). Typically 15 working days from receipt of citation.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the formal corrective action plan (CAP) developed to remediate the cited condition and prevent recurrence. Summarizes the remediation steps, responsible parties, and milestones.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this violation record was first created in the compliance data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX internal control documentation.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured or ill worker was away from work as a result of the recordable incident. Required field on the OSHA 300 Log. Zero if not applicable.']
  days_job_transfer_restriction varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the worker was on job transfer or work restriction due to the recordable incident, as required on the OSHA 300 Log. Zero if not applicable.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG pillar classification for the violation. Environmental violations (EPA, LEED non-compliance) map to E; workplace safety (OSHA) and fair housing (HUD) map to S; financial reporting (SEC, SOX) and data privacy (GDPR) map to G. Supports ESG disclosure and portfolio-level ESG scoring.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority or enforcement agency that issued the violation or citation (e.g., EPA, OSHA, HUD, SEC, local building department). Determines the regulatory response protocol and mandatory reporting timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA|OSHA|HUD|SEC|LOCAL_AUTHORITY|STATE_AGENCY|FIRE_MARSHAL|BUILDING_DEPT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workplace safety incident type for OSHA reporting purposes. Fatalities require separate 8-hour reporting to OSHA; hospitalizations require 24-hour reporting.']
  is_contested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cited party has formally filed a notice of contest challenging the violation citation. When true, the matter is referred to the applicable review commission or administrative tribunal.']
  is_mandatory_report boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation or incident triggers a mandatory regulatory reporting obligation (e.g., OSHA 8-hour fatality report, OSHA 24-hour hospitalization report, SEC material event disclosure, GDPR 72-hour breach notification). When true, drives the mandatory reporting workflow.']
  is_repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a repeat of a substantially similar violation cited at the same establishment within the past five years. Repeat violations carry significantly higher OSHA penalties (up to 10x the base penalty).']
  leed_breeam_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation has a material impact on the property\'s LEED or BREEAM certification status. When true, triggers a review of the certification standing with the U.S. Green Building Council or BRE.']
  mandatory_report_deadline timestamp [note: 'Timestamp by which the mandatory regulatory report must be submitted to the governing body. Calculated from the violation or incident date based on the applicable regulatory timeline (e.g., 8 hours for OSHA fatality, 72 hours for GDPR breach).']
  osha_log_classification varchar [note: 'OSHA 300 Log column classification for recordable workplace incidents. Categorizes the nature of the work-related injury or illness per OSHA 29 CFR 1904 recordkeeping requirements. Applicable only when osha_recordable is true.']
  osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR 1904 and must be entered on the OSHA 300 Log. Applies to work-related injuries and illnesses meeting the recording threshold.']
  penalty_amount_assessed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary penalty amount formally assessed by the regulatory body at the time of citation issuance, in the currency specified by penalty_currency_code. Represents the initial proposed penalty before any informal settlement or reduction.']
  penalty_amount_settled decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final negotiated or settled penalty amount after informal conference, contest resolution, or administrative settlement. May differ from the assessed amount due to reductions for good faith, size, or history.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known citation or case reference number assigned by the issuing regulatory body (e.g., OSHA citation number, EPA Notice of Violation case ID, building code violation ticket number). Used for correspondence and regulatory tracking.']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory code, statute, standard, or rule section that was violated (e.g., \'29 CFR 1926.502(d)(1)\' for OSHA fall protection, \'40 CFR 112\' for EPA Spill Prevention, \'GDPR Article 32\'). Enables precise regulatory mapping.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who first reported or logged the violation or safety incident. Supports audit trail and whistleblower protection documentation.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the violation after the regulatory process is complete. Captures whether the matter was abated, dismissed, settled, or resolved through appeal. Used for compliance program effectiveness reporting.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level root cause classification from the post-incident or post-citation root cause analysis (RCA). Drives systemic corrective action programs and recurrence prevention initiatives.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause findings from the formal root cause analysis (RCA) investigation. Captures the specific conditions, actions, or systemic failures that led to the violation.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'OSHA-aligned severity classification of the violation indicating the degree of hazard or non-compliance. Willful and serious violations carry higher penalties and mandatory abatement timelines. Also applied to non-OSHA violations by analogy.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Building Engines work order ID, Procore inspection ID, OSHA citation number from the portal). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this violation record originated (e.g., Building Engines work order, Procore inspection event, OSHA online portal, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Yardi|Building_Engines|Procore|SAP|Manual|OSHA_Portal|EPA_Portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the violation type (e.g., \'Hazardous Waste Disposal\' under environmental, \'Fall Protection\' under safety, \'Discriminatory Advertising\' under fair housing). Supports detailed compliance analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this violation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to status, penalty amounts, abatement dates, or resolution outcome for audit and change management purposes.']
  violation_date date [note: 'The date on which the violation, non-compliance event, or workplace safety incident occurred. This is the principal real-world event date, distinct from the citation issuance date or record creation date.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the violation or non-compliance event as documented in the citation or inspection report. Captures the specific condition, act, or omission that constitutes the violation.']
  violation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the violation record. Drives remediation workflow, regulatory response management, and compliance dashboard reporting.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the violation category. Drives routing to the appropriate compliance program and remediation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|building_code|zoning|fair_housing|financial_reporting|data_privacy|fire_code|accessibility|labor — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records confirmed regulatory violations, non-compliance events, enforcement actions, and OSHA-reportable workplace safety incidents across all compliance programs. Covers EPA notices of violation, OSHA citations and recordable incidents (injuries, illnesses, near-misses, fatalities with 300/300A log classification), HUD enforcement actions, SEC comment letters, building code violations, and zoning violations. Captures violation/incident type, regulatory body, citation reference, date, severity (willful, serious, other-than-serious, de minimis), OSHA recordability classification, days away from work, root cause analysis, penalty amount assessed, contest status, and resolution outcome. Drives remediation workflows, regulatory response management, and mandatory incident reporting.'
}

Table compliance.jurisdiction {
  jurisdiction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory jurisdiction record in the compliance master. Primary key for the jurisdiction reference product. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — serves as an authoritative code-list/master for regulatory jurisdictions; per-role minimums are exempt.]']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Jurisdictions belong to countries and inherit country-level regulatory properties (GDPR applicability, REIT framework, foreign ownership rules). Real estate compliance teams managing cross-border port']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Jurisdictions define max_penalty_amount in a specific currency (penalty_currency_code plain text). Normalizing to reference.currency_code enables penalty exposure aggregation across jurisdictions with']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Jurisdictions map to geographic hierarchy nodes (MSA, state, city) enabling real estate portfolio managers to aggregate compliance obligations by market geography for regulatory reporting, market-leve']
  accepted_submission_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of accepted filing submission methods for this jurisdiction (e.g., \'online_portal,mail,email,in_person\'). Informs compliance teams of valid channels for regulatory submissions.']
  applicable_property_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of real estate property types to which this jurisdiction\'s regulations apply (e.g., \'commercial,residential,industrial,mixed_use\'). Enables targeted compliance obligation assignment based on asset class within the portfolio.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city or municipality for city-level and special district jurisdictions. Used to assign municipal compliance obligations (e.g., local building codes, rent control ordinances, municipal ESG reporting mandates) to properties within the portfolio.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Primary compliance domain category governed by this jurisdiction. Drives routing of obligations and filings to the appropriate compliance program. Values: environmental (EPA), safety (OSHA), fair_housing (HUD), financial (SEC/SOX/FASB), privacy (GDPR/CCPA), esg (ESG/LEED/BREEAM), zoning (local planning), tax (IRS/state revenue). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|fair_housing|financial|privacy|esg|zoning|tax — promote to reference product]']
  county_name varchar [note: 'Name of the county or parish within which this jurisdiction operates. Applicable for county-level and municipal jurisdictions. Used for property-level compliance mapping where county authorities govern zoning, permitting, and environmental assessments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was first created in the compliance master data store. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this jurisdiction record became effective and its regulatory authority became applicable to the portfolio. Used to determine when compliance obligations under this jurisdiction first apply.']
  esg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this jurisdiction mandates Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting for real estate entities operating within its boundaries. Drives ESG compliance program activation for properties in this jurisdiction.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this jurisdiction record expires or ceases to be applicable. Null for jurisdictions with no defined end date. Used to manage superseded or sunset regulatory authorities.']
  filing_portal_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the electronic filing portal or system used to submit compliance filings to this jurisdiction (e.g., \'EPA Central Data Exchange (CDX)\', \'SEC EDGAR\', \'HUD Integrated Disbursement and Information System (IDIS)\').']
  filing_portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the official online filing portal for this jurisdiction (e.g., EPA\'s CDX portal, SEC EDGAR, HUD IDIS). Used by compliance teams to submit regulatory filings, permits, and disclosures electronically.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies to tenant data processing activities within this jurisdiction. Activates GDPR data privacy controls for tenant records associated with properties in this jurisdiction.']
  geographic_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic boundaries or territorial scope of this jurisdiction (e.g., \'All properties within the five boroughs of New York City\', \'Coastal zone within 1,000 feet of mean high tide\'). Supports GIS-based compliance mapping for portfolio properties.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the specific regulatory or governmental body that administers this jurisdiction (e.g., \'U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)\', \'Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)\', \'U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)\', \'Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)\'). Referenced by compliance obligations, filings, and permits.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Unique, externally-recognizable alphanumeric code identifying the jurisdiction (e.g., US-EPA, US-CA-DRE, US-NYC-DOB). Used as the business key for cross-system lookups and compliance mapping across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and SAP S/4HANA.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the regulatory jurisdiction within the governmental structure. Drives multi-jurisdictional compliance mapping logic — federal obligations cascade to state and local levels. Values: federal, state, county, city, special_district.']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., \'U.S. Environmental Protection Agency\', \'California Department of Real Estate\', \'New York City Department of Buildings\'). Serves as the human-readable identity label for compliance reporting and filing assignment.']
  jurisdiction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the jurisdiction record. \'active\' indicates the jurisdiction is currently enforcing regulations applicable to the portfolio. \'superseded\' indicates the jurisdiction has been replaced by another record. Used to filter valid jurisdictions for compliance obligation assignment.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the jurisdiction by its governmental or regulatory function. Distinguishes between executive regulatory agencies (EPA, OSHA), legislative bodies, judicial districts, and special-purpose authorities relevant to real estate compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory_agency|legislative_body|judicial_district|municipal_authority|special_district|tribal_authority — promote to reference product]']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or BCP 47 language code for the official language of regulatory filings and correspondence required by this jurisdiction (e.g., \'en\', \'fr-CA\', \'es\'). Ensures compliance documents are prepared in the required language.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was most recently modified. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions. Used to detect stale reference data and trigger downstream compliance obligation re-evaluation.']
  leed_breeam_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) or BREEAM (Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method) certification requirements or incentives apply within this jurisdiction. Supports green building compliance tracking.']
  mailing_address varchar [note: 'Official mailing address of the regulatory jurisdiction\'s governing body office. Used for physical filing submissions, certified mail correspondence, and formal regulatory notices.']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum statutory financial penalty that this jurisdiction can impose per violation or per filing period. Expressed in the jurisdiction\'s local currency. Used for compliance risk quantification and penalty exposure reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional compliance context, special instructions, or operational notes about this jurisdiction (e.g., \'Requires wet signature on all permit applications\', \'Annual filing window opens January 1\'). Supports compliance team knowledge management.']
  penalty_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this jurisdiction has statutory authority to impose financial penalties or enforcement actions for non-compliance. True = jurisdiction can levy fines, issue cease-and-desist orders, or pursue legal action. Drives risk prioritization in compliance programs.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address of the primary regulatory contact at the governing body. Used for compliance correspondence and filing submission confirmations.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary regulatory contact or liaison officer at the governing body. Used for compliance correspondence, filing inquiries, and audit coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Official phone number of the primary regulatory contact at the governing body. Used for urgent compliance inquiries and audit coordination.']
  requires_local_registration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether entities operating within this jurisdiction must register with the governing body before conducting real estate activities (e.g., broker licensing with state DRE, REIT registration with SEC, contractor licensing with local authority). Drives onboarding compliance workflows.']
  sec_reporting_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) reporting obligations apply to real estate investment entities operating within this jurisdiction (e.g., publicly traded REITs). Activates SEC filing workflows including 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K submissions.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Yardi Voyager jurisdiction code, SAP S/4HANA company code). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer back to the source system for audit and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this jurisdiction record was sourced or last updated (e.g., \'yardi\', \'sap\', \'manual\'). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: yardi|mri|argus|salesforce|costar|sap|procore|building_engines|docusign|adp|manual — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  standard_filing_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Default number of calendar days from the end of the reporting period within which filings must be submitted to this jurisdiction. Used to auto-calculate filing due dates for compliance obligations assigned to this jurisdiction.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the state or province in which this jurisdiction operates (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Applicable for state-level and sub-state jurisdictions. Null for federal-level jurisdictions.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'Business code of the jurisdiction that has replaced or superseded this record when status is \'superseded\'. Enables compliance teams to redirect obligations and filings to the successor jurisdiction without data loss.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the jurisdiction (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Los_Angeles\', \'Europe/London\'). Used to calculate filing deadline timestamps accurately, particularly for jurisdictions with same-day or end-of-business-day submission requirements.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all regulatory jurisdictions applicable to the real estate portfolio — federal agencies (EPA, OSHA, HUD, SEC, CFPB), state regulatory bodies, county authorities, and municipal/city agencies. Each jurisdiction record captures the jurisdiction level (federal, state, county, city, special district), jurisdiction name, governing body, geographic boundaries, applicable regulatory frameworks, key contact information, and filing portal details. Serves as the authoritative lookup for multi-jurisdictional compliance mapping and is referenced by obligations, filings, permits, and violations to assign jurisdictional scope.'
}

Table reference.property_type {
  property_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the property classification taxonomy. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — exempt from per-role minimums; _canonical_skip_reason: This is a controlled vocabulary / hierarchical code-list serving as the enterprise-wide property classification taxonomy, not a first-class operational entity with its own lifecycle.]']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Property types are associated with a default building class designation (e.g., trophy office properties are Class A, older industrial properties are Class C). Linking to the building_class registry no']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Property types are characterized by their primary construction method (e.g., multifamily residential typically uses wood frame or concrete, industrial uses steel frame). Linking to the construction_ty']
  parent_property_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing foreign key to the parent node in the property classification hierarchy. NULL for Level 1 (asset class) root nodes. Enables traversal of the multi-level taxonomy (L1 asset class → L2 sub-category → L3 quality tier → L4 space use → L5 construction method → L6 amenity classification).']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Property types define the primary space use classification for the asset (e.g., office property type → office space use, retail property type → retail space use). Linking to the space_use_type registr']
  amenity_classification varchar [note: 'Level 6 amenity classification indicating whether amenities associated with this property type are provided at the building level (lobbies, fitness centers, conference rooms), unit level (in-unit washer/dryer, private terrace), or community level (shared outdoor spaces, pools, clubhouses). Used in property marketing, tenant scoring, and lease negotiation.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Top-level (Level 1) asset class classification for this property type node. Denormalized from the root ancestor for fast filtering in analytics and reporting. Aligned with CoStar and NAIOP asset class definitions.']
  bim_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether BIM (Building Information Modeling) digital asset documentation is applicable and required for development and construction projects of this property type. True for complex commercial, mixed-use, and large-scale residential development types managed in Procore. Drives BIM data collection and document control workflows.']
  cam_inclusion_rule varchar [note: 'Standard CAM (Common Area Maintenance) expense inclusion rule applicable to this property type, defining which operating expenses are passed through to tenants. NNN (Triple Net) passes taxes, insurance, and maintenance; NN (Double Net) passes taxes and insurance; Full-Service Gross includes all expenses in base rent. Drives lease structuring defaults in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Full-Service Gross|Modified Gross|NNN|NN|N|Absolute Net|N/A — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cap_rate_benchmark_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Market benchmark CAP Rate (Capitalization Rate) percentage for this property type, representing the expected ratio of Net Operating Income (NOI) to property value. Used as a default underwriting assumption in Argus Enterprise DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) models and investment advisory valuations. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.50 = 5.50%).']
  costar_property_type_code varchar [note: 'CoStar Suite\'s standardized property type code for this classification node. Enables direct mapping and data reconciliation between the enterprise taxonomy and CoStar market research data, comparable sales, and lease analytics. Critical for market benchmarking and CMA (Comparative Market Analysis) workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this property type taxonomy node was first created in the enterprise reference data repository. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) compliance for reference data governance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this property type classification node became effective and available for use in the enterprise taxonomy. Supports temporal validity tracking and ensures historical data integrity when taxonomy nodes are introduced or revised.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this property type classification node is no longer valid for new assignments. NULL indicates the node is currently active with no planned expiry. Supports taxonomy versioning and ensures deprecated nodes are not applied to new properties or leases after their retirement date.']
  far_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Floor Area Ratio (FAR) regulations are applicable to development projects of this property type. True for commercial and mixed-use development types subject to FAR zoning restrictions. Used in development feasibility analysis and Procore project planning.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Integer indicating the depth of this node within the six-level property classification hierarchy. Level 1 = Asset Class (commercial, residential, mixed-use, land, special-purpose); Level 2 = Sub-Category (office, retail, industrial, hospitality, single-family, multifamily, condo); Level 3 = Quality Tier / Building Class (Class A/B/C, trophy, institutional-grade, luxury, affordable); Level 4 = Space Use Type (office, retail, restaurant, medical, fitness, storage, parking); Level 5 = Construction Method (wood frame, steel, concrete, masonry, modular); Level 6 = Amenity Classification (building-level, unit-level, community).']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized full ancestry path from root to this node expressed as a delimited string (e.g., \'Commercial / Office / Class A / Office Space Use / Steel Frame / Building-Level Amenity\'). Enables efficient subtree queries and breadcrumb navigation in reporting tools without recursive CTE traversal.']
  hoa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties of this type are subject to HOA (Homeowners Association) governance, fees, and covenants. Applicable to condominium, single-family community, and certain mixed-use residential property types. Drives HOA fee tracking in property management and tenant disclosure requirements.']
  ibc_fire_resistance_type varchar [note: 'International Building Code (IBC) construction type classification indicating the fire resistance rating of the structural system. Types I and II are non-combustible; Types III, IV, and V include combustible elements. Drives insurance underwriting, OSHA compliance, and maintenance safety protocols. Aligned with IBC Chapter 6 construction type definitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type I-A|Type I-B|Type II-A|Type II-B|Type III-A|Type III-B|Type IV|Type V-A|Type V-B|N/A — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_cam_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges are applicable to leases for properties of this type. True for commercial property types (office, retail, industrial) where CAM reconciliation is standard. False for residential or gross lease property types where operating expenses are not passed through to tenants.']
  is_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this property type is classified as CRE (Commercial Real Estate) for regulatory, tax, and accounting purposes. Drives applicability of CMBS (Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities) financing, DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) underwriting, and commercial lease accounting under FASB ASC 842.']
  is_esg_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reporting metrics and sustainability certifications are applicable and tracked for properties of this type. True for property types subject to LEED, BREEAM, or ENERGY STAR certification programs. Drives ESG data collection requirements and SEC sustainability disclosure obligations for publicly traded REITs.']
  is_gla_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Gross Leasable Area (GLA) measurement methodology is applicable to properties of this type. True for retail and commercial property types where GLA is the standard measurement basis per BOMA and ICSC standards. False for property types measured by other methodologies (e.g., residential measured by GFA or NLA).']
  is_investment_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties of this type qualify as institutional investment-grade assets suitable for REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) portfolios, pension fund investment, and institutional capital allocation. Drives portfolio screening in Argus Enterprise and investment advisory workflows. Typically True for Class A and Trophy commercial assets.']
  is_leaf_node boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this taxonomy node is a terminal leaf node (True) with no child nodes, or an intermediate branch node (False). Leaf nodes represent the most granular assignable property type classifications. Used to enforce that only leaf-level types are assigned to individual properties and leases.']
  is_reit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties of this type qualify as REIT-eligible assets under IRS REIT qualification rules (IRC Section 856). Drives asset classification in financial consolidation (SAP S/4HANA) and SEC regulatory reporting for publicly traded REITs. Distinct from investment_grade as it reflects specific tax and regulatory eligibility.']
  is_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this property type is classified as residential for regulatory, tax, and accounting purposes. Drives applicability of HUD regulations, residential mortgage underwriting standards (PMI, ARM, FRM, DTI), and RMBS (Residential Mortgage-Backed Securities) eligibility. Distinct from asset_class for use in compliance filtering.']
  leed_certification_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification is applicable and pursued for properties of this type. Drives green building data collection, energy benchmarking, and ESG reporting workflows. Relevant for institutional-grade commercial and multifamily property types.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Standard area measurement methodology applicable to this property type. GLA (Gross Leasable Area) for retail; NLA (Net Leasable Area) for commercial office; GFA (Gross Floor Area) for development and planning; IPMS (International Property Measurement Standards) for cross-border portfolios. Drives how PSF (Per Square Foot) rent and valuation metrics are calculated.']
  naiop_type_code varchar [note: 'NAIOP (National Association for Industrial and Office Properties) standardized property type code for commercial real estate classification. Used for industry benchmarking, market research alignment, and CRE (Commercial Real Estate) portfolio reporting.']
  property_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this property type taxonomy node. \'active\' nodes are available for assignment to properties, leases, and valuations. \'deprecated\' nodes are retained for historical data integrity but no longer assignable. \'draft\' nodes are under review prior to enterprise publication.']
  reso_property_type_code varchar [note: 'RESO (Real Estate Standards Organization) standardized property type code for MLS (Multiple Listing Service) data exchange and industry interoperability. Enables syndication of property listings via MLS platforms and ensures compliance with RESO Data Dictionary standards.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer defining the preferred display sequence of this property type node within its sibling group at the same hierarchy level. Used to control the ordering of property type options in dropdown menus, reports, and dashboards across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Salesforce CRM integrations.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'Level 2 sub-category classification denormalized from the taxonomy hierarchy for analytical convenience. Examples: Office, Retail, Industrial, Hospitality, Single-Family, Multifamily, Condo, Mixed-Use Retail/Office. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Office|Retail|Industrial|Hospitality|Single-Family|Multifamily|Condo|Mixed-Use|Self-Storage|Data Center|Life Science|Senior Housing — promote to reference product]']
  ti_allowance_benchmark_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Market benchmark for Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance expressed in dollars Per Square Foot (PSF) for this property type. Used as a default starting point in lease negotiations and deal underwriting. Sourced from CoStar market data and internal brokerage transaction history. Supports deal pipeline analysis in Salesforce CRM.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this property type node within the enterprise taxonomy. Used as the stable business key for cross-system integration with Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, CoStar, and Argus Enterprise. Examples: COMM_OFF_A, RES_MF_LUX, IND_WRHSE.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the property classification node, including defining characteristics, typical use cases, and distinguishing criteria from adjacent nodes. Supports business user understanding and onboarding.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for this property classification node as used in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces across all operational systems. Examples: \'Class A Office\', \'Luxury Multifamily\', \'Triple Net Retail\'.']
  typical_lease_term_months varchar [note: 'Market-standard typical lease term in months for this property type. Used as a default assumption in lease underwriting, WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) calculations, and cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise. Examples: 12 months for residential, 60-120 months for commercial office, 36-60 months for retail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this property type taxonomy node was last modified in the enterprise reference data repository. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and reference data governance workflows.']
  zoning_compatibility_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of standard zoning designation codes compatible with this property type (e.g., \'C-1|C-2|M-1\' for light commercial/industrial). Supports regulatory compliance checks, development feasibility analysis, and GIS (Geographic Information System) integration. Aligned with local municipal zoning ordinances and FAR (Floor Area Ratio) regulations.']

  Note: 'Enterprise-wide property classification taxonomy organized as a self-referencing multi-level hierarchy. Level 1: asset class (commercial, residential, mixed-use, land, special-purpose). Level 2: sub-category (office, retail, industrial, hospitality, single-family, multifamily, condo). Level 3: quality tier / building class (Class A/B/C, trophy, institutional-grade, luxury, affordable). Level 4: space use type (office, retail, restaurant, medical, fitness, storage, parking). Level 5: construction method (wood frame, steel, concrete, masonry, modular). Level 6: amenity classification (building-level, unit-level, community). Each node carries applicable attributes: GLA applicability flags, zoning compatibility codes, fire resistance ratings (IBC Type I–V), ESG/LEED relevance indicators, CAM inclusion rules, TI allowance benchmarks, and measurement methodology (GLA/NLA/GFA). Aligned with RESO, CoStar, BOMA, and NAIOP standards. Referenced by property, lease, valuation, brokerage, development, and maintenance domains as the single source of truth for all property classification.'
}

Table reference.lease_type {
  lease_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lease_type']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: lease_type.area_measure_standard is a STRING describing the area measurement standard applicable to this lease type (e.g., BOMA 2017, IPMS, RICS). The uom table is the enterprise UOM registry that inc']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Lease types are associated with specific market segments (e.g., NNN leases are typical in Core commercial segments, gross leases in Value-Add residential segments). Linking to the market_segment regis']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Industry-standard abbreviation for the lease type used in market communications, CoStar listings, and brokerage reports (e.g., NNN, MG, FS, PCT, GL). Aligns with CoStar Suite and MLS syndication field conventions.']
  argus_lease_type_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding lease type identifier as used in Argus Enterprise cash flow and valuation models. Enables mapping between this reference record and Argus Enterprise lease structure assumptions for DCF modeling, CAP rate analysis, and portfolio reporting.']
  asc842_lease_classification varchar [note: 'Specifies the default lease classification under FASB ASC 842 for this lease type: \'operating\' (lessee recognizes right-of-use asset and operating lease liability), \'finance\' (lessee recognizes finance lease asset and liability with front-loaded expense), \'short_term_exemption\' (leases ≤12 months exempt from balance sheet recognition), or \'not_applicable\' (lessor-side or non-applicable structures). Drives accounting treatment in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager GL.']
  cam_reconciliation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether annual CAM reconciliation is required for this lease type (True). CAM reconciliation compares estimated CAM charges billed to tenants against actual CAM expenses incurred. Required for NNN and modified gross leases; not applicable for full gross leases. Activates CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager.']
  cam_responsibility varchar [note: 'Indicates which party bears responsibility for Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges under this lease type. \'landlord\' means the landlord absorbs all CAM costs; \'tenant\' means the tenant pays CAM directly (as in NNN); \'shared\' means costs are pro-rated; \'not_applicable\' for lease types where CAM is not a relevant concept (e.g., ground lease). Governs CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lease type reference record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls, and reference data governance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this lease type classification became or becomes effective for use in new lease agreements. Supports temporal validity tracking for reference data governance and ensures that historical lease records retain the correct classification at the time of execution.']
  escalation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates the standard rent escalation methodology associated with this lease type: fixed_step (predetermined dollar or percentage increases), cpi_indexed (Consumer Price Index-linked adjustments), market_reset (rent reset to prevailing market rate at intervals), none (flat rent for term), or hybrid (combination of methods). Governs rent schedule escalation logic in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise DCF models.']
  esg_reporting_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reporting obligations — such as green lease clauses, LEED/BREEAM compliance requirements, or energy consumption data sharing — are applicable to this lease type (True). Relevant for institutional and REIT portfolios subject to SEC ESG disclosure requirements and USGBC LEED certification programs.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this lease type classification ceases to be valid for use in new lease agreements (nullable for open-ended classifications). Supports reference data lifecycle management and ensures deprecated lease types are not applied to new leases after their retirement date.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard or regulatory body reference code for this lease type, such as RESO standard codes, NCREIF property type codes, or CoStar lease type identifiers. Enables cross-system interoperability and alignment with industry data exchange standards.']
  ifrs16_lease_classification varchar [note: 'Specifies the default lease classification under IASB IFRS 16 for this lease type: \'single_model\' (all leases recognized on balance sheet as right-of-use asset and lease liability), \'short_term_exemption\' (leases ≤12 months), \'low_value_exemption\' (underlying asset of low value), or \'not_applicable\'. Supports international financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA for entities reporting under IFRS.']
  insurance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines which party is responsible for property insurance premiums under this lease type. Relevant for NNN and modified gross lease structures where insurance cost allocation varies. Used in lease administration billing and CAM reconciliation processes.']
  is_ground_lease boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease type is a ground lease (True), where the tenant leases land only and typically constructs improvements. Ground leases have distinct legal, accounting, and investment characteristics including long terms (50–99 years), land-only rent, and reversion of improvements to the landlord. Relevant for REIT portfolio classification and Argus Enterprise valuation modeling.']
  is_short_term boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease type is classified as short-term (True), typically defined as a lease term of 12 months or less at commencement. Short-term leases qualify for the practical expedient exemption from balance sheet recognition under both FASB ASC 842 and IASB IFRS 16. Drives accounting treatment selection in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.']
  is_sublease boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease type represents a sublease arrangement (True), where the original lessee (sublandlord) leases all or part of the demised space to a subtenant. Subleases require distinct accounting treatment under FASB ASC 842 (intermediate lessor classification) and separate tracking in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  lease_category varchar [note: 'Broad property-use category to which this lease type applies — commercial, residential, industrial, mixed_use, or land. Determines which operational workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software are applicable and which regulatory frameworks (ASC 842, IFRS 16) govern accounting treatment.']
  lease_type_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lease type (e.g., NNN, GROSS, MOD_GROSS, PCT, GROUND, SUBLEASE, MTM). Used as the canonical lookup key in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and downstream analytical systems to join lease records to their classification.']
