  lease_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the lease type, including its defining characteristics, typical use cases, cost responsibility allocation, and distinguishing features from similar lease structures. Used in training materials, lease administration documentation, and business glossary entries.']
  lease_type_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the lease structure classification (e.g., \'Triple Net Lease\', \'Gross Lease\', \'Modified Gross Lease\', \'Percentage Lease\', \'Ground Lease\', \'Sublease\', \'Month-to-Month\'). Displayed in reporting, lease administration screens, and tenant-facing documents.']
  lease_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this lease type reference record: \'active\' (available for use in new lease agreements), \'inactive\' (temporarily disabled but retained for historical reference), or \'deprecated\' (superseded by a newer classification; retained for legacy lease records only). Controls availability in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software lease type selection lists.']
  loi_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Letter of Intent (LOI) is a standard prerequisite in the deal process for this lease type (True). LOIs are standard for commercial leases but typically not required for residential or month-to-month arrangements. Governs deal pipeline workflow in Salesforce CRM and DocuSign CLM.']
  mri_lease_type_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding lease type code as configured in MRI Software Lease Management module. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and the MRI source system code for data integration and reconciliation, particularly for residential and investment management portfolios.']
  noi_impact_classification varchar [note: 'Classifies how this lease type impacts Net Operating Income (NOI) calculation: \'gross_revenue\' (all income flows through gross revenue line before expense deductions), \'net_revenue\' (tenant pays expenses directly, reducing gross revenue line), or \'not_applicable\'. Used in Argus Enterprise NOI modeling and MRI Software investment reporting.']
  opex_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines which party is responsible for property operating expenditures (OpEx) beyond CAM — including insurance, management fees, and administrative costs — under this lease type. Critical for NOI calculation accuracy in Argus Enterprise and financial reporting under FASB ASC 842.']
  percentage_rent_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether percentage rent (a portion of tenant gross sales revenue above a breakpoint) is a feature of this lease type (True). Applicable primarily to retail percentage leases. Activates percentage rent calculation and reporting workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  renewal_option_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether renewal options are a standard feature of this lease type (True) or not applicable (False). For example, ground leases and long-term commercial leases typically include renewal options, while month-to-month leases do not. Informs lease renewal option tracking in Yardi Voyager and WALT/WALE calculations in Argus Enterprise.']
  rent_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the standard unit of measure for rent quotation under this lease type: PSF (Per Square Foot) annual, PSF monthly, flat monthly amount, flat annual amount, or percentage of sales. Aligns with industry PSF/SQF conventions and drives rent schedule generation in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise underwriting models.']
  rent_structure varchar [note: 'Defines the fundamental rent calculation methodology for this lease type: fixed (flat base rent), variable (indexed or market-reset), percentage (percentage of tenant sales revenue), hybrid (base + percentage), or ground_rent (land-only lease payment). Drives rent schedule generation logic in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.']
  security_deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is a standard requirement for this lease type (True). Security deposits are common in residential and commercial leases but may not apply to ground leases or certain institutional arrangements. Drives security deposit tracking and accounting in Yardi Voyager AR and MRI Software.']
  snda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Subordination Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement is a standard requirement for this lease type (True). SNDAs are typically required for commercial leases where the property is encumbered by a mortgage. Tracked in DocuSign CLM and Yardi Voyager lease document management.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of lease type options in user interface dropdowns, reports, and lease administration screens. Lower values appear first. Enables business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID-based sorting.']
  sublease_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subleasing is a standard permitted feature under this lease type (True). Relevant for distinguishing primary lease structures from sublease structures and for compliance with FASB ASC 842 sublease accounting requirements.']
  tax_responsibility varchar [note: 'Specifies which party is responsible for real estate property taxes under this lease type. In a Triple Net (NNN) lease, the tenant bears tax responsibility; in a gross lease, the landlord absorbs taxes. Drives tax pass-through billing logic in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  ti_allowance_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Tenant Improvement (TI) allowances are a standard feature of this lease type (True). TI allowances are common in commercial gross and modified gross leases but less common in NNN or ground leases. Drives TI allowance tracking and amortization in Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and FASB ASC 842 right-of-use asset calculations.']
  typical_term_max_months varchar [note: 'The typical maximum lease duration in months associated with this lease type (e.g., 12 months for short-term, 120 months for standard commercial, 1200 months for ground leases). Used in Argus Enterprise for WALT/WALE modeling, portfolio duration analytics, and investment underwriting.']
  typical_term_min_months varchar [note: 'The typical minimum lease duration in months associated with this lease type (e.g., 1 month for month-to-month, 12 months for short-term, 60 months for standard commercial NNN). Used in Argus Enterprise for WALT/WALE modeling and portfolio duration analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lease type reference record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls for reference data governance.']
  utility_responsibility varchar [note: 'Specifies which party is responsible for utility costs (electricity, water, gas, HVAC) under this lease type. Gross leases typically assign utilities to the landlord; NNN leases assign them to the tenant. Drives utility billing configuration in Building Engines and Yardi Voyager.']
  yardi_lease_type_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding lease type code as configured in Yardi Voyager Lease Administration module. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and the source system code for data integration, reconciliation, and ETL processes from Yardi Voyager.']

  Note: 'Standardized classification of lease structures used across commercial and residential leasing — NNN (Triple Net), gross lease, modified gross, percentage lease, ground lease, sublease, and short-term/month-to-month. Defines rent calculation methodology, CAM/OpEx responsibility allocation, and applicable accounting treatment under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Includes escalation method indicators (CPI, fixed-step, market reset) and renewal option classification. Serves as the authoritative lookup for lease administration across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and all operational domains requiring lease structure classification.'
}

Table reference.transaction_type {
  transaction_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each transaction type record in the controlled vocabulary. Primary key for the transaction_type reference product. [Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — _canonical_skip_reason: This is a controlled vocabulary / code-list entity; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  appraisal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal property appraisal or valuation is required for transactions of this type. Triggers valuation workflow initiation in Argus Enterprise and appraisal order management processes.']
  asset_class_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of real estate asset classes to which this transaction type applies (e.g., \'office,retail,industrial,multifamily,land,hotel,mixed-use\'). Governs which asset classes can be tagged with this transaction type in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  broker_commission_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether brokerage commissions are applicable to transactions of this type. Drives commission calculation and split workflows in Salesforce CRM and the brokerage domain commission products.']
  capex_or_opex varchar [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type are classified as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), both, or neither for financial reporting and budgeting purposes. Drives budget line categorization in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction type record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance reporting requirements under SOX.']
  due_diligence_period_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days allocated for due diligence for transactions of this type, as defined by industry convention or internal policy. Used as a default in deal timeline planning within Salesforce CRM and Argus Enterprise.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this transaction type record becomes active and available for use across all operational and analytical systems. Supports temporal validity management of the controlled vocabulary.']
  environmental_review_required boolean [note: 'Flags whether an environmental site assessment (Phase I/II ESA) is required for transactions of this type. Drives due diligence checklist items in Procore and compliance tracking in SAP S/4HANA.']
  esg_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type require ESG-related disclosures (e.g., energy performance, LEED/BREEAM certification status, carbon footprint). Supports ESG reporting obligations for publicly traded REITs and institutional investors.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this transaction type record is retired and no longer available for selection in new transactions. Null indicates no planned expiry. Supports controlled vocabulary lifecycle management.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding transaction type code used in external systems or industry standards (e.g., RESO transaction type codes, CoStar transaction type identifiers, MLS transaction type codes). Enables cross-system data reconciliation and integration mapping.']
  gl_transaction_class varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) transaction class code used to map this transaction type to the appropriate accounting treatment in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA (e.g., capital event, operating event, financing event). Ensures consistent financial statement classification.']
  is_1031_eligible boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type is eligible for a like-kind exchange under IRS Section 1031, allowing deferral of capital gains taxes. Critical for investment advisory and portfolio management workflows in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  is_distressed_asset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type involves a distressed asset scenario (e.g., foreclosure, REO sale, short sale, deed-in-lieu). Drives specialized due diligence checklists and risk flags in Salesforce CRM and Argus Enterprise.']
  is_fasb_asc842_applicable boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type triggers lease accounting recognition requirements under FASB ASC 842 (e.g., ground lease assignments, sale-leaseback transactions). Used by the finance domain for lease liability and right-of-use asset recognition in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_hud_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type require reporting to HUD (e.g., residential foreclosures, REO sales, FHA-related transactions). Drives compliance workflow routing in DocuSign CLM and SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_ifrs16_applicable boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type triggers lease accounting recognition requirements under IFRS 16 for entities reporting under international accounting standards. Supports multi-standard financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type involves the formation or dissolution of a joint venture (JV) entity. Triggers JV-specific legal entity setup workflows in SAP S/4HANA and investment structuring processes in MRI Software.']
  is_portfolio_trade boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type represents a bulk portfolio trade involving multiple properties transacted as a single deal. Enables portfolio-level aggregation and reporting in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software investment management modules.']
  is_sec_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type must be reported to the SEC under applicable regulations (e.g., material acquisitions or dispositions by publicly traded REITs under Form 8-K or 10-K). Drives automated regulatory reporting flags in SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_taxable_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type constitutes a taxable event under applicable U.S. federal tax law (e.g., a standard acquisition/disposition is taxable; a 1031 exchange defers capital gains tax). Used by the finance domain for tax provision calculations in SAP S/4HANA.']
  loi_required boolean [note: 'Flags whether a Letter of Intent (LOI) is a required precursor document for transactions of this type before proceeding to a purchase and sale agreement. Drives deal pipeline stage gating in Salesforce CRM.']
  mls_reportable boolean [note: 'Flags whether transactions of this type are required or eligible to be reported to the Multiple Listing Service (MLS) and syndicated via CoStar Suite. Drives MLS syndication workflow in the brokerage domain.']
  mri_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding transaction type code as defined in MRI Software, used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the enterprise data lakehouse and the MRI source system.']
  property_sector varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type applies to Commercial Real Estate (CRE), residential properties, both sectors, or is sector-agnostic. Drives sector-specific workflow routing in Salesforce CRM and Yardi Voyager.']
  reit_qualifying_income boolean [note: 'Flags whether income or proceeds from transactions of this type qualify as REIT qualifying income under IRS Section 856. Critical for REIT compliance monitoring and FFO/AFFO calculations in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  required_doc_checklist varchar [note: 'Structured list or reference identifier for the documentation checklist required for transactions of this type (e.g., purchase agreement, title commitment, survey, environmental report, estoppel certificates, SNDA, LOI). Drives document management workflows in DocuSign CLM.']
  snda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Subordination, Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement is required for transactions of this type (e.g., acquisitions of leased properties). Drives document checklist generation in DocuSign CLM.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of transaction types in dropdown lists, reports, and user interfaces across Salesforce CRM, Yardi Voyager, and MRI Software. Lower values appear first.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the parent category providing finer-grained segmentation (e.g., within \'disposition\': voluntary sale, forced sale, REO, short sale; within \'exchange\': 1031 like-kind, 721 UPREIT). Supports detailed analytics in Argus Enterprise and CoStar Suite.']
  title_insurance_required boolean [note: 'Flags whether title insurance (ALTA policy) is required for transactions of this type. Drives documentation checklist requirements in DocuSign CLM and closing process workflows.']
  transaction_type_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the transaction type into a major business category. Supports portfolio-level reporting and analytics segmentation across the transaction and brokerage domains. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition|disposition|financing|leasing|development|investment|distressed|exchange — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_type_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the transaction type record within the controlled vocabulary. \'active\' records are available for use across all domains; \'deprecated\' records are retained for historical data integrity but no longer selectable for new transactions.']
  transfer_tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real estate transfer taxes (deed taxes, documentary stamp taxes) are applicable to transactions of this type. Used by the finance domain to trigger transfer tax calculations and GL entries in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the transaction type (e.g., ACQ, DISP, 1031_EXCH, REO_SALE, FORECLOSURE, SHORT_SALE, GND_LSE_ASSIGN, JV_FORM, PORT_TRADE). Used as the stable business key across all operational systems including Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the transaction type, including its business purpose, typical use cases, and distinguishing characteristics. Used for business glossary documentation, onboarding materials, and data catalog entries.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable, business-facing name of the transaction type (e.g., \'Acquisition\', \'Disposition\', \'1031 Exchange\', \'REO Sale\', \'Foreclosure Sale\', \'Short Sale\', \'Ground Lease Assignment\', \'Joint Venture Formation\', \'Portfolio Trade\'). Displayed in Salesforce CRM deal pipelines, Argus Enterprise reports, and brokerage dashboards.']
  typical_closing_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days from executed purchase and sale agreement to closing for transactions of this type. Used for deal timeline forecasting and pipeline velocity reporting in Salesforce CRM and Argus Enterprise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction type record was most recently modified in the enterprise data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and controlled vocabulary governance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version counter for this transaction type record, incremented each time the record is updated. Supports audit trail and change management for the controlled vocabulary.']
  yardi_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding transaction type code as defined in Yardi Voyager, used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the enterprise data lakehouse and the Yardi source system.']

  Note: 'Controlled vocabulary for all real estate transaction categories — acquisition, disposition, 1031 exchange, REO sale, foreclosure, short sale, ground lease assignment, joint venture formation, and portfolio trade. Includes transaction sub-types, applicable regulatory reporting flags (SEC, HUD), and required documentation checklists per transaction category. Used by the transaction domain and brokerage domain as the authoritative classification source.'
}

Table reference.geographic_hierarchy {
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the enterprise geographic hierarchy. Serves as the primary key for all geographic reference lookups across the real estate portfolio. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP/MASTER_RESOURCE — this is a controlled vocabulary / spatial reference node; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt, but MASTER_RESOURCE minimums are applied given the node carries rich business metadata.]']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Every geographic hierarchy node belongs to a country. Linking to the ISO 3166-1 country registry normalizes the country reference and enables joining to country-level attributes (sovereignty type, pro']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Geographic hierarchy nodes (countries, regions, markets) operate in a primary currency used for financial reporting and benchmarking within that geography. Linking to the ISO 4217 currency registry en']
  parent_node_geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent node in the geographic hierarchy (e.g., a submarket node\'s parent is its MSA node). Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for portfolio roll-up and drill-down analytics.']
  area_sqm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total land area of this geographic node measured in square meters (SQM). Used for density analysis, FAR calculations, and market size benchmarking in portfolio analytics.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city or municipality for this geographic node. Used in property address standardization, lease administration in Yardi Voyager, and MLS syndication via Salesforce CRM.']
  costar_submarket_code varchar [note: 'CoStar-assigned submarket identifier code used for market research, comparable lease and sales data alignment, and submarket-level vacancy and absorption analytics. Primary integration key with CoStar Suite.']
  costar_submarket_name varchar [note: 'CoStar-defined submarket name (e.g., \'Midtown Manhattan\', \'Beverly Hills/West Hollywood\'). Used for market benchmarking, CMA preparation, and BPO analysis aligned with CoStar Suite data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this geographic node definition became effective and valid for use in portfolio assignments, lease administration, and regulatory reporting. Supports temporal validity tracking for boundary changes (e.g., MSA redefinitions by OMB).']
  enterprise_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this geographic node is designated as a state or local enterprise zone, qualifying properties for tax incentives, development grants, or expedited permitting. Used in development feasibility analysis and CAPEX planning.']
  esg_climate_risk_zone varchar [note: 'ESG climate risk classification for this geographic node based on exposure to physical climate risks (flood, wildfire, hurricane, sea-level rise). Used for ESG portfolio reporting, LEED/BREEAM site selection, and insurance underwriting support.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this geographic node definition expires or is superseded (nullable for open-ended nodes). Used to manage OMB MSA boundary revisions, CoStar submarket redefinitions, and historical portfolio reporting continuity.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The identifier used for this geographic node in the primary source system (e.g., CoStar market ID, Yardi region code, MRI geographic code). Enables bi-directional reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and operational systems.']
  fips_code varchar [note: 'FIPS geographic code assigned by the U.S. Census Bureau for this node (state, county, or place level). Used for regulatory reporting, HUD compliance, and integration with government datasets.']
  flood_zone_designation varchar [note: 'FEMA National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) flood zone designation for this geographic node (e.g., \'AE\', \'X\', \'VE\'). Required for property insurance compliance, lender due diligence, and LTV risk assessment.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Integer depth level of this node within the hierarchy tree, where 1 = country (root). Used to optimize hierarchy traversal queries and enforce level-specific business rules in portfolio analytics.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level of this node within the enterprise geographic hierarchy. Defines the spatial granularity: country → region → state/province → Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) → submarket → city → zip/postal code → micro-location. Drives portfolio roll-up logic and GIS-based analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: country|region|state_province|msa|submarket|city|zip_postal_code|micro_location — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full materialized path string representing the node\'s ancestry from root to current node (e.g., \'USA/Northeast/New York/NYC-MSA/Manhattan/Midtown\'). Enables efficient hierarchy traversal and subtree queries without recursive joins.']
  hud_area_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) area code designating this geographic node for federal housing program eligibility, fair market rent (FMR) determination, and affordable housing compliance reporting.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'WGS 84 decimal-degree latitude coordinate of the geographic node\'s centroid. Enables GIS-based portfolio mapping, spatial proximity analysis, and integration with GIS platforms for market visualization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'WGS 84 decimal-degree longitude coordinate of the geographic node\'s centroid. Enables GIS-based portfolio mapping, drive-time analysis, and spatial clustering for investment advisory services.']
  market_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the market\'s investment grade and liquidity profile: primary (top-tier gateway markets, e.g., NYC, LA, Chicago), secondary (mid-size markets with strong fundamentals), or tertiary (smaller markets with higher risk/return profiles). Drives investment strategy, CAP rate benchmarking, and portfolio allocation decisions in Argus Enterprise.']
  median_household_income decimal(14,2) [note: 'Median annual household income for this geographic node in the node\'s applicable currency. Used for residential affordability analysis, HUD area median income (AMI) benchmarking, and tenant demand modeling.']
  msa_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Office of Management and Budget (OMB) five-digit FIPS code identifying the Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA). Critical for market segmentation, CoStar submarket alignment, HUD program eligibility, and CRE investment analysis.']
  msa_name varchar [note: 'Official OMB name of the Metropolitan Statistical Area (e.g., \'New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ-PA MSA\'). Used in Argus Enterprise cash flow models, investment advisory reports, and portfolio benchmarking.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this geographic node (e.g., \'MSA-NYC\', \'STATE-CA\', \'ZIP-90210\', \'SUBMARKET-MIDTOWN\'). Used as the business key for cross-system integration with CoStar, Yardi, and MRI. Aligns with CoStar submarket codes and HUD geographic designations.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the geographic node (e.g., \'New York-Newark-Jersey City MSA\', \'California\', \'Midtown Manhattan Submarket\'). Used in portfolio reports, dashboards, and regulatory filings.']
  node_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used short name for the geographic node (e.g., \'NYC MSA\', \'CA\', \'Midtown\'). Used in space-constrained UI labels, map overlays, and condensed reporting.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this geographic hierarchy node. Active nodes are used in portfolio assignments and reporting. Deprecated nodes are retained for historical data integrity. Pending review nodes are under validation before activation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for data stewards to record boundary change history, OMB reclassification notes, CoStar submarket split/merge events, or other contextual information relevant to this geographic node\'s definition and usage.']
  opportunity_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this geographic node falls within a federally designated Opportunity Zone under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017. Drives tax-advantaged investment structuring, REIT portfolio strategy, and investor reporting.']
  population varchar [note: 'Most recent census or estimated population count for this geographic node. Used as a demand driver in residential market analysis, HUD program sizing, and investment advisory demographic modeling.']
  population_year varchar [note: 'The reference year for the population count recorded in this node (e.g., 2020 for the decennial census). Ensures temporal accuracy in demographic-driven investment analysis.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary regulatory jurisdiction governing real estate activities in this geographic node (e.g., state real estate commission code, municipal zoning authority code). Used for license compliance tracking, NAR/state board reporting, and OSHA/EPA regulatory mapping.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this geographic node was sourced or last updated (e.g., \'costar\' for CoStar Suite, \'hud\' for HUD geographic designations, \'census\' for U.S. Census Bureau). Supports data lineage and master data governance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: costar|yardi|mri|hud|census|manual|argus — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the state or province (e.g., \'CA\', \'NY\', \'TX\'). Used for state-level regulatory compliance, tax jurisdiction mapping, and HUD reporting.']
  state_province_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the state or province (e.g., \'California\', \'New York\'). Used in lease administration, regulatory filings, and portfolio reporting within Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction applicable to properties in this geographic node. Used for property tax assessment, transfer tax calculation, and SAP S/4HANA tax configuration for financial consolidation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this geographic node (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Used for scheduling property inspections, lease event notifications, and coordinating maintenance work orders across multi-market portfolios.']
  unemployment_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recent unemployment rate (as a percentage) for this geographic node. Used as a macroeconomic indicator in CRE investment analysis, NOI forecasting, and portfolio risk assessment in Argus Enterprise.']
  zip_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code (USA) or postal code (international) for this geographic node. Used for property address validation, HUD program area mapping, tax jurisdiction assignment, and GIS-based portfolio analysis.']

  Note: 'Enterprise geographic hierarchy defining the spatial organization of the real estate portfolio — country, region, state/province, metropolitan statistical area (MSA), submarket, city, zip/postal code, and micro-location. Supports GIS-based portfolio analysis, market segmentation, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping. Aligns with CoStar submarket definitions and HUD geographic designations. Each node carries market tier classification (primary, secondary, tertiary) and applicable regulatory jurisdiction codes.'
}

Table reference.market_segment {
  market_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the market segment record. Primary key for the market_segment reference product. Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — exempt from per-role minimums; this entity is a controlled vocabulary / code-set table providing standardized investment strategy and market segment classifications across the enterprise. _canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — small enumeration of standardized market segment codes; no per-role minimum categories apply.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: market_segment tracks financial benchmarks denominated in currency — target_irr_min/max_pct, target_cap_rate_min/max_pct, typical_asset_size_min/max_usd, equity_multiple_min/max. Adding currency_code_']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business data steward responsible for maintaining the accuracy and completeness of this market segment record. Supports data governance accountability and reference data change management processes.']
  development_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ground-up development or major redevelopment projects are permitted within this market segment\'s investment mandate (True = allowed, False = not allowed). Opportunistic segments typically allow development; core segments do not.']
  distressed_asset_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether distressed, REO (Real Estate Owned), or impaired assets are eligible for acquisition under this market segment (True = eligible). Used in deal sourcing filters and investment committee approval workflows.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this market segment definition became effective and available for use in deal classification and portfolio reporting. Supports point-in-time reporting and historical segment definition tracking.']
  equity_multiple_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum target equity multiple for investments in this market segment (e.g., 2.50x for opportunistic). Defines the upper bound of expected return multiples used in fund performance benchmarking and investor reporting.']
  equity_multiple_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum target equity multiple (total distributions / total equity invested) for investments in this market segment (e.g., 1.50x for core-plus). Used alongside IRR in investor return reporting and deal underwriting benchmarks.']
  esg_classification varchar [note: 'ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) classification for the market segment, indicating the level of ESG integration expected in investments. ESG Core = baseline compliance; ESG Enhanced = active ESG improvement targets; ESG Impact = measurable social/environmental outcomes required. Supports LEED, BREEAM, and GRESB reporting.']
  exit_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary exit strategy indicator for investments in this market segment. Defines the expected disposition mechanism: outright sale, recapitalization, refinance and hold, IPO/REIT listing, long-term hold, or portfolio/bulk sale. Used in investment advisory, asset management planning, and investor reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this market segment definition was retired or superseded. Null if the segment is currently active. Enables historical point-in-time queries and audit trail for segment reclassification events.']
  external_benchmark_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary external performance benchmark associated with this market segment for investor reporting (e.g., \'NCREIF Property Index\', \'NCREIF ODCE\', \'MSCI Real Estate\', \'INREV All Vehicles Index\'). Used in Argus Enterprise portfolio reporting and investor presentations.']
  geographic_focus varchar [note: 'Describes the geographic investment focus associated with this market segment (e.g., \'Gateway markets only\', \'Secondary and tertiary markets\', \'National diversified\', \'Sun Belt metros\'). Used in deal sourcing strategy, CoStar market research filters, and portfolio geographic allocation reporting.']
  hold_period_max_years decimal(4,1) [note: 'Maximum typical investment hold period in years for assets classified under this market segment (e.g., 10.0 years for core). Used in fund lifecycle planning, Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) horizon setting, and exit strategy modeling in Argus Enterprise.']
  hold_period_min_years decimal(4,1) [note: 'Minimum typical investment hold period in years for assets classified under this market segment (e.g., 3.0 years for value-add). Used in Argus Enterprise DCF modeling, Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) analysis, and investor fund lifecycle planning.']
  income_return_focus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market segment is primarily income-return focused (True) versus capital appreciation focused (False). Core and core-plus segments are typically income-focused; opportunistic and value-add are appreciation-focused. Used in portfolio allocation and investor mandate classification.']
  inrev_strategy_code varchar [note: 'INREV (European Association for Investors in Non-Listed Real Estate Vehicles) strategy classification code for cross-border fund reporting alignment. Used for European investor mandates and INREV benchmark submissions.']
  investment_strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investment strategy associated with this market segment per industry-standard NCREIF/INREV taxonomy. Core = stabilized, low-risk; Core-Plus = modest value creation; Value-Add = significant repositioning; Opportunistic = high-risk/high-return development or turnaround; Distressed = below-market acquisition of impaired assets. Null for residential segments where strategy type is not applicable.']
  investor_type_target varchar [note: 'Describes the target investor profile for this market segment (e.g., \'Institutional pension funds\', \'High-net-worth individuals\', \'Family offices\', \'Sovereign wealth funds\'). Used in investor relations, fund marketing, and Salesforce CRM deal pipeline classification.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market segment is currently active and available for use in deal classification, portfolio reporting, and investment mandate assignment (True = active, False = retired/inactive). Inactive segments are retained for historical reporting continuity.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this market segment definition was last reviewed and validated by the data steward or investment committee. Supports reference data governance, periodic review cycles, and audit trail requirements.']
  leverage_strategy varchar [note: 'Qualitative leverage strategy descriptor for the market segment. Unlevered = all-equity; Low = LTV < 40%; Moderate = LTV 40–60%; High = LTV > 60%. Complements the target_ltv_max_pct field for narrative reporting and investor mandate screening.']
  ncreif_benchmark_code varchar [note: 'NCREIF Property Index benchmark code associated with this market segment for performance comparison (e.g., NPI, ODCE). Used in portfolio performance reporting, investor presentations, and Argus Enterprise benchmark analysis.']
  occupancy_threshold_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable physical occupancy percentage for assets classified under this market segment at acquisition or during the hold period (e.g., 85.00 for 85%). Used in deal screening, underwriting assumptions, and Yardi Voyager lease administration reporting.']
  property_type_focus varchar [note: 'Describes the primary property type(s) targeted by this market segment (e.g., \'Office, Retail, Industrial\', \'Multifamily\', \'Mixed-Use\'). Used in deal sourcing filters, CoStar comparable analysis, and portfolio sector allocation reporting. Free-text to accommodate multi-type segments.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific regulatory or compliance considerations applicable to this market segment (e.g., HUD affordability restrictions for affordable housing, REIT distribution requirements, SEC reporting obligations for publicly traded vehicles, LIHTC compliance for tax credit properties).']
  residential_tier varchar [note: 'Residential market tier classification for residential segments. Affordable = income-restricted housing (HUD AMI thresholds); Workforce = moderate-income households; Market-Rate = unsubsidized standard rental/for-sale; Luxury = premium amenity-rich product. Null for commercial segments.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk descriptor for the market segment, reflecting expected volatility, leverage sensitivity, and execution complexity. Used in investor mandate screening, portfolio risk reporting, and deal approval workflows. Aligns with INREV risk classification framework.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'Broad asset class category to which this market segment belongs. Distinguishes commercial investment strategies (core, core-plus, value-add, opportunistic, distressed) from residential strategies (affordable, workforce, market-rate, luxury) and other asset classes. Drives portfolio allocation reporting and investor mandate compliance.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-unique alphanumeric code identifying the market segment (e.g., CORE, CORE_PLUS, VALUE_ADD, OPPORTUNISTIC, DISTRESSED, AFFORDABLE, WORKFORCE, MKT_RATE, LUXURY). Used as the stable business key referenced by investment, valuation, brokerage, and transaction domains.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the market segment, including its investment thesis, risk characteristics, typical asset profile, and use cases. Used in investor reporting, investment committee materials, and data governance documentation.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the market segment (e.g., \'Core\', \'Core-Plus\', \'Value-Add\', \'Opportunistic\', \'Distressed\', \'Affordable Housing\', \'Workforce Housing\', \'Market-Rate\', \'Luxury\'). Displayed in investor reports, portfolio dashboards, and deal classification screens.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer value controlling the display order of market segments in dropdown lists, reports, and dashboards. Lower values appear first. Enables business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID-based sorting.']
  target_cap_rate_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) percentage for acquisitions in this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 7.00 for 7%). Defines the upper bound of the CAP rate range used in deal screening and portfolio benchmarking.']
  target_cap_rate_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) percentage for acquisitions in this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 4.50 for 4.5%). CAP Rate = Net Operating Income (NOI) / Property Value. Used in Argus Enterprise underwriting models and CoStar comparable analysis.']
  target_dscr_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) for assets in this market segment (e.g., 1.25). DSCR = Net Operating Income (NOI) / Total Debt Service. Used in lender covenant compliance, debt instrument structuring, and portfolio risk monitoring.']
  target_irr_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) percentage for investments classified under this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Defines the upper bound of the IRR range used in deal underwriting and investor mandate compliance checks.']
  target_irr_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum target unlevered or levered Internal Rate of Return (IRR) percentage for investments classified under this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 8.00 for 8%). Defines the lower bound of the IRR range used in deal underwriting, Argus Enterprise cash flow models, and investor reporting against NCREIF benchmarks.']
  target_ltv_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum target Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio percentage for debt financing on assets in this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 65.00 for 65%). Defines the leverage ceiling used in debt structuring, CMBS underwriting, and DSCR compliance monitoring.']
  typical_asset_size_max_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum typical asset value in USD for investments classified under this market segment (e.g., 500,000,000 for $500M). Defines the upper bound of deal size for mandate compliance and portfolio concentration limits.']
  typical_asset_size_min_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum typical asset value in USD for investments classified under this market segment (e.g., 10,000,000 for $10M). Used in deal sourcing filters, portfolio allocation guidelines, and investment mandate compliance in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  value_creation_approach varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary value creation approach for this market segment (e.g., \'Stabilized income with minimal capital expenditure\', \'Lease-up of vacant space with light renovation\', \'Major repositioning, redevelopment, or change of use\'). Used in investment committee presentations and investor reporting.']

  Note: 'Standardized market segment and investment strategy classifications used for portfolio analysis, deal sourcing, and performance benchmarking. Commercial segments: core, core-plus, value-add, opportunistic, and distressed. Residential segments: affordable, workforce, market-rate, and luxury. Each segment defines risk profile descriptors, target IRR and CAP rate ranges, typical hold period benchmarks, leverage parameters, and exit strategy indicators. Referenced by investment, valuation, brokerage, and transaction domains for deal classification, portfolio allocation analysis, and investor reporting against NCREIF and INREV benchmarks.'
}

Table reference.zoning_code {
  zoning_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each zoning code record in the enterprise reference registry. Primary key. [Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — _canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a controlled vocabulary / code-list table providing standardized zoning designations; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: zoning_code tracks dimensional standards with hardcoded imperial units: min_lot_size_sqf (square feet), height_limit_ft (feet), front_setback_ft, rear_setback_ft, side_setback_ft, min_lot_width_ft. Ad']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Zoning codes are municipal/county designations that belong to a specific country\'s legal jurisdiction. Normalizing country_code STRING to a FK against the ISO 3166-1 country registry ensures referenti']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Zoning codes are enacted by specific municipalities or counties that exist as nodes in the geographic hierarchy. Linking zoning_code to geographic_hierarchy enables spatial queries (e.g., all zoning c']
  affordable_housing_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zoning designation includes an inclusionary zoning requirement mandating a percentage of residential units be designated as affordable housing. True = affordable housing set-aside required; False = no such requirement. Relevant for HUD compliance and residential development planning.']
  conditional_uses varchar [note: 'Narrative description of land uses that may be permitted under this zoning designation subject to approval of a Conditional Use Permit (CUP) by the governing authority. Relevant for development feasibility and entitlement risk assessment.']
  density_class varchar [note: 'Density classification associated with the zoning designation, indicating the intensity of permitted land use. Used in development feasibility analysis and portfolio density reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this zoning designation became legally effective in the governing jurisdiction. Used for historical zoning analysis and regulatory compliance verification.']
  environmental_overlay_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zoning designation includes or is subject to an environmental overlay or restriction (e.g., flood zone, wetlands buffer, brownfield remediation zone, EPA-designated area). True = environmental overlay applies; False = no environmental overlay.']
  esg_designation varchar [note: 'ESG-related zoning designation or incentive associated with this zoning code, indicating whether the jurisdiction mandates or incentivizes green building standards such as LEED or BREEAM for developments under this designation. Supports ESG portfolio reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this zoning designation is scheduled to expire or be superseded, if applicable (e.g., temporary zoning designations, sunset provisions). Null for permanent designations.']
  far_max decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Floor Area Ratio (FAR) permitted under this zoning designation, expressed as the ratio of total building floor area to the area of the parcel. A FAR of 2.0 means total floor area may be up to twice the lot area. Critical input for development feasibility analysis in Argus Enterprise and CoStar.']
  far_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum Floor Area Ratio (FAR) required under this zoning designation, where applicable. Some jurisdictions mandate minimum development intensity to prevent underutilization of zoned land.']
  front_setback_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum required distance in feet between the front property line and the nearest building face, as mandated by this zoning designation. Used in site planning and architectural design.']
  gis_layer_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL for the municipal Geographic Information System (GIS) layer or dataset from which this zoning designation is sourced. Enables spatial data integration with CoStar Suite and enterprise GIS platforms.']
  height_limit_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted building height in feet as specified by the zoning designation. Used in development feasibility analysis, BIM planning, and construction oversight in Procore.']
  height_limit_stories varchar [note: 'Maximum permitted number of building stories as specified by the zoning designation, where the jurisdiction expresses height restrictions in stories rather than feet.']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Name of the municipal or county authority that issued and administers this zoning designation (e.g., \'City of Los Angeles\', \'Cook County\', \'City of New York\'). Enables multi-jurisdiction portfolio management.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Type of governing authority that administers this zoning code, distinguishing between city, county, township, or special district jurisdictions.']
  last_amended_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal amendment to this zoning designation by the governing jurisdiction. Tracks regulatory change history for compliance and portfolio impact analysis.']
  lot_coverage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of the lot area that may be covered by buildings and impervious structures under this zoning designation. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 60.00 = 60%). Used in site planning and development feasibility.']
  min_lot_size_sqf decimal(14,2) [note: 'Minimum parcel area in square feet (SQF) required for a lot to be developed under this zoning designation. Critical for subdivision analysis and land acquisition due diligence.']
  min_lot_width_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum required lot frontage width in feet as specified by the zoning designation. Affects parcel subdivision feasibility and site design.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes maintained by the enterprise data steward regarding this zoning code record, including data quality observations, pending updates, or cross-reference information not captured in structured fields.']
  ordinance_reference varchar [note: 'Official ordinance number, resolution number, or legislative reference that established or most recently amended this zoning designation (e.g., \'Ordinance No. 2023-045\'). Supports regulatory compliance documentation and audit trails.']
  overlay_district_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zoning code represents a special overlay district that applies supplemental regulations on top of a base zoning designation (e.g., historic preservation overlay, flood plain overlay, transit corridor overlay). True = overlay district; False = base zoning designation.']
  parking_ratio_min decimal(8,4) [note: 'Minimum required off-street parking spaces per unit of use (e.g., spaces per 1,000 SQF of GLA, or spaces per residential unit) as mandated by this zoning designation. Used in development feasibility and site planning.']
  parking_ratio_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure denominator for the minimum parking ratio (e.g., \'per_1000_sqf\' for commercial uses, \'per_unit\' for residential uses). Provides context for interpreting the parking_ratio_min value.']
  permitted_uses varchar [note: 'Narrative or semi-structured description of land uses permitted by right under this zoning designation without requiring a conditional use permit or variance (e.g., \'Single-family detached dwellings, accessory structures, home occupations\'). Sourced from municipal zoning ordinance text.']
  prohibited_uses varchar [note: 'Narrative description of land uses explicitly prohibited under this zoning designation. Critical for acquisition due diligence and regulatory compliance screening.']
  rear_setback_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum required distance in feet between the rear property line and the nearest building face, as mandated by this zoning designation.']
  rezoning_pending_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal rezoning application or amendment is currently pending with the governing jurisdiction for this zoning designation. True = active rezoning proceeding; False = no pending rezoning. Relevant for portfolio risk monitoring.']
  side_setback_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum required distance in feet between the side property line and the nearest building face, as mandated by this zoning designation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this zoning code record was sourced or last updated (e.g., \'costar\' for CoStar Suite, \'municipal_gis\' for direct municipal GIS feed, \'yardi\' for Yardi Voyager). Supports data lineage and master data management.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state abbreviation (ISO 3166-2:US) or equivalent provincial/regional code for the jurisdiction in which this zoning designation applies. Supports geographic filtering and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer granularity within the primary category (e.g., \'Single-Family Low Density\', \'Neighborhood Commercial\', \'Heavy Industrial\', \'Transit-Oriented Mixed-Use\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_family_low_density|single_family_medium_density|multifamily|neighborhood_commercial|general_commercial|regional_commercial|light_industrial|heavy_industrial|mixed_use_residential|mixed_use_commercial|transit_oriented|agricultural_general|agricultural_conservation — promote to reference product]']
  variance_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the governing jurisdiction permits applications for variances from the standard development regulations associated with this zoning designation. True = variances may be applied for; False = no variance process available.']
  variance_history_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes summarizing the history of variances granted or denied for properties under this zoning designation. Supports development feasibility analysis and entitlement risk assessment.']
  zoning_code_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping the zoning code into a primary land-use category. Drives portfolio segmentation, development feasibility screening, and regulatory compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|mixed_use|agricultural|special_overlay|planned_development|open_space — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  zoning_code_code varchar [note: 'Official alphanumeric zoning designation assigned by the governing municipality or county (e.g., R1, C2, I1, MU, A, PD). This is the externally-recognized code used in regulatory filings, title reports, and GIS databases.']
  zoning_code_description varchar [note: 'Extended narrative description of the zoning designation, including its planning intent, applicable land use policies, and any special conditions or notes sourced from the municipal zoning ordinance. Provides context for business users and analysts.']
  zoning_code_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the zoning designation as defined by the issuing jurisdiction (e.g., \'Single-Family Residential\', \'General Commercial\', \'Light Industrial\', \'Mixed-Use Corridor\').']
  zoning_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the zoning code record within the enterprise reference registry. \'active\' indicates the code is currently in force; \'superseded\' indicates it has been replaced by a newer designation; \'pending_amendment\' indicates a formal rezoning or amendment is in progress.']

  Note: 'Authoritative registry of municipal and county zoning designations applicable to portfolio properties — residential (R1–R5), commercial (C1–C3), industrial (I1–I2), mixed-use (MU), agricultural (A), and special overlay districts. Captures FAR limits, height restrictions, permitted uses, conditional use requirements, and variance history. Sourced from municipal GIS databases and maintained for regulatory compliance and development feasibility analysis.'
}

Table reference.currency_code {
  currency_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each currency code record in the enterprise reference registry. Primary key for the currency_code reference product. [Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — exempt from per-role minimum-category requirements; this is a controlled vocabulary / ISO code-list table providing standardized currency identifiers for multi-currency financial operations across the real estate portfolio.]']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Currencies are issued by and associated with countries (ISO 4217 maps to ISO 3166-1). Linking currency_code to the country registry enables joining to country-level attributes (VAT applicability, sanc']
  conversion_factor decimal(28,10) [note: 'Fixed conversion factor applied when this currency was replaced by a successor currency (e.g., 1 EUR = 1.95583 DEM). Applicable only to discontinued currencies with a defined successor. Used for historical financial restatement and legacy portfolio valuation in Argus Enterprise and SAP S/4HANA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this currency code record was first created in the enterprise reference registry. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 404 and data governance lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  currency_code_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter alphabetic currency code as defined by ISO 4217 (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD, JPY). Used as the business-facing identifier across all financial systems including Yardi Voyager AP/AR, SAP S/4HANA GL, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise for multi-currency portfolio operations.']
  currency_code_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the currency as defined by ISO 4217 (e.g., United States Dollar, Euro, British Pound Sterling). Displayed in financial reports, Argus Enterprise valuation models, and investor-facing portfolio reporting for clarity.']
  currency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the currency by its underlying nature. Fiat currencies are government-issued legal tender (the vast majority of ISO 4217 codes). SDR (Special Drawing Rights) is the IMF composite currency. Precious metal codes (XAU for gold, XAG for silver) and crypto codes may appear in investment portfolios or alternative asset valuations.']
  decimal_separator varchar [note: 'Character used as the decimal point separator when displaying monetary amounts in this currency (e.g., period for USD/GBP, comma for EUR in many European countries). Critical for correct parsing and display of financial figures in multi-locale real estate reporting environments.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which this currency code was officially discontinued or superseded per ISO 4217 (e.g., legacy national currencies replaced by EUR). Null for currently active currencies. Retained for historical lease and transaction records in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software that reference the discontinued currency.']
  display_format varchar [note: 'Locale-specific display format pattern for rendering monetary amounts in this currency (e.g., #,##0.00 for USD, #.##0,00 for EUR in European locales). Controls how currency values are presented in investor reports, NOI statements, and Argus Enterprise output for international portfolio stakeholders.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this currency code became officially valid and available for use in financial transactions per ISO 4217. Supports historical currency tracking for legacy portfolio transactions, particularly relevant for currencies introduced through monetary union events (e.g., EUR introduction date) or currency redenomination.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authoritative external source used to obtain exchange rates for this currency (e.g., European Central Bank, Federal Reserve, Bloomberg, Reuters/Refinitiv, ICE). Governs which rate feed is consumed by SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager for currency translation in AP/AR and GL consolidation.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of exchange rate applied when translating this currency for financial reporting purposes. Spot rate is used for transaction-date translation; average rate for income statement items; closing rate for balance sheet items per FASB ASC 830 and IAS 21. Budget rate is used for planning in Argus Enterprise DCF models.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is currently active and available for use in financial transactions across the enterprise. Inactive currencies (e.g., discontinued or superseded ISO codes) are retained for historical reporting but blocked from new transaction entry in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_freely_convertible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency can be freely exchanged for other currencies without government restrictions or capital controls. Non-convertible or restricted currencies require special handling in cross-border real estate investment structures, repatriation planning, and REIT distribution calculations.']
  is_functional_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is designated as the functional currency for the enterprise or a specific legal entity under FASB ASC 830 (Foreign Currency Matters) or IASB IAS 21. The functional currency is the primary currency of the economic environment in which the REIT or fund entity operates, used as the base for financial consolidation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_hard_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is classified as a hard currency — a globally accepted, stable reserve currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CHF). Hard currency designation influences lease denomination decisions in international commercial real estate, particularly for NNN leases in emerging markets where rents may be quoted in USD or EUR.']
  is_reporting_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is designated as the reporting currency for consolidated financial statements and SEC/regulatory filings. Distinct from functional currency — a REIT may operate in multiple functional currencies but report in a single reporting currency (typically USD) for investor and regulatory purposes.']
  iso_4217_status varchar [note: 'Official status of the currency code within the ISO 4217 standard. Active codes are currently in circulation; historic codes have been withdrawn or superseded; fund codes represent monetary funds (e.g., XDR for SDR). Drives eligibility rules for new transaction entry across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and SAP S/4HANA.']
  minor_unit_decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for the minor unit of the currency as specified by ISO 4217 (e.g., 2 for USD/EUR/GBP representing cents, 0 for JPY/KWD which has no minor unit, 3 for KWD). Critical for correct rounding in AP/AR processing in Yardi Voyager, rent schedule calculations, and FASB/IASB-compliant financial reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special handling instructions, or regulatory notes associated with this currency (e.g., capital control restrictions, special accounting treatment requirements under IAS 29 for hyperinflationary economies, or notes on currency union membership). Used by treasury and finance teams for operational guidance.']
  numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit numeric code assigned to the currency under ISO 4217 (e.g., 840 for USD, 978 for EUR). Used in electronic data interchange, CMBS/RMBS reporting, and SAP S/4HANA financial consolidation where numeric codes are required for system interoperability.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region associated with the currency (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East, Latin America). Used for regional portfolio aggregation, cross-border investment analysis, and AUM reporting in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding convention applied to monetary amounts in this currency during financial calculations, AP/AR processing, and rent schedule generation. Half-even (banker\'s rounding) is standard for FASB/IASB-compliant financial reporting. Governs rounding behavior in Yardi Voyager rent calculations, CAM reconciliations, and SAP S/4HANA GL postings.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this currency code record was sourced or is mastered (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, YARDI_VOYAGER, MRI_SOFTWARE). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across the enterprise lakehouse Silver layer.']
  source_system_currency_key varchar [note: 'Native key or identifier used to represent this currency in the originating source system (e.g., SAP currency key \'USD\', Yardi currency code field value). Enables cross-system reconciliation and ETL mapping between the enterprise reference registry and operational systems.']
  subunit_name varchar [note: 'Name of the minor unit or subunit of the currency (e.g., Cent for USD/EUR, Penny for GBP, Sen for JPY). Used in detailed financial disclosures, lease rent schedules expressed in subunit precision, and CAM reconciliation statements in Yardi Voyager.']
  successor_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code of the currency that replaced this currency upon discontinuation (e.g., EUR as successor to DEM, FRF, ITL). Used to support currency migration mapping for historical portfolio restatement and financial consolidation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Commonly used typographic symbol representing the currency (e.g., $ for USD, € for EUR, £ for GBP, ¥ for JPY). Used in UI displays, lease rent schedules, NOI statements, and investor reporting dashboards for human-readable financial presentation.']
  symbol_position varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the currency symbol is displayed before (prefix) or after (suffix) the numeric amount per locale convention (e.g., prefix for USD: $1,000; suffix for SEK: 1 000 kr). Used in report rendering engines for investor communications and tenant-facing financial statements.']
  thousands_separator varchar [note: 'Character used as the thousands grouping separator when displaying monetary amounts in this currency (e.g., comma for USD/GBP, period for EUR in many European countries, space for CHF). Ensures correct formatting in investor-facing financial reports and lease abstracts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this currency code record was last modified in the enterprise reference registry. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance under SOX Section 404.']
  volatility_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the currency\'s exchange rate volatility relative to major reserve currencies. Hyperinflationary designation triggers special accounting treatment under FASB ASC 830-10-45 and IAS 29 for real estate entities operating in those economies. Informs FX risk management and hedging strategy for international portfolio investments.']

  Note: 'ISO 4217 currency code registry supporting multi-currency financial operations across international real estate portfolios. Stores currency code, name, symbol, decimal precision, and active status. Includes exchange rate source reference and functional currency designation for REIT and fund-level financial consolidation under SAP S/4HANA. Supports AP/AR multi-currency processing in Yardi Voyager and financial reporting under FASB/IASB standards.'
}

Table reference.uom {
  uom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each unit-of-measure record in the enterprise UOM registry. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a controlled vocabulary / code-list table; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  applicable_property_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property type codes for which this UOM is valid (e.g., \'office,retail,industrial\' for SQF; \'land\' for acres; \'parking\' for stalls). Restricts UOM selection in Yardi Voyager lease setup and MRI Software property configuration to contextually appropriate measures.']
  area_standard varchar [note: 'For area-dimension UOMs, the industry measurement standard that defines how the area is calculated (e.g., BOMA for US office, IPMS for international, GLA for retail, NLA for net leasable area, GFA for gross floor area). Null for non-area UOMs. Critical for appraisal compliance and cross-border portfolio comparability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOMA|IPMS|GLA|NLA|GFA|RICS|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_factor decimal(28,10) [note: 'Multiplicative factor to convert one unit of this UOM to one unit of the base_uom_code (e.g., 1 SQF = 0.0929030400 SQM; 1 LF = 0.3048 LM). A value of 1.0 indicates this UOM is itself the base unit. Used in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software for cross-currency and cross-metric portfolio roll-ups.']
  conversion_formula varchar [note: 'Optional algebraic formula string for non-linear conversions where a simple multiplicative factor is insufficient (e.g., temperature scales). For most real estate UOMs this is null; populated only when the conversion requires an offset or compound expression.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this UOM record was first created in the enterprise reference data registry. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX ITGC controls and data governance lineage tracking.']
  default_region_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country/region code for which this UOM is the default standard (e.g., US for SQF, DE/GB/AU for SQM). Drives automatic UOM defaulting in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software when creating properties in a given jurisdiction.']
  dimension_type varchar [note: 'Physical or financial dimension category the UOM belongs to: area (SQF, SQM, GLA, NLA, GFA), linear (LF, LM), volume (CF, CM), weight (LB, KG), rate (PSF/year, PSM/month), count (units, floors), currency (USD, EUR), or other. Drives validation rules in rent calculations and valuation models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: area|linear|volume|weight|rate|count|currency|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Integer sort order controlling the sequence in which UOMs appear in dropdown lists and selection menus across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise. Lower values appear first; commonly used UOMs (SQF, SQM) are assigned low values.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this UOM record is valid and available for use in new transactions, leases, and valuations. Supports temporal validity management in the enterprise reference data registry.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this UOM record is no longer valid for use in new transactions. Null indicates no planned expiry. Used to phase out deprecated UOMs while preserving historical data integrity.']
  is_base_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is the designated base unit for its dimension type and measurement system (True = base unit; False = derived unit). Base units have a conversion_factor of exactly 1.0.']
  is_rate_based boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is a rate measure combining a quantity dimension with a time period (e.g., PSF/year, PSM/month, $/SQF/year). Rate-based UOMs require both a numerator UOM and a denominator time period for correct rent and NOI calculations.']
  is_regulatory_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether values expressed in this UOM must be disclosed in regulatory filings (True = required). For example, GLA in SQF/SQM is required in SEC REIT filings, HUD disclosures, and LEED/BREEAM certification submissions.']
  is_rentable_area_measure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this UOM is used to express rentable area for lease billing purposes (True = rentable area measure such as SQF or SQM under BOMA/IPMS). Drives rent calculation logic in Yardi Voyager lease administration and FASB ASC 842 / IFRS 16 right-of-use asset computations.']
  iso_uom_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding ISO 80000 or UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20 unit code for interoperability with SAP S/4HANA procurement, ERP integrations, and international regulatory submissions (e.g., MTK for square meters, FTK for square feet, KGM for kilograms).']
  measurement_system varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the UOM belongs to the imperial, metric (SI), US customary, or other measurement system. Critical for cross-border portfolio reporting and regulatory area disclosures where jurisdictions mandate specific systems (e.g., metric in EU, imperial in US).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional governance notes, known exceptions, or migration guidance associated with this UOM record (e.g., \'Use SQM for all EU properties per portfolio policy effective 2024-01-01\'). Maintained by the data governance team.']
  precision_decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to which values expressed in this UOM should be rounded for display and storage (e.g., 2 for SQF area, 4 for conversion factors, 0 for whole-unit counts). Ensures consistent rounding in lease abstracts, valuation reports, and regulatory area disclosures.']
  rate_denominator_uom_code varchar [note: 'For rate-based UOMs (is_rate_based = True), the UOM code of the denominator area or volume measure (e.g., for PSF/year the denominator is SQF). Null for non-rate UOMs.']
  rate_numerator_uom_code varchar [note: 'For rate-based UOMs (is_rate_based = True), the UOM code of the numerator quantity (e.g., for PSF/year the numerator is USD). Null for non-rate UOMs. Enables decomposition of rent rates into currency and area components for PSF/PSM analytics.']
  rate_time_period varchar [note: 'For rate-based UOMs, the time period denominator of the rate (e.g., annual for PSF/year, monthly for PSM/month). Drives annualization logic in NOI statements, rent schedules, and Argus Enterprise cash flow models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|monthly|weekly|daily|per_sqf|per_sqm|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reso_uom_code varchar [note: 'RESO Data Dictionary standardized UOM code for MLS and real estate data exchange interoperability (e.g., SquareFeet, SquareMeters, Acres). Enables consistent data syndication via CoStar Suite and MLS platforms.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this UOM definition was originally sourced or is mastered (e.g., yardi for Yardi Voyager, mri for MRI Software, sap for SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during ETL reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: yardi|mri|argus|sap|costar|procore|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_uom_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier or code assigned to this UOM in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi Voyager internal UOM key, SAP S/4HANA unit code). Enables bidirectional reconciliation and cross-system mapping during data integration.']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Standardized typographic symbol for the unit (e.g., ft², m², lb, kg, $/ft²/yr). Used in printed lease documents, CoStar data exports, and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.']
  uom_category varchar [note: 'Business-facing grouping of the UOM within real estate operations (e.g., \'Leasable Area\', \'Gross Area\', \'Rent Rate\', \'Construction Measure\', \'Valuation Metric\', \'Utility Consumption\'). Supports filtering in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: leasable_area|gross_area|rent_rate|construction_measure|valuation_metric|utility_consumption|land_area|parking — promote to reference product]']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the unit of measure (e.g., SQF, SQM, PSF, LF, CF, LB, KG). Used as the canonical lookup key across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, Argus Enterprise, and SAP S/4HANA integrations.']
  uom_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the UOM, including its definition, applicable measurement context, and any industry-specific nuances (e.g., distinction between GLA and NLA, BOMA vs IPMS methodology differences). Used in data governance documentation and business glossary.']
  uom_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the unit of measure (e.g., \'Square Feet\', \'Square Meters\', \'Linear Feet\', \'Pounds per Square Foot per Year\'). Displayed in reports, lease abstracts, and valuation outputs.']
  uom_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the UOM record: active (in current use), inactive (no longer used for new records), deprecated (superseded by another UOM, retained for historical data), pending_review (under governance review). Controls availability in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software UOM selection lists.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this UOM record. Used to detect stale reference data and trigger downstream cache invalidation in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise integrations.']

  Note: 'Enterprise unit-of-measure registry for all measurement dimensions in real estate operations — area (SQF, SQM, GLA, NLA, GFA), linear (LF, LM), volume (CF, CM), weight (LB, KG), and rate-based measures (PSF/year, PSM/month). Defines conversion factors between imperial and metric systems, default UOM by geographic region, and applicable property types per measure. Critical for consistent rent calculations, valuation metrics, cross-border portfolio reporting, and regulatory area disclosures.'
}

Table reference.industry_classification {
  industry_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each industry classification code record in the enterprise reference repository. Serves as the primary key for this lookup table. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — small enumeration/code-list entity; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  classification_system varchar [note: 'The industry classification standard or system to which this code belongs.
