Supported systems include NAICS (North American Industry Classification System), SIC (Standard Industrial Classification), GICS (Global Industry Classification Standard), NACE (European industry classification), ANZSIC (Australian/New Zealand), and ISIC (International Standard Industrial Classification). Determines how the code is interpreted and which authority governs it.']
  code_version varchar [note: 'The version or revision year of the classification system under which this code is defined (e.g., \'NAICS 2022\', \'NAICS 2017\', \'SIC 1987\'). Classification systems are periodically revised; version tracking ensures codes are interpreted against the correct edition for historical comparability and regulatory compliance.']
  costar_category varchar [note: 'The corresponding tenant industry category as defined in CoStar Suite\'s market analytics taxonomy. CoStar uses its own proprietary tenant categorization that maps to but does not always align 1:1 with NAICS/SIC codes. Populated to enable direct integration with CoStar comparable lease data, market research reports, and tenant mix benchmarking against market peers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this industry classification code record was first loaded into the enterprise reference repository. Supports data lineage tracking, audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls, and change management for the reference data governance process.']
  credit_risk_tier varchar [note: 'General credit risk tier associated with tenants in this industry classification, based on historical default rates and sector credit quality benchmarks. Used in lease underwriting, DSCR analysis, and portfolio risk concentration reporting. Supports Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling assumptions and MRI Investment Management risk-adjusted return calculations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this industry classification code is officially valid and available for use, as published by the governing authority. For NAICS, this aligns with the edition release date (e.g., 2017-01-01 for NAICS 2017). Ensures tenant classifications are assigned using codes valid at the time of lease execution.']
  esg_risk_category varchar [note: 'The ESG risk category associated with tenants or occupiers in this industry classification, reflecting environmental impact, social responsibility, and governance risk exposure. Used in ESG portfolio reporting, LEED/BREEAM tenant engagement programs, and green lease structuring. Supports SEC ESG disclosure requirements for publicly traded REITs and institutional investor reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this industry classification code ceases to be officially valid, typically coinciding with the release of a revised edition of the classification system. Null for codes that remain active in the current edition. Used to enforce code validity checks during tenant onboarding and lease amendment processing.']
  gics_equivalent_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding GICS 8-digit sub-industry code for records originating from NAICS or SIC systems. Used for investment portfolio analysis in Argus Enterprise and MRI Investment Management, where institutional investors and REITs classify tenant and occupier exposure using GICS for equity market benchmarking and ESG reporting. Null when classification_system is already GICS.']
  hazmat_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tenants in this industry classification typically handle, store, or generate hazardous materials, requiring environmental due diligence, Phase I/II environmental site assessments, and specialized lease provisions. Relevant for industrial and laboratory occupiers. Supports EPA environmental compliance, OSHA building safety requirements, and property acquisition due diligence.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level of this code within its classification system. For NAICS: sector (2-digit), subsector (3-digit), industry group (4-digit), industry (5-digit), national industry (6-digit). For SIC: division, major group (2-digit), group (3-digit), industry (4-digit). For GICS: sector, industry group, industry, sub-industry. Enables roll-up and drill-down analysis for portfolio diversification reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sector|subsector|industry_group|industry|national_industry|division|major_group|group|class|subclass — promote to reference product]']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'The official alphanumeric code assigned by the governing classification authority (e.g., NAICS code \'531120\' for Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings, SIC code \'6512\', or GICS code \'60101010\' for Diversified REITs). This is the externally recognized business identifier used in regulatory filings, lease underwriting, and portfolio reporting.']
  industry_classification_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the industry classification code as defined by the governing authority. Explains the scope of economic activities covered, typical establishments included, and any cross-references or exclusions. Supports lease underwriting decisions and tenant mix analysis by providing context beyond the short title.']
  industry_classification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this industry classification code record. \'Active\' codes are available for assignment to tenants and occupiers. \'Deprecated\' codes have been retired by the governing authority but retained for historical record integrity. \'Superseded\' codes have been replaced by a new code in a revised edition of the classification system.']
  is_anchor_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tenants in this industry classification are typically eligible to serve as anchor tenants in retail or mixed-use properties. Anchor tenants drive foot traffic and influence co-tenancy clauses in lease agreements. Used in retail leasing strategy, tenant mix planning, and co-tenancy clause risk assessment in Salesforce CRM deal pipeline management.']
  is_essential_business boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tenants in this industry classification are typically designated as essential businesses under applicable government emergency orders (e.g., grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, utilities). Relevant for lease force majeure clause analysis, rent abatement risk assessment during declared emergencies, and regulatory compliance reporting to HUD and SEC for publicly traded REITs.']
  is_real_estate_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this industry classification code is specifically relevant to real estate operations, investment, or property-related activities (e.g., NAICS 531 Real Estate, GICS sector 60 Real Estate). Used to filter the classification code list for real estate portfolio analytics, REIT regulatory reporting, and property management system integrations.']
  mri_industry_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding industry code as configured in MRI Software\'s lease management and residential management modules. MRI Software uses its own tenant industry classification codes for lease underwriting and portfolio reporting. Enables synchronization between the enterprise reference repository and MRI Software tenant and lease records.']
  naics_equivalent_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding NAICS 6-digit code for records originating from SIC or GICS systems, enabling cross-system concordance. Populated when classification_system is SIC or GICS to allow unified NAICS-based reporting as required by U.S. federal regulatory filings and CoStar market analytics. Null when classification_system is already NAICS.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, cross-references, internal usage guidance, or exceptions related to this industry classification code. May include notes on code transitions between NAICS revision cycles, known mapping ambiguities between classification systems, or specific real estate portfolio application guidance for asset managers and leasing teams.']
  real_estate_use_category varchar [note: 'The primary real estate property use category most commonly associated with tenants or occupiers in this industry classification. Drives retail tenant mix analysis (anchor vs. inline), office sector concentration tracking, and industrial occupier type classification. Used in Yardi Voyager and MRI lease management for automated tenant categorization and CoStar market analytics segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|office|industrial|multifamily|hospitality|mixed_use|specialty|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'The specific code or identifier used when reporting tenant industry classification to regulatory bodies (e.g., SEC REIT tenant concentration disclosures, HUD reporting, CMBS loan-level data reporting). May differ from the standard NAICS/SIC/GICS code when regulators require a mapped or aggregated code. Supports SOX financial controls compliance for publicly traded REITs.']
  retail_tenant_type varchar [note: 'For retail-use classifications, designates the tenant positioning category within a retail center (e.g., anchor, major, inline, kiosk, pad/outparcel, food and beverage, entertainment, service). Critical for retail tenant mix analysis, GLA allocation planning, and CAM cost recovery structuring. Null or \'not_applicable\' for non-retail classifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: anchor|major|inline|kiosk|pad|outparcel|food_and_beverage|entertainment|service|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  sector_code varchar [note: 'The top-level sector code within the classification system (e.g., NAICS sector \'53\', SIC division \'H\', GICS sector code \'60\'). Provides a concise key for sector-level grouping in portfolio analytics, lease underwriting scorecards, and regulatory concentration reporting.']
  sector_name varchar [note: 'The top-level sector name under which this classification code falls (e.g., \'Real Estate and Rental and Leasing\' for NAICS sector 53, \'Finance, Insurance, and Real Estate\' for SIC division H, \'Real Estate\' for GICS sector 60). Used for high-level portfolio diversification analysis and tenant sector concentration reporting in CRE portfolios.']
  sic_equivalent_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding SIC 4-digit code for records originating from NAICS or GICS systems. Maintained for backward compatibility with legacy Yardi Voyager tenant records, MRI Software lease management configurations, and older regulatory filings that reference SIC codes. Null when classification_system is already SIC.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'When status is \'superseded\', the code that replaces this classification code in the current or subsequent version of the classification system. Enables automated migration of tenant classifications during NAICS revision cycles and maintains audit trail for historical lease records in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  title varchar [note: 'The official short title or label of the industry classification code as published by the governing authority (e.g., \'Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings (except Miniwarehouses)\', \'Real Estate Dealers (for their own account)\', \'Diversified REITs\'). Used as the primary display label in reports, dashboards, and tenant categorization screens.']
  typical_lease_type varchar [note: 'The lease structure most commonly associated with tenants in this industry classification (e.g., NNN for retail/industrial, gross for office, percentage rent for food and beverage). Informs lease underwriting assumptions in Argus Enterprise, CAM recovery modeling, and NOI forecasting. NNN = Triple Net Lease. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NNN|gross|modified_gross|percentage|ground|net|double_net — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  typical_space_size_max_sqf varchar [note: 'The maximum typical occupied space size in square feet (SQF) for tenants in this industry classification. Used alongside the minimum to define the expected space demand range for lease underwriting, tenant mix planning, and market absorption analysis. SQF = Square Feet.']
  typical_space_size_min_sqf varchar [note: 'The minimum typical occupied space size in square feet (SQF) for tenants in this industry classification. Used as a benchmark in lease underwriting, space planning, and GLA allocation analysis. Supports PSF rent benchmarking and NLA/GLA ratio analysis in Argus Enterprise cash flow models. SQF = Square Feet.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this industry classification code record was last modified in the enterprise reference repository. Tracks updates such as status changes, description corrections, system code mapping updates, or ESG risk category revisions. Supports data governance audit trails and downstream system synchronization.']
  yardi_industry_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding industry code as configured in Yardi Voyager\'s tenant profile module. Yardi Voyager maintains its own internal industry code list for tenant categorization in lease administration and accounts receivable. This field enables bidirectional synchronization between the enterprise reference repository and Yardi Voyager tenant records.']

  Note: 'Standard industry classification codes for tenant and occupier categorization — NAICS (North American Industry Classification System), SIC (Standard Industrial Classification), and GICS (Global Industry Classification Standard). Used to classify tenant business types for portfolio diversification analysis, lease underwriting, and regulatory reporting. Supports retail tenant mix analysis (anchor vs. inline), office tenant sector concentration, and industrial occupier type tracking.'
}

Table reference.amenity_type {
  amenity_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each amenity type record in the controlled vocabulary. Primary key for the amenity_type reference product. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This is a controlled vocabulary / code-list entity; per-role minimum categories for MASTER_RESOURCE/PARTY/TRANSACTION do not apply.]']
  typical_size_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Amenity types have a typical physical size measured in a specific unit of measure (e.g., square feet for a fitness center, number of spaces for a parking garage). Linking to the enterprise UOM registr']
  amenity_category varchar [note: 'Functional category grouping amenities by their primary purpose for analytics and comparable analysis (e.g., \'Wellness & Fitness\', \'Parking & Transportation\', \'Technology & Smart Building\', \'Food & Beverage\', \'Conference & Collaboration\', \'Green Space & Recreation\', \'Concierge & Services\', \'Security & Access\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Wellness & Fitness|Parking & Transportation|Technology & Smart Building|Food & Beverage|Conference & Collaboration|Green Space & Recreation|Concierge & Services|Security & Access — promote to reference product]']
  amenity_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping amenities by their physical scope and applicability: \'building\' (shared building-wide features such as lobbies and fitness centers), \'unit\' (in-unit features such as in-unit laundry or private terrace), \'community\' (campus or complex-wide features such as shared green spaces), \'outdoor\' (exterior amenities such as parking structures or dog parks).']
  amenity_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-readable alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the amenity type across all operational systems (e.g., FIT_CTR, ROOFTOP_TER, EV_CHRG, CONF_FAC). Used as the stable integration key between Yardi Voyager, CoStar Suite, MRI Software, and the data lakehouse.']
  amenity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the amenity type as it appears in property listings, marketing materials, tenant portals, and brokerage reports (e.g., \'Fitness Center\', \'Rooftop Terrace\', \'EV Charging Station\', \'Conference Facility\').']
  amenity_type_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the amenity type, including its physical characteristics, typical configuration, and business purpose. Used in property marketing copy, tenant attraction materials, and brokerage listing enrichment within Salesforce CRM and CoStar Suite.']
  amenity_type_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the amenity type record within the controlled vocabulary. \'active\' — currently in use across operational systems; \'inactive\' — no longer assigned to new properties but retained for historical records; \'deprecated\' — superseded by a replacement code; \'pending_review\' — submitted for governance approval but not yet ratified.']
  applicable_property_types varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of property type codes for which this amenity type is relevant (e.g., \'OFFICE,RETAIL,MULTIFAMILY\'). Drives filtering logic in property listing platforms, CoStar Suite comparable searches, and Yardi Voyager property setup workflows. Stored as a delimited string per the no-ARRAY-type constraint.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this amenity type definition was formally approved by the data governance committee for use in enterprise systems. Supports governance audit trails and reference data lifecycle management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the data governance authority or individual who approved this amenity type for inclusion in the controlled vocabulary. Supports reference data governance audit trails and SOX compliance documentation.']
  costar_amenity_code varchar [note: 'The amenity identifier code as used in CoStar Suite for property analytics, comparable sales and lease data, and market research. Enables direct mapping between the enterprise reference vocabulary and CoStar data feeds for comparable analysis (CMA) and Broker Price Opinion (BPO) workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this amenity type record was first created in the enterprise reference data repository. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls and GDPR data lineage obligations.']
  display_sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer value controlling the display sequence of amenity types in property listing portals, tenant-facing marketing materials, and brokerage platforms. Lower values appear first. Managed by the reference data governance team.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this amenity type code became active and available for use in operational systems and property records. Supports temporal validity tracking for the controlled vocabulary and audit compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this amenity type code is no longer valid for assignment to new property records. Null indicates the code remains indefinitely active. Used in reference data lifecycle management and downstream system synchronization.']
  icon_identifier varchar [note: 'Reference key or filename for the standardized icon representing this amenity type in tenant portals, property marketing websites, and mobile applications. Enables consistent visual branding across Salesforce CRM tenant portals and Building Engines service request interfaces.']
  indoor_outdoor_indicator varchar [note: 'Specifies whether this amenity type is physically located indoors, outdoors, or spans both environments. Used in property marketing, facility operations planning in Building Engines, and LEED site assessment scoring.']
  is_ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amenity type, by definition, must meet Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility standards. Used in compliance reporting, building inspection checklists in Building Engines, and regulatory filings with HUD.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amenity type is typically subject to a separate charge to tenants (e.g., reserved parking, private storage units, premium concierge services) as opposed to being included in base rent. Drives billing configuration in Yardi Voyager AR and lease charge setup in MRI Software.']
  is_breeam_qualifying boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the presence of this amenity type can contribute credits toward a BREEAM (Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method) certification assessment. Relevant for international portfolio assets and cross-border investment reporting.']
  is_cam_recoverable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operating and maintenance costs associated with this amenity type are recoverable from tenants as part of Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges under NNN or modified gross lease structures. Used in CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  is_esg_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amenity type contributes to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting metrics (e.g., EV charging stations, green roofs, bike storage, energy-efficient HVAC systems). Used in ESG portfolio reporting and investor disclosure packages for publicly traded REITs under SEC requirements.']
  is_leed_qualifying boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the presence of this amenity type can contribute points toward a LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification score. Used in development project planning within Procore and asset valuation models in Argus Enterprise.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this amenity type record was most recently modified in the enterprise reference data repository. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer pipeline and reference data synchronization across operational systems.']
  maintenance_intensity varchar [note: 'Categorical indicator of the relative operational and maintenance burden associated with this amenity type: \'low\' (e.g., bike racks, signage), \'medium\' (e.g., fitness equipment, conference rooms), \'high\' (e.g., pools, rooftop terraces, EV charging infrastructure). Used in OPEX budgeting, preventive maintenance scheduling in Building Engines, and CAPEX planning.']
  market_segment_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of market segments for which this amenity type is commonly relevant (e.g., \'CLASS_A,CLASS_B\', \'LUXURY_RESIDENTIAL\', \'INDUSTRIAL\'). Supports comparable market analysis (CMA) filtering in CoStar Suite and brokerage listing enrichment in Salesforce CRM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CLASS_A|CLASS_B|CLASS_C|LUXURY_RESIDENTIAL|AFFORDABLE_HOUSING|INDUSTRIAL|MIXED_USE — promote to reference product]']
  mri_amenity_code varchar [note: 'The amenity identifier as configured in MRI Software residential and commercial property management modules. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between MRI Software and the enterprise reference data layer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, governance decisions, or implementation guidance related to this amenity type. Used by the reference data stewardship team to document edge cases, mapping decisions, and cross-system alignment notes.']
  regulatory_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing relevant regulatory, zoning, or compliance considerations associated with this amenity type (e.g., ADA accessibility requirements, EPA environmental standards for EV charging, OSHA safety requirements for fitness equipment). Supports compliance reporting and development due diligence.']
  requires_permit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether installation or operation of this amenity type typically requires a municipal building permit, zoning variance, or regulatory approval (e.g., rooftop structures, pools, EV charging infrastructure, food service facilities). Used in development project planning in Procore and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  reso_amenity_code varchar [note: 'The standardized amenity code as defined by the Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO) Data Dictionary, used for MLS syndication and cross-platform data exchange. Enables interoperability with MLS systems and CoStar Suite data feeds.']
  smart_building_feature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amenity type is classified as a smart building or PropTech-enabled feature (e.g., automated access control, IoT-connected HVAC, smart parking systems, digital concierge kiosks). Relevant for Building Information Modeling (BIM) integration and ESG technology reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or authority from which this amenity type definition was sourced: \'RESO\' (Real Estate Standards Organization), \'COSTAR\' (CoStar Suite), \'YARDI\' (Yardi Voyager), \'MRI\' (MRI Software), \'NAR_MLS\' (National Association of Realtors MLS), \'MANUAL\' (manually defined by the data governance team).']
  tenant_attraction_score varchar [note: 'Standardized integer score (1–10) representing the relative importance of this amenity type in attracting and retaining tenants, based on market research benchmarks from CoStar Suite and ULI tenant surveys. Used in brokerage deal pipeline analysis in Salesforce CRM and lease renewal probability modeling.']
  typical_capex_tier varchar [note: 'Indicative tier representing the typical capital expenditure (CAPEX) required to install or construct this amenity type: \'low\' (under $50K), \'medium\' ($50K–$500K), \'high\' ($500K–$2M), \'very_high\' (over $2M). Used in development feasibility analysis in Procore and investment underwriting in Argus Enterprise.']
  valuation_impact_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the general directional impact this amenity type has on property valuation and CAP rate analysis: \'positive\' — typically increases asset value and NOI; \'neutral\' — no material valuation impact; \'negative\' — may reduce value (e.g., high-maintenance amenities with low tenant uptake); \'variable\' — impact depends on market segment. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models and Appraisal Institute valuation reports.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer tracking the version of this amenity type definition within the controlled vocabulary. Incremented each time the record is updated through the reference data governance process. Supports audit trails and change management.']
  yardi_amenity_code varchar [note: 'The amenity code as configured in Yardi Voyager property management and lease administration modules. Used for system-of-record alignment when synchronizing amenity data between the lakehouse silver layer and Yardi Voyager operational data.']

  Note: 'Controlled vocabulary for property and building amenities used in property marketing, tenant attraction, and asset valuation — fitness centers, rooftop terraces, parking structures, EV charging stations, conference facilities, concierge services, green spaces, and smart building features. Categorized by amenity class (building, unit-level, community), applicable property types, and ESG/LEED relevance flags. Used by property, lease, and brokerage domains for listing enrichment and comparable analysis.'
}

Table reference.building_class {
  building_class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each building class record in the enterprise reference repository. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This is a controlled vocabulary / code-list entity providing standardized building quality classifications; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: building_class tracks minimum GLA thresholds (min_gla_sqf DECIMAL) and parking ratios (parking_ratio_min) that are area-measurement-dependent. Adding area_uom_id → uom.uom_id normalizes the unit of me']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Building class benchmarks (typical_psf_rent_min, typical_psf_rent_max) are denominated in a specific currency. Linking to the ISO 4217 currency registry ensures referential integrity for financial ben']
  amenity_requirement_level varchar [note: 'Minimum level of building amenities required for this class designation. Full-service includes concierge, fitness center, conference facilities, and food service; minimal reflects basic building services only. Supports property management benchmarking in Building Engines and Yardi Voyager.']
  asset_segment varchar [note: 'Broad real estate asset segment to which this building class applies. Distinguishes commercial (office, retail), residential (multifamily, single-family), industrial (warehouse, flex), and mixed-use classifications, as building class criteria differ materially by segment.']
  boma_class_designation varchar [note: 'Official Building Owners and Managers Association (BOMA) building class designation (A, B, C, or D) mapped to this enterprise class. Ensures alignment with the industry-standard BOMA classification framework used in lease administration, property management, and benchmarking.']
  building_class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this building class record in the enterprise reference data repository. Active records are available for property classification; deprecated records are retained for historical reference only. Managed through the reference data governance process.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the building class (e.g., A, B, C, TROPHY, LUX, MID, AFF, INST). Used as the externally-known business identifier across operational systems including Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, CoStar, and Argus Enterprise for property classification lookups.']
  class_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the building class, including qualitative criteria such as construction quality, finish standards, location requirements, amenity expectations, and typical tenant profile. Supports underwriting, valuation, and investment advisory workflows.']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the building class (e.g., \'Class A\', \'Class B\', \'Class C\', \'Trophy\', \'Luxury\', \'Mid-Market\', \'Affordable\', \'Institutional Grade\'). Human-readable label used in reports, dashboards, and tenant-facing materials.']
  classification_tier varchar [note: 'Numeric ordinal rank of the building class within its asset segment hierarchy (e.g., 1 = highest/Trophy/Class A, 2 = Class B/Mid-Market, 3 = Class C/Affordable). Enables ranked sorting and comparative analytics across the classification spectrum in Argus Enterprise and portfolio reporting.']
  construction_quality_grade varchar [note: 'Qualitative grade of construction quality required for this building class, reflecting structural integrity, materials specification, and build standards. Premium denotes trophy/Class A+ finishes; economy denotes Class C or below. Informs property valuation and appraisal workflows in Argus Enterprise.']
  costar_class_code varchar [note: 'CoStar Suite building class code used for market research, comparable sales, and lease data alignment. Enables direct cross-referencing between the enterprise classification system and CoStar\'s industry-standard building quality ratings for benchmarking and market analytics.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this building class definition becomes effective and applicable for property classification across enterprise systems. Supports temporal versioning of classification standards as market definitions evolve.']
  energy_star_min_score varchar [note: 'Minimum EPA ENERGY STAR score (1–100 scale) required for buildings to qualify for this class designation. A score of 75 or above qualifies for ENERGY STAR certification. Null if no ENERGY STAR threshold is mandated. Supports ESG and environmental compliance reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this building class definition expires and is superseded by a revised version. Null indicates the definition is currently active with no planned expiry. Enables historical classification lookups and audit trails for regulatory compliance.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External identifier or code for this building class as defined by the source standards body or system (e.g., CoStar property class code, BOMA designation code). Enables cross-system reconciliation and market data integration for benchmarking and analytics.']
  finish_quality_grade varchar [note: 'Grade of interior finish quality expected for buildings in this class, covering flooring, ceiling heights, lobby finishes, restroom quality, and common area standards. Directly influences Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance benchmarks and lease negotiation parameters.']
  hvac_system_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system type required for buildings in this class. Central VAV (Variable Air Volume) systems are typically required for Class A office; split systems may be acceptable for Class C. Informs property management and capital expenditure (CAPEX) planning.']
  investment_grade_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties in this building class are considered institutional or investment-grade assets suitable for REIT portfolios, pension fund investment, and institutional capital allocation. True for Trophy and Class A designations; typically False for Class C and below.']
  leed_certification_level varchar [note: 'Minimum Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) certification level associated with or required for this building class (Certified, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Null if LEED certification is not a classification criterion. Supports ESG compliance and green building reporting.']
  location_grade varchar [note: 'Required location quality grade for buildings in this class, reflecting proximity to Central Business District (CBD), transportation infrastructure, and market desirability. Prime CBD locations are typically required for Trophy and Class A designations. Used in market segmentation and portfolio analytics.']
  max_building_age_years varchar [note: 'Maximum age of a building in years (measured from year of construction or last major renovation) that qualifies for this class. Null indicates no upper age limit. Used alongside min_building_age_years to define the age band for classification eligibility.']
  max_cap_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum typical Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%) associated with properties in this building class. Used alongside min_cap_rate_pct to define the CAP rate band for investment analysis and portfolio benchmarking.']
  min_building_age_years varchar [note: 'Minimum age of a building in years (measured from year of construction or last major renovation) that qualifies for this class. Used as a threshold criterion in automated property classification workflows within Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  min_cap_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Minimum typical Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0400 = 4.00%) associated with properties in this building class. Provides investment benchmarking guidance for asset managers and investment advisors using Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  min_floor_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of above-grade floors a building must have to qualify for this class designation. Applicable primarily to office and mixed-use classifications where building height is a quality indicator (e.g., Trophy office typically requires high-rise status).']
  min_gla_sqf decimal(14,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Feet (SQF) required for a property to qualify for this building class. Null if no minimum size threshold applies. Used in automated property classification and portfolio segmentation analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, classification nuances, regional variations, or governance notes related to this building class definition. Intended for reference data stewards and data governance teams managing the enterprise classification taxonomy.']
  parking_ratio_min decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum parking ratio (spaces per 1,000 square feet of GLA) required for buildings in this class. A key amenity criterion for commercial building classification, particularly for suburban office and retail properties. Expressed as spaces per 1,000 SQF.']
  property_type_scope varchar [note: 'Specific property type(s) within the asset segment to which this class definition applies (e.g., Office, Retail, Multifamily, Industrial, Hotel, Self-Storage, Senior Housing). Aligns with CoStar and RESO property type taxonomies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: office|retail|multifamily|industrial|hotel|self_storage|senior_housing|mixed_use|land|other — promote to reference product]']
  reit_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties classified under this building class are typically eligible for inclusion in a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) portfolio based on quality, income-producing characteristics, and institutional standards. Supports investment advisory and portfolio management workflows.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sort order for this building class in user interfaces, dropdown lists, and reports. Enables consistent presentation of building class options across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and enterprise reporting platforms independent of the classification tier.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the authoritative source system or standards body from which this building class definition originates (e.g., COSTAR for CoStar Suite definitions, BOMA for BOMA International standards, INTERNAL for enterprise-defined classes). Supports data lineage and reference data governance.']
  sustainability_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recognized sustainability certification (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR) is required or expected for buildings to qualify for this class designation. Reflects ESG compliance requirements increasingly embedded in institutional-grade CRE classification.']
  technology_infrastructure_grade varchar [note: 'Required level of technology infrastructure for buildings in this class, including fiber connectivity, building automation systems (BAS), smart building capabilities, and telecommunications readiness. Increasingly critical for Class A and Trophy designations in CRE markets.']
  typical_lease_structure varchar [note: 'Typical lease structure associated with this building class (e.g., Full Service Gross, Modified Gross, NNN — Triple Net Lease). Provides context for lease administration in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software, and informs CAM (Common Area Maintenance) charge modeling.']
  typical_psf_rent_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum typical asking rent expressed in Per Square Foot (PSF) per annum for properties in this building class, in the base currency. Used alongside typical_psf_rent_min to define the rent band for this class in market analytics and investment underwriting.']
  typical_psf_rent_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum typical asking rent expressed in Per Square Foot (PSF) per annum for properties in this building class, in the base currency. Provides market benchmarking context for lease negotiations, rent schedule modeling, and comparative market analysis (CMA) in CoStar and Argus Enterprise.']
  typical_tenant_profile varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the typical tenant profile associated with this building class (e.g., \'Fortune 500 corporations, financial institutions, law firms\' for Class A; \'small-to-medium businesses, local retailers\' for Class B/C). Supports tenant relationship management and leasing strategy in Salesforce CRM.']
  typical_vacancy_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Typical market vacancy rate expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%) for properties in this building class. Used as a benchmark input in Effective Gross Income (EGI) and Net Operating Income (NOI) modeling within Argus Enterprise cash flow projections.']

  Note: 'Standardized building quality classification system for commercial and residential assets — Class A, B, C for office and industrial; luxury, mid-market, affordable for residential; trophy, institutional-grade for investment-grade CRE. Defines classification criteria including age thresholds, construction quality standards, location grade, amenity requirements, and typical CAP rate ranges per class. Aligned with BOMA, NAIOP, and CoStar building class definitions.'
}

Table reference.construction_type {
  construction_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each construction type record in the enterprise reference repository. Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — exempt from per-role minimum-category requirements. _canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a controlled vocabulary / code-list table providing standardized construction method classifications; it carries no independent transaction, party, or agreement lifecycle.']
  cost_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: construction_type tracks cost benchmarks (typical_cost_psf_low, typical_cost_psf_high, replacement_cost_multiplier) that are currency-denominated. Adding cost_currency_code_id → currency_code.currency']
  cost_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: construction_type tracks cost benchmarks per area unit (typical_cost_psf_low, typical_cost_psf_high DECIMAL — PSF = per square foot) and physical measurements (max_building_height_ft, typical_span_ft,']
  acoustic_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative acoustic performance rating (low, moderate, high, very_high) for this construction type based on typical sound transmission characteristics. Relevant for multifamily, hospitality, and office property types; informs tenant improvement specifications and lease negotiations.']
  applicable_property_types varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of property type codes for which this construction type is commonly used or permitted (e.g., \'OFFICE,RETAIL,INDUSTRIAL,MULTIFAMILY,HOTEL\'). Enables cross-domain filtering in CoStar Suite market research, Argus Enterprise portfolio analysis, and development feasibility screening.']
  bim_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this construction type is fully supported by BIM workflows in Procore and related development management systems (True = BIM-supported; False = not BIM-supported). Supports digital construction management and as-built documentation practices.']
  breeam_compatibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this construction type is compatible with BREEAM certification pathways (not_applicable, compatible, preferred). Supports international ESG investment screening and sustainable development planning for cross-border real estate portfolios.']
  construction_category varchar [note: 'Broad grouping of construction types for portfolio-level analytics and reporting (light_frame, heavy_frame, concrete, masonry, modular, mixed, specialty). Used in CoStar Suite market segmentation, Argus Enterprise portfolio dashboards, and investment committee reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: light_frame|heavy_frame|concrete|masonry|modular|mixed|specialty — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  construction_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the building is assembled (site_built, prefabricated, modular, panelized, hybrid). Impacts construction schedule, CAPEX phasing, and Procore project management workflows for development projects.']
  construction_type_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-readable alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the construction type (e.g., WOOD_FRAME, STL_FRAME, CIP_CONC, PRECAST, TILT_UP, MASONRY, MODULAR, MIXED). Used as the stable business key for cross-system integration with Yardi Voyager, Procore, and CoStar Suite.']
  construction_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the structural system, primary materials, assembly method, and distinguishing characteristics of this construction type. Used by appraisers, underwriters, and development teams for technical documentation and cost estimation.']
  construction_type_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the construction type as used in property records, valuation reports, and development documentation (e.g., \'Wood Frame\', \'Structural Steel Frame\', \'Cast-In-Place Concrete\', \'Precast Concrete\', \'Tilt-Up Concrete\', \'Masonry\', \'Modular\', \'Mixed Construction\').']
  construction_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this construction type reference record (active = in use across enterprise systems; inactive = no longer assigned to new properties; deprecated = superseded by a newer classification). Controls availability in downstream system dropdowns and data entry forms.']
  cost_benchmark_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the PSF cost benchmarks (typical_cost_psf_low and typical_cost_psf_high) are valid. Enables cost escalation adjustments in Argus Enterprise DCF models and development CAPEX forecasts.']
  costar_construction_type_code varchar [note: 'Construction type code as used in CoStar Suite property database. Enables market comparables analysis and benchmarking by construction type in CoStar Analytics and supports comparable sales/lease data integration.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Standard depreciation method applied to fixed assets of this construction type for financial reporting purposes. Aligns with FASB ASC 360 and IFRS IAS 16 requirements and drives depreciation calculations in Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this construction type classification is valid and available for use in enterprise systems. Supports temporal data governance and ensures that historical property records reference the correct classification version.']
  energy_efficiency_profile varchar [note: 'Qualitative energy efficiency profile (low, moderate, high, very_high) for this construction type based on typical thermal performance, insulation characteristics, and building envelope properties. Supports ESG reporting, LEED/BREEAM certification tracking, and green building investment analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this construction type classification is no longer valid for new assignments. Null indicates no planned expiry. Supports reference data lifecycle management and downstream system validation rules.']
  exterior_wall_system varchar [note: 'Description of the typical exterior wall assembly for this construction type (e.g., \'Curtain Wall\', \'Brick Veneer\', \'Tilt-Up Concrete Panel\', \'Metal Panel\', \'EIFS\'). Informs building condition assessments, maintenance cost benchmarking, and insurance replacement cost estimates.']
  fire_resistance_rating_hours decimal(4,1) [note: 'Minimum fire resistance rating in hours for the primary structural assembly as required by the IBC for this construction type (e.g., 0, 1.0, 2.0, 3.0). Used by insurance underwriters to determine property insurance premiums and by development teams for code compliance verification.']
  floor_load_capacity_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical live load capacity of floor systems in pounds per square foot (PSF) for this construction type. Critical for industrial, warehouse, and data center property underwriting, tenant improvement design, and building condition assessments.']
  foundation_type varchar [note: 'Typical foundation system associated with this construction type (slab_on_grade, crawl_space, basement, pile, mat, caisson, other). Used in property condition assessments, maintenance planning in Building Engines, and environmental due diligence during acquisitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: slab_on_grade|crawl_space|basement|pile|mat|caisson|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hvac_compatibility varchar [note: 'Typical HVAC system types compatible with this construction type. Informs tenant improvement (TI) planning, building operations management in Building Engines, and energy efficiency assessments for ESG reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: central_air|split_system|vrf|chilled_water|ptac|mixed|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ibc_construction_type varchar [note: 'Official IBC construction type designation (Type I-A through Type V-B) reflecting fire-resistance requirements for structural elements. Critical for building permit compliance, insurance underwriting, and occupancy classification. Aligns with Procore project documentation and CoStar property data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type_I-A|Type_I-B|Type_II-A|Type_II-B|Type_III-A|Type_III-B|Type_IV|Type_V-A|Type_V-B — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_class_code varchar [note: 'Insurer-assigned class code for this construction type used in property insurance policy underwriting and premium calculation. Aligns with ISO Commercial Lines Manual classifications and supports portfolio-level insurance cost benchmarking.']
  is_combustible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this construction type uses combustible materials in its primary structural assembly (True = combustible; False = non-combustible). Directly impacts property insurance classification, fire suppression system requirements, and building code compliance.']
  iso_construction_class varchar [note: 'ISO construction class code (1–6) used by property insurance underwriters to classify buildings by construction material and fire resistance. Class 1 = Frame; Class 2 = Joisted Masonry; Class 3 = Non-Combustible; Class 4 = Masonry Non-Combustible; Class 5 = Modified Fire Resistive; Class 6 = Fire Resistive. Essential for insurance premium calculation and portfolio risk reporting.']
  leed_compatibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this construction type is compatible with LEED certification pathways (not_applicable, compatible, preferred). Preferred indicates the construction type is commonly used in LEED-certified projects. Supports ESG investment screening and green development planning.']
  max_building_height_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable building height in feet for this construction type as defined by IBC occupancy and construction type tables. Used in development feasibility analysis, zoning compliance review, and FAR calculations during property acquisition and development planning.']
  max_stories varchar [note: 'Maximum number of above-grade stories permitted for this construction type under IBC regulations. Supports development feasibility studies, density analysis, and investment underwriting in Argus Enterprise.']
  primary_material varchar [note: 'Dominant structural material used in this construction type (e.g., wood, steel, concrete, masonry, composite). Drives insurance premium calculations, maintenance cost benchmarks, and CAPEX reserve estimates in Argus Enterprise and Yardi Voyager. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wood|steel|concrete|masonry|composite|aluminum|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  procore_construction_type_code varchar [note: 'Construction type code as defined in Procore Construction Management system. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and Procore project specifications, RFIs, and submittal logs for development and renovation projects.']
  replacement_cost_multiplier decimal(6,4) [note: 'Relative cost multiplier applied to base construction cost benchmarks to estimate replacement cost for this construction type (e.g., 1.0000 = baseline; 1.2500 = 25% premium over baseline). Used in insurance replacement cost appraisals and Argus Enterprise valuation models.']
  roof_system varchar [note: 'Typical roof structural system and covering type associated with this construction type (e.g., \'Built-Up Roofing on Steel Deck\', \'Asphalt Shingle on Wood Truss\', \'TPO Membrane on Concrete\'). Used in preventive maintenance scheduling in Building Engines and CAPEX reserve planning.']
  seismic_performance_category varchar [note: 'ASCE 7 seismic design category (A through F) typically associated with this construction type, indicating relative seismic performance and risk. Used in property risk assessment, insurance underwriting, and ESG reporting for portfolio management.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer value controlling the display sequence of construction type options in UI dropdowns, reports, and data entry forms across Yardi Voyager, Procore, and other enterprise systems. Lower values appear first.']
  structural_system varchar [note: 'Primary lateral and gravity load-resisting structural system associated with this construction type. Informs seismic risk assessment, insurance underwriting, and structural engineering review during property acquisition and development. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bearing_wall|moment_frame|braced_frame|shear_wall|dual_system|flat_slab|space_frame|other — promote to reference product]']
  thermal_mass_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative thermal mass rating (low, moderate, high) indicating the heat storage capacity of this construction type. Impacts energy consumption modeling, LEED/BREEAM certification scoring, and OPEX forecasting for building operations.']
  typical_construction_duration_months varchar [note: 'Typical construction duration in months for a standard building of this construction type. Used in development project scheduling in Procore, investment return timing analysis in Argus Enterprise, and construction loan draw schedule planning.']
  typical_cost_psf_high decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the typical hard construction cost range in USD per square foot (PSF) for this construction type. Paired with typical_cost_psf_low to define the cost range bracket used in development pro forma modeling and insurance replacement cost estimation.']
  typical_cost_psf_low decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the typical hard construction cost range in USD per square foot (PSF) for this construction type. Used in development feasibility analysis, CAPEX budgeting, and replacement cost valuation in Argus Enterprise and Yardi Voyager.']
  typical_span_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical clear span capability in feet for the structural system of this construction type. Informs GLA/NLA planning, column-free floor plate design, and tenant improvement (TI) feasibility assessments for commercial leasing.']
  typical_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected economic useful life in years for buildings of this construction type. Used for depreciation schedules under FASB ASC 360 and IFRS 16, fixed asset management in Yardi Voyager, and replacement reserve modeling in investment underwriting.']
  wind_resistance_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative wind resistance performance rating (basic, enhanced, superior) for this construction type based on IBC and ASCE 7 wind load provisions. Supports property insurance underwriting and catastrophe risk modeling for portfolio management.']

  Note: 'Reference classification for building construction methods and structural systems — wood frame, steel frame, concrete (cast-in-place, precast, tilt-up), masonry, modular, and mixed construction. Includes fire resistance rating (Type I–V per IBC), structural system description, typical span capabilities, and applicable property type compatibility. Used by development, maintenance, and valuation domains for construction cost estimation, insurance underwriting, and building condition assessment.'
}

Table reference.space_use_type {
  space_use_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each space use type classification record. Primary key for the controlled vocabulary of leasable space use categories. [Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — _canonical_skip_reason: This is a small enumeration/code-list table providing controlled vocabulary for space use classifications; per-role minimum categories are exempt.]']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Space use types define area measurement standards (e.g., rentable square feet, gross leasable area) that must reference the enterprise UOM registry for consistency. Linking ensures that area benchmark']
  industry_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.industry_classification. Business justification: Space use types are associated with specific industry classifications that define the type of tenant occupying the space (e.g., retail space → NAICS 44-45 Retail Trade, office space → NAICS 52 Finance']
  ti_allowance_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Tenant improvement allowance benchmarks (ti_allowance_benchmark_psf) are denominated in a specific currency. Linking to the ISO 4217 currency registry ensures referential integrity for financial bench']
  ada_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility compliance requirements are mandated for spaces of this use type. Drives compliance checklist generation in Building Engines and regulatory reporting for HUD and local authorities.']
  applicable_lease_structures varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of lease structures typically associated with this space use type (e.g., \'NNN|gross|modified_gross|percentage_rent|ground_lease\'). Guides lease structuring in Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM and informs brokerage deal configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NNN|gross|modified_gross|percentage_rent|ground_lease|net|double_net — promote to reference product]']
  base_rent_benchmark_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Market benchmark base rent rate expressed in currency Per Square Foot (PSF) per annum for this space use type. Sourced from CoStar Suite comparable lease data and used in Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling and brokerage CMA preparation.']
  breakpoint_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the breakpoint type used in percentage rent calculations for this space use type — natural breakpoint (base rent divided by percentage rate), artificial breakpoint (negotiated fixed threshold), or not applicable. Relevant for retail and F&B use types in Yardi Voyager percentage rent schedules.']
  cam_inclusion_rule varchar [note: 'Defines whether Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges are included, excluded, partially included, or not applicable for spaces of this use type. Drives CAM reconciliation logic in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software lease accounting modules.']
  cam_recovery_method varchar [note: 'Specifies the CAM expense recovery methodology applicable to this space use type — pro-rata share, fixed amount, capped recovery, base-year stop, or not applicable. Used in Yardi Voyager CAM estimate and reconciliation schedules.']
  capex_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines the default Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) responsibility for structural improvements and major systems for this space use type — landlord, tenant, shared, or not applicable. Supports CAPEX planning in Procore and fixed asset management in Yardi Voyager.']
  co_tenancy_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether co-tenancy clauses (allowing rent reduction or termination rights if anchor tenants vacate) are typically applicable for this space use type. Primarily relevant for retail and food-and-beverage use types in shopping center lease structures.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this space use type classification became or becomes effective in the enterprise controlled vocabulary. Supports temporal validity tracking for data governance and audit purposes.']
  exclusive_use_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether exclusive use clauses (restricting the landlord from leasing adjacent space to competing tenants) are typically applicable for this space use type. Relevant for retail, food-and-beverage, and medical use types in lease negotiation and Salesforce CRM deal pipeline management.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this space use type classification ceases to be valid in the enterprise controlled vocabulary. Null indicates no planned expiry. Supports deprecation management and controlled vocabulary lifecycle governance.']
  external_code_costar varchar [note: 'Corresponding space use type code as used in CoStar Suite for comparable lease and property analytics. Enables direct mapping between enterprise reference data and CoStar market data for benchmarking and CMA preparation.']
  external_code_reso varchar [note: 'Corresponding space use type code as defined by the Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO) data dictionary standard. Enables interoperability with MLS systems, CoStar Suite, and other RESO-compliant platforms for data exchange and market benchmarking.']
  fire_egress_class varchar [note: 'International Building Code (IBC) occupancy classification for fire egress and life safety purposes associated with this space use type (e.g., assembly, business, mercantile, storage). Drives building compliance checks and preventive maintenance scheduling in Building Engines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: assembly|business|mercantile|industrial|storage|residential|hazardous|not_applicable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fit_out_category varchar [note: 'Standard fit-out delivery condition associated with this space use type — warm shell, cold shell, turnkey, as-is, or not applicable. Informs TI allowance benchmarking, construction scope in Procore, and lease negotiation defaults in Salesforce CRM.']
  food_service_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether food preparation and service activities are permitted for spaces of this use type. Relevant for distinguishing restaurant/F&B use from retail or office use types, and for triggering health department compliance requirements and grease trap infrastructure planning.']
  hvac_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines who bears responsibility for Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) systems for this space use type — landlord, tenant, shared, or not applicable. Drives lease clause defaults in DocuSign CLM and work order assignment rules in Building Engines.']
  leed_relevance varchar [note: 'Indicates the degree to which LEED certification criteria and ESG sustainability metrics are relevant to this space use type — high, medium, low, or not applicable. Supports ESG reporting, green building compliance, and LEED certification tracking per U.S. Green Building Council standards.']
  load_factor_typical decimal(5,4) [note: 'Typical load factor (also known as loss factor or add-on factor) expressed as a decimal ratio for this space use type, representing the difference between rentable and usable area (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Used in BOMA-compliant area calculations and rent roll reporting in Yardi Voyager.']
  max_contiguous_area decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum contiguous leasable area (in the applicable unit of measure — SQF or SQM) typically available for this space use type within a single building. Used in CoStar Suite availability searches and brokerage space matching for large-block tenant requirements.']
  measurement_standard varchar [note: 'Defines the area measurement methodology applicable to this space use type — Gross Leasable Area (GLA), Net Leasable Area (NLA), BOMA standard, IPMS standard, gross, or net. Governs how rentable square footage is calculated and reported for rent roll and lease administration purposes.']
  min_divisible_area decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum leasable area (in the applicable unit of measure — SQF or SQM) to which a space of this use type can be subdivided. Governs space planning and demised space configuration in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software space inventory management.']
  occupancy_density_typical decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical occupancy density expressed as area per person (SQF or SQM per person) for this space use type. Used in building capacity planning, HVAC sizing, fire safety compliance, and ESG workplace analytics.']
  opex_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines the default Operating Expenditure (OPEX) responsibility allocation for this space use type — landlord, tenant, shared, or not applicable. Informs lease structure defaults, CAM billing configuration in Yardi Voyager, and NOI modeling in Argus Enterprise.']
  outdoor_area_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outdoor or exterior leasable area (e.g., patio, terrace, rooftop deck, signage fascia) is applicable and measurable for this space use type. Affects GLA/NLA calculation methodology and lease area definitions in Yardi Voyager.']
  parking_ratio_per_1000sqf decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard parking ratio (number of parking stalls per 1,000 square feet of leasable area) associated with this space use type. Used in property development feasibility, zoning compliance, and lease negotiation for office, retail, and medical use types.']
  percentage_rent_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether percentage rent (overage rent based on tenant gross sales) is a standard feature of leases for this space use type. Typically true for retail and food-and-beverage use types. Drives percentage rent clause inclusion in Yardi Voyager lease setup.']
  property_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of property types to which this space use type applies (e.g., \'office_building|mixed_use|retail_center|medical_office\'). Constrains valid space use assignments during lease and space inventory setup in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: office_building|retail_center|mixed_use|medical_office|industrial|multifamily|hospitality|specialty — promote to reference product]']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Typical rent escalation mechanism associated with this space use type — fixed step increases, CPI-indexed, percentage-based, flat (no escalation), or not applicable. Informs rent schedule configuration in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise cash flow projections.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting revenue recognition method applicable to lease income for this space use type — straight-line, cash basis, percentage of completion, or not applicable. Governs lease accounting entries in Yardi Voyager GL and SAP S/4HANA financial consolidation per FASB ASC 842 and IFRS 16.']
  rou_asset_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Right-of-Use (ROU) asset must be recognized on the lessee\'s balance sheet for leases of this space use type under FASB ASC 842 or IFRS 16. Drives lease accounting classification logic in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software lease accounting modules.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric display sort order for presenting space use type options in UI dropdowns, reports, and rent roll templates. Allows business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID sequence.']
  space_use_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the space use type record in the controlled vocabulary — active (in use), inactive (no longer applied to new leases), deprecated (replaced by another code), or pending_review (under governance review). Governs availability of the code in downstream lease and property systems.']
  sublease_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subleasing is typically permitted for spaces of this use type. Drives default sublease clause inclusion in DocuSign CLM lease templates and sublease approval workflow configuration in Yardi Voyager.']
  ti_allowance_benchmark_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Market benchmark Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance expressed in currency Per Square Foot (PSF) for this space use type. Provides a reference value for lease negotiation, deal underwriting in Argus Enterprise, and TI budget planning in Procore.']
  typical_lease_term_max_months varchar [note: 'Maximum typical lease term in months for this space use type, reflecting market norms. Used alongside the minimum to define the expected lease term range for deal structuring, WALT analysis, and Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling.']
  typical_lease_term_min_months varchar [note: 'Minimum typical lease term in months for this space use type, reflecting market norms (e.g., 12 months for storage, 60 months for office, 120 months for anchor retail). Used in Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) benchmarking and deal underwriting.']
  use_category varchar [note: 'Broad asset class category grouping the space use type (e.g., commercial, residential, mixed_use, industrial, hospitality, specialty). Aligns with CoStar Suite and Argus Enterprise asset class taxonomies for portfolio-level reporting and investment analysis.']
  use_type_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-recognized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the space use type (e.g., OFF, RET, F&B, MED, FIT, STG, PRK, RTF, SGN, CAM). Used as the business key in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and CoStar Suite for space inventory classification and rent roll reporting.']
  use_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the space use classification, including typical tenant profile, permitted activities, and distinguishing characteristics from adjacent use types. Supports training, onboarding, and data governance documentation.']
  use_type_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the space use classification (e.g., \'Office\', \'Retail\', \'Restaurant / Food and Beverage\', \'Medical / Dental\', \'Fitness\', \'Storage\', \'Parking\', \'Rooftop\', \'Signage\', \'Common Area\'). Displayed in lease administration, rent roll, and brokerage reporting.']
  zoning_use_class varchar [note: 'Standard zoning use classification code or category associated with this space use type (e.g., B1, A1, A3, D1 under UK Use Classes Order; or C-1, C-2, O-1 under US zoning codes). Supports regulatory compliance checks and development feasibility analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product if jurisdiction-specific zoning codes are required]']

  Note: 'Controlled vocabulary for leasable space use classifications within a building — office, retail, restaurant/food and beverage, medical/dental, fitness, storage, parking, rooftop, signage, and common area. Defines GLA/NLA measurement methodology per use type, applicable lease structures, TI allowance benchmarks, and CAM inclusion/exclusion rules. Used by lease, property, and brokerage domains for space inventory management and rent roll reporting.'
}

Table reference.country {
  country_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the country record. Serves as the primary key and top-level node in the geographic hierarchy referenced across all real estate domains. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a controlled vocabulary / code-list table providing ISO country codes and geographic metadata; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  alpha2_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., US, GB, DE). Used as the primary short-form identifier in lease agreements, tenant records, property addresses, and regulatory filings across Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  alpha3_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used in financial consolidation reporting in SAP S/4HANA, CMBS/RMBS documentation, and cross-border investment portfolio reporting in Argus Enterprise.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'FATF-aligned AML risk classification for the country. Used in KYC/AML due diligence for real estate transactions, tenant onboarding, and cross-border investment approvals. High-risk countries require enhanced due diligence per SOX and SEC compliance requirements.']
  area_measurement_body varchar [note: 'Name of the primary standards body governing property area measurement methodology in the country (e.g., BOMA, IPMS, RICS). Ensures consistent GLA/NLA calculations for PSF rent benchmarking and CAM reconciliation.']
  breeam_recognized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether BREEAM certification is officially recognized or incentivized in the country. Relevant for ESG compliance, green building asset classification, and sustainability reporting for European and international portfolios.']
  capital_city varchar [note: 'Name of the country\'s capital city. Used as a geographic reference point for market research, CoStar comparable data alignment, and investment market classification.']
  capital_gains_tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether capital gains tax applies to real estate dispositions in the country. Affects IRR and NPV modeling in Argus Enterprise, investment advisory recommendations, and property acquisition/disposition strategy.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Official short-form country name in English as defined by ISO 3166-1 (e.g., United States, United Kingdom, Germany). Used as the display label in property records, lease agreements, tenant profiles, and portfolio reports.']
  country_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the country record within the enterprise reference data registry. \'active\' indicates the country is in use across operational systems; \'inactive\' indicates no current portfolio exposure; \'deprecated\' indicates the country code has been superseded (e.g., post-dissolution or renaming per ISO 3166-1 updates).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country record was first created in the enterprise reference data registry. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX financial controls for reference data governance.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary legal tender of the country (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used as the default currency for property valuations, lease rent schedules, NOI calculations, and CAP rate analysis in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise.']
  dialing_code varchar [note: 'International telephone dialing prefix for the country (e.g., +1 for USA, +44 for UK). Used for formatting tenant and owner contact phone numbers in Salesforce CRM and Yardi Voyager tenant records.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this country record is valid and active within the enterprise reference data registry. Supports temporal data management and ISO 3166-1 change tracking (e.g., country reclassifications, new sovereign states).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this country record is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended active record. Used to manage deprecated or superseded country codes following ISO 3166-1 updates (e.g., dissolution of a country or code reassignment).']
  esg_reporting_framework varchar [note: 'Primary ESG reporting framework mandated or commonly adopted for real estate assets in the country (e.g., GRI, TCFD, SFDR, local regulation). Drives sustainability data collection, LEED/BREEAM certification tracking, and ESG disclosure requirements for REITs and institutional investors.']
  fatf_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country is a member of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF). Relevant for AML/KYC compliance in cross-border real estate investment transactions, REIT capital flows, and CMBS/RMBS structuring.']
  foreign_ownership_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether foreign nationals and entities are permitted to own real property in the country. Critical for investment advisory, portfolio acquisition strategy, and REIT eligibility assessments in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  foreign_ownership_restriction_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any restrictions, conditions, or limitations on foreign ownership of real property in the country (e.g., maximum ownership percentage, restricted zones, required local partner). Supports investment advisory and compliance review.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies to real estate operations conducted in or with residents of this country. Drives data handling policies for tenant PII in Salesforce CRM, Yardi Voyager, and DocuSign CLM.']
  ifrs_adopted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country has adopted International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) for financial reporting. Determines applicable accounting treatment for investment property (IAS 40), lease liabilities (IFRS 16), and financial instrument disclosures in SAP S/4HANA.']
  land_registration_system varchar [note: 'Type of land title registration system in use in the country. Affects title insurance requirements, ALTA policy applicability, due diligence procedures for property acquisition, and LTV calculations for debt instruments.']
  lease_accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The applicable lease accounting standard for real estate leases in the country. Drives right-of-use asset recognition, lease liability classification, and financial statement presentation in Yardi Voyager lease accounting and SAP S/4HANA financial consolidation.']
  leed_recognized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LEED certification is officially recognized or incentivized by the country\'s regulatory or governmental bodies. Supports ESG reporting, green building compliance tracking, and sustainability-linked financing decisions.']
  measurement_standard varchar [note: 'Predominant unit-of-measure standard used for property area measurement in the country. Determines whether GLA, NLA, and GRI calculations are expressed in SQF (square feet) or SQM (square meters) in Yardi Voyager, CoStar Suite, and lease administration systems.']
  multiple_time_zones boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country spans multiple time zones. Relevant for scheduling lease payment processing, maintenance dispatch, and financial period close operations across geographically dispersed property portfolios.']
  numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit ISO 3166-1 numeric country code (e.g., 840 for USA, 826 for GBR). Used in UN trade statistics, ALTA title insurance documentation, and cross-system integration where alpha codes may conflict.']
  official_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the country as recognized by the United Nations (e.g., United States of America, United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland). Used in legal contracts, SNDA agreements, and regulatory filings requiring formal jurisdiction identification.']
  portfolio_exposure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enterprise currently holds active real estate assets, leases, or investments in this country. Used to filter active jurisdictions in portfolio reporting, AUM dashboards, and regulatory compliance monitoring.']
  primary_valuation_standard varchar [note: 'The predominant property valuation standard applied in the country. Determines the methodology used for appraisals, CAP rate benchmarking, DCF modeling, and NAV/GAV calculations in Argus Enterprise and investment advisory services.']
  property_law_system varchar [note: 'Legal system governing property rights and real estate transactions in the country. Determines applicable title transfer procedures, SNDA enforceability, ground lease structures, and tenure type classifications used in lease administration and investment advisory.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region or continent grouping for the country (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Middle East, Africa, Latin America). Used for portfolio segmentation, AUM reporting, and investment strategy classification in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: North America|Europe|Asia-Pacific|Middle East|Africa|Latin America|Oceania|South Asia — promote to reference product]']
  reit_framework_exists boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country has an established legal and regulatory framework for Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) or equivalent listed property vehicles. Used in investment portfolio structuring and AUM classification in Argus Enterprise.']
